#### maya, surrender, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xy0njl-QK-A?t=913
**Verse References:** BG 7.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you surrender to me, if you become my devotee, I will enable you to cross the influence of maya, I will release you from this entanglement in this material."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that unlike visible bonds, the ropes binding us are invisible, and despite material success, entanglement persists, quoting Krishna from the Gita, who says that this Maya is difficult to surmount, but can be overcome through surrender and devotion.

Unlike criminals, unlike a cow, the example which I see, unlike the dog, we see people you know taking for a walk, they all have a leash, unlike that this rope which binds us in this material world is invisible. We are not able to see it, but even if they want to they cannot implement. They get wonderful you know, you can read about lot of success stories, many a times I will not be very pessimist, many a times it doesn't work because all of us are entangled and there is something which is binding us. Krishna in the Gita says deviesha gunamai mamamaya dhuratya, this maya we have come under the influence of maya, Krishna says it is my energy, deviesha gunamai mamamaya dhuratya, dhuratya means it is difficult to surmount, it is not easy to get release. Then Krishna says mam evayye prapadyante maya metam taranti te, if you surrender to me, if you become my devotee, I will enable you to cross the influence of maya, I will release you from this entanglement in this material.

So therefore Krishna says mom aviyah Papa diante if you surrender to me Yoshi energy, so I repeat the first principle is pray for release that's why it is important that we have to offer prayers that's why it is important that we have to Worship the Lord that's why it is important that we have to depend on the Lord

#### devotion, struggle, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xy0njl-QK-A?t=1243
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Somehow there was a feeling of incompleteness."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares a personal story of his initial struggles to visit the ISKCON temple in Bangalore due to a demanding work schedule and long commute, highlighting his longing for spiritual connection and the feeling of incompleteness.

I remember when I first moved to Bangalore I was working in a small company it was in Indira Nagar it was on a very prime location CMH Road Indira Nagar and few days I was working but I had a great longing to go to ISKCON temple because while I was doing my engineering in Kurukshetra I had started going to the temple so it was a great longing that I should also visit the temple attend the Aarthis meet the devotees and then I tried asking people those days internet was not as prevalent as it is today unfortunately I couldn't Google but nevertheless through some source I could figure out where the temple is at that time ISKCON was relatively very very small in Bangalore and not many people knew about it it was not as popular as it is today so I got to know that this temple is in Rajaji Nagar and one day after my office hours after five o'clock 5 30 sorry six o'clock I decided that today I should go to the temple it was a working day I started off from my office and this was fairly long distance between Indira Nagar and Rajaji Nagar where the current temple is by the time I reached the temple I started from office at six o'clock by the time I reached the temple it was 8 15 just before the Lord is given rest for the day that was that had begun and while I entered the small palaki which is taken before the Lord is given rest was happening fortunately I could have darshan and 8 30 the temple closed I tried this for a few more days but it was very exhausting imagine whole day you're working 6 6 30 you leave the office by the time you reach temple it's 8 8 15 8 30 2 hours in traffic and I had a great longing that I should visit the temple somehow there was a feeling of incompleteness I did that for eight times ten times and one day i remember i pray to the lord it's very difficult how is it possible that i come all the way traveling two and a half hours and then After 8.30, after darshan, spend 40-45 minutes, again go back to the place where I am staying. By the time I reach back, it's 10 o'clock. How is it going to work?

---
#### divine intervention, prayer, opportunity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xy0njl-QK-A?t=1457
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "And this company, you won't believe, this company was walking distance from Descon Temple in Rajaji Nagar."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa recounts how, feeling hopeless, he prayed to the Lord, and soon after, a colleague showed him a newspaper advertisement for a job opening, which, despite the application deadline being over, he reluctantly applied for, leading to an interview and the unbelievable discovery that the company was within walking distance of the ISKCON temple.

So I was feeling very sad, very despondent, very hopeless. So one day while I was working in the office, a friend of mine, a colleague, showed me a newspaper advertisement. It was basically an opening for software engineers and very interestingly, he showed me an ad after he has applied. He said, I have applied for this job, have you also applied? And when I saw that ad, the last date for application was already over and that's why probably he very enthusiastically shared that ad with me because this is a competitive wage. So after the last date for application was over, he just showed that paper to me, have you applied? Are you aware of this opening? So I felt sorry that, you know, already the last date is over but very reluctantly, even if it is over, even if there is 0.1% chance that they may consider the application, even if it is submitted after the last date of application, I applied. I sent an email and applied. And after about 10 days or so, I got a call for an interview. And this company, you won't believe, this company was walking distance from Descon Temple in Rajaji Nagar. Literally walking distance. 10 minutes walk. 15 minutes walk. Wow!

#### divine arrangement, interview, honesty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xy0njl-QK-A?t=1561
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Are you trying to cheat us?"
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa describes the location of the company and recounts the interview experience, where a misunderstanding about his current salary led to accusations of dishonesty, and despite not performing well in the technical questions, he remained hopeful.

Rajaji Nagar in those days was not like what it is today. Now it is very developed. The place where Sheraton Hotel is there, that Brigade, whole big township is there, that's the place where Kiloskar had a very big setup, KEC, Kiloskar Electrics and they also had a very big computer division which used to cater to all the requirements of the Kiloskar group. All the IT requirements. So I went for an interview and they asked me few questions, few technical questions, few personal questions. And one of the questions was, what's your present salary? So I told them my present salary is so and so. And then one of them, there was a panel of interviewers. About four of them. So one of them said, what you have mentioned in your CV is something different. And he actually got a little worked up, got a little angry and said, are you trying to cheat us? Don't you think we can make out? He became literally angry as if there was an integrity breach. I tried to cheat them, which is not the case. Generally, you know, in salary they are different. There is a difference. There is a variable component, there is a fixed component, there is CTC, there is a gross salary, the net salary. So there was some confusion because of that. And then they asked few technical questions and I didn't do well. Because of that thing, you know, it really rattled them and rattled me. And after that, few technical questions were asked and, you know, I couldn't do well. So I was quite disappointed after the interview and I was very, very clear. That I will not be selected.

#### divine grace, devotion, opportunity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xy0njl-QK-A?t=1741
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In spite of doing very badly in the technical interview and also irritating some interview panel members, as if I have cheated them, in seven days, I was having appointment letter in my hand."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares that despite a poor interview performance, he received an appointment letter within a week, emphasizing the power of prayer and divine arrangement, and recounts how the job allowed him to visit the temple daily after work, fulfilling his desire for spiritual connection.

You know, sometimes when you give an exam, it becomes very clear that you have not done well. You don't have to really wait for the results. You actually know the result. Mind you, I remember I told you about, you know, in Delhi when I was looking for my dream job, I always used to pray, Lord, give me this, if you so desire. Always I had that prayer. And there are many, many cases where I have done well. I have done exceptionally well in the test, in the interview, but I haven't got a job. But in this case, in spite of doing very badly in the technical interview and also irritating some interview panel members, as if I have cheated them, in seven days, I was having appointment letter in my hand. And to cut the story short, I joined that company. And every day, you won't believe, every day at 5.30, our office would get over. Fortunately, I was in a group, workload was relatively manageable. Sometimes in software companies, IT companies. Even though the official timing is six months. By the time you actually walk out of the office, it's 8.30, 9, 9.30. Fortunately, our group, the workload was quite manageable. At 5.30, I would walk out of my office after doing complete justice to the job which was assigned to me. Not that I was struggling from my duty. And 5.40, 5.45, I would be in the temple. Neat, two hours, three hours, I would spend in the temple, even have my dinner there, and then go back home.

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "As part of our spiritual growth, we realize that how tiny and insignificant we are, otherwise in this material world, we tend to be very proud."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that Krishna is seated in everyone's heart and provides knowledge, remembrance, and forgetfulness, emphasizing the importance of remembering the Lord and realizing our insignificance and dependence on Him for spiritual growth.

Krishna says I am seated in everyone's heart matthas mirTA g компании which are from me comes knowledge and secondary knowledge to Minority. remembrance and forgetfulness. If we are intensely trying to remember the Lord, Krishna says, from me comes knowledge, remembrance and forgetfulness. Krishna will give us the right remembrance. Krishna will give us the right inspiration. What steps we should take? We are like the floating straws on the waves of the river. Just like the straws. How much ability that straw has? How much freedom does the straw has? One wave comes, it's just carried away by that wave. So, waves of Kala are tossing us in this material world. As part of our spiritual growth, we realize that how tiny and insignificant we are, otherwise in this material world, we tend to be very proud. I have so and so ability, I can speak well, I can do this, I can do that and we become proud. In fact, when we get spiritually advanced, we realize how small, how insignificant, how tiny, how weak we are, how dependent we are on the Lord for everything.

Hare Krishna, welcome all of you for today's Gita live session. All of us day in and day out have many choices as far as what to eat, what not to eat, and in today's world it becomes quite confusing when we are overloaded with all kinds of information. On one side you will have videos on YouTube and articles saying that you should eat this, it's very good for your health.

So sattvikahar, what is sattvikahar, what is food in the mode of goodness? I repeat, foods which give strength, health, happiness and satisfaction are classified in the mode of goodness. #### anger management, food habits, lifestyle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=512
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Looks like the anger has something to do with your lifestyle and also your food habits."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about a short-tempered HR executive named Anushka Mehta, whose anger issues were resolved by incorporating Satvik foods into her lifestyle after consulting a nutritionist and Ayurvedic doctor.

Many years back I came to understand the history of nature. I came across an article in Hindustan Times about a lady who was an HR executive in a multinational company. Her name was Anushka Mehta. She was very short tempered, would get angry, small small things. If she misses a ride to the office, she will get angry. If someone speaks something wrong, she will get angry. If things don't happen the way she wants it to happen, she will get angry. If the office boy doesn't bring things on time to her table, she will get angry. And soon many of her friends, colleagues in the office, they had so to say, branded her as a person very, very short tempered. In fact, she lost many of her friends because they were afraid of her. She also realized her mistake. It's not that when a person is short tempered or he was conscious of, she was conscious of her behavior, but she was helpless. She in fact attended many workshops, some seminars on anger management. Some counselling sessions also she went for. Just that, so that she can get some control over her anger, but things were not improving. And it started even affecting her performance because even in the morning when she would get up, there would be some kind of anger in her mind-body system. And even in the night before she goes to bed, something agitated her. Something irritating her. She was not at peace. She knew about it, but did not know any way out of it. So one day the mother, mother also was quite scared of her. So one day the mother casually said, looks like the anger has something to do with your lifestyle and also your food habits.

After all mothers, they understand our nature better. They have seen us right from our childhood growing up. So she said, looks like your anger has something to do with your lifestyle and also your food habits. So she consulted a nutritionist and she also consulted an Ayurvedic doctor and they suggested some changes in her food habits and her lifestyle. And after incorporating some of the Satvik foods in her lifestyle, in her eating habits, she saw that there was a drastic change in her behavior, in her attitude and also her ability to control her temper. So as I mentioned earlier, food plays a very, very important role in our life. In our, in our temperament, the way we deal with people around us. So as I mentioned here in the Gita, Krishna is saying, such foods bring clarity of thought, they are pleasing to heart and gives strength, health and happiness. Examples of Satvik Ahar, cereals, fruits, vegetables, juices, legumes, nuts, sprouted seeds and whole grains.

#### Rajasik Ahar, passion, taste vs nutrition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=753
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Rajas mode of eating focuses on taste, not much on nutrition."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Rajasik Ahar, food in the mode of passion, characterized by being too bitter, sour, salty, hot, pungent, dry, and burning, causing distress, anxiety, and disease, with a focus on taste rather than nutrition.

These all come under the category of Satvik Ahar. Then there is another category called Rajasik Ahar. Food in the mode of passion. Now Krishna says, food, those are too bitter, too sour, salty, hot, pungent, dry and burning fall under this category. Such foods cause distress, anxiety and disease. Many of present day lifestyle diseases have to do with what people are consuming. Rajas mode of eating focuses on taste, not much on nutrition. So the predominant emphasis is on taste. What is pleasing for the tongue is what we want to eat. And they are saying what is good for your tongue may not be good for your stomach. May not be, although we have many Satvik dishes which can be cooked very nicely and can be very pleasing to tongue also.

#### onion and garlic, holistic health, mind-body-soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=967
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Although they may be good for the body at a physical level, they may have some medicinal properties. But when we choose our food, it is not just our physical body which has to be taken into account."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the question of why onion and garlic are discouraged despite their medicinal properties, explaining that while they may benefit the physical body, a holistic approach to food choices must also consider the mental, emotional, and spiritual well-being.

What is the rationale? Onion and garlic come under Tamasik Ahar or at best Rajasik Ahar. Although they may be good for the body at a physical level, they may have some medicinal properties. But when we choose our food, it is not just our physical body which has to be taken into account. We have a mental body, our mind. We have our emotional body. We also have our soul. So when we eat, it's not just for our physical body. Yes there may be some foodstuff which may be very good for the body. They may have high protein or high nutritional content or medicinal properties.

---
#### non-veg, soul degradation, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=1024
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Non-veg or eggs may be good for the body. But we know that it degrades the soul. It involves killing animals which incurs sin."
**Summary:** The speaker uses non-vegetarian food as an example, acknowledging its potential physical benefits but emphasizing its negative impact on the soul due to the act of killing animals and incurring sin.

But we have to consider holistically such food should also be good for the mind, should also be good for our emotional body, should also be good for the soul. For example non-veg or eggs may be good for the body. You may have some articles with saying the nutritional value of these foodstuffs may be good for the body. Maybe you can have an article which proves that yes it's good for the body. But we know that it degrades the soul. It involves killing animals which incurs sin.

#### Krishna consciousness, taste change, spiritual evolution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=1067
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When a person comes to Satva Guna and also becomes Krishna Conscious, he automatically experiences a higher taste. And his taste for lower things, abominable things, wrong things, things which are not good for the soul, automatically fails off."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a personal anecdote about his transition to Krishna consciousness and the subsequent change in his food preferences, illustrating how spiritual evolution leads to an automatic preference for Satvik food and a diminishing taste for lower, abominable things.

Many years ago when I was in college, I used to during vacations go to Delhi. And my cousin, sister, she would eagerly wait for me to come. And I was, before my coming to Krishna Consciousness, I was a big foodie and would love street food. So I would go to Delhi and I would have some pocket money and she would join me and we would go and eat all kinds of street food. Gol Gappas, Aloo Tikki, Paani Puri. And she would eagerly wait when Bhaiya will come and she will have a nice time. And invariably we would have our stomach upset at the end of the day. We would have eaten so much. In the final year, as I mentioned earlier, I came to Krishna Consciousness and I started practicing the principles of Bhagavad Gita. So there was a change in my eating habits also. In fact, it automatically happens when a person comes to Satva Guna and also becomes Krishna Conscious. He automatically experiences a higher taste. And his taste for lower things, abominable things, wrong things, things which are not good for the soul, automatically fails off. We don't have to artificially give up. It becomes very natural.

#### taste differences, spiritual growth, gunas

So when I came to Bangalore, this was in the year 1992, she was very, very eager that Bhaiya is coming and she had fond memories of when I used to come to Delhi. We used to go out and eat lot of things. So she was also expecting that Bhaiya is going to come and we are going to have good time, everyday going out and eating different kinds of things. She was quite young. But Bhaiya had changed. I was very fond of coconut water, coconut water. I was very fond of fruits and salads. And by that time she had started even consuming non-veg. So she was quite disappointed. On one side, we are going together but I want to take coconut water and she wants to go to some food joints, Darshanis, to eat some food. I want to eat some chaat masala and all this golgappas and I don't feel for it. So very soon we reconciled that now the tastes don't match. And she also understood that because I am getting deep into spirituality. Gone case. So, the taste, the choices we make lot depends on the gunas influencing us.

#### spiritual evolution, Satvikahar, Nafi delicacy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=1314
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When a person becomes spiritually evolved, spiritually grows, automatically he will be drawn towards Satvikahar."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that spiritual evolution naturally leads to a preference for Satvikahar and then shares a story about Srila Prabhupada's reaction to the Burmese delicacy called Nafi, which is made from decomposed meat refuse.

But there are people who are having taste for that because they are in the influence of such modes that they are drawn towards Tamasikahar, drawn towards Rajasikahar. When a person becomes spiritually evolved, spiritually grows, automatically he will be drawn towards Satvikahar. So, this change happens with everyone who is practicing spiritual principles seriously. Srila Prabhupada once went to Burma. And in Burma, Srila Prabhupada was staying in somebody's place, some devotee's place. And, we have a concept of festivals, kirtans, inviting lot of people and then having a feast, love feast. In Burma, there is a delicacy called Nafi. What they do is, Burmese people, when they get non-vegetarian foodstuffs into their homes, like fish, eggs, or chicken, there are some parts of that meat which cannot be consumed or eaten. I don't want to get into details. So, what they do is, they take those parts and put it in a jar, keep the jar in one side of the house, and let that decompose. And, over many days, in fact, over many months, it decomposes and ferments, just like the grapes ferment and becomes wine. So, they ferment, they decompose, and a liquid starts oozing out from that, so called, refuse of the meat, which cannot be otherwise consumed. And, then they strain that liquid, and especially on festival days, or on some special occasion, that liquid is also served along with the food, and that's a delicacy. That's called Nafi.

---
#### Prabhupada, Nafi, taste, gunas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=1482
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "How degraded this taste is."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of Prabhupada in Burma, explaining Prabhupada's disgust upon learning about Nafi, and contrasting it with the neighbors' complaints about the aroma of ghee used for frying puris, illustrating the difference in appreciation based on the gunas.

So, when Prabhupada got to know about it, he said, how degraded this taste is. Imagine, a dead animal, the refuse of that, which cannot be otherwise consumed, is put in a jar, it decomposes, putrefies, ferments, and then you strain that liquid, and then you drink it. So, when devotees told about this to Prabhupada, he was completely astonished, if this can be eaten. And, when Prabhupada was there, the devotees were eager cooking a big feast, celebrating the moment that Prabhupada has come to Burma, was with them. And, they were frying some puris. And, that particular day, they were frying the puris in ghee, pure ghee, because they wanted to offer it to the Lord, and then offer to Srila Prabhupada. And, the neighbors started coming in, because that frying in a ghee, that, you know, the smell, the aroma, it was intolerable for them. So, they started coming in, and started complaining, what kind of smell is this? What kind of stench is this? What are you doing? And, mind you, it was pure ghee. So, this shows that, how people with different tastes, people who are in tamo guna, they cannot appreciate, the taste of foods, which are in sattu guna.

#### travel, food choices, principles, circumstances

So, if we have to travel, many of us, who are practicing, Krishna consciousness, we may be vegetarians. So, how do we manage, when we are traveling? Many people, do not have that, sense. So, they say, so long as, it is vegetarian, so long as, it is a Jain meal, it's perfectly fine. Yes, if we have no option, we can, resort to a Jain meal, we can, resort to a vegetarian, food cooked, in a restaurant, outside. If we have no option, in fact, when we travel, either we pack something, along with us, take it with us, or we, take fruits, or some kind of, salads, which do not involve, cooking. And yes, if, it's a long journey, there is no other option, we do, sometimes, compromise, and take some, vegetarian foodstuff. Just like in the Gita, in the 12th chapter, Krishna says, if you cannot do this, do this. If you cannot do this, do this. If you cannot do this, do this. If you cannot do this, do this. If you cannot do this, do this. So, ideal would be, food, cooked by, a devotee, offered to the Lord, pure food, in Satva Guna, in fact, more than Satva Guna, that should be the ideal. That will have, a boost to our consciousness, towards Krishna consciousness. Now again, not everyone, can practice at that level. Then again, the next best, the next best, the next best, we have to use our intelligence. Everyone's, circumstances are unique, everyone's circumstances, are different. So, we have to use our, we should know the principle, and then, we have to, keeping that principle in mind, we should find an application.

#### Sadhu, Brahman Bhoj, stealing, guilt
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=2029
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Why did this, thought of stealing a plate, came in my mind? How degraded I am."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about a Sadhu who stole a steel plate after being served at a Brahman Bhoj, highlighting the guilt and degraded thought that arose in his mind.

One time, there was a Sadhu, who was invited. You know, generally, we have something called as, Brahman Bhoj, when there's a death anniversary, or, some other occasion, the Sadhus are invited, and they are, fed. So, one Sadhu, along with his colleagues, were invited, by a lady. It was the death anniversary, of her husband. And, she, cooked, for all of them. It was a lavish, feast. Lot of items, were served. And, generally, the Sadhus are served on, you know, the leaf plates. On that day, the lady somehow managed, it was a village. The lady managed, to serve them, on a steel plate. And these Sadhus, steel plate, was a big thing. Generally, they are used to eating on, that, patras, you know, made of leaves, leaf plates. So, one of the Sadhus, after eating, slowly, put that steel plate, in her Jhola, in his Jhola, and, walked away. After a while, the Sadhu, started thinking, what is this, these things. And he started thinking, what did I do. Why did this, thought of stealing a plate, came in my mind? How degraded I am.

#### Maya, temptations, forgiveness, ingredients
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=2127
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "I stole the ingredients, I stole the plates just to feed all of you."
**Summary:** The story continues with the Sadhu returning the plate and discovering that the lady had also stolen the ingredients and plates, highlighting the importance of the ingredients and the person cooking.

In fact, It happens! With devotees, sometimes mistakes do happen! Maya will, throw up different temptations, and then we have to, be alert. The intelligence has to be very sharp in discerning what is right, what is wrong, what is violating some spiritual principles. So this sadhu started thinking, why did I do that? How did this degraded thought came in my mind of stealing this plate? And he could not sleep that night. There was a guilt feeling killing him from within. Next day morning he got up and went back to that lady and said, I beg forgiveness. I stole this plate yesterday. I have come here to return this plate to you. And he asked that lady, by the way, I just want to ask you, where did you get this? Where did you get these ingredients from? And who cooked this feast? So this lady said, actually I wanted to feed all of you and I had no means. It was the first death anniversary of my husband. So actually I stole the ingredients, stole these plates and somehow managed to cook all these. Perfectionists. I thought that feeding the brahmanas is the highest thing. Even I had guilt consciousness but then I stole the ingredients, I stole the plates just to feed all of you.

### Form of God | Bhagavad Gita Chapter 11 Verses 1- 5 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Bhagavad Gita Chapter 11, Arjuna's request, Krishna's opulences
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "My opulences are infinite, it's practically impossible to list all the opulences, all the potencies."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Chapter 11 of the Bhagavad Gita, focusing on Arjuna's request to see Krishna's opulences and Krishna's statement about the infiniteness of His potencies.

So, today we are starting with the 11th chapter. So, the title for the discussion today is Form of God, based on the teachings, instructions, guidance received from Srila Prabhupada, the founder-acharya of worldwide Hare Krishna movement. Arjuna said, 'I have heard your instruction on confidential spiritual matters, which you have so kindly delivered unto me, and my illusion is now dispelled.' So, in the 10th chapter, Arjuna requested Krishna to explain to him, to elaborate, to reveal his opulences, his greatness, his glories. And then when he saw that, how Krishna said that, my opulences are infinite, it's practically impossible to list all the opulences, all the potencies.

#### Krishna's opulences, Arjuna's understanding, Dispelling illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=60

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment continues discussing Krishna's opulences, highlighting specific examples and Arjuna's declaration that his illusion is dispelled after hearing Krishna's instructions.

And then some prominent ones, the most glorious ones, were listed out. Krishna says, 'I am the Himalayas, of the mountains, of the unmoving things, I am the Himalayas.' So, all those which are prominent among, let's say, the unmoving thing, among the trees, among the mantras, among the human beings, among the horses, the prominent one, Krishna says, is me. Among the Pandavas. Among the Pandavas, what does Krishna answer? Arjuna. So, now Arjuna is saying, in reply to what he has heard from Krishna, he is saying, 'I have heard your instruction on confidential spiritual matters, which you have so kindly delivered unto me, and my illusion is now dispelled.'

#### Understanding God, Source of knowledge, Limitations of material science
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=134

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that the right understanding of God comes from God Himself, as exemplified by Arjuna, and contrasts this with the limitations of material science in comprehending the cause of the universe.

If we have to have a right understanding about God, from where do we have to get that understanding? From God Himself, just like Arjuna got. He is saying, now my illusion is dispelled. Without that right understanding, which comes in a proper channel, we cannot fathom on our own strength, the right understanding of God. Even big, big scientists, when it comes to what is the cause of this universe, they are breaking their head. It's not clear. A lot of speculation, but maybe it is like this. Very eminent scientists, very eminent scholars. Maybe it is like this. And as we are improving the understanding through various experiments, various observation, maybe it is like this. Different theories are propounded. And sometimes later, maybe a few years later, those theories are, so to say, rescinded or discarded, because we have a better understanding of what could be the possible cause of creation. So, here Krishna, Arjuna is saying, you have so kindly delivered unto me, and my illusion is now dispelled.

It starts with an element of faith, but in due course of time it becomes pratyaksha. Rajvidya Rajguhyam. Pratyaksha Vagmam. A time will come when this knowledge, what is, is the soul there or it is just a faith? We may start with faith but in due course of time it becomes Pratyaksha. What is the meaning of Pratyaksha? #### Faith, Process, Guru's Mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=513
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We cannot be rebellious and simultaneously understand God."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of asking for directions to illustrate how faith in the guru's instructions leads to realization, and emphasizes the need for humility and the mercy of the guru to understand God.

I give this example many times. Suppose I have to, you ask me how do I reach Hirandani hospital, let's say from here. You are new to Mumbai. Then I say, okay, you go out from this school main gate, turn left and then you have to turn right, first right. Then, you know, typically you will get the Kherapanna mall and then go straight, then you will have that garden on the left side. Turn right side, you will get Galleria mall. So you are completely, all these words are completely new to you. Initially you have faith in my words but you are not sure whether, sometimes people give directions without having, even knowing. You go straight and then you start taking a step. And then you have to go all the way to the restaurant, get a sense of how far is it, how much is it, how to get there. Of course, that gives you a reason for your faith. It tells you, what is the scene here? What is the location here? You start learning, you try to change the place. Anyone has come across scenario like this? So, you start off and as those places come on your way as you are approaching towards, evidence can you show me can you prove it to me we follow the process and in due course of time that which appears to be faith becomes very protection faith and revelation it becomes revealed and for that revelation you have to follow a process you have to get the mercy of the guru you have to get the mercy of the Lord we cannot be rebellious and simultaneously understand God in fact in the Bible also God is can be approached by the meek and weak and humble if we have very proud of our erudition yes I know I don't care and I can find out on my own we disqualify so here Arjuna is saying by

But what is His greatness? What is His opulence? Why do we need to know that? Can anyone tell me? Only then we can surrender. Yes. Krishna says, 'I am the source of everything.' Everything is emanating from me. Then He says, anyone knows the third line? With that understanding, bhajante maa. Why should I surrender? Here it says we should surrender to Allah. Why should I surrender? When I have the right understanding that He is the cause of all causes, including me. He is the supreme personality of God. He is the supreme father. I am Amsha of Him. Iti matva. One who has that understanding. The natural consequence of that understanding will be, bhajante maa worships me. Otherwise sometimes, why I should worship? I am doing my duties nicely. I don't cheat anyone. I am honest. I help. Why I should worship God? So it is important to have the right understanding. And this understanding is what Krishna is sharing in the Gita.

#### Cosmic Manifestation, Universal Form, Presence of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=878
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is because of the presence of the Lord, this whole thing is moving."
**Summary:** This segment discusses Arjuna's desire to see how Krishna has entered the cosmic manifestation and explains that the universe is animated by the presence of the Supreme Lord, similar to how the soul animates the body.

O greatest of all personalities, O supreme form, though I see here before me your actual position, I yet wish to see how you have entered into this cosmic manifestation. I want to see that form of yours. Now this cosmic manifestation, earth, huge mass, 6 x 10 raised to the power of 21 metric tons. So it is the Lord who is the Lord of the universe. So it is the Lord who has arranged this whole thing. It is because of the presence of the Lord, this whole thing is moving. Just like this body, it is animated. Why? Why this body is animated? I am able to speak, I am able to see, I am able to move my hands. Why? Because of the presence of the soul. When the soul leaves the body, eyes are there, hands are there, but there is no chetana, there is no consciousness, it won't move. Likewise, this whole universe is moving because of the presence of super soul, the Lord.

Now here, Arjuna, many of the questions which Arjuna is asking is actually not for him. He is Shishyasteham Shadimam. He is the soul surrendered unto the Lord. He is very dear to the Lord. He is a great devotee of the Lord. So then one may wonder, if all these qualifications Arjuna has, how can he be an illusion? Arjuna is saying, now my illusion is gone. How can illusion be there? If there is bright sun, how can there be darkness? So the entire conversation, what is happening in the Gita, Arjuna is putting forth these questions not necessarily to clarify his doubt, but the answers what Krishna gives is for the entire humanity. In order to convince others of future who may think that Lord is an ordinary person, just an ordinary historical personality.

We cannot see, hear, understand, nor perceive the Supreme Lord Krishna by our material senses. So Arjuna is also requesting, can you reveal, can you show, if you feel I am qualified? #### Alexander's Illness, Helplessness, Limitations of Material Possessions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=1664
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "But a time will come, they will also be helpless."
**Summary:** This segment continues the story of Alexander, describing his illness and the realization that his wealth and power could not save him.

So he had camped in a particular place and he fell sick. And typically in his army, there were physicians, there were doctors, there were, you know, all kinds of people required because huge army of soldiers. So he fell sick. And his health was deteriorating, going back to worse. And he had a very great desire, you know, he was very fond of his mother. And as his health was deteriorating, was spoiling, the physicians were trying to attend to him, to help him recuperate, to get well. But day after day, in spite of the medication, in spite of the best of doctors helping him out, his health was becoming bad. He was very weak, body became very frail. And it started dawning on him, you know, sometimes, you know, start getting intuition that who knows, I may not recover, I may not survive. It started dawning on him. Because he saw that the physicians are trying their best, they're giving the best of medicines they have at their disposal. But in spite of their good intentions, in spite of their wanting him to recover, he's not able to recover. Actually, it's a fact. We all have faith, no doubt, in money, in our near and dear ones, in the medication, the doctors, in the hospitals. No doubt, they do contribute in a big way in our lives to protect us, to save us, to recover us. But a time will come, they will also be helpless.

#### Alexander's Last Wishes, Wealth, Mortality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=1776
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Looks like my time has come."
**Summary:** This segment describes Alexander's three unusual last wishes, highlighting the futility of material possessions in the face of death.

So Alexander was going through that phase. He had everything. He had money, he had, you know, all the wealth of the world at his disposal. He had plundered rubies and diamonds and all the valuables, the jewels, the stones. Everything was there. The army was there. Very loyal to him. He would say anything, they would do anything for him, including die for him. The physicians were there and his health was titillating day by day. And when he went through that phase, when he saw that they all are helpless, all what he had with him is not helping him at that critical moment, he called for his close confidants, his ministers, his generals. He said, looks like my time has come. How many of you have heard this term? Looks like my time has come. Time has come. He says, before I leave this body, before I die, I have three last wishes. So one of the generals says, tell us Alexander, which kingdom has to be plundered? Because all along, you know, wishes means do this, do that, conquer this, conquer that. He says, tell us which kingdom you want us to conquer? He says, I have just three simple last wishes. Before I leave, looks like my time has come. Before I leave, I have three last wishes. Please fulfill those wishes. He was in a very helpless state. He says, my first wishes, when I die, let the path leading to the graveyard be strewn with all the jewels, the rubies, the diamonds from my treasury. Let all it be strewn on the path leading to the graveyard. They were completely bewildered with this wish of Alexander. Then he said, the second wish is, let my coffin be carried by, he pointed to those, you know, the physicians who were surrounding him, who were tending to his medical requirements. He says, all of you should carry that coffin. And my third wish is, let both my hands be left dangling out of the coffin.

---
#### Meaning of Alexander's Wishes, Wealth, Mortality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T1mwHzczMM8?t=1936
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Alexander the Great came to the world empty-handed and went back empty-handed."
**Summary:** This segment reveals the meaning behind Alexander's last wishes: to show the world the futility of wealth, the limitations of medicine, and the inevitability of death, emphasizing that he came and left the world empty-handed.

So then, none of them even dared to come forward and ask Alexander and clarify why these wishes. It was, you know, because he was in a very commanding position. Whatever he would order, he would be executed. And he was very courageous, very bold, very risk-taking. They have seen him always in the mode of being a conqueror. So nobody even dared to come forward and clarify what Alexander means by these unusual wishes. So one of them took courage, came forward, stepped forward, took the hand of Alexander, pressed it against his chest. He said, Alexander, why such unusual wishes? What are you trying to communicate? He says, all these wishes have a meaning. When I'm going through this state, my first wish that all the wealth strewn on the path leading to the graveyard so that the world sees Alexander the Great spend all his time and energy conquering kingdoms, accumulating this rubies, jewels, and now it is all left behind. My second wish, let his physicians carry the coffin so the world sees when the time comes the best of doctors, the best of medicines will also be able to help you. And the third wish, both my hands will be left dangling outside the coffin so the world sees, makes a note, realizes Alexander the Great came to the world empty-handed and went back empty-handed.

Hello Krishna, warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita live session. Today we have chosen a very, very important topic, and the discussion is going to be on sleep. As we all agree, sleep is a very integral part of our lives. On an average, even if we keep six hours on an average, although many people sleep more, one-fourth of our lives, between one-fourth and one-third of our lives, we spend sleeping, and sleep is a very important requirement for our body to function at its optimal best. So we will discuss this important subject. We will also take some wonderful insights about sleep which Lord Shri Krishna shared, prayers in the Bhagavad Gita. There are many verses where Krishna is talking about sleep, and we will also discuss some of the very important things which Prabhupada mentions in his purports with respect to sleep. So before we get into the discussion, we would start the session with prayers. Request all of you to join me in offering these prayers. I will be chanting these prayers. Request you all to fold your hands and either repeat after me or along with me, we can go ahead and offer these prayers. Seek the blessings of Srila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya, Lord Shri Krishna so that we can have a very nice wonderful session with lot of takeaways, lot of learnings emerging from this particular discussion.

#### yoga, regulation, sleep
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=478
**Verse References:** BG 6.16, BG 6.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who eats too much or does not eat enough, sleeps too much or does not sleep enough, cannot be a yogi."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Krishna's verses on the importance of regulation in eating and sleeping for a yogi, emphasizing moderation.

The two verses where first verse Krishna talks about one who eats too much or eats too less, sleeps too much or does not sleep enough, cannot be a yogi. So in this verse Krishna is directly talking about the importance of sleep, regulation, neither too less nor too much. One who eats too much or does not eat enough, one who sleeps too much or does not sleep enough cannot be a yogi, even if we want to be a yogi, although we have heard of yogis sleeping very less, conquering their sleep, still Krishna says one who is, there has to be a regulation. There is another verse where Krishna says one who is regulated in his eating, sleeping, recreation and work can mitigate all material miseries arising from material contact by practicing this yoga system.

#### sleep, Krishna consciousness, time management

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the need for good quality sleep for efficient work and optimal health, while acknowledging the desire to minimize sleep in Krishna consciousness to dedicate more time to serving the Lord, viewing sleep as a 'necessary evil'.

Again, regulation, one who is regulated in his eating, sleeping, recreation and work. As I mentioned earlier, we all need good quality sleep. We all need good quality sleep for us to work efficiently, for us to have optimal health, lot of people who have challenges with respect to sleep in terms of insomnia or not getting good quality sleep invariably will also have challenges with respect to their alertness, their ability to work efficiently, even with their health issues. So, sleep is very important, although we know that when we come to Krishna consciousness, we want to minimize the number of hours we sleep because we understand that we have lot more important functions to spend our time on, to engage in the service of the Lord. Time is very precious. We all have limited time. Life is, human life is very precious. Before we take our last breath, we have lot of important things to accomplish, to accomplish the purpose of human form of life. In that sense, we can see that sleep is a necessary evil because we have to spend time which, in that sense, we see that, you know, the time goes away. So, what is that optimal?

---
#### sleep research, insomnia, sleeping pills
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=677
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "Lot of people all over the world are having issues with respect to sleep."
**Summary:** The speaker notes the extensive research on sleep due to widespread sleep problems, highlighting the prevalence of sleeping pills and insomnia.

As we all know, this subject of sleep, if you also Google, a lot of research happening all over the world. Many research papers have been published. There are sleep researchers who are dedicated, researching on the topic of sleep because lot of people all over the world are having issues with respect to sleep. I was reading an article, they are among the top ten selling or prescribed drugs or the medicines. Two or three of them are tranquilizers or sleeping pills. Lot of issues, lot of people are not able to sleep unless they do not have the proper sleep, unless they take some pills to induce sleep. Many people having issues like insomnia. So, let us discuss what is the optimal number of hours a person should sleep in 24 hours.

---
#### sleep duration, sleep quality, research
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=738
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "It is not the quantity of sleep which we have to worry about, which we have to talk about, the most important thing which we have to talk about."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the varying research findings on optimal sleep duration, noting the lack of consensus and emphasizing the importance of sleep quality over quantity.

Lot of research has happened on this particular topic. No doubt, the number of hours varies on various factors. The age when children, when the child, the childhood, maybe the number of hours of sleep required for the body is more than more so. When a person becomes old, the requirement for sleep will increase. So a lot of research was done on this particular subject. So some research threw up the results like okay, we need minimum of seven to eight hours. Some researchers came up with a number of hours. Some researchers came up with a finding that six to seven hours is enough. And over the years, when you collate all the research findings, you will see that there is not much consensus. There is a lot of, you know, different researchers are throwing up different results. In fact, there have been a lot of conferences held on this particular topic. And a lot of people have debated on this subject. And finally, there is some consensus emerging. No doubt, we need six to seven hours of sleep. But today, a lot of people are talking about it is not the number of hours, it is not the quantity of sleep which we have to worry about, which we have to talk about, the most important thing which we have to talk about. The number of hours that we have to worry about or strive for is not the quantity of sleep, but the quality of sleep.

---
#### sleep quality, restful sleep, regulation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=843
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If a person gets five hours of deep, good, restful sleep, it is much better than seven hours or eight hours of sleep where he is tossing and turning in the bed and, you know, mind is racing."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that quality of sleep is more important than quantity, and that following certain principles and regulations can lead to deep, restful sleep, referencing Prabhupada's short sleep duration and high energy levels.

If a person gets five hours of deep, good, restful sleep, it is much better than seven hours or eight hours of sleep where he is tossing and turning in the bed and, you know, mind is racing. He has not got a deep, restful sleep. That is why you would have seen many times. We also, when we wake up in the morning, even after seven hours of sleep, eight hours of sleep, we are not fresh, we feel groggy, we feel tired. So it is important that we learn or we practice, we mould our lives, we follow certain principles, follow certain regulations. So that we get good quality sleep, deep restful sleep. That is very important. No doubt, the number of hours is also important, but relatively speaking, it is secondary. There were yogis who were, you know, having very good control over their mind, over the senses. They would sleep for few hours. Actually, Prabhupada, he would sleep for three or four hours, he would get up at one o'clock in the night and for four hours he would be transcribing Bhaktivedanta purports and five o'clock he would go for a morning walk, do his japa, chanting and whole day he would be busy with various activities, travelling world over at the age of seventy, seventy-five, no jet lag, very energetic, talking to different devotees, answering the letters, meeting so many people, going to different places, addressing students in MIT, different conferences he attended. He would sleep for barely three to four hours.

---
#### mind control, rejuvenation, sleep importance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=976
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sleep is a time when body rejuvenates, re-energizes itself."
**Summary:** The speaker suggests that reducing sleep hours is possible with good control over the mind and senses, emphasizing that sleep is essential for bodily rejuvenation and that the focus should be on quality sleep.

So it is possible that we can reduce the number of hours. But if we are having good control over our mind, our thoughts, our senses and we are able to get good deep restful sleep, then we can manage because at the end of the day body needs time to rejuvenate. Sleep is a time when body rejuvenates, re-energizes itself. So we should not just blindly go for the number of hours of sleep, understand why sleep is important for the body and get good quality sleep. That should be our desired aspiration or goal. Before we get into what we can do, what regulations we can follow, it is very important for us to understand a concept called sleep cycle.

---
#### sleep cycle, sleep stages, rejuvenation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1032
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "Deep sleep stage or stage three is a time when actually all the growth hormones, all the repairing work."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of sleep cycles, explaining the different stages of sleep and highlighting the importance of deep sleep (stage three) for growth, repair, and rejuvenation.

Many people are not aware of this understanding of sleep cycle. When we go to rest in the night, when we sleep, we go through different stages of sleep. It is not that we just go to sleep and then there is a constant, we have deep sleep and then morning when the alarm rings or when we want to wake up, we wake up. There are different stages of sleep and as I said, many people have done research on this. So they have figured out. There are different ways of measuring the brain waves when we are sleeping, just like heartbeat can be measured, heart rate can be measured. So when we go to bed, we go through a stage which is between wakeful stage and deep sleep stage. That is called stage one, where the number of cycles per second, a brain frequency at which the brain, the neurons vibrate, reduces. And then we get into stage two sleep, which is between stage one and stage three which is deep sleep stage. Deep sleep stage or stage three is a time when actually all the growth hormones, all the repairing work. sending signals to various parts of the body, getting signals back from the various parts, because ultimately assuming that a person is, you know, some things have to be repaired, he has got a cut in his body, signals have to go back and forth. Just like I was thinking, just like when airplane comes back and lands, it has to go to a hangar for some repairs and that is a time when all the, you know, the engineers work on the plane, figure out where it has gone wrong, what parts have gone faulty, removing those parts, replacing those parts and then the plane comes back out of the hangar and then it can be used for, you know, for its normal operations. So, then we have deep sleep which is called stage three and this is where actually the stage three is the most important part where the body gets rejuvenated.

---
#### REM sleep, sleep cycles, sleep stages
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1199
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "REM, rapid eye movement where this is a stage where a person will most likely get some dreams."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining the sleep cycle, describing REM sleep and how the body goes through multiple cycles of different stages throughout the night.

And then we have again back to stage two and then we have REM, rapid eye movement where this is a stage where a person will most likely get some dreams. This is when a person tosses and turns in the bed and then again a person goes through the next cycle. So, these cycles, we go in multiple cycles in a particular night, REM, stage one, stage two, deep sleep, stage two, REM, again back to stage two, stage three which is deep sleep and we go to multiple cycles.

---
#### sleep inertia, sleep stages, alarm clocks
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1244
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "If you have woken up when you were in a stage three or deep sleep stage and suddenly because of alarm, you woke up, then you will feel groggy you will feel completely, so to say, uncomfortable, you will feel very drowsy, you will feel like go back and sleep, irrespective of number of hours you have slept."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses the common problem of feeling groggy despite sufficient sleep, explaining that waking up during deep sleep (stage three) causes this, regardless of the hours slept.

Now, many people have faced this problem. They keep an alarm, they have decided the previous night that I will get up at 5:30 a.m or whatever the time may be or 6 a.m and when the alarm rings we somehow force ourselves out of the bed and even if we have slept for six hours, seven hours, assuming that previous night I have slept on time and got good six hours of sleep, six and a half hours of sleep. I wake up and the alarm rings, somehow push myself out of the bed. Quite possible that we will feel very groggy, very sleepy. If we are chanting we are not able to chant properly, not able to concentrate and one might wonder what mistake have I done? I have slept for six and a half hours and I have got a face, but I have slept only six hours and I have had a very good quality of sleep. I did not wake up in the night, what went wrong? The answer is, if you have woken up when you were in a stage three or deep sleep stage and suddenly because of alarm, you woke up, then you will feel groggy you will feel completely, so to say, uncomfortable, you will feel very drowsy, you will feel like go back and sleep, irrespective of number of hours you have slept.

---
#### REM sleep, sleep cycle, sleep discipline
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1351
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "The best stage when a person wakes up is REM stage, rapid eye movement."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that waking up during REM sleep is ideal, discusses the typical sleep cycle duration, and emphasizes the importance of consistent sleep timings for a disciplined sleep schedule.

So therefore, it is said the best stage when a person wakes up is REM stage, rapid eye movement. That is the time when a person is almost awake. So in a particular, the typical sleep cycle, again it varies from person to person, it is around, around 90 minutes. It varies, for somebody it could be 70 minutes, 80 minutes, it could be 110 minutes. It is good that we complete some number of cycles and not wake up in between a cycle, more so when we are in deep sleep stage. So let us say a person has woken up in REM stage after 3 cycles of sleep, he will be more fresh than a person who has woken up in deep sleep stage, stage 3, after having completed 4 cycles of sleep. Now it is a very tricky question. So how do we figure out? How do we wake up? When? We are in a REM stage or stage 1, after having completed one cycle. That is why it is very, very important if you check, you know, on Google or YouTube how to get good quality sleep, invariably you will get a suggestion that be very disciplined as far as your sleep timings are concerned. Sleep as far.

---
#### biological clock, sleep rhythm, sleep apps
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1460
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "We have a biological clock, a rhythm."
**Summary:** The speaker stresses the importance of maintaining a consistent sleep schedule, even on weekends, to align with the body's natural biological clock and mentions apps that can help identify sleep cycles for optimal waking times.

As far as possible sleep at the same time and get up at the same time every day, including weekends. Lot of people think Sunday is a day where we can, you know, catch up with the rest and sleep more, does not matter. But what happens is, we have a biological clock, a rhythm. Assuming, let us say a person sleeps at 10 o'clock. And gets up at 5 o'clock. And suddenly the next day a person sleeps at 10:35 and again tries to get up at 5 o'clock. It will be a big mess. Because 10 o'clock to 5 o'clock, assuming the body has got used to and then suddenly sleeps at 10:30, invariably at 5 o'clock he would be in middle of a sleep cycle. So, without getting too much into technical details about sleep cycle, by the way, there are some apps which can help one identify one's sleep cycles and accordingly wake up a person. So, in other words, there are apps which will monitor based on your bodily movements in the bed. Monitor the sleep cycle. And when you wake up. When you say an alarm of let us say 5 o'clock in the morning, it will not wake you up at 5 o'clock sharp. It will wake up when you are in stage 1. It could be 5:10. It could be 4:45, 4:50. And in the app there are some settings which you can say how many 30 minutes of that buffer time you have to give. So, this is just a concept which we should keep in mind.

---
#### sleep cycle, sleep quality, power nap
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1571
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "Understanding of sleep cycle is very important."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the importance of understanding sleep cycles to address doubts about feeling groggy despite sufficient sleep and introduces the concept of power naps.

Okay. This will resolve lot of doubts which comes in our mind that, you know, in spite of sleeping for 6 hours still why I feel groggy at times when I wake up. So understanding of sleep cycle is very important. Sometimes you would have realized we have woken up for catching a flight or something and we have slept for 3 hours only in the night and we seem to be fresh. We seem to be awake. We seem to be alert. Also these days people are talking about power nap. Power nap is where a person sleeps for 20 minutes or 20 to 25 minutes. This is when the body gets into stage 1 sleep and has not got into stage 2 and stage 3 deep sleep. And when a person wakes up after in a stage 1 sleep, he feels relaxed and regenerated. He has got some sleep but he has not got into deep sleep where to wake up suddenly he will feel groggy and tired.

#### mind vs mattress, snooze button, early morning victory
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1719
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "Between that wake up time and the time we actually get up, there is a battle which goes on."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the struggle between the mind and the desire to stay in bed after the alarm rings, highlighting the difference between wake-up time and get-up time.

So now when we wake up, when we, let us say we have kept an alarm that I want to wake up at five o'clock, when the alarm rings, we have made a resolve the previous night that I want to wake up at 5 a.m. I want to get up and you know, I want to chant my rounds. I want to do so many things. I want to maybe do some exercises and then go to my office, then go to my work, go for my work. So when the alarm rings, many times, many people encounter a scenario where we suddenly hit the snooze button and we want to sleep for another 10 minutes. So practically speaking, there is a difference between wake up time and the time we actually get up from the bed. If we are used to the snooze button option in the alarm clock, wake up time is the time when actually the alarm rings and get up time is the time when we actually get out of the bed. And between that wake up time and the time we actually get up, there is a battle which goes on. And the battle is between our mind and mattress.

---
#### mind control, early morning victory, alarm placement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1823
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "Suddenly at that point, the mind comes up with different reasons, different thoughts, how it is better for you to sleep for another 10 minutes, 20 minutes, it will be more relaxing, so that you can get up fresh."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how the mind rationalizes staying in bed, leading to missed commitments and a difficult day, emphasizing the importance of 'early morning victory' and suggesting placing the alarm away from the bed.

Suddenly at that point, the mind comes up with different reasons, different thoughts, how it is better for you to sleep for another 10 minutes, 20 minutes, it will be more relaxing, so that you can get up fresh. Some article you have read about why it is important to have six hours, seven hours sleep. That article suddenly will pop up in the mind. Suddenly a person will feel, no, I'm still tired, I need more rest. And at times we switch off the alarm and suddenly find ourselves waking up one and half hours to two hours later. And the whole day, it is very difficult. is gone. Then throughout the day we are struggling to catch up with our chanting, catch up with all our you know whatever we had planned for that particular day. That's why it is very important to aspire for early morning victory. We sleep at a particular time and wake up at the time which we have decided the previous night to wake up come what may. Now when the mind gives up different ideas, different thoughts, it is best to wake up, to get up and not keep pressing the snooze button or switch off the alarm. That's why it is also advised that we keep an alarm little far away from the bed. At least to switch off the alarm, we have to physically get out of the bed. This helps.

---
#### personal integrity, commitment, trust
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1935
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** General public
**Key Quote:** "All of us have something called as personal integrity account. That means amount of trust we have on ourselves."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of a 'personal integrity account,' where keeping commitments adds to the balance and breaking them withdraws, affecting self-trust.

Now if we all have something called as personal integrity account, just like we have a bank account, there is some debits and credits. When we deposit the money in our bank account, there is a credit. When we withdraw the money, there is a debit. And then we have a bank balance. Anytime we take a printout of our bank account, pass sheet or whatever, you will see a balance which will have passbook will show some debits, some credits and the final net balance in our bank account. All of us have something called as personal integrity account. That means amount of trust we have on ourselves. When we make a resolve or a commitment, not only at what time we will wake up for any other thing, when we keep up that commitment, there is a deposit in our personal integrity account. Our confidence in ourselves, in our ability to make and keep promises increases. There is a deposit in our personal integrity account. And, whenever we make a commitment and do not keep up that commitment, when we say that I will get up at 5 am and I do not get up at 5 am, there is a withdrawal from my personal integrity account. In other words, over a period of time, if there is lot of withdrawals, the lot of commitments which I have made and I am not able to keep up that commitment many, many times and the withdrawals happen quite often, there could be a negative balance in my personal integrity account. A negative balance indicates that a

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### GIVING PRIORITY TO GOD | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### priorities, material desires, value
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uS09kQJ_FIU?t=487
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If we have that one, those zeros have value."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of '1' and '0' to explain the importance of prioritizing God in our lives, stating that material pursuits (zeros) only have value when God (one) is the priority.

So while we are pursuing these desires, let us always be conscious that do I have that one in front of those zeros which I am trying to accumulate? If we have that one, those zeros have value. If it is one zero, it becomes ten. If it is two, it becomes hundred. So all the zeros have value.

#### time management, spiritual practice, self-reflection

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of making time for spiritual practices like chanting and reading Bhagavad Gita, suggesting that a lack of time for these activities indicates a deviation from the right path.

Sometimes people get so involved, so entangled that they have no time for chanting. At the end of the day, they have taken a commitment and late in the night, 10:30, 11, they are finishing their rounds, somehow wanting their rounds to get over. For days together we have not got time to read Bhagavad Gita, then we can conclude that yes, somewhere we have gone wrong, somewhere we have gone off track, somewhere we have...

#### self-realization, God realization, human life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uS09kQJ_FIU?t=899
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The mission of human life is to realize one's self, which is also called self-realization or God realization. It's a mission of human life, it's a most important goal."
**Summary:** The speaker defines the mission of human life as self-realization or God realization, emphasizing its importance above all other pursuits and warning that wrong priorities can derail this mission.

The mission of human life is to realize one's self, which is also called self-realization or God realization. It's a mission of human life, it's a most important goal. Although we may be striving for hundred things, but this is the number one thing which we should be striving for. If this is not there in our focus then our priorities are wrong.

---
#### human life, spiritual opportunity, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uS09kQJ_FIU?t=931
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "This human form of life is meant especially for God realization, self-realization, realizing one's self. It is not possible in any other life form."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the human form of life is uniquely suited for self-realization and God realization, an opportunity not available in other life forms due to a lack of intelligence and ability to control the senses and mind.

The mission of human life is to realize one's self and then gradually go back home, back to Godhead. The human form of life is especially meant for God realization, self-realization, realizing one's self. It is not possible in any other life form, the 84 lakh species, plants, birds, aquatics, reptiles, so many life forms, but none of these life forms we have an opportunity to understand about ourselves, about God, how we can control our senses, how we can control the mind. That ability, that intelligence is not there.

#### fruitive activities, life's mission, animalistic life

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the human form of life is meant to escape the cycle of fruitive activities, but if one forgets their life's mission and acts like an animal, they fail to utilize this opportunity.

So this human form of life is meant especially to get out of the bondage of fruitive activities, but as long as one is forgetful of his life's mission and acts like an ordinary animal, Prabhupada uses this word quite often in his books, in his writings, and in his books and in his writings...

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that engaging in Krishna conscious activities leads to permanent spiritual credits that accompany the soul in its journey from one body to another.

Gatherings in God and whatever credits he accrues is of permanent nature. In fact, these are the real credits which actually come when the soul moves from one body to another. So what is the sum and substance of the Krishna consciousness movement? This was a question asked to Srila Prabhupada, what is the sum and substance?

#### hearing, purification, divine pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uS09kQJ_FIU?t=1986
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The supreme Lord who is within everyone's heart becomes very pleased when a person hears narration of his activities."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu advising a brahmana to stay in his circumstances but to hear about the Lord, emphasizing that hearing purifies the heart and pleases the Lord.

There was one brahmana who wanted to leave his home, leave his family and accompany Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu told him, wherever you are, whatever circumstances you are in, be there, be there, but do one thing. What is that? Hear about the Lord. And what will happen? The heart will get purified, the knowledge, the real knowledge will awaken. The supreme Lord who is within everyone's heart becomes very pleased when a person hears narration of his activities.

#### mind cleansing, material existence, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/uS09kQJ_FIU?t=2031
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Material existence is caused by dirty things within the mind. If one can cleanse the mind, he comes to his original position of Krishna consciousness and thus his life becomes successful."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that material existence is caused by impurities in the mind, and cleansing the mind through hearing leads to Krishna consciousness and a successful life.

And he personally cleanses the dirt from the mind of the listener. He washes away all the dirts from within the heart and the mind. Material existence is caused by dirty things within the mind. If one can cleanse the mind, he comes to his original position of Krishna consciousness and thus his life becomes successful.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Therefore all great saints in devotional life very strongly recommend the process of hearing. So today we are talking about the right priorities, giving priority to God. Although it's a generic word giving priority to God, here this understanding, from this understanding we get to know that we have to give priority to hearing about God. We have to give priority to the Sadhu Sangha, we have to give priority to the devotees.
