## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Important Precautions for Protecting Spiritual Life | Sri Amitasana Dasa | SB 4.19.9 | 06.11.2025
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### material provision, sacrifice, Vedic principles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 4.19.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Everything is dependent on the people's acceptance of the Vedic principle of sacrifice."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa reads and explains Srimad Bhagavatam 4.19.9, focusing on how material prosperity is linked to Vedic principles and sacrifice, contrasting it with the current Kali Yuga.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, canto number 4, chapter number 19, text number 9. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shri Prabhupada. Translation: King Pruthu was presented with various gifts from the general populace and predominating deities of all planets. The ocean and seas were full of valuable jewels and pearls, and the hills were full of chemicals and fertilizers. Four kinds of edibles were produced profusely. As stated in the Isopanishad, this material creation is supplied with all the potencies for the production of all necessities required by the living entities, not only human beings, but animals, reptiles, aquatic species. The ocean and seas produce pearls, coral, and valuable jewels so that fortunate law-abiding people can utilize them. Similarly, the hills are full of chemicals so that when rivers flow down from them, the chemicals spread over the fields to fertilize the four kinds of foodstuff. They are technically known as charviya, those edibles which are chewed, lehiya, those which are licked up, chhusiya, those which are swallowed, and piya, those which are drunk. Pruthu Maharaj was greeted by the residents of other planets and their presiding deities. They presented various gifts to the king and acknowledged him as the proper type of king by whose planning and activities everyone throughout the universe could be happy and prosperous. It is clearly indicated in these words that the oceans and seas are meant for producing jewels, but in Kali Yuga, the oceans are mainly being utilized for fishing. Shudras and poor men were allowed to fish, but the higher classes like the Kshatriyas and Vaishyas would gather pearls, jewels, and coral. All the poor men would catch tons of fish. They would not be equal in value to the piece of coral or pearl. In this age, so many factories for the manufacture of fertilizers have been opened, but when the personality of Godhead is pleased by the performance of yagnas, the hills automatically produce fertilizing chemicals, which help produce edibles in the field. Everything is dependent on Godhead. Everything is dependent on the people's acceptance of the Vedic principle of sacrifice. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta.

#### illusion, Maya, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=169
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So this is the illusory energy of the Lord, which all of us in this material world are influenced by."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story about a devotee who was asked by Krishna to fetch water and gets caught in the Lord's illusory energy (Maya), experiencing a full lifetime of family and attachment before realizing it was all an illusion.

There was a devotee of Krishna who was in company of the Lord. He was walking with the Lord and then in course of the walk he asked the Lord, Lord Shri Krishna, I have heard about your divine energy Maya. Can you please explain what is this divine energy and how it influences the living entity? Why is it called Dhrittaya? Generally devotees who are especially pure devotees, they are out of the purview of Maya. So the Lord said, well, this is a big subject. In fact, the entire material world is nothing but play of Maya. The living entity wants to enjoy and the whole world, whatever is happening, is the illusory energy of the Lord captivating the living entities. So it's a long story. So anyway, the Lord told that devotee, let's sit and talk whenever we have some long discussion. So we say, okay, let's sit and talk. It's going to take time. So they were, they sat down on one rock and then Krishna, because it's going to be a long story, long description. So Krishna said, before we start, can you give me a, get me a glass of water? So, yes, my Lord. So the devotee immediately went in search for water. It was open land, hot summer day. And then he walked for a while. He could not see anywhere where he could get water. Kept walking, kept walking, kept walking. And then fortunately at a distance, he could see some greenery, some cottages, some kind of a village. So at last he was very happy that for sure here I will get water. And then I'll go back. And then I listened from the Lord. What did he listen? About Maya. So he went to that village and then he saw one cottage from a distance, went to that cottage, knocked on the door and the door opened. That very beautiful, charming lady opened the door. And in earlier times, Vedic times, a guest was considered as an Athito Devobhava. Unlike present times, beware of dogs. Even if there is no dog, they'll put that. Beware, don't come in. So, the moment the door opened, that lady with a folded hand said, Swami, come in. Swami came in, very beautiful, handsome, charming, attractive lady. Come in and sit. She sat down. And then he said, can I have a glass of water? Yes, Swami. And then she went. And while she was walking, the tingling of ankle bells, very sweet, melodious sound. So she got a glass of water. So he drank the water and it was unusually sweet. Taste of water also depends on who serves it. So he liked the taste. And then, Swami, you eat something before you leave. You have come from so far. Okay, eat something before you leave. So now I'll fast forward the video. In video, you know, 1x, 1.5x. Now I'll make 100x. So he had the lunch. And then while the lunch was being cooked and served, they were talking. And her sounds, her talk, her conversation was very sweet. So he felt like, let me continue. He felt like, let me continue my talking. Let me stay for some more time. And then 100x, he developed some kind of attachment to her. And they continued talking, interacting. Again, 100x, he developed affection and love for her and proposed to her. She also liked because very nice, wonderful devotee. So she also accepted the proposal. Again, 100x, they got married. 100x, they got children. 100x, the children got married. Now they got grandchildren. So all along, he was very attached to his wife, very beautiful, very loving, very caring. The children were very nice. And the grandchildren were very, very cute. And more so when the grandchildren would call him Daddu, Daddu. You know, it was so sweet to hear loving, affectionate voice. And he would also very affectionately keep them on his lap, play with them and all this. So years rolled by. And he got very, very attached to the family. Fortunately, the family was very good, very loving, very kind, very affectionate. And then one day it so happened, suddenly, you know, it became very cloudy and thunder in the sky. And it started raining and raining very profusely, very heavily. So much so that everywhere was water. Water got clogged. And so much so that it started flooding. The whole village started, the water started rising and all this. And then because water was gushing from the nearby villages also, there was some kind of a very strong force in the water. And then, you know, they all started getting panicky. And, you know, children, grandchildren, and they started saving and, you know, trying to, the cattle, the lamb, whatever there in the home, they were trying to safeguard whatever the grains were kept. And then soon the water kept on rising. And so much of current, they started seeing that many of the things started flowing away with the water. And they were all desperately trying to save themselves, save everything what they had, the valuables. And in front of their eyes, one by one, one by one, everything started flowing with the water. So much so that the more they tried, the more they tried to hold on to things, but right in front of their eyes, everything was getting wiped away. And then he, with his wife, held each other and then children. But in front of his eyes, some of his grandchildren got swept away. He's trying to save them. Krishna finally, you know, because he was a devotee of Krishna, he said, he's desperate, desperate. Generally when a person goes through tough times, we remember Krishna. So he said, Krishna, Krishna, please, Krishna, please help. Finally, in front of his eyes, everything got swept away. And then finally, you know, pleading the Lord, please, please save me, save me. He fell at the feet of the Lord. Save me, Govinda. And then he hears a tap. He opens his eyes. Krishna says, where is the glass of water?

#### illusion, Maya, sacrifice, Vedic principles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=748
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Under the influence of this illusionary energy, we forget who we are. Forget the goal of life. Forget our relationship with the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the illusory energy of the Lord (Maya) and how it causes us to forget our true identity, the goal of life, and our relationship with Krishna, emphasizing the importance of performing yagyas as described in the scriptures.

So this is the illusory energy of the Lord, which all of us in this material world are influenced by. And in the illusion, under the illusion of the Lord, there is an illusion. Under the influence of this illusionary energy, we forget who we are. Forget the goal of life. Forget our relationship with the Lord. Forget that the goal of life is to act for pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord. To perform yagyas, what is being mentioned here. And when yagyas are performed, Prabhupada in the Prabhupada is explaining, how there is a profuse supply of everything. Even in the Gita, Krishna says, when yagyas are performed, there will be sufficient rains. Then there will be enough grains. There won't be any shortage. And people can live peacefully. In today's world, there is no yagna being performed. Because, forget about performing yagna. People have lost, because of Maya, don't even know that we are amsa of the Lord. We have to please the Lord. We have to serve the Lord. And everyone is engaged in serving their own senses, serving their own bodies, and relatives, friends, family members. This is Maya. And Bhagavatam time and again is saying that, what is that world in which people act. So, Prithu Maharaj, what we see in the description, how there was profuse supply of rain. It was enough, when the rains would fall from the hills, all the chemicals will come down. And because the land was fertile, there was enough grains. There was no shortage of anything. So, Prabhupada towards the end of this purport is explaining, that how everything is dependent on people's acceptance of the Vedic principles of sacrifice.

#### yajna, spiritual service, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=893
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Yagna means any activity, which is done for the pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker broadens the definition of Yagna beyond fire sacrifices to include any activity done for the pleasure of the Lord, including service and chanting, and shares a story about Prabhupada emphasizing that management is also spiritual service.

Yagna does not mean necessarily that fire yagna what we do. That's why Sankirtana Yagna, there is no fire. General, typical when you talk about people, we have to do Yagna. So, Yagna what comes in their mind is that fire sacrifice. But Yagna has a broader understanding. Yagna means any activity, which is done for the pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord. Any activity. Even the service what we do is a Yagna. Even chanting what we do is a Yagna. Yagnair Sankirtana Prayer. So, there was once a devotee who came to Shila Prabhupada. And said that my wife is very unhappy with me and she is quarrelling. So, Prabhupada said, why? She said that she does not want me to do management service. She wants me to do more of Pujari, Deity worship, cooking, Deity related services. She does not want me to do management services. So, Prabhupada, you know, he was very, very at times straightforward, very blunt. He said, your wife is less intelligent. She does not know what is Yagna. He says, management also is spiritual service. And then he gives example. Just like Arjuna was fighting in the battlefield and he was the greatest yogi. So, many times we have some false understanding that, you know, this service is Deity service or Pujari service or some services which have, which quote unquote we get a feeling that they are very spiritual in nature. And there are some services which are not. Which involves lot of management, dealing with outside people. So, Prabhupada gave us a very broader and a holistic understanding of Krishna Consciousness. He said, in Kali Yuga, for propagating this Krishna Consciousness movement, there will be lot of managerial work. But then he says, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu promises that, if we are doing this managerial work, we will not be entangled. So, Yagna, which here we are talking about is not just, you know, doing fire Yagna or doing Sankirtana Yagna. It is broader understanding. All different forms of services which we are doing for the pleasure of the Lord is a Yagna. Management also is a Yagna.

#### spiritual anxiety, Yajna, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=1109
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If we do for our pleasure of our own mind and senses, it is sense gratification. Even apparently it may be a spiritual service."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how even services that cause anxiety can be Yajna if done for the pleasure of the Lord, contrasting it with sense gratification, and emphasizes the importance of intent and motive behind actions.

Sometimes we have to do services which are very, very full of anxieties there. Prabhupada talks about spiritual anxiety. Many times we want to be in a comfort zone. We want to do only things which are very pleasing to the body, pleasing to the mind. Arjuna, what he was doing? He was fighting. There was bloodshed. And bloodshed with whom? His near and dear ones. His elders, his respectable, worshipable elders. But because it was for the pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord, it was Yagna. If we do for our pleasure of our own mind and senses, it is sense gratification. Even apparently it may be a spiritual service. For what we are doing? What is the intent? What is the motive behind our action? If the motive is pleasure of Guru and Krishna, then even apparently some service which involves lot of dealings with materialistic people is Yagna. Srila Prabhupada gave us very nice, wonderful understanding of Krishna Consciousness. Because he himself was Krishna Conscious. In fact, there is one pastime where he says, there was a devotee, it was the month of Makar Sankranti. And he went and in the sky there were lot of kites. You know, Makar Sankranti people fly kites. So, Prabhupada saw the sky, it was full of kites. So, he asked the devotee, so you won't be flying kites? So, the devotee looked at Prabhupada and said no. He says, in my childhood, Prabhupada says, in my childhood, I and my sister, what was sister's name? Pishyam. I and my sister used to fly lot of kites. And then Prabhupada says, her kite would fly always higher than my kite. And there was a competition. Then he said, one day I tried to cheat her. I went on the terrace and started flying the kite. And my kite was flying higher than her kite. See how innocently Prabhupada is saying. I went on the terrace, I cheated her. My kite was flying higher than her kite. Then he said, my sister started crying. And then started saying, praying to Govinda, Govinda, Govinda, my kite should fly higher than my brother's kite. And then her kite started flying higher than my kite. And then Prabhupada says, in our childhood also, we were very Krishna conscious. We were always praying to Krishna, thinking of Krishna. See kite also is not flying. Govinda, Govinda, help me fly the kite higher. So Prabhupada is saying that even in our childhood we were Krishna conscious. So when we are Krishna conscious, automatically our actions will be Yajna. And when we are under the influence of Maya, automatically our actions will be governed by what is pleasing to mind and senses. Sense gratification will automatically ensue, will follow.

#### Maya, spiritual life, sadhana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=1338
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual life is like walking on a razor's edge."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of morning sadhana to combat Maya, describing spiritual life as walking on a razor's edge, and highlights the need for sincerity in following Prabhupada's instructions to avoid falling into illusion.

If we are in Maya consciousness. So that's why our morning sadhana, our morning program, chanting, reading, hearing is very important. Prabhupada said, spiritual life is like walking on a razor's edge. Why he said razor's edge? Because in a moment, in a fraction of a second, we can go in Maya. Moment, fraction of a second. What it takes to go in Maya, just you have to desire sense gratification. That's why it's a razor's edge. And by the mercy of Prabhupada and Krishna, although it is not easy, although it's like walking on a razor's edge, but if we are simple, if we try to follow, if we are sincerely following what Prabhupada has taught, the instructions, then Prabhupada's mercy, blessings will give us the strength to be, not fall in Maya. On a daily basis, when we do the sadhana, we get the strength to pass the test which Maya will offer on a daily basis. Otherwise, in a moment, we'll be in this dress, apparently we'll be doing service, but it is sense gratification. No doubt, the good part is, even service what we do, it is Krishna's service, so there is an element of, worse come worse sake, it's agnata sukruti. Even if we are doing with a contaminated consciousness, still, because it is Krishna's work, 12th chapter Krishna says, if you cannot do this, do this, cannot do this, do my work. Still, there is an element of benefit which a devotee gets, but we should not be complacent. We should always strive that how, we should always be working for the satisfaction and pleasure of the Lord.

Shri Prabhupada had a very broad understanding of Krishna consciousness. He, one place is mentioned in one lecture, he says, a devotee actually, just like he quotes example of Prahlad Maharaj, Prahlad Maharaj says, I have no problem. For me, for a devotee, if he is properly situated, he has the Lord with him, he has everything. But a devotee is very anxious, because he sees that, devoid of this Krishna consciousness, this understanding, there is so much of pain, there is so much of suffering in this world. That's why a person who is Krishna conscious, naturally, an outcome would be, he is compassionate. Prabhupada in one conversation, he mentions, how, you know, we are also preachers, we also have plans, right, we plan, we will make one big temple, we will increase the book distribution, this year we will have 40,000 books. You see, how Prabhupada, how much Krishna conscious he was, what plans he had. In one conversation with the devotees, he said, one day we should buy all the available land in Mayapur. He said, you see, we talk about one acre, two acres. He says, one day, we should buy all the land available in Mayapur, and we should set up a self-sufficient community. Now you see what Prabhupada is going to say. He said, once we are established, then we will declare individualism, then we will declare independence from India. We will have our own country called, the country of Mayapur. And all the temples in different parts of the world will be our embassies. And all the temple president in those temples will be the ambassadors in those, from the country of Mayapur. And all over the world will be exporting, devotional paraphernalia. And we will print our own currency, called Chaitanya currency. One Chaitanya, five Chaitanya, ten Chaitanya. And people from all over the world will apply for visa to visit the country of Mayapur.

Prabhupada used to say, if you go to the forest, and aim for a rhinoceros, rhinoceros, you know, it's huge. You aim for a rhinoceros, if you are not able to kill rhinoceros, at least you will kill one rabbit. But you aim for rabbit, you will kill one ant. In other words, we don't have to kill anyone. But the idea is, Prabhupada showed how we have to think big. For Krishna. What is Yajna? For the pleasure and satisfaction of the Lord. For ourselves, nothing. Nothing is required. Na dhanam, na janam, na sundarim, na kavitam, kuch nahi chahiye. But for Krishna, I want the whole world. Because, the world belongs to Krishna. Everyone is part and parcel of Krishna. Just like Hanuman wanted Sita, for Ram. So we also should get into, the spirit of Shila Prabhupada. What was his consciousness? We are his disciples. So we also, first janma sarthak kari, we have to be Krishna conscious. We should strive to be Krishna conscious. Always be in a mood, how we can please Prabhupada, please Krishna. And then, try our best. Once we are Krishna conscious, then Krishna Prabhupada will use us to be Krishna conscious. There was one devotee, he did something very nice. And he, you know, always we look for some appreciation, some name, fame, adoration. So that devotee wrote a very big mail. I did this, I did this, I did this, I did this, this, this, this. He wrote a big mail to me.

And he marked to someone else also. So he was expecting typically, you know, reply like, very nice, very nice, excellent, well done, keep it up. So he did not get reply. Generally, I would have replied, but there was two, three other devotees also marked in that email. Because everyone could sense that, you know, too much of, too much of glorifying oneself. I did this, I did that, because of me this happened, because of this me happened. So, after two days, we met in my office. So he was little upset. And he again opened up that talk, I did this, I did that, because of me, we could save five lakh rupees. But I am surprised that nobody is appreciating. So I explained to him that how our consciousness, what is material consciousness full, which is there 99.9% of the people, I did it. That is level, zero level consciousness. I did it. I did it. Krishna says, what is that verse he says? Maya Prithagyana? Krishna is not in picture at all. That is level zero. Krishna is not there. I did it. What is level one? Little better than that. Little better than that is I did it, I did it, but by Krishna's help. Krishna's mercy, I did it. Who did it? I did it. Kartam is very prominent, but a little bit Krishna came in between. Krishna's help. Many times we also did only, we only. Then others, so that we get more benefit, by mercy of Krishna. So that the other person feels that I am not only I did it, but I am humble also. So by Krishna's mercy.

#### humility, Krishna consciousness, service, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=2257
**Verse References:** —

**Key Quote:** "At that point, we should not again go back in Maya and think that I did it, or I was capable, I have so many abilities. That's why it's called razor's edge."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna and Prabhupada use us as instruments for their preaching mission, and we should not become proud or think we are capable on our own, and begins to share a story about Prabhupada and a dress made for Tulsi Maharani.

So as devotees also, we should remember that Krishna at times for his preaching mission, Prabhupada at times for his preaching mission, will use us as an instrument and we will end up doing big, big things. At that point, we should not again go back in Maya and think that I did it, or I was capable, I have so many abilities. That's why it's called razor's edge. In a moment, you can be in illusion. So did I last time, I shared that Prabhupada once went to a temple and dress for Tulsi Maharani was made here? So that's also a wonderful pastime. If I have shared, otherwise I thought I will probably share. How many of you were not there when? So Srila Prabhupada, briefly I will share. Because it's a very touching thing and shows how Prabhupada's mood was. Because all the devotees, we all are very fallen. And a pure devotee is compassionate. On one side, yes, he sees the good quality. Srila Prabhupada once was visiting a temple. And so in his room, there was Tulsi Maharani. And the dress was worn out. So the temple president called a mataji and said, can you please stitch a dress? And it was a newly married couple, husband and wife. So the mataji was very excited. She got the service of making a dress for Tulsi Maharani, which is placed in Prabhupada's room. Prabhupada is going to come next day. So whole... So the night she worked and she stitched the dress with no experience whatsoever of stitching. But with a sincere desire to please Prabhupada to stitch a very nice dress. But imagine with no skills, no experience. Somehow she made the dress. And next day morning before Prabhupada's arrival, the temple president saw the dress. He says, what a nasty, what dress have you made? What will Prabhupada think? Scolded her. And said, I don't think we can put this. Better to put the old dress. She started crying, crying, crying. Anyway, the president said, okay, okay, put now. Put that dress to console her. Don't cry. Put that dress. Somehow with crying, crying, crying, she put that dress. You know, what happened? Why done? I've done disservice to Prabhupada. You know, one small service I got, that also I couldn't do properly. So crying, crying, and she put that dress.

#### compassion, devotee community, fault-finding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=2445
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada also saw the good in devotees. Everyone has faults."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story of the Tulsi dress, highlighting Prabhupada's compassion and ability to see the good in devotees despite their shortcomings, and cautions against fault-finding and blaspheming in the devotee community.

Then Prabhupada came. He entered the room. First thing he saw Tulsi Mara. He said, what a wonderful, beautiful dress. Who has made this? And this lady was, you know, scared. Wonderful. Beautiful dress, very nice dress. Who has made this? First thing. And that lady again started crying. Out of sheer joy, sheer compassion, which Prabhupada is saying. So this lesson for all of us, this thing is when we are in a community of devotees trying to practice Krishna consciousness, we'll have all kinds of devotees. So Prabhupada also saw the good in devotees. Everyone has faults. Everyone has shortcomings. Everyone has anarthas. But Prabhupada at times amplified just to encourage devotees. And many times when we are not careful, we try pulling someone down by criticizing, blaspheming, finding faults. We should restrain unless it is our aspiration. Otherwise, there's a maja. We come from material world, in the material world, it's a maja to pull someone down. There's a rasa in criticizing, finding faults. So we as devotees, we have to be careful because I have realized over many years, this is one thing which pulls devotees out from Krishna consciousness. Fault finding, blaspheming. Not so much breaking the regulative principle. Not so much because we are weak in sadhana. Many reasons because of which we can be weak in sadhana or because of conditioning is challenging following the regulative principles at times. Krishna Prabhupada is very merciful. They understand. But when we go on a wrong track, unnecessarily finding fault, blaspheming, focusing on other people's shortcomings, passing sarcastic remarks, then Krishna Prabhupada, don't hold it. It is tantamount to Vaishnava Pradha and then our consciousness is ruined. So if we are Krishna conscious, be careful, sincerely work, all our acts become yagya and as Prabhupada is explaining in this verse, then overall there will be prosperity. Our consciousness will be bright. We will have, you know, we will be Krishna conscious. Krishna conscious, which is ultimately what we are trying to be. So this is a wonderful verse where it says, if Krishna consciousness is there, then there is no shortage. There is happiness, there is joy. One is nicely situated, there's profuse supply of everything. There's no disgruntlement. We can see the hand of Krishna. We can see the reciprocation of the Lord. We can see how the Lord is so loving, so kind to His devotees. We can see how the Lord is so loving, so kind to His devotees. We can see how the Lord is so loving, so kind to His devotees. So all that good things we will be able to experience.

#### modes of material nature, energies of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVHd_GmNbh8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The influence of material energy is characterized in three ways, the three modes of material nature: Rajoguna, Tamoguna, and Sattoguna."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the topic of the lecture, the influence of the modes of material nature (gunas) on the living entity, and acknowledges Srila Prabhupada's contribution to understanding this knowledge.

So today, 14.15 to 18, so four verses from the 14th chapter of the Gita. Even the last class was on the modes of material nature. Can anyone in layman's language help me with an answer: what are the modes of material nature? Raise your hands, anyone. What is modes? What is gunas? Neelamji, would you like to answer, what is gunas? Qualities? Anybody else? Will a little boat elaborate on state of? State of mind? Yes, state of mind. Positive points? Who said material enjoyment? Nature of material enjoyment? Anybody else? What is modes of material nature? Yeah, so broadly speaking, there are two energies of the Lord. What are the two energies of the Lord? Spiritual energy and material energy. The influence of material energy is characterized in three ways, three ways in which the material energy acts on the Jeeva. That is the three modes of material nature: Rajoguna, Tamoguna, and Sattoguna. So these verses will be talking about what is the influence of the modes on the living entity, what is the influence? Our gratitude to Srila Prabhupada because of his purports, his explanation, his teachings, we have access to this confidential knowledge.

---
#### karma, reincarnation, gunas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVHd_GmNbh8?t=149
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Based on the gunas which are predominantly influencing the soul, if a person dies where predominantly he is in tamo guna, even if he is a human being, next life he takes birth as an animal."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the influence of the gunas at the time of death and the resulting type of birth one takes, emphasizing that our choices and actions determine our future.

This is the 15th verse, when one dies in the mode of passion, he takes birth among those engaged in fruitive activities. And when he dies in the mode of ignorance, he takes birth in the animal kingdom. Now all of us, we did not choose which family we'd take birth in. Anyone remembers? You were given a choice, just like when we go for, when we apply for a job, we have options. When we go out looking for a house, we want to buy a house, we have options. Depending on our budget, depending on so many other parameters, we decide, okay, this is good for me. Does anyone remember that? We were given a choice. Which family you will take birth in, what gender you would like to have, which nationality. We all opened our eyes, found ourselves in a particular family and our journey began. So here it is said, based on the gunas which are predominantly influencing the soul, if a person dies where predominantly he is in tamo guna, even if he is a human being, next life he takes birth as an animal. And if he is in passion, he takes karmana, daiva netrena, jantur deha utpatyate, karmana, according to his activities, daiva netrena, under divine supervision, jantur, the living entity, deha utpatyate, gets a body. So, this is what happens. It is not happening by chance. It is not by chance somebody is born blind. It is not that by chance somebody is born in a rich family. Although it appears, just like for a villager who does not know anything about aerodynamics, by chance the airplane is flying in the air. But there are people who understand aerodynamics. They understand the laws which are governing that aeroplane because of which it is flying. So for a common man, it does not matter. But there are well-defined laws why somebody is born in a good family, a bad family, somebody is born hale and healthy. As far as doctors are concerned, they will give an immediate cause. For example, while a person is born blind, the mother was anemic. There was a fetal development. The fetal development got retarded. And as a result, the child is born blind. But now from the perspective of the child, why that atma, that soul, had to take birth through a mother who was anemic. And thereby whole life suffered blindness. Mother was the immediate cause. The ultimate causes are gunas and karma. Guna, karma, vibhagasha. If we get out into the world as a born, can we accept the birth of a dead man? Some aren't born now. Many can be born in childhood. Even if we die. They just can't be born. You have to live with them, even if it's that simple process. They're going to spread out, then you are going to see the qualifications that make other people proud. You can see the self, the potential of the shots, the ways of using them. You try out of yourselves. And when one dies in the mode of ignorance, he takes birth in animal kingdom.

#### human life, opportunity, gunas, spiritual progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVHd_GmNbh8?t=379
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Human life is a great opportunity that we can elevate from tamo guna to rajo guna to sattva guna and from sattva guna to shuddha sattva, pure goodness."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of human life as a junction where one can either elevate or degrade based on the gunas, and encourages striving for higher modes of goodness.

Some people have an impression that once we get human birth, we are always going to get human birth. It is not a fact. Human life is like a junction. We can either go up or we can degrade. Degrade means animal species, up means there are even higher life forms and eventually go to the spiritual world. If one develops mode of ignorance, so human life is a great opportunity that we can elevate from tamo guna to rajo guna to sattva guna and from sattva guna to shuddha sattva, pure goodness. We should not live in a materialistic way, untouched by any material quality. Therefore those who are actually serious about human life, should strive for elevating from the lower modes. Otherwise we are not even conscious of the danger, and we have no clarity as to what happens after death. Will a man be called successful if he passionately works whole life, earns a lot of money, lot of wealth, living in a palatial bungalow and Atma does not die. It's the body which dies. Body is temporary, Atma is eternal. What if in next life the same Atma, the same conscious being takes birth as an animal. The example is given just like spark of fire. When the spark of fire falls on dry grass, it can get reignited as fire. If the spark of fire falls on wet grass, it remains as a spark for some time and gets extinguished. If the same spark of fire falls on water, it gets completely extinguished. So we are spark of consciousness, Mamaiv Amso, we have the consciousness of Krishna, consciousness of God. If the spark of fire falls on wet grass, it remains as a spark for some time and gets extinguished. So we are spark of consciousness, Mamaiv Amso, we have the consciousness of Krishna, consciousness of God.

That Krishna consciousness, God consciousness, understanding of higher things, there is life after death, there is God, there is connection with God, I am accountable for actions all that good knowledge is gone eat drink be merry enjoy life next life is a reality but don't care who cares and if it is tamo guna if that spark falls on water it's completely extinguished so there are people where this Krishna consciousness is completely extinguished this they boldly say there is no God I don't care hell with God so we have the three gunas Satwaguna a person in Satwaguna has a consciousness that this life is temporary I am NOT the body I should not be so much blindly attached to things that higher consciousness is there in Rajaguna a person lacks real knowledge it's all driven by I want more I want more greed lust drives him goads him to action and tamo guna as I said eat drink be merry completely in ignorance goal of life is to enjoy himself.

#### analogy, gunas, happiness

**Summary:** Using the analogy of caterpillars endlessly circling in search of food that is right in front of them, this segment illustrates how those in Rajoguna and Tamoguna search for happiness in the wrong places.

Regarding running caterpillar or the first caterpillar was somehow put in a circle that it is behind the last one. And this experiment was done and found out that these caterpillars endlessly kept on moving in a circle although the food was placed right in the circle of the center of the circle they kept on moving although they were hungry in search of food without realizing the food is right there and they died. So here we have caterpillars when placed in a circle will continuously follow each other around until they die and why are they moving in search of food. So when we are in Rajoguna and Tamoguna we are moving in search of what? Real happiness and in search of real happiness will keep on moving although the real happiness is right there.

#### purification, goodness, distress, foolishness

**Summary:** This segment discusses the results of actions performed in each of the three modes, emphasizing the importance of regulating one's life in the mode of goodness for purification.

By acting in the mode of goodness one becomes purified Satyam, Saucham all these qualities one has to cultivate being truthful, being honest, being clean so therefore there are today morning we were discussing about in the Ashtanga Yoga process. This is the Yama Yoga. Yama means regulated lifestyle, rules and regulations, rules, do's and don'ts. When one regulates one life in mode of goodness in Satyoguna one becomes purified. Work done in the mode of passion results in distress, in misery, in pain. And actions performed in the mode of ignorance results in foolishness.

#### gunas, self-assessment, influence of modes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVHd_GmNbh8?t=815
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even in a day you will realize many a times we are in Sattva Guna, early in the morning yes after we have woken up fresh."
**Summary:** This segment involves a self-assessment of the audience's predominant guna and explains how the modes influence our actions and behavior throughout the day.

How many of us feel that we are predominantly in Tamoguna? Some people are reluctant, some people raise hands and. Okay. How many of us feel predominant in Tamoguna? Some people are reluctant, some people raise hands and. Okay. Predominantly we are in Raja Guna. How many of us feel we are predominantly in Sattva Guna? How many of us feel we are predominantly in Shuddha Sattva? I didn't raise hands, I was just indicating that such pure souls should raise their hands. So yes there could be a person who is predominantly in this thing but always we are influenced by mixture of modes. Even in a day you will realize many a times we are in Sattva Guna, early in the morning yes after we have woken up fresh. After good night sleep we feel very fresh, we feel very calm, we feel very contented, we feel good and then as day passes by Raja Guna increases and at times we are lazy, don't feel like doing anything, feel very cranky, feel very agitated, feel like sleeping more and more, very lazy that is the influence of Tamo Guna. And Krishna in the Gita says these modes are battling among themselves. The modes influence us and influence the way we act, the way we behave attract the influence of the modes. So they all go in a circle.

---
#### gunas, diet, lifestyle, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tVHd_GmNbh8?t=941
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual consciousness involves regulating our lifestyle, eating the right kind of food."
**Summary:** This segment explains how our diet and lifestyle choices are influenced by the gunas and how they, in turn, reinforce those gunas, emphasizing the importance of regulation for spiritual consciousness.

If we are in Raja Guna you will like to eat Rajasic food and because you are eating very Rajasic diet. It will invoke the influence of Raja Guna. Rich becomes richer, poor becomes poorer. If you are in Sattva Guna your actions will be Sattvic, you will eat Sattvic diet as a result it will invoke more Sattva Guna. If you are in Tamo Guna under the influence of Tamo Guna your diet choices, your liking, your taste buds will demand Tamsik Ahar. And because you eat Tamsik Ahar, eat food which is in ignorance, it will invoke more of ignorance. So that's why spiritual consciousness involves regulating our lifestyle, eating the right kind of food. Krishna talks about what is food in the mode of ignorance, food in mode of passion, food in goodness. Sacrifice in mode of goodness, passion in ignorance. Work in the mode of ignorance, passion in ignorance. So work done in the mode of passion results in distress and action performed in the mode of ignorance results in foolishness. Activity performed under the spell of mode of passion, there is definitely great misery. That action itself is very painful or rather very painful. And the result also, immediate pleasure may be there, but in the long run it brings in misery and pain. Ignorance, work done under ignorance, it's misery in the beginning, misery in the end. Passion may have pleasure in the beginning, but poison in the end. Karanam guna sangasya sad asad janam.

### Is God a Person? | Bhagavad Gita Verses 9.11 - 9.12 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### nature of God, Bhagavad Gita, ignorance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AQoBPphvqz8?t=19
**Verse References:** BG 9.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Fools deride me when I descend in the human form. They do not know my transcendental knowledge, nature, and my supreme dominium over all that be."
**Summary:** The lecture begins by posing the question of whether God is a person, energy, nature, or nonexistent, then introduces a verse from the Bhagavad Gita stating that fools deride Krishna when He appears in human form because they do not understand His transcendental nature.

The topic for the day is, is God a person? How many of us say God is a person? How many of us say God is energy? Power and energy. And how many of us say God is not there? Any other option? God is nature. So how many of us say God is not there? How many of us say God is nature? Nature is God. So based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada, we'll be discussing this particular topic, taking insights from his beautiful purports, explanations which he has given. Let's chant this particular verse together: Avajananti Mahamudha, Avajananti Mahamudha, Manushimtanuma Shrita, Manushimtanuma Shrita, Parambhaavam Ajananto, Parambhaavam Ajananto, Mamabhuta Maheshwaram, Mamabhuta Maheshwaram. Translation: Fools deride me when I descend in the human form. They do not know, they do not know my transcendental knowledge, nature, and my supreme dominium over all that be.

#### creation, intelligence, art

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We very well know, it is very, very clear in our mind without any doubt that somebody has painted it, am I right?"
**Summary:** Using the analogy of the Mona Lisa, the speaker argues that just as a painting requires an artist, the creation of the universe requires an intelligent creator, not just energy or chance.

Last time we had discussed a couple of examples. You see, when we see a picture, a beautiful piece of art, like we showed the picture of Mona Lisa, immediately we appreciate the painter, the artist. And we very well know, it is very, very clear in our mind without any doubt that somebody has painted it, am I right? If someone were to tell you that picture of Mona Lisa came on its own, one fine day it manifested, or children were playing with some colors and somehow one of them splashed some colors on the canvas and accidentally this picture manifests, how many of us would like to subscribe to that understanding? Can a picture of Mona Lisa come on its own or accidentally manifest because some colors were splashed on the screen? No. We know there was an artist. Can we say it was some energy, some artistic energy? Can we say it was some artistic talent which manifests the picture? Yes or no? No. There was some artistic talent which made that picture possible. Yes or no? Yes. Whose talent? A person. A person. A person. A person. A person. A person. We see beautiful pieces of art, we see beautiful creation.

#### intelligence, creator, design
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/AQoBPphvqz8?t=232
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** Extending the analogy, the speaker uses the example of a building designed by an architect and satellites designed by scientists to illustrate that intelligence and planning always originate from a person, leading to the conclusion that the intelligent creation of the universe implies a personal creator.

This building was built, there is an architect. Whose intelligence has gone, a lot of intelligence has gone into plan this building? The strength of the beam, the columns, how much concrete has to be poured, what ratio it has to be poured. Concrete mix. So there is architect, although it is a general, architects have designed this building, but when we say architects of design, there is a particular person, Mr. Manjrekar, who was the chief architect who designed this building. So just like the Mona Lisa we have Leonardo da Vinci, likewise behind this wonderful creation, this material nature, like some of us said nature is God, we see beautiful trees, we see colorful plants, we see beautiful birds, wonderful landscapes, yes there is some power, there is some energy, but who is behind that energy, who is that intelligence, who is that intelligent creator, who has planned it, just like we took an example of satellites in space, we have scientists working round the clock monitoring those satellites, who have designed those satellites, who have planned how it will go. It will be moving in their respective orbits, it has not come on its own. Joint venture between, yes, yes you are right. So where I am leading to or heading towards is that whenever we talk about some intelligence, whenever we talk about some artistic skills, it belongs to a person.

That same person who is standing in the battle of Kurukshetra speaking to Arjuna, he is saying, maya adhyakshena prakriti suyate sa chara acharam. My adhyaksh means supervision, under my direction. Prakriti, this material nature, suyate is manifesting. Chara, chara means moving and acharam, non-moving, under my direction. So we saw that how the earth is moving around the sun in a fixed orbit, at a fixed distance, day after day, day after day, so meticulously, so systematically. Ten days from now, what time the sun will rise, we can calculate. Who has designed that system? When orbits is moving in their respective orbits, yes, some scientists have put together their intelligence and designed it. Who has designed the distance between earth and... Who has designed the gravitational... that force? So, one option is, yes, there was a big bang and then survival of the fittest and everything started, you know, falling in place. Generally, things don't fall in place like that. For example, this building didn't happen that all the materials were piled up together and suddenly a big bang happened and then building came up. For the left ear, you have to go to another doctor. For the nose, you have to go to another doctor. Specialization. What is specialization, by the way? Definition of specialization? We see in this world, a lot of people who are rich, they are attractive. We see people who are very beautiful, they are attractive. We see a lot of people who are knowledgeable, may not be beautiful, Stephen Hawking is attractive. So God is a person who is unlimited in these six opulences. **Key Quote:** "Soul evolves, the soul transmigrates from one species to another species. Species don't change."
**Summary:** In response to a question, the speaker contrasts Darwin's theory of evolution with the concept of transmigration of the soul, explaining that the soul evolves and moves from one species to another, but species themselves do not change.

Okay, any other question on this particular point? We'll come to the form of the Lord. Any other question? What is? So we have, just like we have Darwin's theory of evolution, in that we have unicellular organisms, and then we have more complex this thing. What Darwin has told us that these organisms are evolving in terms of, a unicellular organism evolves and becomes a complex organism. And then it evolves based on survival of the fittest into a much more complex organism. This is not the right understanding. The living entity, the soul, transmigrates from one kind of body to another higher form of body. Just like I gave example, in this Mumbai city you have different kinds of houses. You have 2BHK, 3BHK duplex, penthouse, you have bungalows. A duplex doesn't become a penthouse. A 1BHK does not with passage of time, survival of the fittest becomes a 3BHK house. But a resident of that house from 1BHK moves to 3BHK. So soul evolves, the soul transmigrates from one species to another species. Species don't change. A monkey doesn't become a human being. A soul in a monkey can in next life become a human being. And vice-versa. There is transmigration. There is evolution. Evolution of the soul, not evolution of the body.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Is Nothingness the Ultimate Reality? | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### voidism, illusion, reality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KkSF1plGoSg?t=89
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If he is talking so much about something, it has to be something, and if he is talking about it for four hours, how can that something which he is talking about for four hours be zero?"
**Summary:** The speaker critiques the philosophy of shunyavadis, who believe in the ultimate reality of nothingness, by pointing out the contradiction in speaking extensively about something that is supposedly zero.

They say the world is empty and meaningless. They make people repeat this very loudly, and everyone says, "Yes, the world is empty and meaningless." So what? There is no meaning in this world. You have created the meaning. It's all your imagination, all your beliefs. Even so-called God is imagination; it's a belief. Somebody believes in one God, another person believes in another God, and they are fighting, not realizing that it's just an imagination. So there was once a hardcore shunyavadi. It's not just actually in the world; there are mayavadis, there are shunyavadis, hardcore. So one shunyavadi, he thoroughly believed that ultimately the ultimate reality is zero, shunya, and he spoke for four hours about zero, about how this world is zero, ultimately it's zero, shunya. And he also very proudly, very boastfully, very enthusiastically said, "What about four hours? I can speak for four days about shunya," and he had a lot of stuff to talk about. So the point here is, if he is talking so much about something, it has to be something, and if he is able to talk for four hours, it has to be something, and if he is talking about it for four hours, how can that something which he is talking about for four hours be zero?

---
#### preaching, reality, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KkSF1plGoSg?t=191
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It's important that we ourselves get convinced of this fact that these all are not some mythological stories; this is a reality. The Lord is a reality, his past times are reality."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Prabhupada's forceful preaching against mayavadis and shunyavadis, and then describes how even intelligent and well-educated people are swayed by philosophies that deny reality, highlighting the importance of being convinced of the reality of the Lord and his pastimes.

So Prabhupada was very forceful, and actually when he went to the West, or even otherwise, he was very, very, you know, forcefully preaching and defeating all these mayavadis and shunyavadis, and people are getting swayed because it appeals to them. This landmark thing I'm talking about, intelligent CEOs of the company, who's who of the country, you know, very well educated people, it's a costly thing, twenty thousand for three days, and finally they all get impressed, and they go back with this understanding that it's important that we ourselves get convinced, we ourselves get convinced of this fact that these all are not some mythological stories; this is a reality. The Lord is a reality, His pastimes are reality.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Jananivas das Remembers Srila Prabhupada
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### first impressions, spiritual vision, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MmZ_mteihGg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "What they were looking for, they saw it in Prabhupada."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts his initial meetings with Srila Prabhupada, describing Prabhupada's penetrating gaze, the experience of offering a garland, and the realization that Prabhupada embodied the spiritual qualities he sought.

We were waiting for Srila Prabhupada, we were having kirtan, and then suddenly Prabhupada just appeared there. He was like a genie out of the lamp, I guess. It wasn't only me, because I was with Roberto Nanda and he was there chanting and playing the dunga, and he also said, Prabhupada, he just suddenly said, Prabhupada, he just seemed to stop. And Prabhupada was just standing there, very aristocratic, and then he was just looking at all the devotees there, one after another, and then he looked at me, the new bhakti, and then he just looked straight through me. And as if to say, you know, I'm looking for you, I'm coming for you, not what you think you are, all these identities you have, I'm seeing you as Krishna's devotee, I'm coming to get you. And I was thinking that, you know, I'm standing here in Kolkata airport, I'm completely naked, the whole world is watching me, I've got nothing to hide from this man, he's seeing me. So then... Everybody had a garland to give, they went forward and offered their garlands, so I went forward to offer my garland also, and I had a little brass bowl with rose petals, and they said, when Prabhupada comes, you should throw these petals before him. Anyway I went and I offered the garland to Prabhupada, and the little bowl I still had in my hand, it just touched Prabhupada here, and it made a little ringing sound, but I was so close to Prabhupada. At that time, in his eyes, I was looking at his eyes, but it didn't even blink, you know, when something comes close to your eyes, so what to speak of touching your eyes, but Prabhupada, it didn't blink, it didn't even waver, and I thought, wow, this person is completely peaceful, completely tranquil, you know. My ideas of spiritual life was, you know, that complete peace, harmony, you walk into the water, no ripples coming out, and things like that. And... Prabhupada, you know, what I'm looking for, what I'm aspiring for, what I think is spiritual, Prabhupada's got it, he's got what I was searching for, and a lot of other devotees had that same experience when I spoke to them. What they were looking for, they saw it in Prabhupada. I don't think it was Prabhupada manifesting it, but I think Krishna was manifesting it. Here, what you're looking for, you found in my devotee. And then I paid my obeisances. And when I got up, no one was there, I don't know how long I'd been paying my obeisances. Anyway, by the time we got back to Calcutta, Prabhupada was already in the temple. He was giving his lecture, a rival address lecture. So I thought, now I'm going to hear Prabhupada. I've been reading these books, and now I'm going to actually see him. So I sat down, and I was, and he had already started his lecture, so the first thing I kind of heard him say was... So, do not try to see God. And I thought, I thought that was what we were supposed to be doing. We were all out there seeking, and I thought that was the whole idea, we come to look for God. I meet the greatest saint I've met so far, and he's saying, do not try to see God. But then he said, but you act in such a way that God will see you, by a performance of your devotion and bhakti. So that was the first impression of meeting Prabhupada. At that time, we were having a Pandal program in Deshapriya Park in Calcutta. It was during that period. And so then after the program, Prabhupada would generally be invited to someone's, either a relative of his, because they knew him, or a local person, but now Prabhupada was the hero. They were these Western disciples. So we all went to this house. It was one of those typical Bengali community houses, you know, with the open space in the courtyard in the middle. And everyone was there, all the noise, and all the kids. And Prabhupada was kind of completely relaxed, and all the commotion, everyone was coming in and settling down. Prabhupada was just sitting down there, relaxed. All the kids and everything were playing around. And Prabhupada was kind of just getting settled. Yeah. I was in the room, and I was packed up, and I was sitting up in the back wall. I was sitting next to Jadubar's wife, Vishakadevi. She was Jean at that time. Jadubar got initiated at the same time. He was John, right? And, but she didn't get initiated at that time. She got a bit later. But anyway, I was sitting next to her and talking, talking to her, you know, about, talking about Krishna consciousness and how great it is, and Prabhupada and things like that. And, you know, I was just brand new. I didn't know, in India especially, there was that, there wasn't such much emphasis on separation of the men and women. We didn't know at that time, did we, in those days? So anyway, I was rapping away with Vishakadevi and then Prabhupada. By the time he kind of got settled, and then he was, and then he started looking, and he was, I could see he was going. He was kind of straining it a bit, and I'm, he was looking at me, you know. And I thought, am I doing, and he had like kind of a stern look on his face, and he was, and I thought, am I doing something wrong? And I thought, well, I guess I am. I just got initiated. I got this saffron cloth on, and I'm talking to this Mataji, and there's all the family members around. That's their culture. They know the culture very well, and I think Prabhupada was, and I said, I think I better finish talking. I kind of moved. I kind of moved away from Vishakadevi, but I think Prabhupada was showing a little bit of his disapproval that I was doing, but we were pretty innocent in those days.

---
#### initiation, rules and regulations, simplicity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MmZ_mteihGg?t=600
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The purpose was unfazed."
**Summary:** The speaker shares anecdotes about his initiation, Prabhupada's simplicity in Mayapur, and an incident involving a Mayavadi singer, highlighting Prabhupada's strictness and adaptability.

At my initiation, I remember three of the four regs, and then I thought, what was the last one? And I kind of completely slipped. I don't know. I completely slipped my mind, and I said, oh, intoxication, Prabhupada, because that was the only thing I was into, actually. I'd been in India, kind of, so we had, there was no, none of the, I was trying to follow everything, but we were following the sadhus and everything in India, and they were smoking and everything like that, so intoxication. I was surprised. I said all the three, but the one I was not doing at that time, I looked at Prabhupada and smiled, and oh, no, intoxication. He said, okay. He said, okay, good. Another thing Prabhupada didn't like was the mosquitoes. And there were millions and millions of mosquitoes and we were the only light around. We were on the bank of the Ganga. There was no other lights. As soon as we lit the kerosene lamp or candles and immediately all the bugs and mosquitoes would come. There was to burn the cow dung to make smoke. Prabhupada, he quite liked it. We were all crying and everything, stinging, but Prabhupada, it didn't affect him. And he quite liked it. He said, my Guru Maharaj used to have his chapatis cooked on this gobor, these cow dung patties. Gives them a very nice flavour. But he would complain sometimes. He said, I just put my hand under the mosquito net to get my books and immediately they're coming biting my hand. So Prabhupada was completely unfazed in Mayapur. There was no running water. There was no electricity or anything. He had a jute stick, a toilet, a hole in the ground with some jute sticks around it. But still he followed exactly the same schedule. He couldn't use his dictaphone machine because there's no electricity. So he had his typewriter under his mosquito net on his bed and he would type out with his two fingers. The purpose was unfazed. He got up at the same time he went out for a morning walk, come back and greet Nidhi. Even though there was no facility, he didn't faze him. His old schedule he kept maintained perfectly. Another time I was in the Bhajan Kutty of Prabhupada and one, probably an owl or a owl, probably a bowler, these were different groups of spiritualists, or so-called spiritualists, coming, you know, Gauranga Bhaktis, the upper sampradaya, what we call the upper sampradaya. So there were quite a few of these different groups who were often bringing Radha and Krishna pastime down to a mundane level. So anyway, on this one, what they call the ekathara, it's one string, one wire and one gourd, and they make a little instrument. So he would come in and we could hear him and he was singing, playing his ekathara. And Prabhupada said, do you know that song? I said, yes, Srila Prabhupada, I learned it when I was in Kashmir. He said, Mayavad, go and stop him. And I thought, well, now he's a new devotee, I thought, well, he's a sadhu, you don't tell a sadhu to stop chanting. But I had to do it because Prabhupada told me to do it. Anyway, by the time I walked out, he had already started leaving and gone.

#### obedience, devotional service, discernment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/MmZ_mteihGg?t=1200
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He was my god, he was my father, he was everything for me."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts Prabhupada's instructions regarding Mayavadis, his personal service of offering Chanamrita, and Prabhupada's instructions on training pujaris, highlighting the importance of obedience and proper understanding.

But Prabhupada said that in this temple, the TOVP, he said, no Mayavadis can come in. No Mayavadis, no langis. Langi means just a piece of cloth wrapped around, this kind of casual wear. Hindus usually wear it when they go home, and they take it. No langis, and no atheists. They cannot come in, he said. No Mayavadis, no langis, and no atheists. So this is the Mayavad he told me, tell him to stop. 1971, and in Bajangkuti, and Prabhupada was sitting on the veranda with one of his godbrothers. And I was just finishing worshipping the deities, Radha, Madhava, who were there. And I took the Chanamrita ball over to Prabhupada, and I said, Chanamrita Srila Prabhupada? And he just tilted back his head and he opened his mouth. And he let me pour the Chanamrita in. It's, you know, like he was my god, he was my father, he was everything for me. And then he's acting like a little kid, you know, like independent. Prabhupada would always come out with his different, you get different pictures of Prabhupada, he's different. And then his godbrother did the same and put the Chanamrita in his mouth. For the very first time, I gave him Chanamrita. I offered him, and then my hand began to shake uncontrollably. He'd never done that before. He'd never done it since then, but I was, and I was looking at my hand and looking at Prabhupada, and Prabhupada was just smiling, looking at me. And then I stopped, I was able to give him. But I took it as, you know, Maya, this is the first, like a direct service, I'm giving to Krishna's pure devotee and Maya's, you know, this is it. It is the beginning of the end, you're finished. Do a little bit of service for Prabhupada. In 19... Okay, now we're in Bhajangkoti again, in 1972. Prabhupada gave me an instruction. And he was talking with his managers and I was just sitting there. And then he said to the managers, he said, this Srinivasa is an ordinary person. He has to have some help. He cannot do everything. Because I was just one pujari then, so I had to do everything, morning till night. There was hardly any devotees there. So, but I loved it. But anyway, Prabhupada was considerate. He should have some help. He cannot do everything. Then he said, so you should train up pujaris. I said, yes, Shirdi Prabhupada. And I didn't really know what it meant. And then later on he was talking again. And then again he turned to me and said, so train up pujaris. I said, yeah, I think I would have forgotten definitely. Because we didn't really have any system at that time. I mean, there was nothing written down or all these different mantras we didn't have. So, I think I would have forgotten. But anyway, we were leaving Bhajangkoti like I had one foot inside and one foot outside. And I heard, train up pujaris. You hear me? I said, yes, Shirdi Prabhupada. So, good. You get instruction three times, like confirmation.
