#### spiritual purity, karma, ignorance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wS1puVFh_mQ?t=535
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We suffer only because of the laws of nature we have violated in the past."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of the Ganges, the speaker explains that even though a pure devotee may appear to have defects, their spiritual purity remains intact, and our suffering is due to past violations of the laws of nature.

Então, por isso que Rugu Goswami afirma que, por favor entendam, nós todos temos muitos doxas ou defeitos. Então, por isso que Rugu Goswami afirma que, por favor entendam, nós todos temos muitos doxas ou defeitos. E o corpo de um devoto puro também é completamente espiritual. Mas, com a visão espiritual recolhida, temos nossas próprias naturas condenadas a consideração e caridade para kita. E a nossa visão é conduzida, contaminada, influenciada pelos três modos que temos. Dhrishtai Svabhava Janitair Vapuchascha Doshai Vapuchascha Doshai na pracrttattam yah bhakt janasya pashyate. Então, por favor, não considerem os defeitos no corpo de um devoto puro, por favor, para ser defeitos materiais. Então está sendo explicado mais adiante por Rupa Goswami. Gangam bhasam na khalo budbudh fena pankair. Just like Ganga is there. On the river Ganges you will find sometimes foam, bubble and mud. And the water appears to be muddy contaminated. But it is being told in the last line, Brahma dravatvam. It is completely spiritual water. Although it is appearing contaminated, still a person who is wise will take bath in Ganges despite so-called contaminated external appearance of the Ganges. Because the spiritual purity is intact of the Ganges. So it is being explained, we should not think, all of us are suffering, we are having bodily defects because of our past karma, sinful activities. A spiritual master is required who can make us free from these past violations of law of nature which we have done, for which we are suffering. The suffering in our life people think is because of lack of education, because I am not living in a proper country or proper place, state, because of some people around me, because of the weather or some climatic conditions. No it is not because of that. These people do not know. This is ignorance of society. And they try to fix these externals, fix people around you, fix some external conditions, education, beauty, opulence etc. and you will be happy, no. We suffer only because of the laws of nature we have violated in the past.

#### surrender, spiritual master, divine correction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wS1puVFh_mQ?t=624
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna wants to correct us, guide us but we are not willing to listen to Krishna. So that is why Krishna engages his pure devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the role of the spiritual master in relieving us from past sinful reactions and uses the analogy of a mother-in-law correcting her daughter-in-law through her daughter to illustrate how Krishna uses pure devotees to instruct us.

So the spiritual master, if he follows his instructions nicely, who tells us to surrender to Krishna, he gets us relieved from all the past reactions of sinful life. But a person may see, oh spiritual master is also getting old, spiritual master is getting diseased. He himself is getting diseases means he is also having bad karma. In his baggage. So he himself is not liberated, how can he liberate us? This is very wrong mentality Rupa Goswami wants and the scriptures also want. All the instructions of Srila Rupa Goswami are thoroughly based on the scriptural evidences. So this is evidence in Himad Bhagavatam also, when Yudhishthir Maharaj was very much disturbed after the great war of Kurukshetra, where crores of people died and he thought because of my desire. To reinstate me on the position of king. So much loss of life has happened and bereavement and lamentation is there in society now. So he was not able to find solace, very much disturbed. So then he was given this instruction that please understand Bhishma Pitamah lying on the bed of pharaohs, he instructs Yudhishthir Maharaj, please do not think that this suffering is happening to you. Because of your past sinful activities. The example given is just like mother-in-law cannot directly correct or chastise daughter-in-law who has been recently married, she is very new to house. So she may commit many mistakes but who will get the scolding? Not daughter-in-law but the daughter. Daughter for no mistake, no fault of her will get scolding by the mother. Because by scolding daughter, actually mother-in-law wants to correct daughter-in-law. So in a similar fashion, Krishna wants to correct us, guide us but we are not willing to listen to Krishna. So that is why Krishna engages his pure devotees, sometimes he chastises, sometimes there appears some physical punishment given to the pure devotees and through that Krishna is willing to instruct us.

#### Sanatana Goswami, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wS1puVFh_mQ?t=759
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Eu estou indo para o inferno, estou contaminando o corpo do Senhor Chaitanya, que é o próprio Krishna, e que grande pecado eu estou encarando por isso?"
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Sanatana Goswami's illness and his distress at potentially contaminating Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, highlighting Sanatana's humility and concern.

One very beautiful instance comes in Sri Chaitanya Charitamrita, we are discussing the verses given by Srila Rupa Goswami. Brother of Srila Rupa Goswami is Srila Sanatan Goswami. So Sanatan Goswami and Rupa Goswami were given the task of re-excavating the lost places of Krishna's past times in Vrindavan and Sanatan Goswami wanted to meet Chaitanya Mahaprabhu who was residing in Jagannath Puri after taking Sanyasa. So Sanatan Goswami travelled through the forest of Jharikhanda to meet Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, e quando ele estava cruzando aquela floresta, claro, você não tem restaurantes na floresta e até restaurantes não estavam lá naquela época. Então as pessoas ficavam no templo, levavam o prasadam do templo. Na floresta, a comida não pode ser regularmente disponível. Então Sanatana Goswami, quando ele estava viajando, sua dieta era muito irregular e ele acreditava em tomar um banho em um reservatório de água que não era muito puro. Então se nós queremos nos mantermos seguros, o estilo de vida de regulação é importante para os nossos parceiros. Se nós comermos de uma forma regulada e termos uma vida regulada e tomamos banho duas ou três vezes por dia, então nós seremos seguros e saudáveis. Então simplesmente não por tomar medicina ou fazer x, y, z, você pode se tornar seguro. A menos que o estilo de vida seja regulado, a segureza nunca virá. Então Sanatana Goswami, de alguma forma, por não ter comida adequada e tomar banho e contaminar, ele desenvolveu muitas fontes em seu corpo, que usavam sangue e sangue. E nesta condição, Sanatana Goswami, mesmo que tivesse um corpo infeccioso, Sanatana Goswami, com grande aflição, quando ele olhava Sanatana Goswami, ele iria abraçar Sanatana Goswami e toda a sangue e sangue iria esmerar o corpo de Sanatana Goswami. Então Sanatana Goswami ficou muito distorcido, que eu vim aqui para a minha purificação, mas o que eu estou fazendo? Eu estou indo para o inferno, estou contaminando o corpo do Senhor Chaitanya, que é o próprio Krishna, e que grande pecado eu estou encarando por isso?

#### suicide, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wS1puVFh_mQ?t=892
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ninguém pode alcançar o segredo de Krishna, simplesmente por deixar esse corpo voluntariamente. Um tem que alcançar Krishna, executando o serviço devotional, essa é a única maneira."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of Sanatana Goswami, explaining his plan to end his life and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's rebuke, emphasizing that perfection is achieved through devotional service, not suicide.

Então ele pensou, eu vim aqui para a minha purificação, para o meu melhoramento, mas o resultado é o contrário. Então ele pensou, se eu continuar a viver aqui, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu irá continuar me abraçando. E ele estava se sentindo muito, muito envergonhado. Que Chaitanya Mahaprabhu iria me abraçar. E ele estava se sentindo muito, muito envergonhado. não realmente pertencem, eles nasceram em uma família de Brahmin, mas por causa da associação dos reis musulmanos, eles eram ministros no governo de Nawab. Então, por causa da associação com os reis musulmanos, eles foram ostracizados da sociedade Brahminica e eles foram considerados só musulmanos. Então, naquela época, a sociedade era muito, muito rígida. Mesmo se alguém que está seguindo alguma outra religião, como o Islãm, ele espreme água, eles vão te dizer que você também foi convertido, você se tornou contaminado. Então, é por isso que toda a cultura é perdida por causa destes crentes extremistas. Então, eles também foram ostracizados da sociedade e eles foram considerados musulmanos. E no templo de Jagannath Puri, só os hindus são permitidos entrar. Mesmo alguns dos devotos de Hare Krishna que vêm de outros países, eles não são permitidos entrar. Então, sua única esperança de ver os Jagannaths é quando o Senhor Jagannath sai durante a festa da Ratha Yatra, no mês de junho e julho, isso acontece todos os anos. Então, Sanatana Goswami, essa foi a sua única esperança, que quando o Senhor Jagannath sai, eu vou poder ver o Jagannath. Então, ele estava esperando, quando eu ver o Jagannath na carta da Ratha Yatra, e as cartas são muito grandes, com grandes rodas, eu vou deixar meu corpo caindo na frente da roda da Ratha. E dessa forma, quando ele estava contemplando, então, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, ele é Krishna, ele sabe a mente de todos, ele veio para Sanatana Goswami, e então ele o castigou, que você está pensando em deixar esse corpo, então, que tipo de compreensão você tem? Você acha que esse corpo pertence a você? E você acha que cometendo suicídio, você vai alcançar perfeição espiritual? Então, quando discutimos esse Shloka de Bhagavad Gita, que a perfeição da vida pode ser alcançada por Yamyam Vapi Smaran Bhavam Tyajate Ante Kalevaram.

#### surrender, body as property, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/wS1puVFh_mQ?t=1030
**Verse References:** BG 8.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Esse corpo é minha própria propriedade, como você pode destruir a propriedade de alguém?"
**Summary:** The speaker explains Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's instruction that one cannot attain Krishna simply by voluntarily leaving the body, emphasizing that the body is not our property and should be used for devotional service.

Krishna está assegurando em Bhagavad Gita, na época da morte, uma pessoa que pensa em mim, ele vem para mim, vem para a minha planeta, e ele recebe um corpo da minha natureza. O corpo de Krishna não é mudado pela influência do tempo, ele não fica velho, ele não morre, é eterno, cheio de conhecimento e cheio de prazer. Então, qualquer entidade viva também recebe um corpo espiritual, se ele é capaz de pensar em Krishna na época da morte. Então, algumas pessoas estão perguntando, então, Prabhu, se eu cometer suicídio agora, e pensar em Krishna, então eu vou para Krishna, para a terra de Deus? Então, é por isso que Chaitanya Mahaprabhu queria nos instruir, então é por isso que ele fez essa Lila, Sanatana Goswami ficando infeliz, e por isso nós estamos recebendo instruções. Então, muitas pessoas podem pensar, oh, deixe-me cometer suicídio, de qualquer forma, esta vida está cheia de desgraça, pensando em Krishna, eu serei perfeito, não há luta necessária, purificação é necessária. Então, o Mahaprabhu disse que ninguém pode alcançar o segredo de Krishna, simplesmente por deixar esse corpo voluntariamente. Um tem que alcançar Krishna, executando o serviço devotional, essa é a única maneira, senão é tão fácil, apenas veja a forma de Krishna e atire-se, você irá voltar para Krishna? Não, é uma atividade sincera, disse Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Então, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu explica a Sanatana Goswami, outro fator muito importante, que você se surrendera a mim, e você pensa que esse corpo pertence a você, para que você possa deixá-lo, esse corpo é minha própria propriedade, como você pode destruir a propriedade de alguém? Então, é por isso que nunca se pode pensar em cometer suicídio, porque esse corpo não pertence a nós, para um devoto ou um não-devoto,

**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a weapon to illustrate that the body is a resource given to us, and its proper use lies in serving Krishna, not in independent desires.

nós não criamos esse corpo, nós fomos dado esse corpo, esse corpo é uma ressource, se usarmos essa ressource muito bem, suponhamos que uma pessoa seja dada uma arma, é uma ressource muito poderosa, agora, se essa arma for usada no serviço do governo, uma pessoa pode ganhar um monte de dinheiro, um monte de nome, fama, promoções, propriedade, tudo, ele pode se tornar um general, e se essa mesma arma for usada para a sua própria satisfação pessoal, desejos visíveis, então a pessoa pode ser dada uma punição capital também, ou ele sofreu um longo tempo na prisão. De outra forma, esse corpo foi dado a nós como uma ressource, se usarmos no serviço da pessoa, Deus, que nos deu esse corpo, então nós podemos alcançar a felicidade ilimitada, e se usarmos visivelmente, apenas para satisfazer a mim mesmo, essa pessoa está nos dando problemas, deixe-me usar essa arma para o matar, então nós estamos perdidos, nós estamos terminados, a arma também será levada e nós seremos colocados em prisão, então não vamos conseguir outro corpo humano, e tanto punição será dada a nós, então a vida humana não é destinada a desejo independente, mas para dar prazer a Krishna, dessa forma, todos os sentidos de uma entidade viva são realmente satisfeitos, essa ciência as pessoas não sabem, então Chaitanya Mahaprabhu instruiu Sanatana Goswami, esse corpo não é sua propriedade, além disso, quando você se desistiu completamente de mim, então o Mahaprabhu explicou, eu estou aqui em Jagannath Puri, mas Vrindavan, onde o Senhor Krishna aparece, através desse lugar, eu quero espalhar as glórias do Senhor Krishna em todos os lugares, e eu quero fazer todas essas tarefas do seu corpo, seu corpo específico, então outra instrução muito importante que o Senhor Chaitanya deu, que nunca considere que você é o que faz, você está fazendo qualquer coisa, tanto no mundo material quanto no mundo espiritual, no mundo material, a energia material, a energia espiritual,

### ROOT CAUSE OF ALL PROBLEMS | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE  Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### introduction, prayer, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/opBl_lcYU5Y?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** introduction
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We can go away from this discussion with a lot of takeaways, a lot of insights which can help us lead our lives in the right direction."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa begins the Gita Life session by introducing the topic: the root cause of all problems, and invites the audience to join in prayers to Shila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya, pancha tattva, and Lord Shri Krishna.

Hare Krishna, warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita live session. Today we are going to talk about a very, very important topic, and the topic for the day is root cause of all the problems. Every one of us encounter challenges in life. We all face different problems, but if we do root cause analysis, the root cause of all the problems, each one of us are in different circumstances, different situations, encountering different problems, but the root cause is the same, and that is very nicely analyzed by the Lord in the Bhagavad-gita, and we are going to talk about the root cause in today's discussion. So thank you for joining us. We will start the discussion with prayers. I request all of you to join me in offering the prayers, seeking the blessings of Shila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya, pancha tattva, Lord Shri Krishna, seeking their blessings so that we can do justice and go away from this discussion with a lot of takeaways, a lot of insights which can help us lead our lives in the right direction.

#### suffering, categories of problems, adhyatmic klesha, adi bhautic klesha, adi daivic klesha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/opBl_lcYU5Y?t=272
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the problems we encounter can be categorized as in three categories: Adi Bhauti kleshas, Adhyatmi kleshas, and Adi Devi kleshas."
**Summary:** The lecture is structured into five parts, beginning with the types of problems encountered, classified into three categories: Adhyatmi kleshas (body and mind), Adi Bhauti kleshas (other living entities), and Adi Devi kleshas (natural calamities).

So today we will structure our discussion on this subject in five parts. The first part would be the types of problems we encounter. They've been very nicely classified in our scriptures in three broad categories. What are those three categories in which all our problems will fall into? First, we'll talk about the three categories. The first key category is that which has been classified in our scriptures. The second key category is that which has been classified in our scriptures. All the problems we encounter can be categorized as in three categories: Adi Bhauti kleshas, Adhyatmi kleshas, kleshas means miseries, difficulties, challenges, Adi Bhauti kleshas, Adhyatmi kleshas, and Adi Devi kleshas.

---
#### suffering, categories of problems, adhyatmic klesha, adi bhautic klesha, adi daivic klesha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/opBl_lcYU5Y?t=484
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Adhyatmi kleshas are the problems, the challenges which we will face because of our body and mind."
**Summary:** The lecture continues to define the three categories of problems: Adhyatmi kleshas (body and mind), Adi Bhauti kleshas (other living entities), and Adi Devi kleshas (natural calamities).

Adhyatmi kleshas are the problems, the challenges which we will face because of our body and mind. For example, today already we have one question here today. Why did Sushant Singh Rajput, today it's the news, today he committed suicide, very famous Bollywood actor. Why did he resort to such an extreme step? He was a celebrity, very famous actor. But whatever I read briefly, it mentioned that he was going through depression last six months. So our problems could stem from our body and mind. Even in this present pandemic, lot of people who have bodily ailments, complications, already pre-existing diseases, they are succumbing to the COVID-19 and many of them are losing their lives. So that's called Adhyatmi kleshas. Adi Bhauti kleshas are the problems which we get because of other living entities. It could be our neighbours, it could be our family members, it could be our peers in the company where we work, it could be our superiors, it could be even animals. For example, many of us have a challenge. We have lot of dogs barking in the night and not able to get good sleep. It comes under the category of Adi Bhauti kleshas. Adi Bhauti kleshas are the problems which we face. Adi Bhauti kleshas are the problems which we get because of other living entities. And the third one is Adi Devik kleshas. The miseries or the problems which we will encounter, which we encountered due to natural calamities, cyclones, floods, drought, tsunamis, earthquakes, too much of heat, global warming, all this comes under Devik kleshas.

So, if we analyse in our lives, although yes, we should not be pessimistic, we should have an optimistic outlook, we should be positive, but we should not turn a blind eye to the realities of life. We should be very objective, objective in our outlook. It is said that when a rabbit is being chased by a tiger, the rabbit runs, runs very fast. But at one time the rabbit realises that the tiger is chasing, imagine the speed at which the tiger can run is phenomenal compared to the speed at which rabbit can run. At one point the rabbit realising that it cannot escape. It just sits dumb in one place and closes its eyes, and thinks, thinks, that there is no tiger. Just by thinking that we have no problems and turning a blind eye to the problems which we are actually encountering in life is not what our scriptures are recommending. Our scriptures also not recommend a tiger. Why? not recommending that we law people these days they take to drugs they take to alcohol and somehow find some relief to the problems our scriptures say we can boldly face the problems and find a solution let's not turn a blind eye in fact a person who is in knowledge will ask a question why am i suffering why there are so many problems in this material world none of us want disease but much against our wish we have to face so many problems on one side it may look that the person is pessimistic the other side it's an item of knowledge.

Krishna says one who can see the light of the world can see the light of the world can perceive that janma mritu jara viadi these are the problems for which we have to find a solution he is in knowledge so the example is given that just like a straw which is floating on an ocean gets helplessly tossed by the ocean waves the straw practically has no independence it just gets tossed left and right up and down sometimes submerged sometimes floating on the water our predicament in this material world is like that: we are tossed by the waves of time, the kala, and much against our wish sometime we are a submerged, sometimes we are a float, sometimes we have tossed left right at times much against our wish the way things are unfolding in this world today this coronavirus infections spreading peaking it's much against our wish but we see even big big powerful leaders governments relatively they're helpless much against our wish so that way in this material world the living entities the Jeevas are struggling punarapi janma punarapi marna and just like the example is given fish if you take it out of water the fish will wriggle wriggle it will never be comfortable till it is put back in water so although it may appear that I am suffering from this material world the living entities the Jeevas are struggling sounding quite pessimistic but if we analyze that's the harsh reality of this material existence a fish out of water.

In the life of different people and that law is the law of karma as you sow so shall you reap newton's third law of motion says every action has an equal and opposite reaction so in that sense let us all be very very clear each one of us each one of us is getting what is due to us based on the karma we have done in this life or our previous life we might have forgotten some of us may think that why me i have not harmed anyone i have not i have to the best of my knowledge learned lived a very regulated life pious life i have helped people i have been very spiritual person still why me quite possible that it could be because of the karma the reactions of the deeds we have done this life and the previous lives so law is very simple if we engage in pious activities good karma then we enjoy the reactions in this material world and enjoyment of reactions come in the form of good births good births good births Aishwarya, opulence, Shruta, education, Shree, beauty, handsome, good looking, good well built body, healthy body. And the reverse is also true. If we engage in sinful activities, sinful deeds, Papa Karma, then we suffer the reactions in the form of difficulties, challenges, miseries in our lives, poverty, abject poverty, no food to eat, too many problems, financial problems, health problems, family problems. And at times it can be very overwhelming, so much so that people even take the extreme step of committing suicide. And we see that happening quite frequently. #### karma, suffering, suicide, sinful activities, lust
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/opBl_lcYU5Y?t=1277
**Verse References:** BG 3.36
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker argues against suicide, stating that it doesn't end problems as the soul is eternal and karma follows, and introduces Arjuna's question to Krishna about what impels one to sinful acts, even unwillingly.

But believe me, suicide is not the answer because we think that when we put an end to this life, the problems will vanish, the problems will be over. But the soul is eternal. And wherever we take it. Wherever we take birth, our Karma will follow. And we have to suffer the reactions of the deeds which we have done, because of which we were suffering in this life, added to that the karmic reaction of putting an end to our life, which also is a sinful act. So sinful activities result in sinful reactions. The reactions manifest in our life. We get good reactions in the form of various problems, difficulties and challenges. And if we engage in pious activities, we get good reactions in the form of happiness, pleasure, fame in this material world. Good birth. So now all of us, after we have understood this law, the natural consequence or the natural take away from it. Take away from this understanding would be, let me not engage in any sinful activity. And let me just engage in all pious deeds. But Arjuna, in the third chapter of the Gita, this is the 36th verse, asks a very, very brilliant question to Krishna. Now this question is really brilliant because he says. He says. He says. He says. He says. He says. Oh, descendant of Vishnu is referring to Krishna. By what is one is impelled to sinful acts? Impelled to sinful acts. Even unwillingly, as if engaged by some kind of force. Many of us know that I should not be doing this.

#### lust, sinful activities, analogy, forbidden fruit
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/opBl_lcYU5Y?t=1641
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "If you take apple from this tree, this apple tree, you will become as powerful as God himself."
**Summary:** The speaker tells the story of Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden, where they were tempted by a snake to eat the forbidden fruit from the apple tree, leading to their fall into the material world.

For example, the Lord created Adam and Eve and they were in the gardens of Eden, full of lush greenery, lot of flower and fruit bearing trees. And the Lord created them and said, have a good time, enjoy your life. You can take fruits from any of the trees, eat as much as you want. But this one tree, the Lord pointing to an apple tree, which was laden with a lot of apples, this one tree you avoid. After all, it was not a bad proposition. You can eat from any tree, anywhere, as much as you want, just avoid eating from one particular apple tree. So they agreed. Not a bad proposition. But one day, while Adam and Eve were passing by this apple tree, one of the snakes from that particular tree, which was somehow hanging down from that tree, hissed in the ears of Eve and said, wait a minute. And then it started speaking, why don't you take an apple from this tree? So Eve said, no, the Lord has forbidden us. We shouldn't be taking apple from this tree. So the snake told Eve, it says, you know why, you know why the Lord has forbidden? Actually the true reason I know and the reason is that if you take apple from this tree, this apple tree, you will become as powerful as God himself. And you know, God does not want others to be as powerful as him. Therefore he has told both of you to eat as much as you want from any other tree, but from this tree. The real reason is if you take this apple, you will be as good as God, as powerful as God and Lord doesn't want that. Anyway. Why don't you take one apple? So Eve consulted Adam and both of them decided if that is the case, then we can have it. So as soon as they had the apple from that apple tree, the forbidden fruit seems they fell from the garden of Eden to Eden gardens in Kolkata. We have the famous Eden gardens. Okay. In Kolkata, they fell in this material world, the world of dualities.

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna identifies lust as the root cause, comparing it to smoke covering fire, dust covering a mirror, or an embryo covered in the womb, illustrating different degrees of covering.

So here Krishna is telling it is lust only. And then Krishna says as fire is covered by smoke, as mirror is covered by dust or as embryo is covered. So, Krishna gives three examples to differentiate because the degree of covering might vary. Somebody is covered too much with lust or consciousness. So just like fire is covered by smoke, little bit you fan the smoke just turns away and you can have the fire back again. And then. Okay. And then the mirror covered by dust and the embryo covered in the womb. So the living entity is similarly covered by different degrees of lust. And then Krishna says thus the wise living entities pure consciousness becomes covered by his eternal enemy in the form of lust, which is never satisfied and burns like fire. Okay. So, we all have lusty desires. By the way, before we get into more discussion about lust, many of us have an understanding that lust has something to do with sexual desires or sexual pleasures or sensual pleasures. Lust has a little more broader understanding. Yes, sexual desires or sensual pleasures. Okay. Physical desires is a physical manifestation of lust. But lust has a much bigger perspective.

#### lust, relationship with God, enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/opBl_lcYU5Y?t=1950
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We are meant to serve the Lord. We are meant for giving pleasure to the Lord. We are meant for enjoyment for the Lord and not that Lord is selfish that we serve the Lord and we give enjoyment to the Lord and we are like slaves."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that lust is a desire to be the Lord rather than serving Him, and that true pleasure comes from acting for the satisfaction of the Lord, not from independent enjoyment.

To give you a background, the Lord, we are his amsa, we are amsa part and parcel of the Lord. We are his children. Okay. Okay. And we are created just like the amsas, just like this hand in this body or different organs in the body. They are serving the whole, they're meant to serve the interest of the whole body. And in turn, their interest is served. The hand does not independently enjoy the eatables. Okay. Okay. Okay. Okay. Nothing. Nothing. Nothing. Nothing. In fact the food is present in the body. The food is present in the body by picking up the food objects, putting in the mouth, the stomach apparently enjoys that. And the different organs get their real respective requirements. So likewise we are amsa of the Lord. The Lord is the purusha. He is the enjoyer. In that sense we are enjoyed by the Lord. We are his part and parcel. Now, mind you, pruusha. Purusha is the enjoyment. Mind you, that we are enjoyed does not mean we are like slaves, just like this hand. They are part of this body, they do their respective functions and get their required nourishment, happiness. If any of the parts try to independently enjoy without doing the function in the interest of the whole body, then that's an abnormal situation. So what is lust? We are meant to serve the Lord. We are meant for giving pleasure to the Lord. We are meant for enjoyment for the Lord and not that Lord is selfish that we serve the Lord and we give enjoyment to the Lord and we are like slaves. No, when we do that, we get our quota of enjoyment. We get our quota of pleasure. That pleasure is real pleasure. That pleasure is the spiritual pleasure. That pleasure is the one which will actually fulfill us, satisfy us. But when the living entity desires to be the Lord, rather than being enjoyed, giving pleasure to the Lord, acting for the satisfaction of the Lord, when the living entity desires to enjoy, giving pleasure to the Lord.

#### senses, mind, intelligence, regulation, brahmacharya, lust

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of regulating the senses from the very beginning, as taught in Vedic times through the brahmacharya ashram, and contrasts this with the modern emphasis on enjoying life.

If we are bewildered by a lot of bhoga and aishwarya, we will say that the steady mind, the steady intelligence to understand the sublime subject matter will not come about. So as Krishna is saying, from the very beginning regulate the senses. That's why in Vedic times, the first training which a person would be given is to how to regulate and train his senses and mind, the brahmacharya ashram. Even before a person enters the grhastha ashrama, he would be learning how to control the mind, how to control the senses. Because they were intelligent, they knew what is the root cause of all problems. And right from the very beginning of life, a person would be educated, given that kind of a training. Unlike in present time, the reverse is true. We are taught by hook or crook, enjoy life. We have different advertising, you know, advertisements telling us, eat like this, drink like this, sleep like this, enjoy like this. And then Krishna says, thus knowing oneself to be transcendental to material senses. We are not this body, we are not the senses. One should study the mind by spiritual intelligence. Steady the mind and thus by spiritual strength, conquer this insatiable enemy called lust. So Krishna is giving us a clue what is the root cause, what is the greatest enemy of the living entity. It is lust because of which we are goaded much against our wish to act in sinful ways, sinful activities.

#### Krishna consciousness, purification, love of God, desires
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/opBl_lcYU5Y?t=3522
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When we hear about the Lord, when we glorify the Lord, when we chant the names of the Lord, all that lusty desires slowly starts getting transformed, starts getting purified. And that lust gets transformed into pure love of God."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the practices of Krishna Consciousness, such as chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra and hearing Bhagavad Gita, gradually purify sinful and lusty desires, transforming them into pure love of God.

So the do's and don'ts are recommended. And the practices of Krishna Consciousness, Prabhupada is so nicely recommending chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, hearing to Kirtans, hearing Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, will slowly, gradually purify all our sinful desires, all our lusty desires. NASHTA PRAYESHU ABHADRESHU NITYAM BHAGVATA SEVAYA, BHAGVATI UTTAMA SHLOKE BHAKTIR BHAVATI NASHTIKI. So when we hear about the Lord, when we glorify the Lord, when we chant the names of the Lord, all that lusty desires slowly starts getting transformed, starts getting purified. And that lust gets transformed into pure love of God. It is said that initially we have pure love of God. But when we come in this material world, just like that Adam and Eve came in this material existence, this world of duality, this original love of God, which is there in our hearts, gets transformed into pure love of God. It gets transformed into lust. Prabhupada gives an example, just like when milk comes in touch with a sour substance, it becomes curd. So the process of Krishna consciousness, the instructions which Krishna is sharing in the Bhagavad Gita is how to transform that lust which is there in our heart, which is covering our consciousness, covering our real knowledge, covering our desire for self-realization, how that lust can transform. We gradually transformed into love of God. This whole process of Krishna consciousness, bhakti yoga, is all about that transformation. We cannot stop desiring. We cannot annihilate the desires. We cannot be desire-less. We cannot be inert like a stone. But we can have pure desires. Just like that. Just like that. Just like this organ, part and parcel of the body. It is not meant to remain inert. They are created for a purpose. So when we do justice to that purpose, using our body, using our senses, using our mind, using our intelligence, we serve the Lord. We serve the interest of the Lord. We get aligned with the desires of the Lord. Our desires get purified and our life starts getting transformed.

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So now question is, are we just some bundles of chemicals?"
**Summary:** The speaker presents a scientific analysis of the chemical composition of the human body and its market value, questioning whether humans are merely bundles of chemicals.

Now in the earlier years of 20th century, a scientist analyzed the constituents of human body, what this body is composed of. And they came up with very interesting finding. We all know body is composed of chemicals. The human body has enough lime to make, if you were to use those chemicals, you can, the lime which is there in the body, you can make 5 cakes of soap. Enough iron to fabricate 6 small nails. Enough phosphorus to make 20 matchboxes. Enough sugar to sweeten 10 cups of coffee. A little sulfur, a little calcium and other trace elements. So we have minerals, we have elements and we have other elements. And we have other things which we discussed, phosphorus, sugar. If we were to dismantle these chemicals, this body is constituted with all the chemicals. If we were to dismantle this body and sell all the chemicals which constitute our body at the market value of these chemicals at present time, can anyone help me with an answer? What will be the value of our body? How much we will earn? How much will we get? If we dismantle the body and sell all the chemicals which constitute our body at the market value today, how much will we get? 10 rupees. Why phosphorus? Sugar is quite costly these days. So if you were to sell those chemicals at the wholesale price, not retail price, you will get 240 rupees. That's the value of chemicals which constitute our body.

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of the soul (Jeeva) from the second chapter of the Gita, explaining how the soul continuously changes bodies even within this lifetime and at the time of death.

So in the second chapter of the Gita, Krishna introduces the concept of Jeeva or the soul. He talks about that how even in this body, even in this very life, we are changing bodies. There was a time when we had a baby body. And then as we grew up, we had another kind of body. In fact, medical science says that every seven years, all the cells in our body are replaced by new set of cells. In other words, if I had a body when I was a child, when I was let's say 15 years old, today I have a completely different set of cells which constitute my body. But still when I look at my family album, and I say this is me when I started going to school. This is me when I graduated. Although the body has changed completely, the constituents of the body, the cells have changed completely, but still I say it is me. So what has not changed? The soul. So there is a verse in the Gita where Krishna says, just like even in this body, we had childhood, boyhood, youth, old age, just like even in this life we are changing bodies, from childhood to boyhood to youth to old age, exactly like this, Dehantra Praptir, Deha means body, Anta means at the end, Praptir, we accept another body at the point of death. If you see the cameras, like we are shooting today, the video, the camera is also looking at me. You are also looking at me. When I crack a joke, hopefully if the joke is good, you all will smile. The camera will not smile. It is registering what I am saying, it is capturing that video bite, but it is not having a reaction. But it is not having an emotion to express. So the lens of video camera may see, but it has no consciousness. It has no feelings. It doesn't go through an experience. It has no judging capacity. The camera will, at the end of session, it was a very nice talk. It just simply is registering. It has no emotions. It has no experience. The best of cameras may have best of features, but consciousness is not there. Emotions is not there. Feelings is not there. Now we may have a robot, we may say, the robot is programmed and says, I love you. It can say so, but it cannot have that emotion. A human eye also sees and has consciousness, has judging capacity. We can judge, yes, what was told makes sense. We have emotions. We may feel sad, we may feel elated, we may feel joy, we may feel sorrow. We also go through experience. #### death, consciousness, near-death experience

**Summary:** The speaker discusses the experience of death, noting the absence of consciousness in the body after death, and references a book by a cardiac surgeon about out-of-body experiences and near-death experiences (NDEs).

dies, many of us would have experienced death, close quarters, some near and dear ones departing. We see the body is lying, but there is no consciousness. That very body which, you know, a small pinprick and there was a feeling of pain, that body is cremated, put in a funeral pyre, nothing happens in terms of pain and pleasure, because the consciousness is not there. Even in science today, in fact there is a book written by a person, this book is all about out of body experience, he is a very big surgeon, cardiac surgeon, talks about out of body experiences. Many times it so happens that while a person is on the operating table, critical case, and sometimes casualties happen. They are not able to save lives, big cardiac arrest. And at times it so happens that the person is declared dead, clinically dead, ECG, all the pulse, it's all dead, zero, flat. And the doctor has declared that the person has died. And there have been many many cases where half an hour later or one hour later, the person awakes, becomes dead. And not only that, the person comes and shares all what he has observed during that half an hour, 45 minutes of period. Doctor saying, he has passed away. Or the nurses talking, there was a case where this body talks about where a person was very angry at the doctor, because they were speaking something very casually while the whole operation was going on. So, these days, out of body experiences, near death experience, NDE it's called. There is a lot of literature on that. And even scientists do agree, many of them agree that reincarnation is a fact.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces Text 2 from the Bhagavad Gita, explaining the difference between the body (Kshetra) and the knower of the body (Kshetragna), which is the soul, emphasizing that bodily conception of life is the root cause of misery.

Past life therapy, hypnotic regression, these are all very common these days, where a person can using hypnosis make to remember his previous lives. Text 2. The blessed Lord said, this body, O son of Kunti, is called the field. And one who knows this body is called the knower of the field. So, there are two things. In bodily concept of life, we think, I am the body. And we discussed in one of the sessions, that is the root cause of our misery. And that's why people get completely shattered when someone near and dear one passes away, because all along, we have lived with that understanding, with that belief. So, there are two things. One is the body, the Kshetra. Just like, you know, you have the field of activities. For a farmer, there is a field. So, this body is a field of activities. It's called the Kshetra. And there is a Kshetragna. Kshetragna is the knower of the field of activities. Who is the knower of the field of activities? The soul. The Lord said, this body, O son of Kunti, is called the field. And one who knows this body is called the knower of the field. The body is called Kshetra, or the field of activities. And Kshetragna is the knower of the field. The soul, the spirit soul, the Jeevatma.

Mind, intelligence, and false ego. And then, within this is the soul, which is Sat, Chit, and Ananda. Somebody wants to sleep a lot, Kumbhakarana, which body is more conducive? Polar bear, six months. Carefully. Some people are born with some specific talents. This is all there in the sukshma sharira, which is the subtle body.

#### soul, karma, eternality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xZDAEDOXf9A?t=1423
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The right understanding is the atma is eternal. We have to pay for whatever we do. As you sow, so shall you reap."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the Charvak Muni philosophy, which believes everything ends with death, with the understanding that the soul is eternal and must face the consequences of its actions, even in future lives.

So when the body dies, what gets cremated, what gets burnt, what gets buried is the gross body, panch bhuta. So there is a philosophy which says that everything is over, everything is burnt to ashes. This is Charvak Muni philosophy, we talked about it. He says ultimately, endless, it has to become ashes. So eat, drink, be merry, enjoy life. But this is not the right understanding. The right understanding is the atma is eternal. We have to pay for whatever we do. As you sow, so shall you reap. May not be in this life, next life. And that's why sometimes people get perplexed when they see this small child, so innocent, what mistake he did, he had to go through all this. The child in that form, in that body may look innocent, but the soul is not you know, two year old or three year old. Soul is eternal. This is one of the reason why there is lot of bewilderment, good things happening to bad people, bad things happening to good people and people sometimes think, where is God? Where is justice in this world? And then people say, okay, I don't believe in God.

#### karma, time, justice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xZDAEDOXf9A?t=1495
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "What makes us bewildered is there is a time lag between what we have done and what results we receive in our lives."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of silos storing grain to explain the time lag between actions (karma) and their results, which often leads to bewilderment when good actions seem to yield bad results, or vice versa.

This reminds me in the farm houses, in olden times, even now it may be practiced, they used to have huge silos for storing grain. So what they used to do, whenever they would harvest the crop, they will put those grains, finally the grains which gets harvested, they will put in the silos from the top. And throughout the year, the grains will remain in that silos and at the bottom of the silos, there was an opening, some kind of a tap, some kind of a wall and whenever they wanted to draw some grains out of that silos, they would open the wall and the grains would fall. So one time, a man was observing very close closely, so in a particular year, there was a very good harvest, very good quality of grains which were produced and they put in the silos. Unfortunately, next year, it was a big drought and the whole crop got affected and unfortunately the grains were of not good quality and after harvesting, again that same thing was put in the silos. A person who was watching from a distance he saw that what we have put from the top is bad quality of grains. But when we are opening the wall, what is happening? Good quality grains are coming. So then he is wondering how this is happening. You are putting bad quality of grains but you are drawing good quality of grains. Some magic is happening in the cauldron. Is the magic happening? No. What has been put earlier, you are now drawing. What you have put recently, you will draw in times to come. Same is the case with our karma. What makes us bewildered is he is doing good things but he is drawing bad results. Or vice versa. He is doing bad things but he is drawing good results. Net net is everyone will reap as you sow so shall you reap. What makes us bewildered is there is a time lag between what we have done and what results we receive in our lives.

#### Supersoul, consciousness, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xZDAEDOXf9A?t=1644
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Perfect knowledge of the body and the individual soul and the Supersoul is known in terms of Vedic literature as Gyanam."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna is present in every body as Paramatma (Supersoul), possessing supreme consciousness and awareness of all bodies, and that perfect understanding of the body, soul, and Supersoul is Gyanam (knowledge) according to Vedic literature.

Now here Krishna is saying, I am also present in every body as Paramatma, as Supersoul. Lord is present as Supersoul in the bodies, knows everything about all bodies. So Prabhupada in the purport explains that just like a king may know about the entire kingdom but a man may know about his house. Just like for example our chief minister Padnavesh will have will be conscious of everything happening in different districts of the state. What is happening in Nagpur, what is happening in Amravati. We may not be at all conscious of that. We may at best be conscious of what is happening in my society or what is happening in Pavai or at best what is happening in Mumbai. So here we have a crude example that how a person who is much more bigger or powerful has much broader awareness. So Lord who is a Supersoul, who is a Paramatma he is not only conscious of our body he is conscious of everyone. That's why it is called supreme consciousness. Super consciousness, Paramatma the Supersoul. And just like in a state, the entire kingdom actually belongs to the government. But there are secondary proprietors. Government might have leased the property to someone or might have sold the property to someone. So likewise we are the secondary proprietors of this body. So perfect knowledge of the body and the individual soul and the Supersoul is known in terms of Vedic literature as Gyanam. Perfect understanding of who we are, who the Supersoul is, what this body is, what this karma is, perfect understanding as per the Vedic literature is Gyanam. This is the opinion of Lord Shri Krishna.

#### soul, reincarnation, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xZDAEDOXf9A?t=1842
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As per the law of nature, it is designed that all living forms move from lower forms to the higher forms."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the soul's transmigration through various life forms, from fish to trees to butterflies, eventually leading to human birth, which is a result of many lives of karmic reactions.

this is what is happening. The soul in one life can be body of a fish. Next life, it can be a tree. It can be a butterfly. It can be a bird. It can be a tiger. And after many many lives of this transmigration from one body to another, the soul is fallible. The soul gets human birth. Now, somebody asked me this question. This fish or maybe the tree does not have the ability to choose. It does not have, although it has a free will, but it is very dormant. So how does it do any karma and why does it has to, on what basis does it move to another body? Anyone knows the answer? So, as per the law of nature, it is designed that all living forms move from lower forms to the higher forms. Plants, birds, aquatics, reptiles, then move on to higher and higher forms. They complete their so called quota of karmic reactions and move to the next body, next body, next body, next body, next body, and after many many lives, mahunam, janmanam, ante, a soul gets this human birth.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Real Renunciation | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### renunciation, detachment, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0VdamPvV1No?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Ravana wants Sita for himself. Hanuman wants Sita so that he can take it and give it to Rama."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts real and false renunciation, explaining that renouncing objects without seeing their connection to the Lord is false, and illustrates this with the analogy of Ravana and Hanuman's different motivations regarding Sita.

What is real renunciation and what is false renunciation? That's why Rupa Goswami says, what is real renunciation and what is false renunciation. When one gives up objects thinking that they are material without seeing a connection with the Lord, that renunciation is false renunciation. That Vastu, that thing, if that relationship with the Lord is not seen and one gives up that object, money, suppose somebody has money, this is Maya. Money is Maya, but does not see a relationship with the Lord, a connection with the Lord. Budhya means with that understanding, with that consciousness, it is material. It is of this material world. There is Sambandha. Money has Sambandha with the Lord or not? Lakshmi is a consort of Narayana. But if we don't see that connection and we want to enjoy that Lakshmi, that is material. Ravana wanted to enjoy Sita. He is called Ravana, a demon. We burn the effigy and we celebrate that. When Ram wants to enjoy Sita, nothing wrong about it. Hanuman is also wanting Sita, yes or no? He went all the way to Lanka to get Sita, but for Ravana. You see, both are after Sita. Ravana is also after Sita. Hanuman is also after Sita. Both want Sita, but Ravana wants Sita for himself. Hanuman wants Sita so that he can take it and give it to Rama. Both are wanting Sita.

---
#### devotion, service, spiritualization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/0VdamPvV1No?t=135
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To use it in the service of the Lord, thinking that it belongs to the Lord, it is energy of the Lord, that spiritualizes that thing."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that wanting money is not inherently Maya, and that true renunciation involves using everything in the service of the Lord, recognizing it as His energy, which spiritualizes it.

Now both of us can be, all of us can be wanting money. We can be gunning after more money to earn more money, want to have more money. Is it Maya? Yes or no? If one thinks money is Maya, I don't want money, or gives up all this, this house is Maya, that is Falcovaira. Hari Sambandha Vastuna. There is Hari Sambandha. To use it in the service of the Lord, thinking that it belongs to the Lord, it is energy of the Lord, that spiritualizes that thing. Yukta Vairagya Ucyate. That is Yukta Vairagya. A devotee of the Lord, not only the things he has in this body, is whose energy? The Lord's energy, given by the Lord. This intelligence, this mind, this ability, this talent, is given by the Lord. Use it in the service of the Lord, and that spiritualizes the existence.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Realizations of Steve Jobs at death bed | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### material success, worldly achievements, power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JvxDb27DEYM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "In others' eyes, my life is an epitome of success. However, aside from work, I have little joy."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Steve Jobs and his immense success, highlighting his achievements and the perception of his life as the epitome of success, but also hinting at a lack of joy beyond his work.

These are the words of Steve Jobs. We all know Steve Jobs. He's known all over the world for his business acumen. It's because of him today Apple is what it is today. World over people are talking about Apple products, and it's one of the most valuable companies, billions of dollars of cash reserves, most profitable. And although it's being run now, everyone knows it, the work of Steve Jobs—the whole company, the innovation, the products. So now what Steve Jobs is saying, when he was in the last days, some of you may be aware he was suffering from pancreatic cancer. So he was bedridden, he was hospitalized. So he writes, "I reached the pinnacle of success in the business world." There can't be anyone more successful than him. Although there are many Fortune 500 companies, successful companies, but someone, if they can remember top CEOs of the world, he'll be one among them. "I reached the pinnacle of success in the business world. In others' eyes, my life is an epitome of success." It's like a benchmark. Success means there is success. However, aside from work, I have little joy. In the end, wealth is only a fact of life that I am accustomed to. So here I am talking about somebody may have power, position, may have all the wealth in the world, but unless you have power, position, you cannot be successful.

#### death, detachment, purpose
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JvxDb27DEYM?t=160
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "God gave us the senses to let us feel the love in everyone's heart, not the illusions brought about by wealth."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Steve Jobs' reflections on the insignificance of worldly achievements in the face of death, emphasizing the importance of pursuing matters beyond wealth and recognizing the true purpose of life.

All power, position, name, fame, what we hanker for, especially the demons, get so much puffed up with, they all pale into insignificance in due course of time. In the darkness I look at the green lights from the life supporting machines and hear the humming mechanical sound, he was in the ICU with all kinds of medical gadgets attached to his body. I can feel the breath of God of death drawing closer. Now I know when we have accumulated sufficient wealth to last our lifetime, we should pursue other matters that are unrelated to wealth. We are so fortunate we are directly pursuing that. Should be something that is more important than wealth, because after having achieved the epitome of success, something is missing, something more than what wealth can give us. Non-stop pursuing of wealth will only turn a person into a twisted being just like me. In one sense he was... deriving wealth from the other. He was a nice-hearted person. He came to India. He also learned yoga. He was practicing Buddhism. Some element of God-consciousness was there. God gave us the senses to let us feel the love in everyone's heart, not the illusions brought about by wealth. The wealth I have won in my life, I cannot take with me. Whichever stage in life we are at right now, with time we will all face the day when the curtains come down.

And it covers the real knowledge and makes us feel proud, makes us feel great.

**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the topic of the Secret of Happiness based on the teachings of Bhagavad Gita and the writings of Srila Prabhupada, emphasizing the universal desire for happiness.

You. Thank you. Hare Krishna. A warm welcome to all of you for today's Geeta Live session. The topic for the day is Secret of Happiness. We all are wanting and trying to be happy. We are seeking permanent happiness. There might be difference in terms of the professions we have chosen, the kind of work we are doing, the kind of circumstances we are in. Irrespective of gender, caste, color, nationality, everyone, a common denominator in all our lives is we all are seeking happiness. So today we are going to discuss the secrets, how we can pursue this desire of being happy based on the teachings of Bhagavad Gita, the writings of Srila Prabhupada. So before we get into this discussion, let's start the session with prayers. Request all of you to kindly join me. We can offer the prayers together. You can fold your hands and be with me on these prayers. And immediately after the prayers, we will get into the discussion.

---
#### prayers, Vaishnava tradition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bCyuPt_3TLU?t=146
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna Hare Hare. Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama Hare Hare."
**Summary:** The speaker leads the audience in a series of Vaishnava prayers, invoking various deities and expressing reverence within the Krishna Consciousness tradition.

Nama Om Vishnupad. Radhaya Krishna Prishtaya Bhutale. Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nidinamine. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine. Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Pashchiatya Deshatarine. Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya. Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadhadhara. Siva Sadhi Gaurabhakta Vrinda. Namo Mahavadanyaya Krishna Prema Pradayate. Krishnaya Krishna Chaitanya. Namne Gauratujhe Namaha. Namo Brahma. Devaya. Go Brahmane Hithaya Cha. Jagaddhitaya Krishnaya. Govindaya Namo Namaha. He Krishna Karuna Sindho. Dina Bandho Jagatpate. Gopesha Gopika Kanta. Radha Kanta Namostute. Taptakancham. Gaurangi Radhe Vrindavaneshwari. Vrishabhanu Sute Devi Pranamami Hari Priye. Vanchakalp Tarubhyascha Kripa Sindhubhya Evacha. Patitanam Pavnebhyo Vaishnavebhyo Namo Namaha. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna. Krishna Krishna Hare Hare. Hare Rama Hare Rama. Rama Rama Hare Hare.

#### soul, material body, subtle body

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The main thing is the soul."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the first point: 'Keep the main thing, the main thing,' explaining that the soul is the most important aspect of our existence, contrasting it with the gross and subtle bodies.

So like we have been doing in our previous sessions today, although we can talk from various perspectives, there are many many instructions in the Gita which will help us move in the right direction, pursue happiness in the right way. But today we will cover three aspects only. The first point, the first aspect is to keep the main thing, the main thing. I repeat, keep the main thing, the main thing. Although it might look a little difficult, it may look a repetition of words, but there is a lot of wisdom in these words, to keep the main thing, the main thing. Now we all know, we all have now been practicing the principles of Krishna Consciousness according to Bhagavad Gita, according to our scriptures. The main thing is the soul, the Atma, the real self. This body is like a covering. We have two kinds of covering. One is the gross body made of pancha bhuta, earth, air, water, fire and ether, which gets cremated, which gets burned, which gets buried when we leave the body, when the soul leaves the body. And there is a subtle body, Sukhshma Sharira. Sukhshma Sharira: Anas, buddhi and ahankara, mind, intelligence and false ego.

#### detachment, material attachment, story
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bCyuPt_3TLU?t=394

**Key Quote:** "So he approached his dearest wife, the fourth wife, and said, you know, I don't want to leave."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story of a merchant with four wives to illustrate the concept of attachment and detachment, highlighting how the wife he cherished the most refused to accompany him in death.

So the main thing is the soul, the Atma. Let me share with you a nice interesting story about a merchant. This I have shared in many forums, but I think it is quite pertinent that in today's discussion, we can also, so to say, hear it again, because there is a very, very important learning for all of us as far as happiness is concerned, as far as keeping the main thing, the main thing is concerned. So there was a merchant who had four wives. He was very, very attached to the fourth wife. He spent a lot of time with her, pampered her with all the comforts, all the luxuries, whatever her demands were, were always fulfilled. In one sense, you could say she was pampered, just like you have some pampered child. Always her demands were met. Inordinate amount of time was spent with her, although he had other wives also. The third wife, she was the most beautiful of the four wives and whenever he would go to some social gatherings or some parties, he would make sure that the third wife accompanies him. And he would take pride in introducing, she's my wife. She was very handsome, very beautiful, very good looking and she's my wife. The second wife, of all the four wives, she was the most loyal. Whenever he would go through some ups and downs in life, she was always there to be with him, to lend him a shoulder and hear him out, give him the moral support, emotional support. And the first wife was completely neglected, was completely ignored. He never spent any time whatsoever with his wife. Although she felt the pain that, you know, he's spending so much time with the fourth wife, third wife, but no time is being spent, none of her demands were being met. Years rolled by. A time came when he became old and he became quite sick. He was lying on the bed and he was getting treated, various kinds of medications, and it started dawning on him that his end is quite near, he has to leave. All along he has been in company of his wife and now it started dawning on him that he will have to leave and he will be all alone. He'll have to embark a journey in which he will be all alone. So he approached his dearest wife, the fourth wife, and said, you know, I don't want to leave. And she said, my dear wife, we have spent so much time together. I cherish the memories of the time we have spent. We have loved each other. We are attached to each other. I long for your company at this juncture when I have to leave. Would you kindly come with me? Would you kindly accompany me? The wife looks at him, says, no way, no way I can come with you. Just turns and walks away. It was like somebody has pierced a knife in his heart. Pain was unbearable for him. Nevertheless, fortunately he had other options.

---
#### detachment, family, wealth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bCyuPt_3TLU?t=634
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Maximum, I can come with you till the graveyard."
**Summary:** Continuing the story, the merchant seeks companionship from his other wives, but each offers limited support, highlighting the temporary nature of material relationships and possessions.

So he turned to his third wife and he said, my dear wife, would you kindly come with me? I need your company. I'm longing for your company. The wife said, life is very good out here. She was quite upfront. She didn't mince words. And she said, I'm quite young. Life is very good out here. To tell you frankly, after you're gone, I have plans to get married again, to remarry. So his heart sank, became cold hearing this answer from his third wife. He turned to his second wife and said, my dear wife. And this time he was practically begging, pleading because, you know, if she also refuses, then probably he has no option. He says, please, I beg you. I request you kindly come with me. I need your company. So the wife, quite in contrast to the previous wife, said, definitely I will come with you. I want to come with you. I want to give you company because she was the most loyal. He says, but, but. When he heard the word but, his heart again sank. He said, but, I have limitations. Maximum, I can come with you till the graveyard. As if, you know, this pain, he was intolerable. At this moment when he's about to leave, the body is in pain, he's in a diseased condition. And on top of it, he has to hear these answers from his wives. He was completely crestfallen, dejected, devastated. And then he hears a feeble voice, I will come with you. And when he turns back and he sees his first wife sitting in one corner, and he says, please, sitting in one corner of the room, completely emaciated, very thin, lean, maybe for days together she has not eaten, not wearing proper clothes, I will come with you.

**Key Quote:** "The fourth wife represents our body."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the analogy of the four wives, representing the body, wealth, family, and soul, emphasizing that only the soul accompanies us beyond death.

So now each one of us, each one of us without an exception, including me, each one of us have figuratively speaking four wives, each one of us. The fourth wife was very, very, was very attached to spend a lot of time and when the time came she said would you like to come with me, can you please accompany me and the wife said no and just walked away. The fourth wife represents our body. If we analyze morning to evening, we all are busy doing a lot of things. If we do some bit of introspection, some bit of thinking, where is our time going? What are we spending our time for? You will see a lot of time is being spent in fulfilling the bodily demands, bodily needs, bodily urges. We have five senses, each of the senses have some demands, some urges and morning to evening we are busy. You see so many restaurants are there, the whole food industry is there, the whole clothing industry is there, it's all about fulfilling the demands of the body. So many fitness centers are there, people are spending inordinate time in going to gym, keeping the body fit. Hospitals are there to keep the body healthy. So we spend inordinate amount of time taking care of the body but when the time comes, no matter how expert we are, how great we are, how well we have taken care of the body, how much we have met the demands of the body, no way it comes with us. That same body which we have so painstakingly maintained, fulfilled all the demands is just cremated, burned to ashes. The same hairs which we have so painstakingly groomed and combed and used different kinds of shampoos, in a second it's burned to ashes.

#### wealth, family, soul, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bCyuPt_3TLU?t=923
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The first wife represents the soul."
**Summary:** Continuing the analogy, the speaker identifies the third wife as wealth, the second as family, and the first as the soul, reinforcing the importance of prioritizing the soul's needs.

The third wife who was very, very handsome, very beautiful and who would take a lot of pride in introducing, she's my wife, represents our acquisitions, our wealth, our properties, our wealth, our so-called assets, bungalows, cars, we take a lot of pride in showing off. We invite our friends, near and dear ones, they admire, oh such a beautiful house, oh you bought a new car, it gives us a lot of pleasure. But when the time comes, everything is left behind. Not only it's left behind, it gets married to someone else. Someone inherits the property. And that's why we all have heard the saying, and this is true for everyone, whether it's a billionaire, whether it's an American, whether it's Indian, whether it's a rich man, poor man, literate, illiterate, it's true for everyone. It's not that if you are rich, if you're very smart, you've expertly managed your wealth, you've invested it well, so you'll be able to take some wealth. But it's not that. There is no investment bank where you can invest and take the wealth with you when you leave the body. So the third wife represents our wealth, our acquisitions, all the properties, assets which we have so painstakingly accumulated. The second wife, she was very loyal, she was always there to give emotional support, represents our family members, our near and dear ones, our relatives, our kinsmen. They may be very close to us, they may give us the required support, financially, emotionally, all kinds of support. But again, but they also have limitations, maximum, they can accompany us till the graveyard. Beyond that, they do have limitations. They can accompany us till the graveyard. Beyond that, they do have limitations. And the fourth wife, the first wife represents the soul, the main thing, the atma, the real self.

So the point what I am making, the first point is to keep the main thing, the main thing. We all get so busy, so engrossed, you know, earning, career growth, taking care of the body, socializing, developing a network, taking care of our near and dear ones. It's all fine. You're not saying that it is wrong. To earn money is not wrong. To take care of near and dear ones is not wrong. To have an aspiration to accumulate some assets is not wrong. But if we lose the perspective, what is the main thing, and get completely lost in fulfilling the main thing, that's not right. If we are not fulfilling all these demands, then we have we have done a big blunder.

If you analyze, even in animal form, they take care of near and dear ones. They take care of their bodily needs. They also have some element of pride in terms of, you know, a dog may bark and claim that this street is my street. There might be some demarcations among them. A dog who is quite well-built might exert his so-called supremacy on a particular area. All that is common, but one thing which human life is different, that only in human form of life we can take care of the main thing. And the main thing is the soul.

first thing this lady would do before even you know brushing her teeth would go and start so to say talking to the parrot and there was a reciprocal relationship as you all know the parrots also you know mimic and try to repeat what we say and there was some kind of attachment developing between the two just like people get very attached to their pets so one day and this queen would take care of this bird parent and would personally tend to the needs cleaning the cage spending time with the bird talking to the bird playing with the bird how are you and the bird in turn would you know talk back some days passed by and one day the queen had gone out of the palace for some official work and when she returned as soon as she saw as soon as she entered this particular room where she was staying she was quite surprised to see that on that particular day the bird quite it was not a usual sight the bird was lying still with no movement no emotions whatsoever generally when the queen would enter the bird would start clapping the wings and you know show excitement and become very very happy so the queen became quite surprised seeing this behavior is the bird okay is she healthy or is she sleeping so she came close to the cage and started you know a little bit knocking on the cage maybe who knows if the bird is sleeping she can probably wake up she kept on doing that still there is no at this point she became panicky and she called for her ministers and you know dear dear and dear ones and said please come please come see what has happened to the bird see what has happened to the bird and she became quite panicky and the doctors rushed in and you know one of them was expert in veterinary science started checking the bird and after thorough check you know became quite sullen quite morose came to the queen and says i don't know how to convey this message to you i'm so sorry to tell you that the bird is dead

so here this bird represents the main thing the soul the atma and the cage represents our modern civilization with all the so-called comforts luxuries a palatial house with air conditioners with all kinds of wonderful gadgets around to make the whole living very pleasant and comfortable it's like a golden cage so if we have not taken care of the main thing which is the soul the atma if we have not nourished if we have not given the required nourishment to the soul we might have taken good care of the soul the atma and the cage so if we have not taken care of the main thing which is the soul the atma nourish the body fulfill all the bodily demands not only our body but our extended self our near and dear ones our relatives you might have taken good care of them but if the soul is not nourished spiritually if the main thing is not taken care of come what may we will not be happy yes situational happiness we get we may eat something which is very relishable for the tongue for a moment we will be happy we may go through some good time for one or two days we may get suddenly a good promotion big hike something good happens in our life yes we will get that happiness we are not being fanatical and say that no you cannot be happy without nourishing the soul but that's situational happiness that's temporary happiness it's like a bubble it just comes and goes we'll be happy for a day you buy a new car yes you feel so good about it you have a nice drive friends also drive in that car you feel so good you feel a sense of accomplishment but it lasts for a couple of days in economics i'm sure all of you have heard about the law of diminishing returns the same object which gave us pleasure starts giving lesser and lesser pleasure
