#### material pleasure, senses, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bCyuPt_3TLU?t=1774
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "That very pleasure which we think is the main thing which will give me lasting happiness, real happiness, which will satisfy myself, my inner thirst for being happy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that material pleasures are temporary and follow the law of diminishing returns, and that the very pleasure which we think is the main thing will give me lasting happiness, real happiness, which will satisfy myself, my inner thirst for being happy.

and in fact a scripture say that very object which we think is very pleasurable a material object when our senses come in contact with such objects sense objects there is a sensation of pleasure there is just like when a man comes in touch with a woman there is a sensation of pleasure we cannot deny there are material pleasures which are very captivating very alluring very tempting we cannot deny it there are some very pleasant nice eatables which you put on your tongue you will get a sensation of pleasure and you would like to have it more and those pleasures which come in contact with a expose are actually human kinds of pleasures but such pleasures are temporary it's situational and follows the law of diminishing returns on top of it our scripture says that very pleasure which is situational which is temporary which we get by senses coming in contact with the sense object very pleasure very pleasure objects. Samrsparjabhoga. Bhoga means enjoyment. Yehi samrsparjabhoga dukhayonahi evate. That very pleasure which we think is the main thing which will give me lasting happiness, real happiness, which will satisfy myself, my inner thirst for being happy. We think like that and that thinking is under the influence of Maaya. Maaya means that which is not. Maaya sukhaaya, there is a term used in our scriptures, there are some sukhaaya, there is some happiness which is happiness given to us, served to us by an illusory energy of the Lord, which is called Maaya. The example is given just like the fish gets attracted to a bait which a fisherman has caught. The fisherman has put it on the hook while he has gone for fishing. The fish gets attracted, it's very pleasant, it gives a sense of happiness, a joy. The fish laps on that bait but while fish may get some blazing sensations while it eats the bait, simultaneously the hook gets into the mouth of the cook and the fisherman gives a sudden jerk and the fish is caught, is dead. So likewise, there are a lot of pleasures in this world, which because we want to be happy and because we don't have a higher knowledge, we don't have Divya Gyan, we pursue them with a lot of vigour, with a lot of jest, with a lot of enthusiasm, not knowing that very pleasure will entangle us, will mess up our lives. These days, we hear a lot of these things.

#### soul, Bhagavad Gita, wisdom
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bCyuPt_3TLU?t=1993
**Verse References:** BG 2.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The main thing is the soul."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna is giving us this valuable instruction in the second chapter of the Bhagavad Gita where he is saying, the main thing is the soul.

Krishna is giving us this valuable instruction in the second chapter of the Bhagavad Gita where he is saying, the main thing is the soul. When Arjuna is in dilemma, is crying, should I fight, not fight? Krishna kept aside all of his arguments, they were ethically, morally upright. What will happen to my family? What's the use of getting this kingdom at the cost of blood of my own kinsmen? Everything was right. Krishna set aside everything and started talking about the main thing. And the main thing is the soul. Just like this body, we change body from childhood to boyhood to youth. Likewise, the soul will get another body at the point of death. A sober man, a wise man, is not bewildered by such a change. Krishna is saying a wise man. In other words, if we are not conscious that the main thing is the soul and we have to spend a lot of quality time and energy in nourishing the soul in our spiritual growth, then we are not wise. We are not dhira. We are not a sober man as per Krishna's instructions in the Gita.

**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### prayer, blessings, Vaishnavism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** prayer
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "And join me in offering these prayers so that we can have the blessings of Prabhupad, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Lord Shri Krishna and with their blessings we get into this discussion."
**Summary:** The speaker begins the session with prayers to Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and Lord Shri Krishna, seeking their blessings for the discussion on Ekadashi Vrata.

A warm welcome to all of you for today's Geeta Live session. Today we are going to talk about Ekadashi Vrata. What is the significance of this Vrata? Why do people fast on Ekadashi? What is the significance of this fasting? So we will talk about all these things in detail. Before we start our discussion, we will start our session with prayers. Request all of you to kindly fold your hands and join me in offering these prayers so that we can have the blessings of Prabhupad, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Lord Shri Krishna, and with their blessings, we get into this discussion.

---
#### fasting, sense gratification, spiritual nourishment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=226
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So, on fasting days, the idea, the overall understanding is that how we can minimize the requirements, the time we spend for our bodily needs, and focus more on nourishing the soul."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the general significance of fasting, emphasizing the need to minimize bodily needs and focus on nourishing the soul, which is often neglected due to the constant pursuit of sense gratification.

Before I talk about Ekadashi Vrata or fasting on the Ekadashi days, let me in general talk about why do we fast, what is the significance of fasting in general. We fast on some important festival days like Janmashtami, Ram Navami. We also fast on the appearance and disappearance day of our acharyas, Slapro Pad appearance day, disappearance day. We all end up spending a lot of time on our bodily needs, our sensual needs. As we have been discussing, we have five senses: eyes, ears, nose, tongue, and touch. And each of these senses are constantly looking for sense objects, for gratification, for enjoyment. For example, the tongue is always looking for nice palatable eatables. Eyes are looking for beautiful forms, something which is pleasing for the eyes. And we all end up spending a lot of time gratifying the needs of our senses, gratifying the needs of our body. In other words, maintaining this body. And in the process, we end up not spending sufficient time to nourish the soul. If you see an average person, 5 minutes, 10 minutes, 15 minutes he spends doing some pujas, doing some prayers, doing some worship. But throughout the day, all the activities are all pertaining to fulfilling the bodily needs. So, on fasting days, the idea, the overall understanding is that how we can minimize the requirements, the time we spend for our bodily needs, and focus more on nourishing the soul.

---
#### fasting, spiritual practice, soul nourishment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=352
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So, on fasting days, the best thing to do is minimize our bodily needs, spend less time in eating, sleeping... Like chanting, reading about Krishna, reading Bhagavad Gita, reading Srimad Bhagavatam, reading Krishna book, hearing about the Lord, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu Smaranam, offering prayers."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how to observe fasting days, suggesting minimizing bodily needs like eating and sleeping, and instead focusing on spiritual practices such as chanting, reading scriptures, and offering prayers to nourish the soul.

So, on fasting days, the best thing to do is minimize our bodily needs, spend less time in eating, sleeping, like chanting, reading about Krishna, reading Bhagavad Gita, reading Srimad Bhagavatam, reading Krishna book, hearing about the Lord, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu Smaranam, offering prayers. That is the basic idea that how we can minimize our bodily needs and focus more on nourishing the soul, our spiritual needs. At times, caught up in this rat race, we end up neglecting the requirements of the soul, our spiritual needs. And that's why at times people are spiritually starved.

---
#### Ekadashi, fasting, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=433
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu folded hands, pleaded her mother, please follow the Ekadashi Vrata. He didn't ask any other thing... He had only one request from the mother. And mind you, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is none other than Lord Shri Krishna himself, who has come in this age of Kali, Kali Yuga."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Ekadashi, explaining its meaning and significance, and recounts the story of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu requesting his mother to follow the Ekadashi Vrata, highlighting its importance.

So now let's come to Ekadashi. Ekadashi, as the word, it's a Sanskrit word. Ekadashi means 11. Ekadashi, Dvaadashi, Treyodashi. So on the 11th day, twice in a month, 11th day of the waxing moon or the rising moon. The new moon or the waxing moon, the 11th day is called the Ekadashi. And on Ekadashi, we follow the Vrata. We fast. And with the same purpose of minimizing our bodily requirement, and we can focus more on remembering the Lord, chanting more about the Lord. Shri Prabhupada said that on Ekadashi day, it will be nice if devotees can chant 25 rounds. Usually, he recommended chanting 16 rounds minimum. And he recommended that how we can chant more rounds on Ekadashi, with the same principle in mind. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, when he took sannyas, he was leaving home. When he was parting from his mother, so he fell at her mother's feet and said, I have only one request from you. I just need one gift from you. So imagine a mother, the son is leaving the home, is taking sannyas and going away for good. So mother was overwhelmed with lot of emotions, feelings. The son is leaving home and she said, yes, whatever you want I am willing to do for you. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu folded hands, pleaded her mother, please follow the Ekadashi Vrata. He didn't ask any other thing. He didn't say you should chant the names of the Lord. He didn't say you should read Bhagavad Gita daily. He didn't say you should read Bhagavatam. He had only one request from the mother. And mind you, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is none other than Lord Shri Krishna himself, who has come in this age of Kali, Kali Yuga. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu requested the mother, I have only one request with folded hand. He pleaded, please follow the Ekadashi Vrata. Please do not eat grains on Ekadashi.

#### Ekadashi, pastime, Vyasadeva, Jaimini Muni
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=623
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Ekadashi is so glorious, fasting on Ekadashi has so much significance that only Narayana, only Lord who is Shad Aishwarya Purnam, unlimited strength, unlimited knowledge, unlimited wealth, unlimited fame, unlimited beauty, unlimited renunciation, only Lord can properly describe the glories of Ekadashi."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a pastime about Jaimini Muni inquiring from Vyasadeva about the origins and significance of Ekadashi, emphasizing the immense glories of Ekadashi that only Lord Narayana can fully describe.

Now, why Ekadashi? Why on Ekadashi V fast? There is a very nice pastime, a story associated with this particular Vrata, Ekadashi Vrata. Once, Jaimini Muni, who is the disciple of Vyasadeva, Vyasadeva is the compiler of all the Vedic literatures. So, Jaimini Muni asked Vyasadeva, I want to understand more about Ekadashi Vrata. When did it start? How did it start? From where did this Ekadashi come about? And what is the significance of following this Ekadashi? So, Jaimini Muni, very, very carefully, very humbly requested Vyasadeva, I sincerely desire to know about Ekadashi, the glories of Ekadashi, the significance of Ekadashi. How did it all start? From where did this Ekadashi came about? Sometime it would have started. What is the benefit of fasting on Ekadashi? Please, please enlighten me. So, this was the request. Now, when a night is wonderful, intelligent question is asked by a disciple to a spiritual master, it is very pleasing to him. Because it is a very relevant question, Ekadashi is a very important day, it is important Vrata. A disciple is asking, I am very eager to know about it. So, Vyasadeva was very pleased to hear this enquiry from his disciple Jaimini Muni. And he said, actually, I am incompetent to explain about the glories of Ekadashi. So, I said, Jaimini Muni, why did you ask this question? What is the significance of this Ekadashi? Ekadashi is so glorious, fasting on Ekadashi has so much significance that only Narayana, only Lord who is Shad Aishwarya Purnam, unlimited strength, unlimited knowledge, unlimited wealth, unlimited fame, unlimited beauty, unlimited renunciation, only Lord can properly describe the glories of Ekadashi. Only He can do justice if He were to describe the glories of Ekadashi. But still, since you have asked me this question, I will try my best to share with you the benefits, the glories of fasting on Ekadashi, Ekadashi Vrata.

#### creation, sin, Papa Purusha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=778
**Verse References:** BG 15.7
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Since the living entity has the desire to be independent, desire to misuse his free will to be an independent enjoyer, the Lord also created an embodiment of sin, which is also called Papa Purusha."
**Summary:** Vyasadeva explains that when the Lord created the world, He also created Papa Purusha, the embodiment of sin, to facilitate the sinful activities of living entities who desire to be independent enjoyers.

So then Vyasadev says that when the Lord created this world, as we all have heard from the Bhagavad Gita, when the living entity, Ichcha, Dvesha, Samutthena, Dvandamohena, Bharata, when the living entity was overwhelmed with Ichcha, desire, and Dvesha, envy of the Lord, he fell in this material world. Which material world? Dvandamohena, a world which is full of Dvandva, duality, heat and cold, day and night. Fame and infamy, happiness and distress. It's a world of duality. So the living entity came in this material world to fulfill his propensity to be the Lord, to be the enjoyer, to be the controller, to be the center of all attraction, to be the Lord. Now, since it is, the living entity has the desire to be independent, desire to misuse his free will to be an independent enjoyer, the Lord also created an embodiment of sin, which is also called Papa Purusha. Just like embodiment of Kali Yuga is called Kali Purusha, or the embodiment of time is called Kala, time, personification of time. Like the ab例えば y儆 involvement is called the embodiment of death, Yamaraja ...So, Kali Purusha was sin personified, and what is the duty of, this Kali Purusha this Papa Purusha? Now, all the living entities who lead a life of, cheating, deceit, engaged in sinful activities, this Papa Purusha will facilitate that, will facilitate it. anyone wants to engage in sinful activities they engage Krishna has given a free will engage in sinful activities

#### Ekadashi, compassion, sinful reactions, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=1146
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "His heart melted. And from his heart came the deity of Ekadashi. And he gave the benediction that if someone follows the Ekadashi Vrata, on the day of Ekadashi, all the sinful deeds, all the sinful reactions which a person has accumulated knowingly, unknowingly, will be burned."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates how the deity of Ekadashi emerged from the Lord's compassion upon hearing the suffering of those in hellish planets, granting the benediction that following the Ekadashi Vrata burns away sinful reactions.

His heart melted. And from his heart came the deity of Ekadashi. And he gave the benediction that if someone follows the Ekadashi Vrata, on the day of Ekadashi, all the sinful deeds, all the sinful reactions which a person has accumulated knowingly, unknowingly, will be burned. And such person can get freed of these sinful reactions. Mind you, when we are talking about sinful reactions will be burned, just like it is said that when we chant the names of the Lord, all the sinful reactions are burned to ashes. But we should not take this as a license. Yes, we can go on continuing sinful activities and we can chant the names of Lord to nullify those reactions. Yes. Or we can go on committing sinful activities and we do Ekadashi Vrata and all the sinful reactions will be burned. No. These are all sinful reactions for the sins we have unknowingly, unintentionally we have done in the past. All of us if we look back in our lives many times, many of us may be eating non-veg, all kinds of nefarious things. Now we realize that it was wrong. What about the reactions of such deeds? So such reactions can be burned to ashes if we engage in activities like chanting the names of the Lord or following the Ekadashi Vrata.

#### Ekadashi, Papa Purusha, shelter, grains
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=1302
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "On Ekadashi day, you can take shelter of the grains. And you can reside on the grains. So Papapurusha was very satisfied. At least he has a place. He has a place where he can go and be stationed. Be survived."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, explaining how Papa Purusha, realizing his diminishing influence due to Ekadashi, sought shelter from the Lord, who granted him residence in grains on Ekadashi day.

Few days later, this Papa Purusha realized that because of the influence of Ekadashi, his own influence on the people, on the living entities in general is diminishing. Because people are following Ekadashi Vrata and all their sinful reactions are getting burned to ashes. They are becoming pious. Now practically he has no work. Even though people are engaging in sinful activities, it's getting burnt and they are becoming pious. Less and less people are committing sins. So he approached the Lord. That Lord looks like my effect on the people in general is diminishing. People are committing less and less sin. They are getting more and more purified. Where do I go? What engagement I do have? And he said, I have gone to the forest. I have gone to the mountains. I have gone and taken shelter of the human beings. I have gone and taken shelter of animals. Everywhere I have gone but I don't find a place where I can take shelter of and thrive. The influence of Ekadashi is so strong. That practically I will be finished. Where do I go? How do I survive? So the Lord, since he has created that Papapurusha and has a role to play, because people will engage in sinful activities in this material world. He said, please do not worry. I understand that Ekadashi is very powerful. The influence of Ekadashi is very powerful. It's a very significant thing. On Ekadashi day, which is a very powerful, potent day for spiritual activities. Everyone who follows the Vrata, all his sinful reactions will be wiped to ashes, will be burnt to ashes. On Ekadashi day, you can take shelter of the grains. And you can reside on the grains. So Papapurusha was very satisfied. At least he has a place. He has a place where he can go and be stationed, be survived. So on Ekadashi day, it is said that the sinful reactions, not sinful reactions, sin personified, Papapurusha resides on the grains.

#### Ekadashi, benefits, Krishna consciousness, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=1531
**Verse References:** SB 9.4.29, SB 1.17.38
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Those interested in advancing in Krishna Consciousness must observe Ekadashi Vrata regularly... Neither Ganga, Gaya, Kashi, Pushkara, Kurukshetra, and Yamuna. None of them are equal to the day of Lord Hari, Ekadashi."
**Summary:** The speaker shares quotes from scriptures highlighting the benefits of observing Ekadashi Vrata, including pleasing the Lord, advancing in Krishna consciousness, and being freed from sinful reactions, emphasizing that Ekadashi is superior to even holy places.

To observe Ekadashi Vrata means to please the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Lord can be very easily pleased if we follow the Ekadashi Vrata. We can get the mercy of the Lord. We can get blessings of the Lord. Those interested in advancing in Krishna Consciousness must observe Ekadashi Vrata regularly. This is from Srimad Bhagavatam, 9th Canto, 4th Chapter, Text number 29. Those interested in elevating in Krishna Consciousness, to be Krishna Conscious, to become devotees of the Lord, should follow Ekadashi Vrata very sincerely. When one observes fasting, chanting becomes easier. Srila Prabhupada wrote in a letter to Jadurani in Los Angeles on 9th of July 1971. If we follow the Ekadashi Vrata, chanting becomes easier. In the Brahma Vahevarta Purana, it is cited that one who observes fasting on Ekadashi day is freed from all kinds of reactions of sinful activities and advances in pious life. Unless we have enough piety and all the sinful reactions have been burned to ashes, we will not be able to advance in our spiritual life. Neither Ganga, Gaya, Kashi, Pushkara, Kurukshetra, and Yamuna. None of them are equal to the day of Lord Hari, Ekadashi. O King, even if one fasts on Ekadashi is unknown to others, all of his sins are at once burnt and he easily attains the spiritual world.

#### Ekadashi, economic benefits, health benefits, fasting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=1672
**Verse References:** SB 1.17.38
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Even from economic point of view, such two fasting days in a month will save tons of food. And the system will also act very favorably on the general health of the citizens. Fasting also has lot of health benefits."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the economic and health benefits of fasting on Ekadashi, highlighting that it saves food and provides rest for the body's organs, promoting better health.

This is spoken by Pashishtha Muni in Narada Purana. Then there is another quote from Srimad Bhagavatam, 1st Canto, 17th Chapter, Text number 38. Even from economic point of view, Prabhupada writes in the Bhagavad. From economic point of view, you see. We are all talking about, you know, cutting cost. We are talking about Atma Nirbhar Bharat. Even from economic point of view, such two fasting days in a month will save tons of food. And the system will also act very favorably on the general health of the citizens. Fasting also has lot of health benefits. Just like, you know, we all need some rest to recuperate, to regenerate. Likewise, all our organs also need some rest. Imagine, every 4 hours, 5 hours, we keep inputting something into our mind body system. And all the organs are constantly working. At times, even in the night time, we have taken a heavy dinner and we sleep. Even though the body may be taking rest, but the organs, the digestive organs, are all functioning. And that's why many times we have malfunction. For example, the kidney failure. Or, you know, we have liver problem. Because they are functioning throughout the day. So they need some rest. So even from health point of view, fasting is very very good, recommended. If one fasts, it will decrease the fat within the body. So that one will not sleep more than desired and will not become inactive and lazy.

So having talked about the significance of Ekadashi Vrata and the benefits which can be accrued by following this Vrata, now let's talk about what can we do, what kind of Vrata, what kind of fasting we can do on Ekadashi. Some people may be completely new to fasting and they may not be used to fasting for the whole day. So in the Bhagavad-gita also, just like in the twelfth chapter Krishna has given various gradations. In the twelfth chapter Krishna says always think of me with your mind and intelligence absorbed in me. If you cannot do this then Krishna has another recommendation. Then he says if you cannot do this then there's another recommendation knowing that people are at different levels. You cannot expect everyone to chant 16 rounds. Okay if you cannot chant if you want to chant you cannot chant 16 round okay chant 8 rounds. If you cannot chant 8 rounds you chant 4 rounds if you cannot chant 4 rounds chant 1 round. Something is better than nothing. So same way the best thing which is recommended is that you just fast without even taking water that is called nirjal fasting. Nirjal means without even water throughout the day you just fast don't even take water and focus more time on chanting reading hearing about the Lord that's the the highest way of following Ekadashi Vrata now it is very difficult for a person who is working outside with family responsibilities to do nirjal fast and Prabhupada also did not recommend that knowing that the disciples are engaged in various services and sometimes people started following Ekadashi Vrata by doing nirjal fasting and you know they'll get so tired so weak they practically go and sleep and sleep for three hours four hours and wait for when this fasting will get over so that's it defeats the purpose Ekadashi is not meant that we become weak because we haven't eaten anything and then we sleep for extra hours and take extra rest to recuperate it defeats the purpose so Shri Prabhupada said that eat on Ekadashi avoid grains and drink on the next day do your service so the highest level is nirjal fasting where we don't even take water we do not recommend that it will be very difficult for most of you to follow it may be very impractical although there are devotees in Vrindavan and Dharams who do nirjal rata on ekadashi next if it is difficult to follow nirjal ekadashi we can do fasting on ekadashi by taking just

#### Ekadashi, calendar, tithi, fasting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xYuEHCq--FA?t=3304
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "It depends on the tithi... because we are following the Christian calendar, the conventional calendar in this world, therefore such disparities arise... we can break the fast of Ekadashi next day morning after sunrise."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the reason for the difference in Ekadashi dates, attributing it to the use of the Christian calendar and the importance of the tithi. He reiterates that the Ekadashi fast should be broken the next morning after sunrise.

You see these are all Vedic calendars based on the lunar month and the solar this thing calculations but currently the world in our conventional world we are following the Christian calendar you know we have January February March 31 day 30 days and all those kind of things and we have the day starting at 12 this thing and then 24 hours time cycle so now Ekadashi can so I was sharing with you sorry for the inconvenience cost there was some technical issue connection our internet connection so I was mentioning why there is sometimes a confusion some people follow on a particular day and there is a difference between some people say today is Ekadashi some people say no tomorrow is Ekadashi some different sampradayas may vary days may vary by a day or so it depends on the tithi as I mentioned today let's say it's 11th and Ekadashi tithi starts at 8 p.m. in the night because it has started today many people follow Ekadashi on 11th but some people believe that since major part of the tithi is on 12th so it is better to why are we following on a particular day it is to do with tithi because we are following the Christian calendar the conventional calendar in this world therefore such disparities arise so as I mentioned we can offer regular grain prasadam to Lord on the day of Ekadashi in case we have deities we can even offer grain prasadam but we avoid honoring such prasadam on the day of Ekadashi we can honor such prasadam on the Dwadashi day a day after Ekadashi also shared with you that we can break the fast of Ekadashi next day morning after sunrise so we start the Ekadashi Vrata after sunrise on the day of Ekadashi and break the fast on after sunrise the next day so in other words 24 hours approximately that's Ekadashi

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### SIX PRINCIPLES OF SURRENDER | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### surrender, sharanagati, maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So if we want to safeguard ourselves, keep ourselves safe from maya, the best thing for us is to be surrendered to the Supreme Lord."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the topic of the six principles of surrender, defining surrender as yielding control to a higher power, specifically the Supreme Lord, and emphasizing its importance as a means of protection from maya, referencing Krishna's instruction in the Bhagavad Gita to surrender.

Thank you. Hare Krishna, a warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session. The topic for the day is six principles of surrender. In the month of August, we had a session on surrender, but in that particular session, we covered only three principles. So, although there are six principles, we covered three principles and in today's session, we will briefly cover those first three principles and talk at length, in detail, the next three principles. So, before we get into the discussions, I request all of you to kindly fold your hands and we will all offer the prayers together. Seek the blessings of Shila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya, Lord Shri Krishna and with their mercy, get into this discussion. In the name of the Lord, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. Amen. In the name of the Lord, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. In the name of the Lord, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. So, what is surrender? Surrender is also called sharanagati. Surrender means to give oneself to, yield to someone's power or control. Just like at times we have seen in wars, people raise their both hands and say, I surrender to you. They yield their control or power. They surrender to someone else. And in other words, they are saying, now that I am surrendering to you, do not shoot me. Do not kill me. I am willing to yield myself to your control or your power. So here, in this case, let us understand what are the principles involved in surrendering to the Supreme Lord. Yielding to the power and control of the Supreme Lord. In fact, in the 18th chapter, towards the end, Krishna says, sarvadharman parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vrajaḥ. Surrender to me. Why surrender is important? Why Krishna is asking us to surrender to him? Because, constitutionally, we are amśa. But, we should not surrender to anyone. We are part and parcel of the Lord and part and parcel's duty, just like this hands, legs are part and parcel of this body, the duty of this hands and legs, the part and parcel is to be surrendered to the well-being of the whole, to the interest of the whole. Just like if I have some itch in the back, immediately my hand will go there and help me relieve the itch. So likewise, we are amsa of the Lord and our constitutional position is to be completely surrendered to the Supreme Lord, being an amsa of the Lord. It is not unreasonable, just like this hand, it is not unreasonable for the hand to not act independently. It is not unreasonable. It is best suited if it is serving the interest of the whole. So likewise, Krishna is requesting maam ekam sharanam raja and we all are expecting to be happy, wanting to experience bliss, all that is possible if we are situated in that situation. And when we surrender to the Lord. Basically it is saying, Lord, take charge of my life, do not shoot me. Shoot me with what? If we are not surrendered to the Lord, we are shot by arrows of maya. In fact we are killed by arrows of maya. So if we want to safeguard ourselves, keep ourselves safe from maya, the best thing for us is to be surrendered to the Supreme Lord.

#### devotional service, regulative principles, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=438
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To give up all activities which are unfavorable and heat unfavorable for Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to explain the first two principles of surrender: Anukulyasya Sankalpah, accepting what is favorable for Krishna consciousness, and Pratikulyasya Varjanam, rejecting what is unfavorable, drawing an analogy to a doctor's instructions.

So Sanatan Goswami, in Hari Bhakti Vilas, talks about six principles of surrender. As I said, we had covered three of them, surrender, why and how. This was the session which we had covered in the month of August. So the first principle is anukulyasya sankalpahav, which means that surrender to the Lord. Now we have to remember that in the practice of devotional service we have to accept all the things which are favorable for Krishna consciousness. There are many do's and don'ts prescribed by Acharyas, Vaishali Prabhupada. Some things which we should do, it is good for our spiritual growth, we should accept wholeheartedly, enthusiastically. Like for example, we have to worship the Lord. We should pray to the Lord. Chanting of the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. It is very very important for our spiritual growth. For example following the Ekadashi Vrata, for example getting up early in the morning, daily hearing Bhagavatam, Nityam Bhagavata Sevaya, accepting all those activities, all those instructions which are very favorable for our growth in Krishna consciousness, if we accept them wholeheartedly, enthusiastically, the principle is called as Anukulyasya Sankalpah. Anukul means it is conducive, it is favorable for our spiritual growth. The second principle is Pratikulyasya Varjanam. So every time we have do's and don'ts, what we should do and what we should not do. Even when we go to the doctor, he will give us a medicine, he will also give us some don'ts. Do not eat oily food, some restrictions with respect to food, take the medicine after food, do not take the medicine empty stomach. So there are many many restrictions like four regulative principles, we should not eat meat, we should not engage in any illicit activities. We should not do intoxication, we should restrict or restrain ourselves from the activities which will pull us down, which will degrade our consciousness. So if one is very very enthusiastic to follow such restrictions, that principle called as Pratikulyasya Varjanam. Varjanam means to give up.

#### faith, protection, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=614
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "And when she finally surrendered to the Lord with both hands raised, having given up all attempts to protect herself, with her might, with her strength, finally Krishna supplied unlimited strength of, unlimited length of saree."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the third principle of surrender, rakshashyati iti vishwaso, which is having firm faith that Krishna is one's protector, illustrating it with the story of Draupadi in the Mahabharata, emphasizing that true protection comes when one completely surrenders to Krishna.

To give up all activities which are unfavorable and heat unfavorable for Krishna consciousness. Reading newspapers. To be oppressed with the latest news, it's fine. But reading newspaper for a couple of hours, watching television shows mindlessly, these all activities, if we indulge in, it will derail, it will pull our consciousness down. The third principle is rakshashyati iti vishwaso. To have that vishwas, to have that conviction, to have that intense feeling that Krishna is my protector. Rakshashyati iti vishwaso. To have that firm conviction that Krishna will surely protect me. In the Mahabharata, as we all know, the Pandavas lost the gambling match. It was rigged and they lost even Draupadi in that match. And we have heard that how Dushashan attempted to strip Draupadi in front of everyone in the assembly. So when Draupadi was brought in the middle of that assembly and Dushashan was holding her sari, attempting to pull and disrobe her, she pleaded for help, she looked at the five Pandavas with imploring eyes as if imploring, please step forward and save me, see what's happening. But none of them came forward. In fact the heads were down in shame, looking downwards. Although Draupadi implored, pleaded for help, the Pandavas, the Bap más, were not the firsts to ask help, the Pandavas were the last. It was the humble narrowminded people. To have all the good people—now, let us go on. It is a struggle not to be ashamed but it is a struggle not to be ashamed of things. That constructed totally walking in the world-tcially. I keep nothing dirty except for a few of the good ones. It is a struggle of two people to survive. Because Word has a face that helps you not to give everything away, but I do, without any fear, I love, can I save implored, pleaded for help but nobody stepped forward. Then she turned her face to Bhishma, the Grandshire of the Kuru dynasty. She expected that Bhishma would not allow this injustice to happen, that too in an open assembly in front of everyone. She asked for help from Bhishma, even Bhishma did not step forward. Then finally she decided in a very very helpless state of mind that I will have to fend for myself. I will have to protect myself. So Dushashan was holding the saree and trying to disrobe her and Draupadi with both hands very tightly held to her saree as if she will not allow Dushashan to pull that saree. But Dushashan was very powerful. This is Draupadi. Relatively a powerful person. A weak lady, relatively speaking as far as physical strength is concerned. There was no match between the power of Dushashan and Draupadi. And Draupadi tried holding the saree with both hands but she soon realized that she will not be able to save herself. So she raised one hand and said Krishna Govinda and still one hand is holding on to the saree. But again she felt that she cannot save herself. So she raised one hand and said Krishna Govinda and still one hand is holding on to the saree. But again she felt that she is helpless. And finally realizing that she is utterly dependent on the mercy of Govinda. She cannot protect herself. She has to be dependent on the Lord. She completely surrendered to Govinda, raised both hands and said Govinda. And when she finally surrendered to the Lord with both hands raised, having given up all attempts to protect herself. With her might, with her strength, finally Krishna supplied unlimited strength of, unlimited length of saree.

#### surrender, faith, reciprocity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=863
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate."
**Summary:** The speaker continues on the topic of surrender, relating the story of Kunti Maharani praying for more miseries to maintain dependence on Krishna, and Prahlad Maharaj's unwavering faith amidst persecution, emphasizing that Krishna sometimes puts devotees in situations where they have no choice but to surrender, and that the Lord reciprocates according to the degree of surrender.

On one side Dushashan was trying to pull and he could never disrobe Draupadi. In fact Krishna sometimes takes devotees, his good devotees, sincere devotees to a situation where they have no option but to surrender to the Lord. In fact Kunti Maharani, when the Pandavas got the kingdom back, she is requesting Krishna, Krishna now that we have got the kingdom back, we will all be safe, we all will be living amidst comforts, luxuries, opulence, we will forget you. She is praying to Krishna, oh Krishna give us more and more miseries. Because when we were in forest, we were in the forest. Different demons were attacking me, we were completely dependent on you, on your mercy, we were surrendered to you. Now that we will be comfortably situated in our kingdoms, we will forget you. So Kunti Maharani is praying, oh Lord give us more and more miseries. Prahlad Maharaj was completely surrendered to the Lord. Hrndikashyapu was very unhappy that Prahlad, his son, was completely surrendered to the Lord. He was spending time, devoting time talking about Krishna, remembering Krishna, chanting Krishna's holy name. So he felt very hurt, in fact he scolded the teachers who were put in charge of schooling or teaching Prahlad Maharaj. And then finally knowing that Prahlad Maharaj is not willing to stop, he is continuing to do his devotional activities. He finally tried punishing Prahlad. He threw him from the top of a cliff. He threw Prahlad in a pit full of snakes. He tried crushing Prahlad under the feet of some elephants. But Prahlad was completely surrendered to the Lord, not at all fearful, completely dependent on the Lord. And confident, abhashya siddhiti rakshasate, Lord will surely protect me. Just like a child in the lap of mother, especially a young child, is completely dependent on the mother. Mother, if the mother forgets to feed the child, the child will cry, but will not make a sound. The child will cry. She cannot make its own arrangement. In fact, it cannot make its own arrangement. So in that sense, completely surrendered. So the mother is always conscious of the child. Same way, when we are completely surrendered to the lord, completely dependent on the lord, dependant on the protection of the lord, the lord also takes charge of such a devotee. And the surrender is reciprocal.

---
#### free will, surrender, anu karan, anu sharan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=1049
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anu Karan would be doing Rasa dance because Krishna also did Rasa dance. I imitate the activities of the Lord. He danced with the Gopis. So I can also do that. That's called Anu Karan, which is not recommended."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna reciprocates with our surrender, giving us free will to choose, and that surrender is not black and white but exists in degrees, using the analogy of shades of grey, and addresses a question about vaccination, distinguishing between Anu Karan (imitation) and Anu Sharan (following footsteps), cautioning against imitating advanced devotees without their level of surrender.

Krishna in the Gita says, As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. If our surrender is 100%, we are completely dependent on the Lord, then Krishna's reciprocation is also 100%. If our surrender is 0%, we are completely dependent on our own protection, our money, our bank balance, our relatives, our own power, our own possessions, then all the best. Krishna has given us free will. We can use that free will to surrender to the Lord or not surrender to Him. And surrender, by the way, is not black or white. Either we are surrendered or not surrendered. Just like between black and white, there are different grades of shape or... of shades of grey colour. Likewise, there are different degrees of surrender and spiritual advancement means we surrender to the Lord more and more. Yesterday, I received a question from one devotee who is practicing Krishna consciousness very sincerely. She asked me, is it okay to get vaccinated? So I said yes. I asked her, is it something wrong in the sense from the perspective of is there some non-vegetarian... In fact, there was some news that while manufacturing vaccine, they use some ingredients which is taken out from bovine calves, something to do with non-vegetarian ingredient. So, the question was that vaccine is material and we are devotees, why should we depend on something material for our protection? Why not depend on Krishna? It's a very valid question. If we are talking about surrender, then why don't we completely be surrendered to the Lord and make no attempt to protect ourselves. We don't have to wear a mask or do some hand sanitization or do some precautions. We just be dependent on the Lord. Actually, the two things which our scriptures talk about Anu Karan and Anu Sharan. Anu Karan means we imitate the activities of pure devotees or completely surrender devotees and Anu Sharan means we follow in their footsteps. Anu Karan would be doing Rasa dance because Krishna also did Rasa dance. I imitate the activities of the Lord. He danced with the Gopis. So I can also do that. That's called Anu Karan, which is not recommended. Anu Sharan means following in the footsteps of great Acharyas of the Lord and following their instructions. We cannot imitate. Lord Shiva drank whole ocean of water. Lord Shiva drank whole ocean of poison and kept it in his neck. That's why it's called Neelkanth. As devotees of Lord Shiva, we cannot also drink Bhang and all kinds of intoxicant thinking that Lord Shiva also did. We can also do it. Krishna danced with Gopis. So we can also do it. No.

---
#### faith, duty, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=1269
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But maintain that we don't put firm and complete faith. We don't completely surrender to the medicine, completely surrender to the doctor thinking that he will save my life."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that while advanced devotees may not require external protection, those on the path of surrender should follow in their footsteps by fulfilling their duties, such as going to the doctor, but without placing complete faith in material solutions, recognizing Krishna as the ultimate protector, and uses the example of temple security to illustrate the balance between duty and faith.

So there are very advanced devotees who are completely surrendered to the Lord, completely dependent on the Lord. Who have complete faith. They have complete faith in the protection of the Lord. Nishtha, firm faith. For them, they don't require any protection. Just like Prahlad Maharaj. They are very confident that Krishna will protect them. And because they are fully surrendered, Krishna also protects such devotees. But because we are on the path of surrender, we are not fully surrendered. We cannot imitate such devotees. We have to follow in the footsteps, which means that as a matter of duty, we go to the doctor. But maintain that we don't put firm and complete faith. We don't completely surrender to the medicine, completely surrender to the doctor thinking that he will save my life. Prahlad Maharaj says we may have the best of doctors, the best of medicines, but if Krishna does not protect, all this will be of no use. So we go to the doctor, we take the medicine as a matter of duty. But knowing that, that medicine will be effective and I will get cured if Krishna sanctions. If Krishna protects me. We don't, sometimes people when they get some surgery done, they fall at the feet of the doctor and they say you have given me the second life. Yes, we can be grateful to the doctor. But remember, the doctor is a mere instrument in the hand of the Lord. If I was supposed to get cured, the doctor is a mere instrument in the hand of the Lord. The ultimate cause, sarvakarana karanam daivan, it is the Supreme Lord. So that consciousness a devotee has, as a matter of duty, he will make arrangements. Just like in our temple, there was a couple of years back when the terrorists attacked Akshardham. So we beefed up the security. There were a lot of security guards protecting the temple in Bangalore. Now we cannot say that we should be dependent on Krishna, surrender to Krishna. Why do we have to depend on security? Yes, we have to do that as a matter of duty and maintain the consciousness. We are making arrangements. But faith is not necessarily faith. If we have 100% faith on the arrangement, the faith is on the Lord. By the intelligence given by the Lord, as a matter of duty, we are making the required arrangements.

---
#### faith, maintenance, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=1440
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But ultimately we should have faith that not only me, my entire family, everyone is being maintained by the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the fourth principle of surrender, Goktritve Varanam Tatha, which is having faith that the Lord is the maintainer, explaining that while one may fulfill their duties as a breadwinner, the ultimate conviction should be that everyone is maintained by the Lord, and that we often take for granted the arrangements made by the Lord for our sustenance.

So that is practical application of faith in the Lord. Because we are not fully surrendered, we are not pure devotees. Our surrender has not completely manifested. Or as I mentioned earlier, a fully surrendered devotee like Prahlad, need not make such arrangements. So this is the third principle. The fourth principle is, which means that one should have the faith and the conviction that Lord is my maintainer. He is maintaining me. Sometimes, you know, a head of a family can get a feeling that I am maintaining my family. The children, wife, everyone is dependent on me. I am the breadwinner for the family. Again, as a matter of duty, as an immediate cause, I may be the breadwinner, but ultimately we should have faith that not only me, my entire family, everyone is being maintained by the Lord. Just like Brahma is also part of the Creator. But he is secondary Creator. The primary, supreme Creator, the cause of all causes. Govindam Adi Purusham Tamaham Bhajami. He is cause of all causes. So yes, as a matter of duty, I may be maintaining some of my family members, but we should have conviction that not only my family members, including me, everyone is being maintained by the Lord. We take many things for granted. Just like one day if sun does not rise, the whole, it's very cloudy, we feel so gloomy. We have taken it for who has made the arrangement that the sun is rising daily, giving us heat, light, sunshine, brightens up our day. Who has made arrangements that the water gets evaporated from water bodies, the clouds get formed, and it rains. So all this arrangement, background arrangements are being made by the Lord. Many times we are not conscious of it. We are not aware of it. We are blinded to such arrangements made by the Lord for maintaining us. So a surrendered devotee always have a feeling, the consciousness that the Lord is my maintainer.

---
#### faith, maintenance, surrender, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=1604
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Somehow the Lord is arranging food even for this thing. Why should I not have faith and conviction that my baby also will be protected by the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker shares the story of Sridhar Swami, a grahastha who desired to take sannyas but was bound by his responsibilities, and how witnessing a baby lizard catching food inspired him to trust in the Lord's maintenance, illustrating the principle of Goktritve Varanam Tatha.

There is one Sridhar Swami who is a very erudite scholar in our parampara, who has written a commentary on Srimad Bhagavatam. Although he was a grahastha, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was very much in appreciation of his wonderful commentary. And if anyone tried pointing out some mistakes or lacuna in his commentary, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu would get very angry. So it so happened that the Sridhar Swami, he was a grahastha, he was having a wife, but deep within he had a desire to completely surrender himself and devote himself completely in the service of the Lord. In other words, take sannyas. He was engaged completely in spiritual activities, and lost interest for all material activities. And in fact, one day he told his wife that I need your permission. I want to give up all these material responsibilities and I want to completely devote myself to serve the Lord. So the wife understood. She was a good chaste wife. So she told him that I am caring. She was having a child. She told him that let me, let the child get born. And after that, you can go. I'll give you permission. She agreed. Sridhar Swami also agreed for whatever the wife had recommended. She waited for the childbirth. Unfortunately, while childbirth there were some complications and the mother expired. Now the child was all alone. Again Sridhar Swami, because the understanding he had with his wife that after the childbirth he can leave home. Now the child is there, mother is not there. The child is completely dependent on the father for his maintenance. So he could not leave home. And then somehow he accepted and continued to maintain the child. But deep within he had that longing. That eagerness. That how I can completely, cent percent, devote myself. My energy, my time, my all the abilities, whatever the Lord. To broadcast the glories of the Lord. To serve the Lord. To chant the names of the Lord more and more. So one day it so happened that from the ceiling, a small baby lizard, fell from the ceiling. And he was sitting and he was watching from a distance. And although it fell from the ceiling, it fell to the ground. It somehow survived. Not only it survived, the baby lizard, suddenly the baby lizard saw one small fly or insect. And instinctively, instinctively she pulled out the tongue and caught hold of that insect and ate it. So that time, Swami realized who is maintaining this baby lizard. Somehow the Lord is arranging food even for this thing. Why should I not have faith and conviction that my baby also will be protected by the Lord. Again, Anukaran Anusharan. We cannot imitate. Sridhar Swami was a great exalted devotee of the Lord. Completely surrendered to the Lord. With pure desire and intention to serve the Lord wholeheartedly. This is not that we can give up our responsibilities and say Sridhar Swami did that. We can also do that. That would be Anukaran.

---
#### faith, maintenance, surrender, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=1885
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even my child will be taken care by the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the story of Sridhar Swami, explaining how he eventually left home with faith that the Lord would care for his child, and how a childless couple found and cared for the child, illustrating the Lord's arrangement and the importance of not imitating advanced devotees, and announces a kirtan before resuming the discussion on the remaining principles of surrender.

So, when he saw that lizard, being supplied by food, and being maintained by the Lord, he said, even my child will be taken care by the Lord. And one day, he left home, with the child sleeping back in the home. With that faith, with that conviction that Lord will take care, Lord will maintain this child. So many, Prabhupada says, so many hundreds and thousands of elephants in African birds, lakhs of them, who is maintaining them? They eat tons of food. So who is maintaining them? The Lord is maintaining them. So he left the home. And after a while, the child, as usual, got up from the sleep, started crying. And there was a couple passing by that side. And they were returning from a temple and that couple had gone to the temple and offered prayers. They had not, they were not having a child. And they had prayed to the Lord for giving them, blessing them with a child. And when they saw the child crying, just out of curiosity to find out why the child is crying, they entered the house. And they saw no one there. They waited for a while, a couple of hours, and they realized that this child is abandoned. They took charge of the child, took it home, and were very, very grateful to the Lord, that the Lord has, that the Lord has listened to their prayers and has blessed them with the child. So in that sense, the Lord can do multiple things with one act. With that one act, bless Shri Raswami with fulfillment of the desire to wholeheartedly serve him. And also this couple who wants, longing to have a child. So that's the fourth principle, which is Goktritve Varanam Tatha. The fifth principle, before we get into the fifth principle, fifth and sixth principle, we'll get into some bit of details. Let us all enthusiastically participate and have nice kirtans. Request all of you to participate by if possible. You can also join and clap your hands and enthusiastically participate. And after the kirtans, we'll resume the discussion and talk about the fifth and sixth principle. Let's have kirtan now.

#### faith, maintenance, surrender, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=2753
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Which means that one should have all his activities and desires completely aligned to the desires of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker resumes the lecture after the kirtan, reiterating the fourth principle of surrender, faith in Krishna as the maintainer, and then introduces the fifth principle, Atma Nikshepa, which means aligning one's activities and desires with the desires of the Lord, as understood through the teachings of the Acharyas.

The fourth principle we talked about is To have that strong understanding, feeling that Krishna is my maintainer. He is maintaining me. Not only maintaining me, maintaining everyone. Including so called my dependents. To have that firm conviction. Who is maintaining all the animals in dense forest. Who is providing them food? It's Lord. Lord has made wonderful arrangements to maintain the living beings. Although we see scarcity in this world, people dying of starvation. It's actually man-made. We have very badly meddled with the arrangements made by the Lord. Because of greed, because of pride, because of lust and we have messed up things. Otherwise this world means this world is complete in itself and it's coming from the complete whole. The fifth principle is Atma Nikshepa Which means that one should have all his activities and desires completely aligned to the desires of the Lord. In the Bhagavad Gita and through the teachings of great Acharyas, we understand what are the desires of the Lord. What he wants us to do. So if our desires and our activities are completely dependent on the Lord and are completely aligned. Krishna says surrender to me and we are trying our best to surrender to him. Krishna says manmana, think of me. We are trying our best to be conscious of Krishna. Krishna says serve me. Engage in my activities. We are trying our best to dovetail our activities in the service of the Lord. So that means Atma Nikshepa. Our activities and desires are completely aligned with the desires of the Lord. We don't have independent desires. We don't think ourselves independent. We feel that we are dependent on the Lord. Our desires should be the desires of the Lord. The Lord expects we to act in a particular way. To think in a particular way. To feel in a particular way. And we try our best to mould our lives according to the Lord's desires. And how do we understand what is the Lord's desire? What the Lord expects us to do, think, feel? It is through the Acharyas. The Acharyas, we cannot approach the Lord directly. Krishna is the property of his pure devotees. They understand the mind of Krishna. So through their teachings, through their instructions, if we follow, we can be completely aligned and understand what the Lord is expecting us from us. And we can mould our lives accordingly. So that is Atma Nikshepa.

---
#### humility, insignificance, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DfjDADmpF_I?t=2964
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I am just a mere puppet in the hands of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the sixth principle of surrender, Karpanya, which means believing oneself to be insignificant, meek, and humble, completely dependent on the Lord, and contrasts this with pride in one's own accomplishments, using the example of Srila Prabhupada's humility despite his great achievements.

And the sixth principle is Karpanya. Karpanya means to believe. To feel that I am very insignificant. To be meek and humble. I am poorest of poor. Poorest of poor does not mean literally. A person may be living amidst opulence in a nice house just like we had Raj Rishis. But to feel that I am completely dependent on the Lord. The Lord is maintaining me. I am nothing. I am an insignificant speck in this creation. If we don't have this understanding, we may feel that I am great. I have amassed this much wealth because of my doing, my hard work, my abilities. Time can come. Whereas a devotee who is completely surrendered may accomplish big, big things. Like Shri Prabhupada went to America with 40 rupees at the old age, 70 years old. And in short 12 years, worldwide movement with 108 temples, farm communities, gurukuls, ashram, 10,000 disciples. But there was not a speck of pride. Completely dependent on the Lord, knowing that I am just an insignificant beggar. I am just a mere puppet in the hands of the Lord. So surrender devotees feel like that. I am nothing. I am not dependent. I am just an insignificant speck in this creation. He does not have faith in his abilities. He has faith in the Lord's mercy. Whatever is happening, whatever wonderful things are happening, he understands it's because of the mercy of the Lord. He understands that not a blade of grass moves without the Lord's sanction. So a devotee who is completely surrendered is called Anagata Bhakta. He is completely surrendered to the Lord. And as I mentioned, it is not black and white, not a binary logic, zero or one. A person is surrendered or not surrendered. No, we all are surrendered to different degrees. In fact, a person is said to be more spiritually advanced if he has surrendered more and more. In fact, as we advance spiritually, as we become better devotees, we are more and more surrendered. We will be knowingly or unknowingly practicing these principles in our life. A pure devotee, if we look into his life, all the six principles would be followed in total perfection.

#### invocation, blessings, devotional service

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker begins by welcoming the audience and introducing the topic of six things that destroy bhakti, followed by prayers for blessings and guidance.

Hare Krishna, a warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita live session. The topic for the day is six things that destroy bhakti. We're going to get into details, what are those six specific things which we should avoid doing, which we should take care of on the path to devotional service. So please join me offering the prayers with all chanted prayers together and then we'll get into the discussion.

#### bhakti, Rupa Goswami, Nectar of Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=249
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So in that book, Rupa Goswami talks about six things which will destroy Bhakti, Shadbhir Bhaktir Vinashyati."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Rupa Goswami's book, Bhakti Rasamrita Sindhu (Nectar of Devotion), and its discussion of six things that destroy bhakti, emphasizing the importance of being careful as practitioners.

So there is a book written by Rupa Goswami, who is one of the six Goswamis of Vrindavan. The name of the book is Bhakti Rasamrita Sindhu, which in English we call it Nectar of Devotion. So in that book, Rupa Goswami talks about six things which will destroy Bhakti, Shadbhir Bhaktir Vinashyati. Vinashyati means to destroy. So what are those specific six things which we as practitioners also should be careful of, so that there is no impediment in our path to spiritual advancement.

---
#### spiritual advancement, chanting, impediments
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=313
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is not enough that water has to go in the bucket, it is also important that there is no leakage."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of filling a bucket with holes to illustrate how spiritual progress can be hindered despite consistent efforts, emphasizing the need to address the 'holes' that cause leakage.

Many times we might wonder, I have been chanting for so many years, I still have so many lusty desires, it appears that I have not advanced. It appears that still I am far, far away from what is being mentioned as a symptom of a spiritually advanced person. One may wonder, where am I going wrong? I am trying to chant my rounds, I get up in the morning, I visit the temple regularly, I attend the Gita classes, I am doing my best. Still, why am I not advancing? The example is given just like, if we are filling water in a bucket, one thing is yes, we have to keep the tap open, the water inflow is there in the bucket, then for sure the bucket will get filled up. We also have to do one very important thing while we are filling the water in the bucket, and that is to ensure the water is not leaking from the bucket. Imagine a bucket in which there are big holes, now in line with the topic what we are discussing today, there are six big holes, and we have the tap on, the water is getting filled, but when you look at the bucket, practically there is very little water which has got accumulated. You may wonder, the tap is on, water is flowing inside the bucket, but why the bucket is not getting filled? It is not enough that water has to go in the bucket, it is also important that there is no leakage. If there are big holes, then practically all the water will get drained out.

#### bhakti, Atya Ahara, regulated life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=503
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Here it not only refers to food, no doubt over eating is not good, not only material, not only spiritually, even materially."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the first principle that destroys bhakti: Atya Ahara, which means excess. This refers not only to overeating but also to collecting too much, emphasizing the importance of simple living and high thinking.

So, Rupa Goswami says, the first, I request all the devotees to kindly make note of it, I will be asking you to write down in the live chat section, at the right time. We will discuss three principles now, and we will have a kirtan break. We will, I won't call it a break, we will participate in kirtans, and then we will again resume, and cover the balance three. The first principle, the first thing which destroys bhakti is, atya ahara. Ahara means, ahar also means food. Atya ahara means, atya means excess. Here it not only refers to food, no doubt over eating is not good, not only material, not only spiritually, even materially. We should resort to eating simple foodstuff, sattvic foodstuff. Krishna in the Gita says, one who is regulated in his eating, sleeping, recreation and work, can mitigate all material pains, arising from this material contact. The soul is in the material body, it's a material contact.

#### Atya Ahara, eating habits, lifestyle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=586
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "There is a difference between, eat to live, and live to eat."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Atya Ahara, discussing the negative impacts of overeating and the modern tendency to 'live to eat,' leading to health issues and lifestyle disorders.

Krishna also says, one who eats too much, or eats too little, one who sleeps too much, or does not sleep enough, cannot be a yogi. So atya ahara means, when we eat too much, something which is very palatable, some dishes made, it is very tasty, and we eat too much, predominantly to satisfy our taste buds. There is a difference between, eat to live, and live to eat. A lot of people in today's world, are encountering various health issues, lifestyle disorder, because, the philosophy of life has become, live to eat. And in today's world, we have many companies, who are doing door delivery of foodstuff, Zomato, Swiggy, you can choose a restaurant, and you can order the thing online, and next half an hour, it will be rushed, hot, to your doorstep.

#### Atya Ahara, simple living, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=699
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But if we over endeavor, to maintain those relationships, socialize too much, collect too much, hoard too much, and not, practice the principle of simple living, and high thinking, we can be violating this principle, and it may endanger, our spiritual advancement."
**Summary:** The speaker expands on Atya Ahara, including the concept of collecting too much and not practicing simple living and high thinking, which can endanger spiritual advancement.

Not only eating too much, collecting too much. Human being is expected to, lead a simple life. Prabhupada used to say, simple living, high thinking. That should be the philosophy of life. Yes, we require resources, we require money, we require some, near and dear ones, some relationships. But if we over endeavor, to maintain those relationships, socialize too much, collect too much, hoard too much, and not, practice the principle of simple living, and high thinking, we can be violating this principle, and it may endanger, our spiritual advancement.

#### Atya Ahara, hoarding, spiritual path
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=761
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So as devotees, if we also, get into this, habit of, hoarding, collecting more than required, eating more than required, then, for sure, in the long run, it will also, destroy our, bhakti, or it will, retard our progress in, the spiritual, path."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of pigeons and humans with grains to illustrate the hoarding tendency, explaining how it can lead to famines and shortages, and how it can destroy or retard a devotee's progress on the spiritual path.

Atyahara. Prabhupada gives an example that, when, you throw some grains, we have seen that, when you throw some grains, the pigeons, the sparrows, they come, they eat few grains, and then they, fly off. Once their, hunger is, satiated, they fly off. They generally don't, hoard. But if you throw a bag of grains, in the street, a man will come and he'll take the whole bag, because there's a hoarding tendency. Prabhupada said that, there are many times, famines, and shortages of food, because, not because of, shortage in production, but because of this, hoarding tendency, profiteering tendency, where people, do not release, food grains in the market, so that, they can, hoard, and, make more money. So as devotees, if we also, get into this, habit of, hoarding, collecting more than required, eating more than required, then, for sure, in the long run, it will also, destroy our, bhakti, or it will, retard our progress in, the spiritual, path.

#### Prayasas, priorities, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=1206
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In human form of life, primary is emancipation of the soul, the spiritual advancement. But unfortunately in today's time because we are in ignorance, we don't have clear understanding as to what is the ultimate goal of life, what is the purpose of life, we over endeavor for methi-ness."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the primary goal of human life is spiritual advancement, but due to ignorance, people over-endeavor for material things, leaving little time for spiritual practices.

Many times we should be very clear what is primary and what is secondary. In human form of life, primary is emancipation of the soul, the spiritual advancement. But unfortunately in today's time because we are in ignorance, we don't have clear understanding as to what is the ultimate goal of life, what is the purpose of life, we over endeavor for material things and if we are left with some time, we keep it for spiritual practices. Our spiritual practices are at the mercy of our material goals and aspirations. If material goals and aspirations eat up too much of our time, then we are left with no time for our spiritual activities. So if one is over endeavouring for his material accomplishments, material goals, accumulating too much, nobody is satisfied.

#### Sat Chit Ananda, material happiness, lasting pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=1286
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We all are looking for Sat, Chit and Ananda. Sat means eternity, permanence, Chit means knowledge and Ananda means bliss, happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that everyone is looking for Sat, Chit, and Ananda (eternity, knowledge, and bliss), but material happiness is temporary, while the soul seeks permanent pleasure.

We all are looking for Sat, Chit and Ananda. Sat means eternity, permanence, Chit means knowledge and Ananda means bliss, happiness. And we all have some aspirations, some goals, hoping that once we achieve those goals, I will be happy. I will meet my requirement of Sat, Chit and Ananda. But Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita says, material happiness, material accomplishment will definitely give pleasure but it will be temporary pleasure. So, what the soul is expecting and wanting to enjoy is permanent pleasure, lasting pleasure, lasting happiness.

#### rat race, material goals, unattainable happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=1344
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Because we are not getting it, we keep one goal after the other, one goal after the other, keep running, keep running, keep running, that's why it's called a rat race."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a rat race and a donkey chasing a carrot to illustrate how people keep running after material goals, hoping to find happiness, but it remains unattainable.

Because we are not getting it, we keep one goal after the other, one goal after the other, keep running, keep running, keep running, that's why it's called a rat race. Figuratively speaking, our position, unfortunately, is like a rat race. It's like a rat race. It's like a rat race. It's like a donkey, we have seen that image of a donkey with a carrot dangling through a stick right in front of him. As it moves, the stick and the carrot also keeps moving further because it's tied to him, tied to the donkey and it's hoping that in the next leap, in the next jump, in the next move, he will catch hold of that carrot but it does not happen. The more he desires to have it, the more quickly he runs, the more fast he runs and the further the carrot goes away from him.

#### Prajalpa, unnecessary talk, circle of influence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=1443
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Prajalpa means talking unnecessarily. Loose talk, frivolous talk, talking unnecessarily on some mundane subject matter."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the third principle: Prajalpa, which means talking unnecessarily, including loose talk, frivolous talk, and discussing socio-political issues outside one's circle of influence.

The third principle which Rupa Goswami says which will destroy our bhakti is Prajalpa. Prajalpa means talking unnecessarily. Loose talk, frivolous talk, talking unnecessarily on some mundane subject matter. Talking and discussing on some socio-political issues unnecessarily. It's not in my circle of influence. It may be in my circle of concern but predominantly I am spending lot of time discussing issues which are in my circle of concern but I am not able to do anything about it. Covey talks about the circle of influence and circle of concern in that Seven Habits book. So it is a search and discovery. Many times people are engaged in unnecessary discussion, wasting their precious time.

#### Prajalpa, time management, spiritual advancement

As spiritualists, when we take up commitment to chant our rounds, read Bhagavad Geeta, hear Bhagavatam, time is. Everyone has only 24 hours in a day. And at times devotees might wonder, you know, I am not getting time for my chanting. I am not getting time to read. Quite possible the time is getting leaked, as I mentioned the water is falling in the bucket but there are big holes and the water is leaking. So we should be little cautious, careful, discern where my time is going.
