#### time allocation, materialist vs spiritualist, daily activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/iO4r2Cq9GZc?t=1557
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The difference between a materialist and a spiritualist is those 11 years."
**Summary:** The speaker cites an article that breaks down how an average lifespan is spent, highlighting that after accounting for essential activities, only about 11 years remain, and the difference between a materialist and a spiritualist lies in how those 11 years are used.

There was one article which says that if we were to take an average of lifespan of a human being, let's say 65 years or 70 years or 80 years, practically 22 years in those 65 years, one third of our life, we sleep. Maybe as adults we may, because of a rat race, we don't get time even to sleep, we may be sleeping 6 hours. Barely in a day but in childhood days and also when we grow up, when we become old. So on an average, let's say 8 hours a day, so practically 22 years in a whole lifespan, we end up sleeping. And there are activities like daily ablutions, brushing our teeth, bathing, travelling to work, social engagement. Social engagement. Which we cannot avoid. Man is a social being, we cannot, our near and dear ones, we cannot avoid going to the weddings, some important functions, some bit of socialising is important. More so if we are in a grahastha ashrama as staying in a family, we have to attend to some family responsibilities. So if you want to live in a family, you have to attend to some family responsibilities. So if you want to live in a family, you have to attend to some family responsibilities. So if you want to live in a family, you have to attend to some family responsibilities. If you want to engage in all the activities which are required, sleeping is required, eating is required, daily ablutions is required, some bit of travel is required. So in this article it was mentioned that if you were to take out the time which is required for all these activities, barely in the whole life, you'll be left with 11 years. The difference between a materialist and a spiritualist is those 11 years. The difference between a materialist and a spiritualist is those 11 years. Those 11 years out of 65 years otherwise as spiritualist, as devotees, yes we also have to sleep.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### SOLVING PROBLEMS WITH LORD'S HELP | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### problems, challenges, Lord's help
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So today's session, we are going to discuss about how we can remain strong, calm and not get completely bewildered when we encounter such problems if we depend on the Lord."
**Summary:** Introduction to the topic of solving problems with the Lord's help, emphasizing the universality of challenges and the importance of seeking divine guidance.

Welcome to our channel. Welcome to today's Gita Live session. It's a very interesting topic today, and the topic is Solving Problems with Lord's Help. We all encounter challenges, difficulties, trials, tribulations, and problems in our life. Nobody is an exception. The degree may vary from person to person, but everyone encounters challenges. And at times, the challenges and difficulties can be very overwhelming. We can be completely shattered or we can be completely rattled because of the problems we encounter. So today's session, we are going to discuss about how we can remain strong, calm, and not get completely bewildered when we encounter such problems if we depend on the Lord, if we seek Lord's protection and guidance to take us through such challenging times.

---
#### prayer, relationship with God, problems
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=269
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If we only pray when we are in problem, we are in problem."
**Summary:** The first principle is introduced: it is problematic to only pray when in difficulty, emphasizing the need for a constant relationship with the Lord.

Like we have been doing in our previous sessions, we will cover three important principles today. The first principle is, kindly make a note of it. As we have been doing it, I will ask all of you to kindly type it out in the live chat a little later. Only pray when...

#### prayer, relationship with God, dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=306
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This God is not our order carrier that we just use him whenever we want."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the first principle, using the analogy of fevicol to illustrate how God should not be treated as a mere tool to be used only in times of trouble.

If we only pray when we are in problem, we are in problem. In other words, this reminds me of a Doha from Kabir Das. Many of us definitely remember the Lord when we are in problems, not necessarily when we are going through some good times. Just like we have invariably in our house a tube of fevicol. Whenever we have to fix something, we take out that tube, apply that fevicol, fix it, and once it's done, we keep the tube back in the drawer to be taken out only if we have to fix something again. We cannot deal with God like this. This God is not our order carrier that we just use him whenever we want. In other words, we have to invest and cultivate a relationship with the Lord, not necessarily at the time when we are going through some challenges and difficulties.

---
#### faith, prayer, difficult times
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=400
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Many of them were quite shattered, bewildered, and also lost faith in God."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how people often pray intensely during difficult times but lose faith if their prayers are not answered, using the example of wives of German soldiers during World War II.

If we do that, many times people offer prayers very, very intensely, feelingly, when they are going through some difficult times, and at times they are dejected. If their prayers are not fulfilled, they often lose faith in the Lord. When the World War II was happening, a lot of wives of the German soldiers were very careful to pray at home for their husbands. They were quite intensely praying in the church, praying for the safe return of their husbands from the battlefield. And unfortunately, many of them got killed in the battlefield and hence could not return. And needless to mention, many of them were quite shattered, bewildered, and also lost faith in God.

---
#### prayer, duty, calmness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=468
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "While the ocean is calm, I pray, and when the sea is rough, I handle my boat, I do my duty."
**Summary:** The speaker shares the story of a retired Navy captain who continues to pray daily, even when the seas are calm, and focuses on his duty during storms, illustrating the importance of both prayer and action.

There was a captain. He was working in the Navy, and he got retired, and he took up a small job, a small ferry which would take people from one island to another. He took up with this job, and before he would start every day, without fail, he would offer prayers. He would kneel down, and he would offer prayers quite feelingly. And one day, while some young students were on some excursion, and they happened to cross this, you know, ocean from one island to another, and they had to be taken on a ferry, they saw this captain praying. And on that particular day, it is quite clear skies, very calm waters. So one of them mocked, sarcastically remarked, why does he have to pray and waste time? After all, the sea is quite calm. Why do we have to waste time praying to the Lord? But the captain, as usual, offered the prayers and then started with his duty. It was a long drive, and unfortunately, on that day, suddenly the storms started blowing, and the winds, you know, very, very strong winds started blowing, and the boat, the ferry started tossing up and down, and all of them were quite panic-stricken. Many of them unfortunately did not know how to swim. So all of them gathered one place, and you know, they started praying, and they also requested the captain, captain, why don't you join us? Let's all pray together. Let's pray to the Lord for his help, for his guidance so that we can reach the shore safely. While they were all praying, the captain was busy handling the boat, the ferry, doing his duty. And the captain slowly remarks, says, while the ocean is calm, I pray, and when the sea is rough, I handle my boat, I do my duty. So we have a very important lesson from this particular anecdote.

---
#### surrender, reciprocity, free will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=622
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of remembering the Lord during peaceful times and quotes Krishna from the Gita, stating that He reciprocates according to one's surrender, highlighting the concept of free will.

When we go through nice, peaceful times, we often forget the Lord. Dukh mein sumiran sab kare, sukh mein sumiran kare na koe. And when we go through difficult times, we all use God to solve our problems, to take us through difficult times. Often it does not work. Krishna in the Gita says, as you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. Ye yatha maam prapadyante tam sthitaiva bhajaamiham. As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. Krishna does not force himself upon us. He has given us a free will, a choice. We can choose to remember him, or we can choose to forget him. So Krishna says as you surrender, if you remember him, if you surrender to him, he will reciprocate, but to the degree of your surrender.

---
#### relationship, investment, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you invest time and energy, that relationship will crystallize, it will manifest."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a child failing an exam to illustrate how the depth of a relationship determines the level of reciprocation, emphasizing the need to invest time and energy in cultivating a relationship with the Lord.

Just like in any relationship, if you invest time and energy, that relationship will crystallize, it will manifest. If there is zero investment in a relationship, there is practically no relationship. We have so many people. Let's say, for example, a child fails in the exam. The father who is related thickly with the child will feel very pained to hear the results of his child, but a neighbor with practically minimal relationship, he may not be pained as much as the father. So if we crystallize the relationship with the Lord by following a process, which our scriptures have mentioned, not necessarily when we go through the process of a relationship with the father, but when we go through troubled times, on day-to-day basis, on daily basis, if we follow the process, cultivate that relationship, invest that time and energy, then when we are in need of Lord.

Going through some challenges, Lord will definitely reciprocate. In fact, a devotee develops that faith, that conviction, he has that vishwasa that Lord will surely protect. This is that conviction which Prahlad had when he was thrown in a pit of snakes, when he was thrown from a cliff. He had complete faith in the Lord's protection because he was completely surrendered to the Lord.

#### faith, surrender, conditional surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=807
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Many times our faith in the Lord, our surrender to the Lord is conditional surrender."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the concept of conditional surrender, where faith in the Lord is dependent on the fulfillment of desires, and how people often switch deities when their wishes are not granted.

Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says whether you embrace me or you make me broken hearted by not being present before me, you are my Lord unconditionally. Many times our faith in the Lord, our surrender to the Lord is conditional surrender. If my wish is fulfilled, then my faith in the Lord is fulfilled. If you are not, then your faith in the Lord is suspended. If your desire is not fulfilled, then your desire in the Lord is suspended. If you are not satisfied, then your desire in the Lord is not fulfilled. Whatever I have demanded, whatever I have requested is not fulfilled. I turn to some other God. Fortunately, in India we have many options. So that's how people go from temple to temple. Just like many times people, if they don't get cured, they switch doctors. So likewise, many times people also go from one God to another.

Often get disappointed or disheartened when the wishes are not fulfilled. The first point is, if you only pray when... if you are in difficulty, then you are in difficulty, you are in problem. The second point is, the second principle is, a devotee who has nurtured that relationship, who has invested his time and energy in cultivating that relationship with the Lord, he has that faith, he has that nishtha faith in the Lord's protection.

#### Lord's protection, devotees, spirit soul
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=919
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We always see Lord's protection from the perspective of this body because we are in illusion thinking that we are the bodies, but actually the fact is that we are a spirit soul."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that even great devotees face trials, but this doesn't mean the Lord isn't protecting them. He emphasizes that Lord's protection is often misunderstood because we view it from the perspective of the body, rather than the eternal spirit soul.

He is not, when he goes through some difficulties, he knows for sure Lord will surely protect him. Mind you, having said that, we have examples of great devotees who have gone through a lot of trials and tribulations, a lot of challenges, a lot of difficulties. We have devotees who have gone through very painful desires, and we have devotees who have gone through very painful desires and conditions. We have devotees of God killed. Now that does not mean we have our own understanding of what is Lord's protection. Just because somebody has got diseased or just because some devotee has got killed does not mean that Lord has not protected. We always see Lord's protection from the perspective of this body because we are in illusion thinking that we are the bodies, but actually the fact is that we are a spirit soul.

#### karma, devotion, faith
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=979
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "With every difficulty they encountered, their faith in the Lord increased more and more."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the Lord may take devotees through difficult times to impart valuable lessons, not necessarily due to bad karma. He uses the example of the Pandavas, who faced numerous hardships but whose faith in the Lord only increased.

Atma and Atma is eternal. So many times Lord may take his devotees through some difficult times just to impart some valuable lesson to that devotee, not necessarily because of his bad karma. Generally, we go through difficulties because of bad karma. The example is the Pandavas. They were great devotees of the Lord. All along the Lord was with them, but still they had to go to the forest. They were wandering. They had no fixed place to stay. They encountered many, many demons. Abhimanyu was killed. They participated in a match which was rigged. Draupadi was attempted to be stripped in a whole assembly. They'd go through lot of difficulties, but because they were devoted to the Lord, they had that relationship with the Lord. With every difficulty they encountered, their faith in the Lord increased more and more. Generally, if we don't have that relationship with the Lord based on knowledge, based on understanding of who the Lord is, then quite possible difficulties can shatter us, can completely bewilder us, and we can get lost.

#### relationship, Lord's love, Lord's protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=1068
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Same mouth carried by the same cat, but one is experiencing death, another one is experiencing warmth of mother's love."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a cat carrying a rat and a kitten to illustrate how the same situation can be experienced differently based on one's relationship with the Lord, emphasizing the Lord's love and protection for devotees.

Our scriptures give us example of a cat carrying a rat and the same cat carrying a kitten. Many of us must have seen that when a cat carries a rat, the rat in its mouth, the rat is experiencing death. It knows that any moment it's going to die. It knows that any moment it's going to die. It's going to be killed and it will be swallowed. In the same mouth, the kitten is also carried by the rat. But the kitten, unlike the rat, experiences warmth of mother's love. Same mouth carried by the same cat, but one is experiencing death, another one is experiencing warmth of mother's love. Likewise, if that relationship is crystallized, if we have invested time and energy in building that relationship with the Lord, then even if the devotee goes through some difficulties, some challenges, he will experience Lord's reciprocation, Lord's protection, Lord's love.

#### devotee, Lord's protection, conviction

Although superficially, just like the cat carrying the rat or the kitten, superficially it appears, both of them are going to get killed. Superficially, it may appear that the devotee is not protected by the Lord. Devotee is also suffering like any ordinary person. But devotee, genuine devotee, who has evoked that relationship, experiences Lord's love, Lord's protection. So the conviction, that Nishtha, that Avashe Rakshabhi Krishna, a devotee has that conviction, that Lord will surely protect me. This is the conviction which even Prahlad Maharaj had.

#### difficulties, remembering the Lord, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=1203
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's a fact that we tend to remember the Lord when we are in difficulties."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights Kunti Maharani's prayer for more difficulties to remember the Lord, emphasizing that remembering the Lord leads to freedom from the cycle of birth and death, and contrasting this with judging the Lord's protection from a place of ignorance.

As I mentioned earlier, he was tormented by his father just because he was a devotee of Lord Shri Krishna. But he, in spite of all the challenges, all the difficulties he encountered, his faith in the Lord was unshaken. In fact, Kuntimaharani in the Srimad Bhagavatam, prays to the Lord, O Lord, let more and more difficulties come, so that it's an impetus for us to remember you. It's a fact that we tend to remember the Lord when we are in difficulties. And there is a possibility of we forgetting the Lord when we go through good times. So Kuntimaharani, Kunti Maharani is saying, oh Lord, let more and more difficulties come. Now that we have got the kingdom back and you are leaving us, we'll all forget you. So let more and more difficulties come so that it's an impetus for us to remember you and remembering you means freedom from the cycle of birth and death. So Kunti Maharani was in knowledge. Unfortunately, we judge Lord's protection, stay deep in ignorance.

#### faith, Lord's control, devotee's conviction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=1272
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Yes, the storm may be quite strong, turbulent, but the man behind the storm is my man."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about a Mulla and his wife during a stormy boat ride to illustrate the conviction a devotee has that the Lord is in control, even in the midst of difficulties.

And many times we mistake or we wrongly conclude about Lord's protection, more so when it comes to Lord giving protection to his devotees. Once there was a Mulla, he was on a vacation and he was having his family, his wife and was having a meal. And he was having a boat ride. And on that particular day, the weather was quite stormy, the winds were blowing. And when they were middle of an ocean, it became quite stormy and huge waves started tossing the boat. And it appeared that any moment the boat could capsize and they could be in water. And the life of Mulla started screaming, Help! Help! Help! Obviously, she was quite panic-stricken because she did not know how to swim. And while she was screaming was completely anxiety, fearful of death. She saw her husband, Mulla sitting in one corner of the boat, offering namaz, offering prayers. Could it be because of the prayers, sincere prayers, because of his relationship with the Lord? Somehow, the storm subsided miraculously and they could reach the shore. Moment they reached the shore, the wife rushed towards Mullah and said, What kind of person are you? Are you not afraid of death? We could all be dead today. If we were in water, if the boat would have got capsized, this could be the last day of our life. And I was seeing that you were completely silent, quiet, offering prayers. Are you not afraid of death? So Mullah, instead of replying to her question, he had a sharp knife in his pocket. Took out that knife and put it at the neck of his wife. And as soon as that sharp knife was put on the neck of the wife, the wife started smiling. So the Mullah asked, Tell me one thing. Don't you see that it's a sharp knife? Next moment I could slit your throat and you could be dead. Are you not afraid of death? The same question which the wife had asked. So the wife remarked, The knife may be sharp, but the man holding the knife is my man. So Mullah also replied, Yes, the storm may be quite strong, turbulent, but the man behind the storm is my man.

---
#### conviction, knowledge, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=1472

**Key Quote:** "That conviction has to be cultivated. That conviction comes with real knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that this conviction cannot be brought about just by offering prayers in difficult times but must be cultivated through knowledge, satsang, and a developed relationship with the Lord.

This is the conviction which a devotee has. It is my Lord who is in control. It is my Lord who is the cause of all causes. And unfortunately, this conviction cannot be brought about just by offering prayers in difficult times. That conviction has to be cultivated. That conviction comes with real knowledge. That conviction comes when we have heard about the Lord, His relationship with the devotees, from the right sources, attended satsang. When that relationship is cultivated, when that relationship is manifested, then, whenever a devotee goes through difficult times, he has that faith, he has that conviction, Avashe Rakshivya Krishna, Krishna will surely protect. He is my man. I have surrendered my life to the Lord.

#### devotion, Lord's will, acceptance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=1529
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In case for some reason things don't turn up as per devotee's wish or as per devotee's desire, let's say something happens to the devotee, still the devotee because he has that relationship with the Lord, he knows that there is, it's the will of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that devotion is not a gamble and that even if things don't go as planned, a devotee accepts it as the Lord's will without losing faith.

Lord will surely protect me. In that sense, it's not like a gambling match. It is not some kind of a business, a gamble. Yes, sometimes we may win or lose in a gambling match. But when it comes to the Lord, a devotee has that strong conviction, Avashye Rakshibe Krishna. Krishna will surely protect. And in case for some reason things don't turn up as per devotee's wish or as per devotee's desire, let's say something happens to the devotee, still the devotee because he has that relationship with the Lord, he knows that there is, it's the will of the Lord. There's a higher plan of the Lord and hence he accepts it wholeheartedly without any grudge, without his faith getting shaken in any way.

#### devotion, center of life, altar
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=2393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "One floor is dedicated to the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an experience of visiting a devotee's hospital and home, where dedicated spaces for the Lord are central, emphasizing the importance of keeping the Lord in the center of our lives.

Just last week I was in Jodhpur, had gone to meet a person. This lady runs a big hospital, four-storied hospital. And I was very impressed. She is a devotee of Lord Shri Krishna. And one floor of the hospital, when you run some commercial place or let's say business, every square feet matters. In business terminology, it's called ROI, return on investment. Every square feet matters. So, in that hospital also, I could make out that, you know, there were small, small cabins. There were, you know, operation theater. So, obviously, when the hospital is running well, you need space for expansion and for carrying out day-to-day operations. And I was very pleasantly surprised that in that hospital, one floor is dedicated to the Lord. Nice, beautiful altar with deities of Sitaram, Lakshman. We have Radha and Krishna and we have Ganpatiji. And throughout the day, the worship goes on, the bhoga offering happens. It's truly keeping the Lord in the center of our lives. Then she invited us to her home. And as soon as I entered the home, again, it was a very nice, pleasant experience. One room is dedicated to the Lord. The Lord is there in the center and the Lord is carrying the Govardhan hill, Giri Govardhan. And you can do a parikrama. It has Tulsi plants planted all around. So, it was very nice experience.

#### Lord in the center, remembering Krishna, forgetfulness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fBkvM-PyWrU?t=2512
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Always remember Krishna and never forget Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how to lead our lives by keeping the Lord in the center, contrasting this with having a small, insignificant altar and emphasizing the essence of Vedic literature: always remember the Lord and never forget Him.

That is how we have to lead our lives, keeping the Lord in the center of our lives, in the center of our homes. Many times, in the homes, we do have some altar, but it could be in a small, insignificant place, let's say next to a store. Only in the morning, somebody goes, just removes the curtain, does some Agarbatti puja and then it's closed. So, if we have Lord, if we have the Lord in the center of our lives, every moment we are trying our best to remember the Lord. Kirtaniya Sada Hari, Sada means always. Always remember Krishna and never forget Krishna. This is the essence of all the Vedic literatures. Always remember the Lord and never forget the Lord. But if we are always forgetful of Lord and remember the Lord, only when we have difficulty, definitely there is a problem.

#### prayer, relationship, surrender

And if we have that relationship, we don't even have to offer the prayers because Lord is there in our heart. He knows everything. Maa Sucha, He gives His commitment that you surrender to Me.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### STAYING CALM AMIDST STORM | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### suffering, materialism, adhyatmik kleshas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UR5C2fA6teI?t=420
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Pray to God that He very soon takes me away. And there was some kind of tears in his eyes. Enough. I've seen enough of life. Now it's too much, Swami. I just want to die."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the concept of three types of storms or challenges in life, focusing on Adhyatmik kleshas (miseries from body and mind) through the story of a wealthy and powerful man who, despite his opulence, suffered immensely from a severe backache and pleaded for death.

So, coming back to the discussion, the first part, what are the three types of storms which we can encounter in our lives? Mind you, when I am talking about storms, difficulties, challenges, as we go on in the session, you will see that these storms are irrespective of caste, color, creed, religion, rich man, poor man, black, white, Indian, American, anybody will have to encounter these kind of challenges. Our scriptures have classified the challenges we encounter in life, or the storms which we will have to face, broadly speaking in three categories. The first category is called Adhyatmik kleshas. Kleshas means difficulties, challenges, Adhyatmik kleshas, which broadly means miseries arising from our body and mind, Adhyatmik kleshas. And the second category is Adhibhautik kleshas, miseries due to other living entities. Adhyatmik kleshas. Other living entities means other living beings. It could be animals, it could be other human beings. And the third category is Adhidevik kleshas, miseries due to natural calamities. The super cyclone which Kolkata had to brace a few days back, Amphan, would come and destroy it. It would come under that category. Miseries due to natural calamities are the Devik kleshas. So we will go one by one, before we get into the discussion, why these calamities land up in our lives, why we have to encounter these challenges, first we will understand what these categories are. And then we will discuss. And then we will get into the solution part of it. The first thing I mentioned was Adhyatmik kleshas due to our body and mind. This was way back in 2003 or maybe 2005. There was a very eminent gentleman, very powerful person. His name was Nanak Dev. He was a very powerful man. He was a very good man. position. He was working in the, he was part of a finance committee at the national level which was chaired by the finance minister of the country. So there was a committee of about 10 to 15 people and he was part of that committee, the eminent committee which would make you know the financial, the financial planning, the budgets, allocation of resources, so being part of the committee obviously he was in touch with the who's who of the country. He would just, he could have just picked up a phone and called Mr. Ratan Tata or could be anybody, any big industrialist or any eminent person of the country. So he visited our kitchen in Bangalore, Akshaya Patra had just started, it was in 2001-2002 and he was based out of Delhi. He was very impressed with this initiative and helped us in many ways. Today Akshaya Patra is a very big brand but in those days it was relatively unknown, people were not aware of this initiative and he would call many eminent people and somehow get them connected or we would fix up some appointment so that we could go and give a presentation, tell them, about what we are doing and many of them got connected with Akshaya Patra and started supporting Akshaya Patra and then a few years passed somehow we lost touch with this gentleman and in around 2010 a vice chairman gets a call from him and in a very feeble voice he says, Swamiji how are you? Long time we haven't met. Nice talking to you over the phone. He called up, how is Akshaya Patra doing, how many children are you feeding these days. He was inquiring and then towards the end of the call he said, Swamiji when you are coming to Delhi please find time to visit me. Come back to my place it will be my privilege to have you with us so yes yes why not we will surely come we will definitely meet we are privileged to come to your place and spend some time and then few days later a vice chairman happened to visit delhi and went to his place and this gentleman comes the bell rang gentleman comes up personally to open the door and take swamiji inside and from his looks he could make out that he was a completely different man with what we had seen him in 2005 2003 he was completely different completely frail, weak, barely able to walk. He had a cane and then he made Swamiji sit on the sofa in his drawing room and then started off with some discussion and at a certain point he held the hands, both hands of Swamiji and said, Mami ji, you are very close to God. You all are spiritual people. You are spending your time. He was aware of our daily schedule. He had visited our temple also. You all spend a lot of time in chanting prayers, chanting mantras, doing meditation. So you are very close to God. I have a request. Please pray to God that He very soon takes me away. And there was some kind of tears in his eyes. Enough. I've seen enough of life. Now it's too much, Swami. I just want to die. So Swamiji held his hand and said, no, no, why are you saying like that? Because he was also caught unaware. Rich, eminent person, part of that finance committee, deciding the budget for the country. Allocating resources, dealing with hundreds and thousands of crores. Maybe regularly meeting the finance minister of the country. That person is saying, I've seen enough. I just want to leave. Pray to God that I leave this. Swamiji became a little curious. He says, why are you saying so? He said, for the last 4 years, 5 years, I have a severe backache. I can't sit and sleep. I can't walk. Even now, when I am sitting, talking to you, there is so much of pain. Name a hospital. It's his name of country he traveled. It is his name of country. So you know different countries because he was very eminent person rich person he tried you know taking different kinds of treatment different best of hospitals in the world in the new york singapore best of the hospitals he had gone consulted the best of doctors and eventually because the pain was so unbearable he was hii was to kill that give that temporary relief he was given some prescription involves some painkillers and because the pain was so intense he had even done surgery where the search written although back surgery is quite risky not very much recommended but still as a Last resort he had to undergo a surgery and still the pain was so unbearable that he was prescribed some painkillers and because he had to take 8 to 10 painkillers in a day at different times of the day, his body, there was a reaction, ulcers, blisters had got formed. He couldn't eat properly, there were ulcers in his stomach, he couldn't digest the food properly, could not sleep, could not sit, could not walk and he was just, you know, pleading, pray to God that I should just keep this, you are close to God, he will hear your prayers. Now mind you, this adhyatmic kleshas which I mentioned about.

#### suffering, universalism, adhibhautik kleshas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UR5C2fA6teI?t=1059
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is not that only poor people, only people from third world countries, now we have, you know, the US, just two days back I was reading an article, they have 5,000 people have succumbed to this Coronavirus pandemic."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa emphasizes that Adhyatmik kleshas are not limited to the poor, citing examples of rich people succumbing to diseases and the impact of the Coronavirus pandemic in wealthy countries like the US. He then transitions to the second category, Adhibhautik kleshas, miseries caused by other living entities, using the example of locust attacks.

It is not that only poor people, now here we have migrant labourers, you know, they are Shramik Express going and we hear day in day out that they don't have food to eat and they had to travel in this kind of conditions, walking barefoot. We can understand, sometimes we may even feel that because, you know, poverty, unfortunately they had to go through all this. Maybe rich people are exempted, yes, maybe they are exempted from one kind of adhyatmic kleshas. But we have examples of even rich people succumbing to diseases like cancer, so many other diseases. So it is not that only poor people, only people from third world countries, now we have, you know, the US, just two days back I was reading an article, they have 5,000 people have succumbed to this Coronavirus pandemic. With such advanced medical facilities, people are by and large very rich. There is lot of opulence, there is lot of technology. Still people are falling sick, people are getting infected and people are dying. So, adhyatmikta, miseries due to our body and mind. So, this is the first category. As I mentioned, everyone will encounter this. Everyone will encounter this. We may have a sense that because I have a policy, because I am rich, because I am quite strong, because I go to gym daily, because I have, you know, I am quite physically fit. I am very conscious about my diet, just a question of time, just a question of time, body will become frail, weak, vision will become blurred, our legs will start wobbling and eventually we have to leave. Adhyatmik kleshas. Then we have adhyatmik kleshas. Adhyatmik kleshas. Adhyatmik kleshas. Adhybhautik kleshas. Miseries due to other living entities. Every one of us aware couple of days it has been in the news. The locust attack. Swarms of locusts, millions of them, 80 to 100 millions just get into a particular area and the whole crop can be devastated in few hours. And at times such powerful, the governments with such powerful machinery are helpless. Although yes, some precautions is being taken, some prevention is being planned but then by and large helpless. Miseries due to other living entities.

#### suffering, loss, adhibhautik kleshas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UR5C2fA6teI?t=1265
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "These are the kind of storms which we can face in our lives it's not that we will lose only our near and dear ones who are 85 plus years of age it can happen with anyone anytime and at times people even lose faith in god they start wondering where is god"
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares a tragic story of a young girl from a wealthy family who died from dengue fever contracted after participating in a school race, illustrating the unpredictable nature of Adhibhautik kleshas and how such events can lead to a loss of faith.

Just other day I heard about. A girl. She. Very. The parents are both are working. Father and mother. Father is working in a software company. Very good position. And the mother is also working in a bank. So they have two daughters. One of them is in seventh class. The elder one. And in the school there was Sports Day. And as part of different activities being played in that particular day. She also participate in a race in which in which, so you have to get into a we all have at least seen those kind of races if not participate in one of them. so get into a gunny bag and then the gunny bag is tight, practically like your legs are legs are within the gunny bag and then you have to hop and there's some kind of a race so who will reach the finishing line first that becomes a winner unlike a normal race where you run your both legs are free for you to run here it's within a gunny sack so this race happened on that particular day so this girl also participated unfortunately the gunny bag because they had probably got it from some place and was stacked or stored those gunny bags had a lot of mosquitoes again so imagine it's kept there and some of them may get wet so they attracted mosquitoes and some of them were inside the bag so once the gunny bag was worn and maybe tied around the waist so all along for the duration of the race which was about maybe five minutes or ten minutes I don't know the details so there were mosquitoes which were biting and this girl comes back home and it was okay that particular day but then she had a lot of mosquito bites on her legs and then two days later she developed fever and it was quite so initially when a person develops you know you give a paracetamol you try to bring down the fever and do some kind of home remedies so this happened and then they saw that now it's on the rise 102 degrees and shivering so she was rushed to the hospital Apollo hospital want to get into details uh city the place the name of the family and uh she was diagnosed blood tests were recommended and she was diagnosed with dengue. Dengue is a fever uh this kind of disease which comes because of mosquito bite and because it was dengue and you know blood platelets came down she was admitted in the icu and you know different kinds of medicines were administered but you know her health condition kept on worsening and you won't believe sixth day after she was admitted all the doctors were trying their best to monitor all her in icu you know it's well equipped best hospital and all the latest gadgets uh you know nurses around the clock monitoring all the health parameters so the best of doctors were uh you know involved they were empaneled they were called for to discuss this particular this thing and the sixth day from the day she was admitted she collapsed and died now from a very rich family both the parents are in good positions good hospital best of doctors good medicines and it's not that she's old she just went to the school participated in a particular game on a sports day she was physically fit participated in a race who knew that in seven days she would collapse and die the parents were completely crestfallen these are the kind of storms which we can face in our lives it's not that we will lose only our near and dear ones who are 85 plus years of age it can happen with anyone anytime and at times people even lose faith in god they start wondering where is god

#### theodicy, suffering, divine justice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UR5C2fA6teI?t=1929
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The answer is our scriptures very forcefully, very emphatically say that the Lord is our supreme father, the loving father, we are his part and parcel."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa addresses the question of why calamities occur if God is all-powerful and loving. He refutes the idea that God is either helpless or sadistic, asserting that the scriptures affirm God's loving nature and our connection to Him.

Now the second part which we want to discuss today is why these calamity. On one side we say God is all-powerful, God is our loving father, he has created this world. So one possibility is that things have gone out of his control. He doesn't want his children to suffer but unfortunately what to do, there is another powerful force which is much more powerful than him, maybe because of which he is held helpless. You know at times in a family the father, he wants his child, wants good for his child but at times he is helpless. In front of his eyes he can see his child suffering. So one possibility is that maybe there is another powerful force, maybe a demoniac force which is much more powerful and Lord who is our loving father, who is kind, who is very kind. He is also helpless. The answer is no, it is not that the Lord is helpless, the other part of it then somebody may say if he is not helpless, if he is all-powerful, maybe he is not very compassionate, he is not very loving. Otherwise why do we see so much of suffering in this world? So much of misery. So many lakhs and lakhs of people dying, either he is not all-powerful or if you say that he is all-powerful, maybe he is not very merciful, he is a sadist when he sees that his children or his people all around in his creation are suffering, maybe he enjoys, he gets some kind of a rasa, some kind of a pleasure. So there are people who are called sadists who get some kind of a pleasure when they see suffering of others. The answer is our scriptures very forcefully, very emphatically say that the Lord is our supreme father, the loving father, we are his part and parcel.

It's all superstitious. And that's how there are people who are very well-read, very knowledgeable so-called scholars. They become atheists. They write books on why God doesn't exist. We have to get the right understanding. God does exist. He is the creator. He is sarva-karana-karanam. He is the cause of all causes. He is all-powerful. He is omnipotent. He is omniscient. He knows everything. And He is all-kind. He is all-merciful. It is possible that all these qualities can be there in that Supreme Lord person

**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that we are in the material world (bhava-sagra), an ocean of birth and death, while Krishna speaks of a spiritual world (parastasmatu bhavanyo vyakta-avyakta-sanatana) that is eternal and free from suffering. #### Bhagavad Gita, knowledge, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/UR5C2fA6teI?t=2584
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "This Gita is given for us to understand what is this material world. What is our relationship with God? Why are we suffering? What happens to us after death? What is law of karma? Why there is so much of suffering in this world?"
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa emphasizes the importance of the Bhagavad Gita as a source of knowledge to understand the material world, our relationship with God, the reasons for suffering, and the law of karma. He then transitions to the third part of the discussion: how to handle these storms and remain calm.

Krishna is sharing this knowledge in the Gita. This Gita is given for us to understand what is this material world. What is our relationship with God? Why are we suffering? What happens to us after death? What is law of karma? Why there is so much of suffering in this world? Perfect answers have been given by the Lord Himself. But great Acharyas were explained, pages after pages. Unfortunately, we are so busy, we have no time. And when these calamities land up, we say there is no God. We are completely shattered, we are completely bewildered. So, the root cause of these calamities is that we have got a material body, we are in the material world. And Bhava Sagara, material existence, and Krishna in the Gita is inviting us. The whole Gita is spoken so that we can bring an end to our material existence. He is inviting us to His abode, the spiritual world. The whole Gita has been spoken that how we can come back to Him and enjoy in His company, in His association. So, this is the second part of it. Why calamities in the first place? Why these storms arise from nowhere in our lives? The third part, what we would like to cover in today's discussion is how we can handle these storms. The topic is how to remain calm while we encounter, or rather face these storms.

How to remain calm, how to maintain balance. Again, in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna gives his valuable instructions. The whole Bhagavad Gita was spoken in a battlefield. And mass destruction was about to happen. The battle was about to begin. And irrespective of the situation, the battle was about to begin. And irrespective of the situation, the outcome, lakhs and lakhs of people would lose their lives. It was, devastation would have happened. And we know in the first chapter, Krishna, Arjuna tells Krishna, Krishna, I am bewildered. My mind is reeling. I am unable to stand. My bow, Gandiva, is slipping from my hand. And then finally, he sits down, gives up his bow, and says, Krishna, I won't fight. In the Gita, now, Arjuna is in a battle. He is in a very precarious situation. It's a very, very difficult situation for him because he has to fight against his enemies, his own kinsmen, his own relatives, his own near and dear ones. Bloodshed will happen, and there won't be ordinary people who are going to die. There are going to be his own relatives, own kinsmen, near and dear ones. And Krishna spoke the immortal message, the instructions, the teachings of Krishna. The instructions of the Gita. Arjuna heard them. And in the Gita, Krishna

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### SURRENDER - WHY AND HOW? | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### surrender, Vaishnavism, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Let us seek the blessings of Srila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya, Lord Sri Krishna so that we can have a very very meaningful discussion today and there are lot of takeaways and learnings from the discussion we have."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa begins the Gita Life session by introducing the topic of surrender (Sharnagati), outlining the principles to be discussed, and initiating a prayer seeking blessings for a meaningful session.

A warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session. We are going to discuss a very wonderful topic, and the topic for the day is Surrender, Why and How. Surrender is also called Sharnagati. So today we are going to discuss some principles which are recommended in the process of surrender. So before we begin our discussion today, our session today, let us all pray together. Let us seek the blessings of Srila Prabhupada, Lord Chaitanya, Lord Sri Krishna so that we can have a very meaningful discussion today and there are a lot of takeaways and learnings from the discussion we have.

#### surrender, control, higher power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=256
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Surrender also means where we raise our hands and give ourselves to the control of Lord, saying that Lord, please take charge of my life now. I am surrendered unto you."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa defines surrender as giving control to a higher power, relating it to the concept of surrendering to the Lord and allowing Him to take charge of one's life, and introduces the principles involved in leading a life of surrender.

Sharna Gati or Surrender, if we were to define it, it basically means to give one's control to someone, to give oneself to someone. In other words, one surrenders to a higher power. So there are six principles of surrender which is given in our scriptures. Today, we will be covering three of them, the first three of them, and in subsequent sessions, we will cover the balance three. All these six principles are very important, and if we practice these principles, we can very quickly advance in our Krishna consciousness. Just like we have seen in movies, especially during war times, we have seen people raising both hands as if saying that I surrender, I give myself to your control. We have seen that kind of scenes. Surrender also means where we raise our hands and give ourselves to the control of Lord, saying that Lord, please take charge of my life now. I am surrendered unto you. So what are the principles involved if we have to lead a life of surrender to the Lord? What are the principles involved?

#### desire, prayer, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=370
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It's more like, I am giving you this, in return you give me this. Higher the demand, the bigger the donation."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares a personal story about seeking a dream job after college, highlighting the mentality of trading with the Lord through donations and prayers, and emphasizing the importance of adding 'if you so desire' to prayers.

Let's learn those principles and understand them in detail. So as I mentioned, we will be covering three principles today, and in subsequent sessions, we will catch up with the other three, the balance three. Many years back, when I passed out from my college, I was in Delhi for a short while, and there was a dream job which all of us were chasing. Everyone has a dream job. Like for example, in present times, people would love to work with Google, Facebook. So in those days also, there was a dream job for all of us who had passed out. I had done my engineering in computer science. Anyway, I don't want to name the company because that company no longer exists, but it was a dream job. Everyone was aspiring to be in that company, to get a job in that company. And I remember, I used to visit the temples with a prayer that Lord, somehow get me this job. And I remember, generally when we go to the temple, we have some mannat, we have some desire. So to please the Lord, this generally happens, we give some donation. It's more like, I am giving you this, in return you give me this. Higher the demand, the bigger the donation. So in those days I remember, I used to go to the temple and donate 5 rupees in the Thundi. And before I would put that money in the Thundi, I would very clearly show to the Lord, see here I am donating. And after that act of putting the money in the Thundi, I would offer the prayers, asking the Lord for this job, dream job. Few days passed by, I had applied in that company. In fact, I got a call for an interview also, but somehow, there was a delay happening, and I used to regularly visit the temple. And I saw that, I started increasing my donation amount. The intensity was quite high. Somehow or other, I wanted that job. And, this is typical mentality. You know we all, so called religiosity involves some kind of a trading with the Lord, some kind of give and take. But one good thing I remember, while I was very specific with my prayers, I want this job. Can you please help me get this job? One very specific thing which I was doing in those prayers was, if you so desire.

---
#### desire, ignorance, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=594
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Goal of life is not to kill the desires, goal of life is to purify our desires, to have right kind of desires."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa reflects on not getting the job, attributing it to the Lord's higher plan, and discusses how material desires stemming from ignorance are the greatest enemy, emphasizing the importance of purifying desires rather than eliminating them.

That was getting added in my prayers every day when I would visit to the temple. If you so desire. And I will not get into details. Somehow, I did not get that job. And you won't believe me, many of my friends who are much behind me in the rankings, in the college ranking, in my batch, some of them got an offer, got an appointment letter. And it was very difficult for me to comprehend, why did the Lord not sanction this job for me? I was aspiring for that job. I had the required knowledge, required marks. I was among the toppers in my batch. Why did the Lord not sanction this job for me in spite of my praying so hard? It was difficult for me to fathom what mistake I did, that I did not get this offer, whereas my friends who were much behind me in marks, in the overall ranking, they got the offer. And I remember, one time, I visited this company because it was a dream job. I visited that company to meet my friend and I was at the reception. The receptionist called that person. He came and he met me. I was stopped at the security before I entered. Whom do you want to meet? So I had to write the name of my friend whom I was visiting. And while I was walking out of that job, there was a lot of sadness in my heart. Alas, I could have got that job. Today, if I look back, Lord had a higher plan. Had I got that job today, quite possible, I would not be sitting here and talking to you on the subject of surrender. The saving grace for me, if you ask me, was, although I was specific in my prayers, I wanted a particular job. I was very very clear to the point, Lord give me job in this particular company. But the saving grace, if I now recollect, if I look back and try to look back, the saving grace was the statement which I added at the end of my prayer, if you so desire. We have been discussing in our previous session, that how kama esha, krodha esha, rajo guna, samudbhava, it is the desires, kama, material desires, which is the greatest enemy of the living entity. We all keep desiring, we desire so many things. We all want many things to happen in a particular way, in our lives, for us to be happy.

---
#### desire, ignorance, Lord's desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=791
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is very very important for us, to pray to the Lord, to beg the Lord, to infuse in us, those desires, which are His desires for us."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa elaborates on desires stemming from ignorance, leading to actions, and emphasizes the importance of praying to the Lord to infuse His desires within us, drawing a parallel to a parent's aspirations for their child.

Now, many of our desires are stemming from ignorance. Avidya, kama, karmabhi. We all are acting, behind our actions is desires, which are driving those actions, and behind those desires are different kinds of avidya. Avidya, kama, karmabhi. We are ignorant to different degrees, and out of that ignorance, we come up with so many desires, and then we pursue those desires, which leads to plethora, many kinds of actions. Lot of people are very very busy, morning to night they are busy, no time for anything, just very, so to say, from one task to another, one activity to another, very very busy. It's all about pursuing their aspirations, their desires. So that's why, we have been discussing that how goal of life is not to kill the desires, goal of life is to purify our desires, to have right kind of desires. So it is very very important for us, to pray to the Lord, to beg the Lord, to infuse in us, those desires, which are His desires for us. The whole Bhagavad Gita started, where Arjuna said, I will not fight, better for me is to just beg and live. But, Krishna spoke the Bhagavad Gita, and expressed that it is His desire, Lord's desire, that we should live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live, and live. That is his desire, that this battle should happen. And then, when Arjuna was asked, what do you want to do? Now you decide. So, Arjuna said, karishe vachanam tara. I will do, as you desire. As you have asked me to do. So it is in our self-interest, that we act, as per the desires, of the Lord. And our prayer should be, infuse in me those desires which are your desires for me just like a father a mother has some desires for their son or daughter they have some aspiration if the child is acting as per their desires becoming a responsible citizen getting well-educated the parent feels happy so likewise the Lord also has some desires for us if we act in a particular way the Lord feels happy if we act in certain ways desires certain wrong desires the Lord feels unhappy all of us have material desires let's accept this reality.

#### surrender, favorable activities, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=979
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The first principle of surrender is anukulyasya sankalpah, which means that to accept only those things to undertake only those activities which are favorable to our growth in Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the first principle of surrender: *anukulyasya sankalpah*, which means accepting only activities favorable to growth in Krishna consciousness, distinguishing between activities that uplift and those that distance us from the Lord.

And at times we are very specific in our prayers, we have many material desires to fulfill and we go to the lord with, you know, our checklist that this this this things wants, I want to happen. It is always good if we can pray to the lord and add the statement if you so desire because the lord knows what is best for us. Out of avidya, out of ignorance, many times we desire things which is not in our self-interest, we desire things which will eventually entangle us, bind us and lead us to misery. So the first principle of surrender is anukulyasya sankalpah which means to accept only those things, to undertake only those activities which are favorable to our growth in Krishna consciousness. So there are many activities which will uplift our consciousness, which will take us closer to lord and there are many activities which will take us away from the lord. So the first principle of surrender is anukulyasya sankalpah which means that we undertake or engage in those activities which will take us closer to the lord.

#### surrender, unfavorable activities, restrictions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=1078
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So that's why the second principle of surrender recommends that we should avoid those activities which will take us away from Krishna, which will derail our Krishna consciousness, which will bring us down, which will pull us down towards the lower modes of material nature, towards Rajoguna and towards Tamoguna."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the second principle of surrender, *pratikulasya varjanam*, which means giving up unfavorable activities, drawing an analogy to a doctor's prescriptions and restrictions for recovery, emphasizing the importance of avoiding activities that derail Krishna consciousness.

Actually this Srila Prabhupada in his books, according to his book, have given us many many instructions, practical instructions, instructions which will enable us to become more and more closer to the lord, which will enable us to purify our desires, which will enable us to elevate our consciousness. So, that's the first principle of surrender. The second principle of surrender is Pratikulasya Varjanam, Varjanam means to give up. Just like we go to a doctor, the doctor gives them some prescriptions, he gives us a prescription with certain medicines which we have to take. He also gives us some don'ts, something which we should avoid. I remember one time I was suffering from jaundice and then the doctor definitely gave some prescription with medicines. And he also very specifically told me that avoid eating oily foodstuffs. All that was very very difficult but then he made it very clear that during this period when you are recovering avoid all kinds of fried food, oily foods, eat less oil, eat you know as far as possible bland diet.

#### restrictions, Vedic culture, experience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=1176
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So that's why the second principle of surrender recommends that we should avoid those activities which will take us away from Krishna, which will derail our Krishna consciousness, which will bring us down, which will pull us down towards the lower modes of material nature, towards Rajoguna and towards Tamoguna."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa continues discussing *pratikulasya varjanam* and shares a personal story from college where his parents restricted him from attending a party with girls, reflecting on the wisdom of maintaining healthy distance even in Vedic culture.

And in fact the restrictions are equally important. So that's why the second principle of surrender recommends that we should avoid those activities which will take us away from Krishna, which will derail our Krishna consciousness, which will bring us down, which will pull us down towards the lower modes of material nature, towards Rajoguna and towards Tamoguna. I remember when I was in the college, second year. We came back home and typically in college when you first go to college you know you get exposed to so many things. All kinds of friends, people with different kinds of habits and generally you know you start intermingling with your classmates, with your batchmates and their friends both among the boys and among the girls. I remember when I came back, one time among our friend circle, although those days it was quite unlike present times where boys and girls freely mixed together, we planned a party and in those party, in that particular party we had planned some of the, you know in the township where we were staying, some girls also were invited. And the party was supposed to start at say 8pm in the night and we had planned that by the time we will be back home it will be 11.30, 12. We were supposed to go to a particular place, so about 6-7 of us and then few girls also who were living in the neighborhood, in the township, they all had joined some college. So I remember my parents specifically refused. No, you should not go. So I was quite surprised because this is in thing, everyone is involved in partying and here my parents are that sense very orthodox. No, you should not intermingle with girls. And somehow I did not go for that party. and I was quite upset I remember speaking to my parents that times have changed I even told them that looks like there's a generation gap here is a time where boys and girls are intermingling very freely maybe you are from a previous generation where that was not allowed but still my parents suggested better for you not to go and then it was very difficult for me to comprehend but if I look back there is a reason why there is some element of distancing maintained even in the Vedic culture and

#### regulative principles, freedom, restrictions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=1391
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually it's a fact that if we follow these Regulative Principles, we become eligible to enjoy genuine and true freedom."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains the scientific reason behind maintaining distance and discusses the regulative principles of freedom (no meat-eating, no intoxication, no gambling, and no illicit sex), highlighting the paradox of regulation leading to true freedom.

this is scientific reason behind it although yes as souls we are not man woman but still ask scriptures give us recommendation of maintaining a healthy distance now experience matters a lot that's why sometimes elders may give some recommendations which later on we feel it's not okay it may appear that they're very the very very concertative so likewise our scriptures also give up some restrictions some prohibitions which looks like the chocking our freedom in fact therefore regurgitative principles which are mentioned in our scriptures with a broad definition like ideology of freedom involving human condition it also refers to the fact that we are poor or we happen to have which is no meat-eating, no intoxication, no gambling and no illicit sex. We call it Regulative Principles of Freedom. Now although we call it Freedom and Regulative Principles, if you analyze this, these two words are contradictory. On one side we are saying Regulation, the other side we are saying Freedom. Actually it's a fact that if we follow these Regulative Principles, we become eligible to enjoy genuine and true freedom.

#### freedom, regulations, senses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=1496
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So Shastras are giving us some restrictions. Some regulations, some don'ts, which if we follow, will help us surrender to the Lord, will help us move closer to the Lord."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa uses the analogy of traffic lights to illustrate how regulations, though seemingly restrictive, are essential for safety and order, and explains that Shastras provide restrictions to help us surrender to the Lord and move closer to Him.

I'll give you one crude example. Just like when we are driving on a highway and there comes a traffic light, red light, we are supposed to stop. It's a regulation. It regulates our free movement. But that regulation is important because if everyone just drives without any hindrance of traffic lights, there could be collisions, there could be accidents. So such regulations are important. So Shastras are giving us some restrictions. Some regulations, some don'ts, which if we follow, will help us surrender to the Lord, will help us move closer to the Lord. So the second principle is Pratikulasyavarjanam, to give up those things which will retard our progress towards Krishna Consciousness. As I mentioned, the principle of them being meat-eating, intoxication, gambling, sexism, gambling and illicit sex. In the media, we are hearing a lot about Shushant Singh Rajput, whether it was a suicide or whether it was a murder. If we analyze, if we get into the details, it's all about women which were involved in his lives and people partying, boozing, getting married. It's all about women. Now then, understand that touchy talk is a discrimination, it's a big one. Don't if you want to take it jobless, it is not. It's not. That's why I talk of evolves a little bit. But in the middle of the day, you might have to be more in a clues about some things that may exist in your life. But you massively might get a little bit into the dance of situations of belief. For example, I coaxed a woman to go and have a drink with me because that's what she had made history, that the truth is supposed to be in social media. And we are the people who walk into blah blah blah, blah blah blah… Lazily she said you are going to go home and that's how now here this world goes, because of shushant Singh Rajput. discussion going on. Likewise, if we do not follow the regulative principles, in the long run, it backfires. Maybe in short term, we'll get some enjoyment. We'll get some pleasure. We'll be happy in the short term. But in long run, it backfires and brings a lot of pain and misery in our lives. So that's why Bhagavad Gita is full of instructions of the Lord, some prescriptions of the Lord, and Lord also suggests certain restrictions, like Krishna in the Gita says, so from the very beginning, regulate the senses and slay this destroyer of knowledge and self realization. So restrictions are very important, and we should not feel, get assaulted about it way too much. And therefore, you have to take care of the sense that these restrictions is curtailing my independence or curtailing my freedom. In fact, certain restrictions are good for us. These restrictions will actually award the true freedom which we are wanting to experience in our lives.

#### surrender, faith, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=2597
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee of the Lord is completely dependent on the Lord. For His protection, He is completely surrendered to the Lord."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that a pure devotee is completely dependent on the Lord for protection, drawing an analogy to a child's dependence on its mother, and introduces the third principle of surrender.

As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. If we remember, when we were young, for a child who is completely dependent on the mother, the mother is always thinking of the child. In fact, the mother is conscious, now my child will be hungry, now it's lunch time, I should feed the child. Now it's dinner time, it's already four hours ever since he had had the last feed, let me go in. The child is completely dependent on the mother. The child does not make any plan whatsoever. That's a different thing, the child cannot make any plans. But the child is completely dependent on the mother and hence the mother is completely taken charge of the child. And as we grow up, we start making our own plans, proportionate to that the mother also starts relinquishing the hold and the care she has for us. Although mother is always thoughtful, always loves the child, but in the sense that when a person starts making his own plan for his own life, yes, once in a while the mother may call, mother may ask, how are you? But it's not that from time to time, from meal to meal, what have you eaten, have you eaten, are you hungry. So, same is the case. A pure devotee of the Lord is completely dependent on the Lord. For His protection, He is completely surrendered to the Lord. conviction has that faith our share Rakshabhi Krishna the Lord will surely protect me and he also has the conviction Mara Krishna Raka Raka Krishna Marika if Krishna protects if the Lord protects no one can kill me no one can do any harm to me and if the Lord kills me or if the Lord decides to hurt me no one can protect me Mara Krishna Raka Raka Krishna Marika

#### faith, protection, relationship with the Lord

**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares a story about Srila Prabhupada and a child on a tram to illustrate complete dependence on the Lord for protection, emphasizing the third principle of developing faith and conviction that the Lord is our protector.

one time Shila Prabhupada was traveling in a tram in Calcutta and along with him was a child his child and while they were traveling in the tram the the conductor came and asked for the ticket from the child and generally you know that the treaty was quite playful he asked the child where is your ticket show me your ticket so the child was quite surprised someone coming and asking for ticket so the child said I don't have so the treaty said if you don't have the ticket then you get down you can't travel in this tram so the child looked at Shila Prabhupada his father indicated the conductor though the TT you can't ask me to get down if you want anything please ask my father so in other words the child was completely dependent on the father if I have to have a ticket please ask my father if I don't have my ticket you can't ask me to get down ask my father so same as the case of a person who is completely surrendered to the Lord he always looks upon the Lord for his protection so that is the third principle where a devotee develops that faith that conviction that Lord is my protector and he is able he can give me the protection what I am seeking he's capable of giving me the required protection. Avasya Rakshibhi Krishna E Vishwasa Palana Palana means we have to cultivate that conviction, that faith that Lord will surely protect me.

#### faith, relationship with the Lord, devotee's suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=2860
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That's why it is very important for us to build that relationship with the Lord. To spend quality time and energy in building that relationship with the Lord in prayers, in chanting, in reading His instructions, trying to practically apply those instructions in our life."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa emphasizes the importance of building a relationship with the Lord through prayer, chanting, and studying scriptures, and addresses the apparent contradiction of devotees suffering despite the promise of protection, explaining that the Lord's reciprocation depends on our surrender.

But mind you, when we are talking about this faith, we have to remember the principle that as you surrender, so shall I reciprocate. That's why it is very important for us to build that relationship with the Lord. To spend quality time and energy in building that relationship with the Lord in prayers, in chanting, in reading His instructions, trying to practically apply those instructions in our life. The first two principles of accepting all those things which are favorable to our Krishna consciousness and rejecting those things which are unfavorable to our Krishna consciousness. So when we are living that kind of a lifestyle, the third principles come in play. The Lord takes charge of our lives and protects the devotee. We have many many examples where Lord has gone out of His way to protect His devotees. In fact, He tells Arjuna to declare it boldly Naame Bhaktia Pranashyati Declare it boldly that My devotees are not going to be never perish. In fact, we have also seen the Lord breaking His own promise for the protection of His devotees. At times we may doubt. We may have some examples of devotees who are also suffering. We have examples of Pandavas themselves who were very devoted to the Lord, surrendered to the Lord, but they were suffering. And at times it can be very bewildering. On one side we say the Lord will surely protect. The Lord is capable of protecting His devotees. On the other side we see many times devotees suffering miserably. And we may doubt. Where is the Lord? Where is Lord's protection? This is all nonsense. And sometimes if people do not have the right knowledge, backed with right transcendental knowledge, their faith can get shattered very badly if some untoward incidents happen in their life.
