#### suffering, purification, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=2997
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Same way, the Lord may take a devotee through some trials, some tribulations, some difficulties, some challenges, some miseries, to teach some important lessons."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa uses the analogy of a doctor treating boils to explain that the Lord may subject devotees to trials and tribulations for their purification and to teach them important lessons, emphasizing that this world is a place for our purification.

Just like when a person goes to a doctor let's say with boils, the big fat boils in the body and filled with pus. I am sure you would have come across situations, you have seen people with big boils filled with pus. When a person goes to the doctor, at times the doctor may give an injection, which is very painful. And at times the doctor even squeezes the boil so that the pus which is accumulated there gets removed, comes out. In fact, the whole activity which the doctor does, giving an injection or removing that pus can be even more painful than the boil itself. But that is required to be done for the good of that person, for him to recover, for that so-called problem to subside. Same way, the Lord may take a devotee through some trials, some tribulations, some difficulties, some challenges, some miseries, to teach some important lessons. This world is a place where we have offended the Lord, we have rebelled against the Lord, we have come in this world, and this world is a wonderful place for our purification. If we disobey the Lord, if we do not follow the Lord, we will get beaten, we will face different kinds of miseries and difficulties, and over many many lifetimes, we may experience different kinds of hardships till we come to our senses. That's why Krishna in the Gita says, A person who is in knowledge will come to Me, will surrender to Me. So Prabhupada says, if that is what we have to do after many many lifetimes, now that we have got human birth, now that we have got a wonderful opportunity, why delay? If after many many lives we have to come to the point of surrender to Lord, Vasudevam Sarvamiti, Samahatma Sudurlaba, why not in this very lifetime?

#### surrender, fear, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1n9TqhZg8Eg?t=3151
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sarvadharman Parityajya. Parityajya means give up. Maam Ekam. Ekam means me alone. Sharanam Raja. Sharanam Raja means surrender to me."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa quotes Krishna's final instruction in the Bhagavad Gita, *Sarvadharman Parityajya*, urging complete surrender and assuring protection from sinful reactions, and summarizes the three principles of surrender discussed.

That's why in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 18th chapter, after having spoken so many instructions, Krishna finally says, Sarvadharman Parityajya. Parityajya means to give up. We all have so many concocted ideas. What is good for me? What is my dharma? What is my duty? What I should be doing? What I should not be doing? Our head is full of ideas which could be morally right, ethically right, but may not be in our real self-interest. So that's why in the 18th chapter, Krishna says, Sarvadharman Parityajya. Parityajya means give up

---

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Seeing God Face to Face | Bhagavad Gita Chapter 11 Verses 45 - 49 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### universal form, fear, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So, he is glad that the Lord is so opulent. All the energies, all the potencies, all the devas are within him. He is the source of everything."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the topic of seeing God face to face, discusses Arjuna's reaction to seeing Krishna's universal form, and explains the simultaneous feelings of gladness and fear.

So today we are covering text number 45 to 49 from the 11th chapter of the Gita, and the title for today's discussion is Seeing God Face to Face. By the way, how many of us have seen God? Others? Yet to see? Hoping to see? Okay. Prabhupada was asked, have you seen God? And depending on the audience, whether they were devotees or they were completely raw, he at different times gave different answers. Not that because he hasn't seen God, but depending on the audience, he gave different answers. I'll share that as we move forward in this discussion today. So, based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada, his wonderful insights in the purports, let's chant this verse together. Adrishta purvam rishito asmi drishtva, bhayena cha pravitham mano me, da deva me darshaya deva roopam, prasida devesha jagan nivasa. Translation: After seeing this universal form, which I have never seen before, I am gladdened, but at the same time, my mind is disturbed with fear. Therefore, please bestow your grace upon me and reveal again your form as the personality of Godhead, O Lord of Lords, O Abode of the Universe. These are the words spoken by Arjuna to Krishna after having seen the universal form. So, here he said, I am gladdened. Can anyone help me with an answer? Why is Arjuna glad that he saw the universal form? Why is he glad? And apparently there is a contradiction. Simultaneously he is saying, I am fearful. On one side he is saying, I am glad. The other side, he is also mentioning that he is fearful. So, he is glad that the Lord is so opulent. All the energies, all the potencies, all the devas are within him. He is the source of everything. So, that made him glad that Krishna is not an ordinary person. That person who is with him, who is his companion, who is his sarathi in the battle, accompanying him, with him, is not an ordinary personality. It is the Supreme Lord Himself, who is all powerful. So, that is the source of his happiness. And why is he fearful? He is fearful because till now he never had the understanding that Krishna whom he was treating like a friend, like an ordinary driver, was not an ordinary personality. And in the previous verse we discussed, he said, unknowingly, unintentionally, I might have committed many, many mistakes. He might have committed many, many offenses by calling him by casual names. Sometimes, you know, when we don't realize somebody's greatness, maybe we may go to an office and we don't know who the boss is. And we may find somebody walking around and, you know, may not respect him. But later on when we recognize that he is the boss, maybe we have mixed feelings. Some element of, you know, I shouldn't have acted like this. So, same is the case with Arjuna here. Now, the universal form which Arjuna saw, it's not the eternal form of the Lord. It is a material form and it is temporary. It was shown to Arjuna, and it was wound up. Now, Arjuna is requesting Krishna, yes, I have seen your universal form. Now, I am keen on seeing your original form, the spiritual form, the four-handed form. The next verse is about Arjuna requesting for that. So, the original form of the Lord, this brings us to the point that many people believe that Lord has no form. God is some kind of a power, some kind of energy. From Him, everything has come.

---
#### form of God, logic, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=547
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We have to understand about the Lord from Lord Himself and His pure devotees."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the concept of God having a form, addressing common misconceptions and emphasizing the importance of understanding God through scripture and the guidance of pure devotees.

But the understanding we get from the scriptures is that God, no doubt, has innumerable energies. But behind that energy is the energetic. There is universal intelligence which is running the whole cosmos. But behind that intelligence is the intelligent creator. Whose intelligence? Whose energy? From where is it emanating? So, just like you and me are a person, God also is a person. But He is not an ordinary person. Generally, why do we feel difficult to accept God as a person? Because, in our experience, we have come across people, individuals, beings, who are limited in time and space. Suppose today, you are here in the class. I can very confidently say that you are in Mumbai. If somebody comes and says, no, so and so is out of station. No, I saw him in the class. Because, it is very, very obvious that if you are here in the class, you are not out of station. On the other side, we know that Lord is omnipresent. He is present everywhere. So, if we give a form to the Lord, the Lord has a form, He is a person, then that quality of the Lord that He is omnipresent, He is present everywhere, He is present in each and every atom, He is present in everything, becomes difficult to comprehend. So, to safely reconcile with this understanding, people conclude that God is not a person. It's an energy. It's in power. Another thing which we, why we come to the conclusion that God is not a person, because whenever we see a person, we know that person has a body, which grows, stays for some time, reproduces, dwindles, decays and finally dies. It goes through transformation. And we know that Lord is not go through this transformation. He is transcendental. He is beyond these six transformations. Hence, we conclude that since our body goes through transformation, we go through diseases, our body becomes old, we die, but God doesn't do that. He doesn't die, He doesn't become old, He doesn't become diseased. So, it is safe to conclude that He doesn't have a form. But our scriptures point out that the Lord has a form. But His form is Sat Chit Ananda Vigraha. It is not an ordinary form. It is not a material form. It is a spiritual form. It is a transcendental form. And what is the quality or nature of that form? It is Sat. It is eternal. Chit, full of knowledge. And Ananda, full of bliss. That's why it is important that when we come to understanding of spiritual subject matter, it is difficult to come to right conclusion sheer by logic and rational thinking. Is that clear to everyone? What I mentioned right now, logically it appears right. He is omnipresent, therefore He doesn't have a form. That's a logical conclusion. But we cannot comprehend the Lord or come to understand about the Lord merely by our logical conclusion or analytical skills. We have to understand about the Lord from Lord Himself and His pure devotees. So here Arjuna is saying, please show me that form, the four-handed form, the Lord in innumerable spiritual planets which are also called Vaikuntalokas. He has spiritual form, transcendental form with Shankha, Chakra, help me with other two, Gada and Padma. O Universal Lord, I wish to see you in your four-armed form, with helmeted head and with club, wheel, conch and lotus flower in your hands. I long to see you in that form. Now many times when we have some picture of the Lord, so sometimes people say, is it some kind of imagination? Somebody has painted a picture. Are these imaginary forms? How does the Lord actually look like? So in the scriptures, there are vivid descriptions about the form of the Lord. Like here you said, with helmet, head, with club, wheel, conch and lotus flower in your hands. So we get some elements of what the form has. It has hands. What is in the hands, what is on the head. So like that, the vivid descriptions about the Lord in the spiritual world and basis which Acharyas have written commentaries, given description about the Lord, glorified the Lord. King Kuleshekha Alwar, he has Mukundamala Shruta, he is describing the glories of the Lord. How does he look like? How his hairs are? How his facial expressions are? How his eyes are? And that's how we understand about the Lord. Now here we have the Lord replying. The blessed Lord said, My dear Arjuna, happily do I show you this universal form within this material world by my internal potency. No one before you has ever seen this unlimited and glaringly effulgent form. So Arjuna wanted to see. Krishna gave him that divine vision. And before that, no one had seen that universal form. As I said, it's not an eternal form. No one had seen this universal form of Lord before Arjuna. Now just like Arjuna was blessed with the divine vision, even when we are blessed with divine vision by the Lord, the Lord revealed himself to us. That is the process of seeing God. Prabhupada in one place mentions when Prabhupada was asked, Have you seen God? He says, Do not be eager to see God. Act in a way God sees you. In other words, Lord has given innumerable instructions for us. What will please him? And if we follow that instructions, the Lord will be pleased with us. He will bestow his mercy. And then we can experience the love, the reciprocation of the Lord. Otherwise, in one sense, we are disqualified. In the battlefield, Arjuna was also there. Duryodhana also was there. Arjuna recognized Krishna as the supreme personality of God that Duryodhana could not recognize. Arjuna said, O best of Kuru warriors, no one before you has ever seen this universal form of mine. But neither by studying the Vedas, nor by performing sacrifices, nor by charities or similar activities can this form be seen.

---
#### divine vision, self-realization, God-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=824
**Verse References:** BG 7.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "One cannot have the divine vision without becoming divine."
**Summary:** This segment explains that divine vision requires becoming divine, emphasizing the relationship between self-realization and God-realization, and highlighting the eternal relationship between the Atma and the Lord.

This is very important. By being a Pandit, by being a very learned scholar, by mugging up the entire Gita or the Vedas, or all the shlokas, you cannot comprehend the Lord. By doing elaborate sacrifices, you cannot comprehend the Lord. By doing charities or similar activities, this form cannot be seen. Only you have seen this. Can anyone help me having understood this much? What is the qualification to see the form? Huh? Divine vision. One cannot have the divine vision without becoming divine. Right now we are, although we have a relationship with the Lord, mamaiv amsho jiva loke, we have a very tangible, concrete relationship with the Lord. It is not just a concept. Just like Prime Minister, Narendra Modi is Prime Minister of the country, that understanding is not just a concept, it is a tangible reality. So and so is my father. How confidently I see, if I have to fill a form, father name, mother name, I very confidently fill it up. It is not a belief. It is not a faith. So likewise, eternally that Atma, mamaiv amsho jiva, we have a relationship with the Lord, but that relationship is now forgotten. Why is it forgotten? Just like the sunshine is there, but sometimes we say, it is very cloudy, there is no sun today. Right? The clouds may be just half a kilometer, but then they have no power to cover the sun. The sun is 14,000 times bigger than earth, but we say today there is no sun, because it is very cloudy. So when the clouds of maya covers our consciousness, the Lord becomes invisible. Just like sun is there, clouds cannot cover the sun, but when there is cloud, the sun is not visible. There are countries where people are not blessed with sunlight. It is not possible. It is not that the sun is not there. So when there is cloud of maya covering our consciousness, covering our intellect, then we are not able to perceive the Lord, we are not able to see the Lord, we are not able to experience the reciprocation of the Lord. So human form of life is a wonderful opportunity to remove that covering. So one of the important purpose, in fact the only purpose of human form of life is self-realization or God-realization. Self-realization and God-realization are synonyms. Can anyone tell me why? Why are they synonyms? Many times we have heard this word self-realization. In fact we have a book, Science of Self-Realization. Goal of human life is self-realization. And we also have word God-realization. These words are synonyms. Why? Self-realization is not only the understanding that I am the Atma. Some people reach to that point, Aham Brahmasmi. That is definitely a step towards the highest understanding. I am not the body, I am Atma. Why that self-realization is also God-realization? Because real self-realization involves understanding who that Atma is. And what is that Atma? Is Amsha of Lord. So when you realize Atma in its pure form, you will also realize that Sambandha. Sambandha means the relationship with the Supreme Lord. Who is Amsha? Whose part? Where has it originated from? So right understanding of the self or self-realization includes or is also part of God-realization.

---
#### purification, human birth, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=1103
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Human form of life is a wonderful opportunity to remove that covering."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the rarity and importance of human birth for purification, explaining how the soul becomes polluted by material nature and the need to strive for internal and external cleanliness to experience real happiness.

So one cannot have divine vision unless a person becomes divine. There is a very nice verse in the Srimad Bhagavatam which says, After many, many births, this Atma has got this human birth. Manushya Janma and this Manushya Janma is Durlabham. Durlabham means Durlab. Durlab means Sulab and Durlab. Durlab means it is very difficult. We have to very quickly, quickly, there is a sense of urgency. Yateta means we have to strive for, aspire for. Shudhyet means for purification. Brahma saukhyam to anantam. Why purification? When we get purified internally and externally, right now, we have become dirty. Our consciousness has become polluted. Polluted of what? Base qualities. Lust, greed, envy. Just like a drop of water. It's pure. When it falls on the ground, it becomes muddy. So, this Amsha, in its pure form, is devoid of all these base qualities. It's pure. It's divine. But when it comes in contact with this material nature, gets overpowered by Maya or illusion, all these divine qualities gets lost. So, many times, we strive for developing qualities like some of us may be very angry, short-tempered. Or some of us may find that, you know, we are very impatient. Or some of us may find that, you know, we are not able to control our appetite. We eat lot of things. We are not able to restrain or restrict ourselves. These all are symptoms of a bigger disease. Just like when a person may have fever, yes, you can obviously take a paracetamol and fever comes down. But the fever is symptomatically addressed by taking a paracetamol. But the real cause, why the fever has come, is not addressed. Just few days back, one of the devotees, you know, had high temperature, two days fever. So, the doctor told, two days you take this tablet, and if it does not come down, we will do a blood test to check whether it is dengue or some other. So, two days, it was symptomatically treated, thinking that it could be because of various other reasons. The fever comes, could be viral, could be something. And if it does not come, after two days, we will do a blood test. So, that soul, which is amsa of the Lord, in its pure form, is Sat Chit Ananda. And when it contacts this material nature, it becomes polluted. So, Bhagavatam is saying, we have to quickly endeavor for shudhyet. Shudhyet means for purification. And what happens when we get purified? Shudhyet, brahma saukhyam tu anantam. Then we will experience brahma saukhyam. Brahma saukhyam means the real happiness which we all are actually wanting to experience. How many of us want to be happy? We all are trying different ways and means to be happy. Am I right? Yes or no? Different things we are trying. We also become happy at times, but that happiness does not last. It's not lasting happiness. It's not real happiness. Many times that so-called happiness is followed by a misery. In fact, Krishna says, what is illusion? That very bhoga, that very enjoyment which one seeks by senses coming in contact with the sense objects, sparsha means touch. Senses coming in contact with the sense objects, that is the breeding ground or the source of misery. God does not want us to be miserable. Many of the miseries which we have attracted in our lives is because of we having acted in ignorance wrongly.

#### cleanliness, godliness, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=1411
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Cleanliness is next to godliness."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of cleanliness, both external and internal, as a path to godliness, sharing a story about Prabhupada emphasizing cleanliness in the temple.

And we have missed things. Why this school is there? Why these colleges are there? Why so much of money is spent on education? Because people know ignorance means a person will not act wisely. Why awareness is about traffic laws? Why this concept of driving license? You should know all these laws before you get a license to drive. Because if a person does not know these rules, he will meet with accidents. So, now it is also mentioned what are the various aspects of godliness or becoming divine? How many of us have heard this statement? Cleanliness is next to godliness. Now, cleanliness is one is external and one is internal. External cleanliness is definitely being very careful of our daily hygiene, taking bath, being clean, clean clothes and things like that. Internal cleanliness is cleansing our body, cleansing our consciousness. So, the sadhana bhakti which our acharyas have recommended is to make us pure, clean and divine internally and externally. I was speaking to someone yesterday. He was talking about his children. He says, they are very unclean. I said, why? He says, they eat in the bed, they expect in the bed and they don't take bath. I said, what? He says, yes. Every day they don't take bath. I said, is it true? He says, yes. So, when we become unclean, when we are dirty, just like you see sometimes laborers, two days, three days, they don't take bath, they are comfortable with it. They get conditioned. That impure state one becomes clean. Prabhupada says, brahmanas, they take bath minimum three times. Because we perspire. No, not everyone. I am not recommending you all start taking bath three days. There will be water shortage in Mumbai. But nevertheless, cleanliness is important external. So, likewise internal cleanliness. Once, Shri Prabhupada, he visited the temple and after the Darshan Aarti, Govindam Adi Purusham, that song and all that, the whole morning thing happened. And then, Prabhupada was walking around in the temple and he happened to see, happened to visit the Pujari room. The room next to the altar where all the Aarti plates after the Aarti is done, that's taken and it's washed. So, Aarti had got over, and in that Pujari room all the Aarti plates were lying completely unclean. They are not washed. So, Prabhupada went in and he was apparently disturbed. He was not very happy seeing that this Aarti plates are not washed. Generally, in a temple, if you visit any of Aarti, if you visit any of our temples, you will find it is very, very clean. The standards are very high. If you have to enter the altar, you have to change your dress. If you go to a bathroom, to a loo, you have to change the dress and after you come back, you have to take a bath before you enter the altar. The very high standards and there is a reason why it is not permitting the devotees. There is a reason because you are dealing with the Lord who is all pure and to also instill in something false that the way the plates are kept, everything is meticulously clean. We have a concept called Maha Cleaning. Have you heard of this word Maha Cleaning? So, weekly, twice, all of us in the temple, all of us irrespective of cadre, that Maha Cleaning is other than the regular cleaning which happens every day. So, Prabhupada went and he saw the aarti plates were unclean and it was not washed. So then, he was little not very happy and then you know, he started cleaning himself. So, immediately the words Prabhupada is cleaning somebody saw and the devotees are like please don't do it, please don't do it, devotees will do it, devotees will do it. So, Prabhupada looked at them and said, am I not a devotee? Should I not clean? So, an Acharya is a person who walks the talks. He leads by example, shows by example. So, Prabhupada himself was very clean and he also demanded that cleanliness from his disciples. So, divine means to become pure devotee of the Lord. Now, how do we become pure devotee of the Lord? When we are externally pure for sure and we are internally pure. Our consciousness is pure. It is not possible to decry Krishna and at the same time have the divine vision. In the heart we have jealousy, we have resentment, we have hatred, we have envy, we have lust. We cannot comprehend the Lord. We are disqualified to have a relationship with the Lord. One can perform bodily penances, give charity, study the Vedas, etc. But unless he is a pure devotee, unless he becomes a devotee. Now, pure devotee also yes, long way to go but it is not a zero-one logic, binary logic. As we become more and more pure, the more and more we will relate with the Lord, appreciate the Lord.

But the supreme authority presides on our n em p eu as a desperate sale and somebody was selling it so Prabhupada took that house the devotees went inside the house did nice kirtan and stayed in that house because when we chant the names of the Lord all the inauspiciousness these days very people are very cautious of vastu and you know all this what are the shungsai what is that all these things no doubt as far as possible we can take care but there are too many parameters to handle in this world vastu wise you might be compliant other parameter wise you may not be compliant as far as possible we take care but the brahmastra is chant the names of the Lord it ushers in all auspiciousness in your life where you are staying and everywhere so all what we do is basically all the dirty things which are there in our heart means inside means dirty impure means it destroys just like mountains are broken into pieces by a thunderbolt by hearing those auspicious messages about the Lord by chanting the glories of the Lord which is full of auspiciousness full of potency all the impurities all the dirty things which are there covering our consciousness in our heart and one becomes purified.

#### devotee, prasadam, kirtan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=2024
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The philosophy was good, the devotees were good, you know what they were sharing the message was good, but one thing which was very very difficult for me to digest."
**Summary:** The speaker shares his initial experiences at the ISKCON temple, appreciating the philosophy, devotees, and prasadam, but struggling to understand the practice of jumping and dancing in kirtans.

So when I first started going to the Iskand temple that was way back in 1989 so first I met the devotees in the temple I was very impressed because very educated good families the chairman whom we were interacting with those days he was from IIT Mumbai alumni and our vice president he was from Indian institute of science very premier institute in Bangalore so I was talking to them and you know they are science graduates they could I could relate with them I could talk to them sometimes when we deal with sadhus you know they are a different species yes or no if you go to kumbh mela they are you know with ashes on their body not having you can't relate with them yes or no bole nahi bole kya hai kaa ye now here I saw you know well meaning individuals educated also talking about God so that was my initial you know relationship with them so I really appreciated them for their intelligence for the philosophy what they were sharing the knowledge about God we should have some discussion I would have some doubts I will ask and very satisfying answer I used to get so I appreciated that and then invariably when I used to go to the temple I used to have prasadam that was also I was very fond of prasadam and I used to appreciate prasadam a lot but one thing was a big question mark in my mind the philosophy was good the devotees were good you know what they were sharing the message was good but one thing which was very very difficult for me to digest can anyone guess what is that difficult difficult difficult It took lot of time. It was people, devotees dancing, jumping and dancing wildly in Kirtans.

#### kirtan, envy, sin
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=2225
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Two kinds of people cannot dance: those who are envious of God and those who are loaded with lot of sins."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts how he overcame his reluctance to participate in kirtans after learning that those who cannot dance are either envious of God or burdened with sins.

And why like this? How many of us have seen ISKCON, Kirtan, people jumping and dancing? Anyone? Most of you have seen, yeah. So, I was very reluctant. So one day, so I used to like hearing Bhagavad Gita class. Would like talking to the devotees. Would like reading Prabhupada books. And would also obviously like Prasadam. But the jumping and dancing part was alien to me. So one day I was hearing in a class, the speaker told, you know two kinds of people cannot dance. So, you know my eyes, ears, what is he saying, he is talking about me. Two kinds of people cannot dance. Today we will dance, let us see who can't dance. Two kinds of people cannot dance. So I said, you know, I was curious to know what are these two kinds of people. He says one, those who are envious of God. Those who are not in God's party. Again it was difficult, how can he say that I am envious of God and therefore I am not dancing? How can I be, otherwise why will I come to the temple? So then I thought, maybe it is just one way of enthusing me to dance. It appeared to me that whole conversation was directed, keeping me in mind. One thing is, who is envious of the Lord, a person who is not part of God's party. And then, I raised my hand. I said, how do you say that, God's party and not part of, envious of God. So he said, just like when these elections happen, you know you have political rallies. You will have a political rally, you know, congressman, Rajiv Gandhi Jindabad, Rajiv Gandhi Jindabad. You know BJP people, will they say Rajiv Gandhi Jindabad? They will feel, why they are chanting Rajiv Gandhi? Yes or no? When Modi will come, they will say Modi Jindabad. You cannot chant the names of person who is of the opposing party. Yes or no? Agreed what I am saying? If says Jai Shri Ram, Jai Shri Ram, who will say? Hanuman will say Jai Shri Ram. What will Ravan say? Will say Jai Shri Ram? He will not say, he cannot say. Because he wants Sita, he doesn't want Ram. So then he said, just like when it is name of Rama, Hanuman will chant Jai Shri Ram. Ravana will not chant. Ravana is in the opposing party. When the battle happened, Hanuman was on the side of Ram. Ravana was in the opposing party. So if you are part of the opposing party, you will not chant the names of Lord. Does it make sense? Yes? Second, he says those who are loaded with lot of sins, burdened with sins, the sins are so overwhelming. The burden of sin is, imagine if you have figuratively, if you are holding 50 kgs of bag in your left hand, right hand and I ask you to jump. Can you jump? 50 kgs on the left hand, 50 kgs on the right hand, jump. You can't jump. Because there is a load. So if we are loaded with lot of sinful reactions, we are very sinful, we are very impious, we are not pure, impurities are weighing on us, we will not be able to jump.

#### kirtan, purification, divine vision
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3yE6RJ4IflI?t=2463
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When the holy name of the Lord is uttered, all the sinful reactions which are overpowering us start fleeing away."
**Summary:** The speaker describes his first experience participating in kirtan and explains how chanting and dancing help to purify us by driving away sinful reactions.

So after having heard that, I felt internally offended. It is not fun. How can he say that I am impure, I am chanting so much, I come here. So one day I went, the Kirtan was going on. And you know, by the side of the wall, I was also shy. You know, what is dancing in front of everyone? It was not a, what will people think, you are shy. So I went, the Kirtan was going on. And by the side of the wall and slowly I raised my hands. Left side and right side. Almost till this point I raised the hand. I said now, see. At least I am able to raise the hands. That means some element of piety is there. There is some sin. And then the beats of the Kirtan was so, you know, inclusive that I saw that, you know, slowly the Kirtan is going on. The legs started moving. And I was very conscious, anybody is not looking at me. He will say, you know, he has started dancing. Because I was very, very known at that time, that Kirtan time, I am away from Kirtans. And then slowly from that day onwards, even before I could realize, I saw that my legs are in the air and I have started little bit jumping and dancing. So when we do Kirtans, when we dance, when we clap, what happens is, all the sinful reactions which are there, just like in a jungle when a lion enters, what happens? All the animals flee out of fear. Am I right? So likewise, when the holy name of the Lord is uttered, all the sinful reactions which are overpowering us, over many, many lifetimes we have completed, those sinful reactions, those sinful tendencies start fleeing away. They start running out of fear. Bhīr apiyat bhiveti. Fear personified is fearful of Lord Shri Krishna. So all the sinful reactions get, so to say, wiped out. And we become, vidu nauti surut satām. We become purified. So now, part of the purification we talked about, if we have to perceive the Lord, the Lord who is divine, we should have divine vision. We cannot have divine vision unless we become?

#### demigods, sacrifice, Vedic knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Oblations offered in sacrificial fires are meant for demigods, and on behalf of demigods, the three sons of Agni and Swaha, namely Pavaka, Pavamana and Shuchi, accept the oblations."
**Summary:** The lecture begins with a description of Swaha, the 14th daughter of Daksha, and her three sons who accept oblations offered in sacrificial fires meant for demigods.

After describing the descendants of the 13th wife of Dharma, who were all daughters of Daksha, Maitreya now describes the 14th daughter of Daksha, Swaha, and her three sons. Oblations offered in sacrificial fires are meant for demigods, and on behalf of demigods, the three sons of Agni and Swaha, namely Pavaka, Pavamana and Shuchi, accept the oblations.

#### creation, intelligence, atheism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=33
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Behind any creation there is intelligence which goes in, so there has to be an intelligent being, an intelligent creator."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa narrates a story about Newton and his atheist friend, using a three-dimensional model of the solar system to illustrate the necessity of an intelligent creator behind complex creations.

We all know Newton was a very God-conscious scientist. And his friend, who was by and large an atheist, whenever they would get into some kind of discussion about God, this friend would really put forth many arguments. And one day when they were discussing about the existence of God, Creator, the Supreme Being, this friend said, "Gone are the days when man believed it rained because of rain God." Today we are living in a world of science where we have scientific explanation for all the observed phenomena. Vedas say Indra has to be pleased, when Indra gets pleased, rains come. This is no, it's very clear now, the rain comes because of clouds, clouds is because of evaporation of water. There is no gods involved in the whole process. It's a scientific phenomena. So Newton argued that, even if there is sun because of which the water is getting evaporated, there is someone who has created the whole system. Someone who has intelligently, behind any creation there is intelligence which goes in. So there has to be an intelligent being, an intelligent creator. And we all know that Newton took his friend to his lab and there was a three-dimensional model of the solar system where the sun was there and all the planets moving in their orbits. So the friend, as soon as he saw that, he says, "Wonderful, beautiful, who has made it?" So Newton said, "It just landed from nowhere. Yesterday night I was working late in the lab, it was not there. Today morning when I came, opened the door, I saw this model." So friend said, "Come on, don't joke, how can this be possible? Tell me who simulated the model?" So Newton said that just like for you to reconcile the fact that this has come from nowhere is so difficult, how can you be so confident this wonderful creation with so much of orderliness, so much of intelligence has just come from nowhere?

---
#### preaching, skepticism, scriptural understanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=197
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Suddenly Bhagavatam has another world view."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares a personal experience of sending Bhagavatam verses via email to relatives, and how he skipped a verse about Chitraketu having a million wives because it was difficult to digest and might be met with skepticism.

Many years back when e-mails were relatively new, when the e-mails were just introduced, first time when I sent e-mail to my brother and few of my cousins, I got their e-mail id and sent it and suddenly after few hours I got their reply. This was way back in I think 90s, 93, 94, even later. So then I said it's a wonderful tool to preach. So what I started, I collected all their e-mail ids, whosoever had in my relatives and every day one verse of the Bhagavatam, translation, I would copy and paste. It was a big thing that time. Now it is all copy-paste, copy-paste, WhatsApp, forward. It's a world of... I keep forwarding things. But those days it was not like that. If at all you have to communicate, it was either through letters or telegram. And e-mail was free. So I said it's a wonderful preaching opportunity. Every day I would copy-paste one verse from the Bhagavatam and send to all of them. About 10, 12 e-mail ids I had. And it was so wonderful, you know, feeling of satisfaction I am preaching to them. They are getting Bhagavatam messages daily. And I had that feeling that, you know, if I give them book Bhagavatam, nobody is going to read. But if daily if I send them in doses, they will read. And one day they would have read the whole Bhagavatam. So I came to that chapter where... And some of them were replying back appreciating what I was sending. Then I reached that particular verse in the Bhagavatam. I said, one Chitraketu had million wives. I did Control-C, copy. I was about to paste. I said, no, no, no, no. I can't paste this. Because all of them were skeptics. You know, they are not... Chitraketu had million wives. For me also too, at that time, it was difficult to digest. You know, initially you are in a lot of things you are hearing and you have a world view. And suddenly Bhagavatam has another world view. Chitraketu had million wives. We all heard about, you know, somebody having four wives, five wives. And our Krishna had 16,100. For that also it is so difficult to understand. Chitraketu had million wives. Sorry. Sorry. Sorry. So I stopped. I consciously remember that day I stopped. I didn't... Not stop in the sense. That verse I did. I skipped that verse.

#### paradigm shift, worldview, scriptural authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=380
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Paradigms means the model through which we look at things."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how scriptures offer a different worldview, contrasting it with scientific understanding and prevalent beliefs, introducing the concept of paradigms and paradigm shifts.

So now how do we understand? Now scriptures give us... Like here we are talking about not only Fire God, Wife of Fire, Swaha, and the three children. And when we offer oblations, these children, namely Pavaka, Pavamana and Shuchi, accepts the oblation. For a common man, fire is fire. But here our Bhagavatam is talking about the wife of fire, the children of fire, and which of the children will eat what. So we get another world view. Now those who are skeptics, they will say this is all... all unscientific, mythological stories. All fictitious characters have been created. Actually it's not a fact. Our scriptures are giving us a world view based on what we have studied in science, what we have studied in schools and colleges. We have another world view. And because that is what we have learned, and that's what everyone has learned, that's the prevalent understanding, we think that world view is perfect. These are all in scientific parlance called paradigms. Paradigms means the model through which we look at things. These days the people call about paradigm shift. I will give you examples of few paradigms. What is paradigm? And how Bhagavatam, Bhagavad Gita is basically shifting our paradigm many times the wrong paradigms what we have to absolute paradigms which is given by Creator Himself, Krishna Himself.

#### paradigm shift, warfare, cultural change
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=497
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Trenches were no longer, so to say, safe places for soldiers to be in. They would be in a trench, suddenly bomb will fall from the top. So there was a paradigm shift."
**Summary:** The speaker provides examples of paradigm shifts, including the shift from trench warfare in World War I to aerial bombing in World War II, and the change in perception of the drink 'AIDS' after the emergence of the disease with the same name.

When the World War I happened, you know the enemies when they would fight with their guns and all kinds of arms and ammunition. In World War I they dig some trenches. And when the soldiers went inside the trench, trench means some kind of pits. And when the soldiers would go inside the pit or in the trench, unless they come out they cannot be shot. So the whole paradigm was, if you have to win the war, build trenches. Go further, build trenches, get into the trench, nobody can shoot you, go further, escape the enemy, build trenches, go in that and nobody can shoot you. Keep proceeding and occupy the other's territory. So then, you know everywhere after World War, it was all about expertise in building trenches. Which country could build better trenches, more faster trenches, and could hide themselves better. And before World War II, paradigm shifted. People started bombing from air. Trenches were no longer, so to say, safe places for soldiers to be in. They would be in a trench, suddenly bomb will fall from the top. So there was a paradigm shift. In the early 50s in America, there was one drink, which was very very popular, just like Coca-Cola these days, or Pepsi these days. The drink was called, it's a very wonderful name. Now many of you will laugh when I tell the name. The name of the drink was AIDS, A-I-D-S. And the tagline was AIDS Stamina, AIDS Energy. And it was very popular. You know all, just like these days, Pepsi and all, so commonly, every store has these Pepsi bottles being sold. So this drink was very very popular. And anybody, sports people, after their matches, they would drink this. And suddenly, they discovered a disease, which was also called AIDS, Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome. And suddenly this, just like Ebola these days, and so many other dreadful diseases become so, you know, panic gets in the people's minds. So AIDS started spreading. And all over the world, AIDS, AIDS, AIDS, scares of AIDS, people became fearful of AIDS, HIV, and all those things. And this drink suddenly, AIDS, all the goodwill it had, the brand just tanked. No time. Because in people's minds, paradigm shifted. They started dreading AIDS, fearing AIDS.

#### paradigm, perception, scriptural understanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=691
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Paradigms means what we think is right, what we think is wrong, what this world is all about, who we are, who God is."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that paradigms shape our understanding of the world, and if things in the Bhagavatam don't align with those paradigms, we tend to dismiss them as fiction or superstition, using the example of Dr. Frog to illustrate this point.

So these are all examples where, now, they cannot have better advertisement with the same name. They cannot have. They cannot change the tagline. They cannot spend more dollars in promoting their drink. Paradigm has shifted. AIDS is no longer an acceptable word. Nobody is longing for AIDS. I want AIDS. So paradigm shifted. So likewise, we all have paradigms. Paradigms means what we think is right, what we think is wrong, what this world is all about, who we are, who God is. We have paradigms. And when we hear things in the Bhagavatam or our Vedic literatures, if it does not tally with those paradigms, we call it fiction. Or we call it superstitious beliefs. Prabhupada very famously gives this example of Dr. Frog. Dr. Frog had a paradigm of a deep well, three feet, four feet, at times five feet water in the rainy season. But suddenly when a dog, when a frog comes and says that I am coming from an ocean which is miles and miles deep, it does not tally with this paradigm. So he says, how can that be? Just writes it off.

#### perception, relativity, paradigm
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=777
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Their paradigm cannot accommodate anybody can have two legs. But for us, this is normal."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of centipedes to illustrate how different beings have different paradigms, and how something normal for one being might be inconceivable for another.

I heard in one more course, I heard in one more class that centipedes, centipedes have hundreds of legs. In fact, depends on the body, how many parts they have, different body parts. So pair of legs for each part, they could be centipedes with as less as 35 legs and it can go as much as about 350 legs. Now for us to fathom or imagine or rather think of a being who has 300 legs, the question comes, why so many legs? Because for us, the paradigm is two legs are enough. Now for a centipede, for them, if they were to conceive a person with only two legs, only two legs, because among them, they will see one more centipede with 220 legs, somebody with 240 legs, somebody with 300 legs and they will be comparing and suddenly they hear in their scriptures, there is one being with two legs, they will go off. This is how can they balance? How can they walk? Their paradigm cannot accommodate anybody can have two legs. But for us, this is normal. So this is all about how...

#### knowledge, humility, scriptural authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=983
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Just that we have to accept the fact that we are too small, we are tiny insignificant creatures in this whole gigantic creation."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of hearing from authoritative scriptures and bona fide spiritual masters with humility, acknowledging our limited understanding in the vast creation.

But from our scriptures, we get living entities are Sarvagatha. All pervading. Every planet, every part of the universe, living entities are there. So it is, now some people who are relatively new, they think that it is all blind belief, descending process of acquiring knowledge. Kuch bhi bollo. Anything you say, you have to accept. Actually it is not blind faith. Just that we have to accept the fact that we are too small, we are tiny insignificant creatures in this whole gigantic creation. They say now they have figured out some millions of galaxies. Our Milky Way galaxy is so huge. It is just one of those millions of galaxies. And in that galaxy, you have millions of stars. Each of them having its own family of planets. And we are sitting in one small, tiny planet. And thinking that our understanding of the world and the universe is perfect. Just like an ant, which is there in this hall somewhere. Ant has a world view, has an understanding of world, has an understanding of, and it also has, it also has senses, it also has, you know, it is perceiving data. But its world view does not have an understanding of America, of what is happening in India, in Delhi, who won the elections. So that's why hearing from authoritative scriptures, from the right source, from a bona fide spiritual master, coming down in a parampara, submissively, tad vidhi pranipatena pariprasnena sevaya, nothing wrong in having doubts. We are not expected to blindly accept whatever. The whole Bhagavad Gita is all about no questions. But in what mood, not out of arrogance, not out of pride, with the understanding that I am so small, I am so insignificant, being in this whole creation. Hear with an open mind, and then slowly we will be able to understand it's not blind belief.

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the context of the Bhagavad Gita, describing Arjuna's request to Krishna to position the chariot between the two armies and his subsequent bewilderment upon seeing his relatives and elders as opponents.

It's not superstitious belief. In the Bhagavad Gita, we are talking about paradigms. In the Bhagavad Gita, in the first chapter, we all know that how Arjuna requests Krishna to draw the chariot between the two armies so that he can have a look at who the opponents are. And seeing the opponents who are his own brothers, cousins, Bhishma, Dronacharya, Karna, his elders. We know that Arjuna became bewildered. He starts speaking, Krishna, my mind is reeling. I am unable to stand. My bow, Gandiva, is slipping from my hand. And eventually gives up his bow and says, Krishna, I will not fight.

#### compassion, dharma, war
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=1134
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This whole book is about war."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to classify Arjuna's reasons for not wanting to fight, starting with compassion, and addresses the common misconception that the Bhagavad Gita is solely about war.

Now if we look at the reasons what Arjuna gave why he should not fight. Broadly speaking, I did some classification. Broadly speaking, the reasons fall in five categories. The first category is, compassion. When Krishna went to Duryodhana, it's not that the war was the first option. Krishna went as a mediator. He went to Duryodhana's camp and said that, Pandavas, the Kshatriyas, the administrators, even without asking Pandavas, Krishna proposed to Duryodhana, you give them, you don't have to give the kingdom back. Just give them five villages. I am confident. Krishna spoke on behalf of the Pandavas. I am confident they will agree. They will be happy with it. Just give five villages. And we all know Duryodhana, very puffed up, very arrogant. He says, forget about five villages. I will not give land which can even fit on the tip of a needle. And we know how eventually, when all these attempts, somehow to reconcile and mediate failed, eventually war was the, it was Dharma Yuta. Many times people say, especially when we distribute Gita, and we tell that, Bhagavad Gita has instruction of the Lord. It gives you peace of mind. One time a person asked me, you are talking about giving peace of mind. This whole book is about war. It is about fighting. And that's how many people have a paradigm that we should not keep Gita in home. Fights will happen. Again, wrong paradigm. He says, you are talking about peace of mind. You will get all answers to all questions. Here we see, you know, fight is, the whole Gita is all about why you should fight.

#### compassion, family traditions, consequences of war
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=1273
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Maybe people like Duryodhan, out of arrogance, out of pride, they are not able to see the inevitable destruction, which will happen because of war."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to elaborate on Arjuna's reasons for not wanting to fight, including compassion for those who will be killed, the futility of enjoying a kingdom without family, and the fear of the destruction of family traditions and dynasty.

So then, when Arjuna was, Arjuna was in dilemma, Krishna told that, why he should fight as a Kshatriya. It's his duty. But Arjuna, out of compassion, he says that, maybe people like Duryodhan, out of arrogance, out of pride, they are not able to see the inevitable destruction, which will happen because of war. But why should I, who knows the moral principles, who knows this, why should I engage in this ghastly warfare, which will lead to so much of, so many people getting killed. Sort of compassion for his, all the people who are assembled in the battle, Duryodhan and all the other Kauravas, not only them, including his people on his side, Arjuna was reluctant. So one, was compassion. Second, it was not, in that sense, a very great, reason, morally speaking. Arjuna says that, what is the use of enjoying the kingdom, when, the people with whom we have to enjoy, you know, we all enjoy in company of our friends, relatives, family members. Man is a social being. We cannot enjoy alone. So this is, what is the use of kingdom, when all the family members, with whom we, should be enjoying, are not there. They are getting killed, in the whole battle. What is the use of that kingdom? That's the second reason, which second, group of reasons, or rather, bucket. The third reason Krishna, Arjuna talks about is, family traditions and dynasty, will get destroyed. And then he gives that how, women will become unchaste, able bodied men will get killed, and women will be unprotected, unscrupulous men will take advantage, of such women. Varna Sankara, unwanted population will come about, and subsequent to that, the family traditions and dynasty, all that will get destroyed. So fear for, the destruction of family tradition, is another reason why, Arjuna was reluctant to fight.

#### sin, moral principles, long-term consequences
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=1428
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna is not acting recklessly. He is considering dharma. He is considering papa punya. He is considering long term."
**Summary:** The speaker continues outlining Arjuna's reasons, including the fear of sin and the inability to decide what is best, emphasizing that Arjuna's considerations are morally and ethically correct from his paradigm.

Fourth reason which, Arjuna, few verses talk about, sin will overcome us. It will be very sinful. What is the use of enjoying a kingdom, which is tainted by the blood, of our own kinsmen. Duryodhana may not know, the Kauravas may not know, but we know the moral principles, Krishna. We know what is right, what is wrong. Why we should engage in this ghastly warfare, which involves so much of killing, which will have so much of sinful reactions. How will we enjoy that kingdom? And the last reason was, I am not able to decide, indecision. He is not able to decide, what is good for me, whether to fight, or to abstain from fighting, and live by begging. Now if we look at, Arjuna, all the reasons what he gave, from moral point of view, they are correct. From dharma, adharma point of view, they are correct. From papa punya point of view, they are correct. Just like people, sometimes act very recklessly, with no idea whatsoever. Lot of people now engage, have started eating non-veg. No, they don't bother about that, there is some sin involved, there is some killing involved, there is some pain involved, unnecessary killing involved. Who cares? But Arjuna is not acting recklessly. He is considering dharma. He is considering papa punya. He is considering long term. He is not short sighted. He is saying family dynasty will be lost. Varna Sankara will be born. He is far sighted. He is not looking at the immediate thing. He may get the kingdom, but long term what will happen? So from all perspective, if you look at moral, ethical perspective, paradigms, what Arjuna spoke was quite correct.

#### self-realization, soul, Krishna's teachings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=1753
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna started speaking about the right understanding of the self."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights that Krishna, instead of directly addressing Arjuna's concerns, begins to teach about the soul and the difference between the soul and the body.

Sometimes somebody asks question, he expects that, you know, family dynasty will, no, no, no, it will not happen, don't worry, family dynasty will not do this thing. Or somebody is saying, you know, you give a direct answer to the question. Arjuna is talking about compassion, no, on compassion you give a big lecture, no. Krishna started speaking about the right understanding of the self, the soul, how this body is like a dress, how the soul is a real thing. We know of, know how a woman whose husband was drowning in the water and another friend jumps in and catches hold of him, but somehow because of the current he flows away and what he comes back is a coat. Now he cannot be awarded that he has saved some part of him. If there was some award of saving the person, you cannot prorate of his, the coat is saved. The main thing, the real person is gone. What you have saved is this, the coat, which has, relatively speaking, no value. So likewise, Krishna starts speaking about the soul, the self, how we are different from the body, how the soul is eternal.

#### paradigm shift, body vs. soul, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=1844
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This paradigm that I am the body, it's a very deeply entrenched paradigm, more so in Kali Yuga, everyone is operating with that paradigm."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that Krishna's teachings aim to shift the paradigm from identifying with the body to understanding oneself as a spirit soul, highlighting how the world is geared towards sense gratification based on the false identification with the body.

None of the points which is raised by Arjuna is directly addressed, but Krishna starts talking about another important paradigm because of which all his doubts, all his dilemmas, all his confusions are all, so to say, automatically erased. And that's how Arjuna, after having that right understanding, empowered. This paradigm what we have of I being the body, I, understanding that I am the body, that's why Prabhupada says the first understanding in spiritual life is I am not the body, I am a spirit soul. This paradigm that I am the body, it's a very deeply entrenched paradigm, more so in Kali Yuga, everyone is operating with that paradigm. That's why the understanding of happiness is eat, drink, be merry. I am the body, your body means senses, senses means sense enjoyment. Your source of happiness is sense enjoyment. The whole world is geared up with that wrong paradigm that I am the body, my pleasure is bodily pleasure and all advancement, you see big, big Nobel laureates, scientists, people passing out from Ivy League universities, what are they engaged in? What kind of work they are doing? What kind of consulting these big four consultants these are doing? All about how to gratify the senses more royally.

#### spiritual happiness, material happiness, paradigm
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/TD3LivaIqMA?t=1970
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is stemming from a wrong paradigm that I am the body."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material and spiritual happiness, explaining that the pursuit of material happiness stems from the wrong paradigm of identifying with the body, while spiritual happiness comes from understanding oneself as a spirit soul.

It is stemming from a wrong paradigm that I am the body. Happiness means enjoy the senses, enjoy bodily pleasure and because we are spirit soul, intrinsic Anandamayo Bhyasat, it is an intrinsic desire to be blissful, to be happy and with the wrong paradigm it is it is very easy for us to get into that understanding that my happiness if I have to be happy means I have to enjoy. All of us had that understanding. We all had dreams, you know, when we grow up we will do this, this kind of a job, this kind of money, this kind of this thing. But after hearing Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, we got a different paradigm. From the paradigm of I am the body we are pursuing happiness that happiness is not lasting. It is not pure. It is not unending. It is illusory. There is a sense of happiness. We get a sense that yes, if I get this I will be happy. That's why everyone is so ambitious chasing different things. It is not that after they have achieved what they want to achieve they are satisfied because that satisfaction is elusive

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Amitasana Dasa I SB 3.26.61 I 06.04.2022
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### consciousness, false ego, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6GaaOZQc-wU?t=55
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as the consciousness is purified, the false ego disappears."
**Summary:** This segment explains the relationship between consciousness, false ego, and the process of purification through Krishna consciousness, highlighting the roles of various deities and the importance of worshipping Gaurachandra.

And the deity presiding over consciousness. Purport by Srila Prabhupada: the moon appeared after the appearance of mind, and this indicates that the moon is the presiding deity of mind. Similarly, Lord Brahma appearing after intelligence is the presiding deity of intelligence, and Lord Shiva who appears after false ego is the presiding deity of false ego. In other words, it is indicated that the moon god is in the mode of goodness, whereas Lord Brahma is in the mode of passion, and Lord Shiva is in the mode of ignorance. The appearance of consciousness after the appearance of false ego indicates that from the beginning, material consciousness is under the mode of ignorance, and that one therefore has to purify himself by purifying his consciousness. This purificatory process is called Krishna consciousness. As soon as the consciousness is purified, the false ego disappears. Identification of the body with the self is called false identification, or false ego. Lord Chaitanya confirms this in his Shikshastaka. He states that the first result of chanting the Mahamantra Hare Krishna is that dirt is cleared from the consciousness, or the mirror of the mind, and then at once the blazing fire of material existence is over. The blazing fire of material existence is due to false ego, but as soon as the false ego is removed, one can understand his real identity. At that point he is actually liberated from the clutches of Maya. As soon as one is freed from the clutches of false ego, his intelligence also becomes purified, and then his mind is always engaged upon the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The Supreme Personality of Godhead appeared on the full moon day as Gaurachandra, or the spotless transcendental moon. The material moon has spots on it, but on the transcendental moon, Gaurachandra, there are no spots. In order to fix the purified mind in the service of the Supreme Lord, one has to worship the spotless moon Gaurachandra.

Those who are materially passionate, or those who want to exhibit their intelligence for material advancement in life, are generally worshippers of Lord Brahma, and persons who are in the gross ignorance of identifying with the body worship Lord Shiva. Materialists like Hiranyakashipu and Ravana are worshippers of Lord Brahma or Lord Shiva, but Prahaladha and other devotees in the service of Krishna Consciousness worship the Supreme Lord, the Personality of Godhead. In our previous class we discussed that how Atma, being Amsha of the Lord. Amsha means part, just like a drop of water from an ocean is Amsha of the ocean. It has same quality that of the ocean. Likewise we also have the same quality of the Lord originally. The Lord is Sat Chit Ananda, and Damsha of the Lord also has the same quality, so pure consciousness is the original quality. What is pure consciousness called? Amsha of the Lord, the part and parcel of Lord, being Amsha of the Lord is qualitatively one with the Lord, is pure consciousness. What is pure consciousness called? Krishna Consciousness, he is experiencing Sat Chit Ananda. What is pure consciousness called? Krishna Consciousness, Krishna Consciousness. What is Krishna Consciousness? The living entity understands that I am Amsha of Krishna, Amsha of Krishna, clear? I am servant of God, I am servant of God, I am servant of God. I am part and parcel of Krishna, I am part and parcel of Krishna. Like we discussed that when this part, mamaiv amsho jiva loke jiva bhuta sanatana, Amsha of the Lord, but mana shashta indriyani with the mind and the senses it is battling. So this when Amsha wants to be the Lord, be the enjoyer, then...

#### contamination of consciousness, material existence, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6GaaOZQc-wU?t=443
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The goal of whatever we are doing in Krishna consciousness is how to purify our consciousness."
**Summary:** This segment explains how the desire to be the lord contaminates consciousness, leading to imprisonment in the material world and identification with the body, and emphasizes that the goal of Krishna consciousness is to purify our consciousness to its original state.

Consciousness that I am amsha, I am not the Lord, I am not the enjoyer, but that amsha when develops the desire to be the Lord, it is contamination in the consciousness comes in this material world, gets imprisoned. Just like a prisoner is imprisoned, how is that atma imprisoned in this world? He is imprisoned in the material body, you're right. And that atma which is sat chit ananda is imprisoned in the body which is asat. Is that clear how that original property of satchitananda gets distorted because you are imprisoned just like a prisoner is imprisoned in a prison house is shackled, is engaged. Likewise this atma which is sat chit ananda is engaged in a material body visit asat, it is temporary, ajit it's full of ignorance and nirananda this pain it goes through transformation, sixth transformation it decays, it becomes old, it dies, it smells. And original consciousness which is identifying with Krishna and mamsha of Krishna, now that soul starts identifying with the body. I am the body. When we identify with the body the natural consequences because the living entity wants ananda, how do we get pleasure? I am the body so bodily pleasure, sensual pleasure, is that clear? Because living entity wants ananda, sat chitananda, now because you want to experience ananda and we are identifying with the body then more bodily pleasure. So summon substance of how Krishna consciousness philosophy, the Bhagavad-gita Bhagavatam is so wonderfully explaining what is happening all what is happening in this world, different people are doing different things. They are on vacation, they are on boat ride, they're going on a cruise ship, they're going you know hill stations, they're going underwater scuba diving all about how to increase ananda and because I am the body it's all about sensual pleasure. So this is contaminated consciousness so Prabhupad says the goal of whatever we are doing in Krishna consciousness is how to purify our consciousness.

#### pure consciousness, material energy, modes of nature

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** Using the analogy of rainwater and a spark of fire, this segment explains how pure consciousness becomes contaminated by contact with material energy and the three modes of nature, and how the influence of these modes affects one's consciousness.

To purify it to the original state which is pure Krishna consciousness. Just like a drop of water, rain water, rain when it falls from the sky it is pure, but when it falls on the ground it becomes muddy. Proper gives another example, spark of fire is original quality of fire, but when the spark falls on the ground it extinguishes again it depends on where it falls. If it falls on water immediately, if it falls on green grass some time it takes, if it is on where on dry grass it probably retains its firing quality for some time. So when this jiva comes to this world it also this comes in contact with material energy which is comprised of comprised of three gunas, tamoguna is compared to water spark of water falling on on water. So when a person a pure soul gets predominantly influenced by tamoguna the entire consciousness is extinguished. It's like water falling in the gutter becomes like utter. Then when a person and the soul is influenced predominantly by rajamuna it is like the green grass, and when a person is influenced predominantly by sattva guna it is like the dry grass that some spark of consciousness pure consciousness is there and just like it falls in dry grass by fanning that thing it can again become fire. Likewise in sattva guna all the rules and regulations how satvik does that.

#### modes of nature, false ego, material existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6GaaOZQc-wU?t=885
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The root cause of our material existence is false ego."
**Summary:** This segment uses the analogy of acids and alkalis to explain how people influenced by different modes of nature interact, and explains that the root cause of our material existence is false ego.

So likewise when two sattva guna people come, no reaction. When two tamo guna people come, both are comfortable. When sattva guna and tamo guna people come together, reaction. Have you seen crows and swans together? And the swans you will not see them together. Swans you will find them where there is clear ponds, lotuses are blooming. Crows you will find where there is a dead body, garbage filled is there. So they are saying birds of same feather flock together. So tamasic people who are predominantly tamo guna will like tamasic activities, will like being in association of tamasic people. Sometimes that's why when we come to Krishna consciousness you know initially there is rubbing with family members, there is some kind of discomfort. So these gunas are influencing us. Tamo guna means that consciousness has become very cross and how do we identify with the body, dehatma buddhi. How do we identify because of what, because of false ego. So the thing is like this, soul is there, then the body is there, body has two components, what are the two components? Subtle body, sukshma sharira which is made of manas, buddhi and ahankara and there is a gross body which is pancha bhuta, earth air, water, fire and ether. So the soul identifies with this body and that identification is called ahankara of false ego. And the root cause of our material existence is, material existence is false ego.

#### false ego, identification, joy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/6GaaOZQc-wU?t=1042
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That identification is called what? False ego."
**Summary:** Using the example of the FIFA World Cup and personal experiences with cricket, this segment illustrates how identification with external things leads to emotional ups and downs, and explains that dismantling false ego leads to joy and freedom from hankering and lamentation.

These days football, FIFA World Cup is happening. Anyone watching? I will tell you later. So if you see the, yesterday I saw one video of a blind man, he is not able to see, but somebody is helping him through this thing. And when goal happens, that blind man is ecstatic. Seen the video? Whatsapp? So some of us who are not watching the World Cup, we are neither getting excited nor we are getting feeling sad. Yes or no? Our life is going on. Whether you score a goal or not, we are happy. But for the people who have identified with the team, just like the German team, they would have completely got messed up because they have identified themselves. That identification is called what? False ego. If you don't identify the team, team wins or loses, you have no, it doesn't affect you. It is very gross. Are you able to understand? Just like, I have told you this example earlier, when I was, before joining, I was very fond of cricket. Every ball, getting excited or getting sad? On a roller coaster ride, especially when Srikanth used to bat. But now, Indian team is playing, whether it is winning or losing, although I am in that sense, in Indian, it is not affecting me because the identification with the Indian cricket team is not there. So this false ego is Dehatma Buddhi, I am the body. When I am the body, then body, bodily relations, all that affects me. Affects my Sat, Chit and Ananda. That's why Krishna says, Brahma Bhoota Prasannatma. When that false ego gets dismantled, Brahma Bhoota, one more, understands that I am Brahman, I am spirit soul, I am amishwa of Krishna, then what happens, natural result of that? Prasannatma. He becomes joyful. Na Shochhati Na Kangshati. There is no hankering, there is no lamentation. So the chain which is binding us to this material world is identification with the body, identification with the bodily relations.

So to sum up what we have discussed today whole scheme of thing what is happening in this world atma is there atma is originally pure because it is amsa of the lord and being amsa of the lord it is sat chit anandam and that consciousness is called krishna consciousness chit the atma is aware that I am amsa of the lord atma is aware that I am servant atma does not have dehatma buddhi that is pure consciousness when the ichha come desires come to be the lord to be the enjoyer we come in this material world we come under the influence of material nature nature has three gunas what are the three gunas sattva guna raja guna and tamo guna based on the gunas influencing us our consciousness gets pulled down if we are influenced by tamo guna consciousness becomes very base it is almost like a animal goal of life becomes to enjoy the senses there is no higher goal because dehatma buddhi I am the body if I have to become happy means the body has to become happy then so that is our original state influenced by base consciousness so slowly samskara by chanting reading hearing by accepting the teachings of a spiritual master slowly we elevate in the modes and regain our pure consciousness. So this whole krishna consciousness movement is all about regaining our lost consciousness or pure consciousness pure consciousness is when we don't identify with the body and that identification with the body is called the false ego and krishna consciousness involves sublimating or removing this false ego and replacing it with the what is ego basically what is ego identity who I am false ego means I am the body I am the enjoyer I am the controller what is the real ego I am unsure of the lord I am not the enjoyer I am subordinate to god I am dependent on god so this purificatory process purifies our consciousness when this consciousness gets purified and real ego gets awakened then we are qualified to go back to the spiritual world. Real ego means identification with the lord unsure of the lord I am servant of god.
