#### devotion, ethics, materialism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=208
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So it is very important for us to understand why it is important to be a devotee of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the common argument that being a good, honest person is superior to being a corrupt devotee and emphasizes the importance of understanding why devotion to the Lord is essential.

I don't worship, but I am honest, I am a good human being, and very sarcastically, basically, they are referring to so-called devotees, so-called religious people who may be chanting, who may be doing all kinds of so-called devotional activities, may be corrupt, may be indulging in bribery, may be speaking lies. So referring to such devotees, they say that better I am. I may not go to temples, I may not worship, I may not chant the names of the Lord, but I am honest, I am truthful, I am ethical, I don't hurt anyone, I try within my means to help others. And they actually believe that that position is superior. So it is very important for us to understand why it is important to be a devotee of the Lord.

**Summary:** The speaker poses the question of why being a good human being is not enough and addresses the doubts that may arise when observing devotees with questionable behavior compared to seemingly virtuous non-devotees.

Why just being a good human being is not enough? Why? All the superficial, it appears that yes, what is the use of so-called devotion? A person is telling lies, he is getting angry. The other person is calm, composed, a good human being. Sometimes we can also get bewildered. So many years he is chanting, but his behavior is like this. But other person, he doesn't chant, he doesn't go to the temple, he doesn't do any so-called, he is very calm, he doesn't get angry, he is very loving, very kind, very gentle. So sometimes we may even doubt, you know, is the process really working? Am I following the right process?

#### devotion, material qualities, supremacy of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=335
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But the most important thing is a person should recognize the supremacy of Supreme Lord and recognize one's subordination."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the necessity of recognizing the supremacy of the Lord and one's subordination to Him, even if one possesses good material qualities, highlighting that a tinge of devotional service is essential.

So let's first understand why it is important, why a devotee, why even a so-called, just like here it says, the so-called process of attunement, even if they perform them very well, even if a person is assuming not a devotee of the Lord, but he is a very nice human being, even though no matter how good he may be, ornamented with some so-called good qualities in the material mundane sense, he is honest, he is loving, he is kind, he is gentle, he is soft-spoken, he is well-mannered, he doesn't hurt anyone. He is helpful. Although these qualities are considered very highly in this world, which is no doubt has to be there, these qualities are good, we are not, so to say, denying the fact that these qualities are very much wanted. But the most important thing is a person should recognize the supremacy of Supreme Lord and recognize one's subordination, that I am subordinate, I am Lord. Too many different, we are not talking about being a pure devotee where a person realizes that I am Krishna's. Whatever strength I have, whatever ability I have, whatever talent I have, whatever I have, my possessions, all belong to Krishna. That's a very elevated stage of consciousness. So being devoted means to understand the supremacy of Supreme Lord in some sense, may not be in a pure form, I belong to the Lord, I have to serve the Lord.

#### devotional service, material platform, mental speculation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=455
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Unless one has a tinge of devotional service, even the so-called atonement done very nicely has of no use."
**Summary:** The speaker asserts that without a touch of devotional service, even the best atonement is useless, using the analogy of an elephant bathing and then covering itself in dust, and emphasizes that goodness on the material platform is merely mental speculation.

So here it says, unless one has a tinge of devotional service, even the so-called atonement done very nicely has of no use. Even the same question Parikshit Maharaj asks, what is the use of Kunjara Sochavata, elephant taking bath in the river, getting cleansed, and then it comes out and again sprays dust on the body. So Prabhupada explains, when one is on a material platform, ae bhalo, ae manda, this is good, this is bad, is all mental speculation. There is nothing good in that sense. From absolute perspective, in the material world, so-called goodness is also, Prabhupada gives example of dry stool and a wet stool. It appears that dry stool, one side is dry, but both are stool. Why this element of recognition of the supremacy of the Lord...

#### supremacy of the Lord, ownership, analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=523
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "At the end, it is expected that you recognize that these items belong to that mall."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of shopping in a mall to illustrate the importance of recognizing the Lord's ownership, comparing it to paying for items after collecting them in a mall.

...and we subordinate to the Lord is important. We see people go for shopping in malls, go to a shop. In malls, we have unlike traditional shop where you stand across the counter and you tell the shopkeeper, he picks up the items and gives you. In mall, when you enter, you have a trolley, a basket, and then you keep walking in the mall freely and pick up whatever items you want. Traditional shops, you can't pick up items. You are, you know, you can't touch items. You tell the shopkeeper, he will come and give it to you. But in this case, you freely walk around in a shop, in a mall and pick up items. And in case your trolley gets filled, another, you know, salesperson will say, come up with another trolley. You want something more. So in other words, he wants you to pick up items. And after you have picked up all the items, whatever you want, at the end, it is expected that you recognize that these items belong to that mall. And because you want to take it with you, you have to pay the price. So at the billing counter, they quickly bill whatever items you have taken and you pay the price and you walk out and with great respect. And actually, these days, they are trained to treat customer like a king, customer loyalty. They are very happy that you have picked lot of items. And they will also say, thank you, sir. And they may even, again, depends, they may even want you to come back again to give them more business.

---
#### ownership, theft, analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=646
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "But what differentiates between a good customer and a thief is a person recognizes that it is his property, mall's property and since I am taking, I have to pay for it."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts a good customer with a thief in the mall analogy, emphasizing that the key difference is the recognition of ownership and the obligation to pay for what is taken.

But imagine a person who walks into that mall, he also picks up items, but not a full trolley or a basket, just two or three items. And he tries to come out of the mall without paying for it. In every mall, there is, there is a terminology they use in accounting. It's called shrinkage. Shrinkage means, you know, just like clothes, when you, you wash, it shrinks. So, in every mall, it's understandable that there will be some shrinkage. Shrinkage is because of people who go and flick some items. When we were in college, so, there were some friends from very rich family, very rich family. And not that they were dearth of scarcity of money, but it was adventure for them to go into a shop, pick up some greeting card, Archie's greeting card. And that love becomes even more, you know, a greeting card for your lover. And with great adventure, with lot of risk, you know, it really adds more rasa to the whole valentine day or whatever. So, lot of this to do that, flick the items. Now, you see, and if a person is caught, there was one Indigo pilot. It was in the news. It was in a very senior position, pilot. And he, you know, flights layover happens when they go to a particular, they've flown for 16 hours, 20 hours. So, they overnight stay there in the destination. So, this person in a duty free shop in the airport while coming, he picked up some item and did not pay for it. Which later on CCTV cameras captured that. And finally, he was removed from job. He was suspended and then probably he went to the court or something like that. That suspension was revoked and finally, net net was he lost his job. A pilot, very reputed airline, international flights and he lost his job. So, what's the difference between a person who takes items, these two, two different individuals. Both of them are picking up items from the shop. A person, trolley load or many many baskets of item picks up and he walks out. Whereas a person, just one or two items, one or two items, he's also picked up and he's trying to walk out without paying for it. He's called a thief. You see, many things are common. Both are walking in the mall. Both are looking at items. Both are wanting some items. Both are picking some items. Both are coming out of the mall. Many things are common. But what differentiates between a good customer and a thief is a person recognizes that it is his property, mall's property and since I am taking, I have to pay for it. Whereas a thief wants to enjoy that without recognizing that it is mall's and I have to pay for it. That's the essential difference.

#### devotion, materialism, intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=884
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without a tinge of devotion, devotion means being a devotee, devotion means to recognize the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that without a touch of devotion, which means recognizing the Lord, all abilities and talents are seen as one's own, leading to pride and even atheism, as individuals fail to acknowledge the source of their intelligence.

So, likewise, without a tinge of devotion, devotion means being a devotee, devotion means to recognize the Lord. If I have some ability, if I have some talent, if I have this body, if I am able to do something, if I am able to see something, if I am able to lift something, that ability is coming from the Lord. Yes, when a person is a pure devotee, he recognizes that I am not doing anything. The Lord is doing everything. I am just an insignificant jiva, a puppet in the hands of the Lord. Just like Prabhupada, when he is going to the West, he is writing, I am an insignificant beggar. I have no power to speak. I don't know how to convince them of your message. That is the consciousness of a pure devotee. But a materialist, so called, who is morally, ethically upright, he believes, he believes that his ability is his. If he is very intelligent, he believes that he is intelligent. In fact, if he is too intelligent, he becomes arrogant, he becomes proud. So called intelligent people at times even become atheists. Because they see that they are able to control things, they are able to comprehend things, they are able to analyze things, they are able to understand things, without recognizing that this intelligence is coming from the Lord.

#### devotion, humility, materialism

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

Some people have lot of abilities, some skills, some talents, and people glorify them for that. And they really believe that yes, they have done it, they have made it. You see any newspaper when some sports people, they won some trophy or some match or something like that. Invariably, there is some interview, man of the match, how did you do that? So they will invariably end up glorifying, I thought no, I should be very patient and I should stick on to it. And they glorify themselves. I thought like this, so I did like this and that's how it happened. But so many people think, so many people plan, but then things don't unfold necessarily based on your planning, your thinking. So here Prabhupada mentions that unless that tinge of devotion is there, the understanding is there that I am Lord's. Whatever I have, that is the essential difference between a materialist and a devotee.

So here it says, unless one has that tinge of devotional service, recognition of the Lord, all this so-called atonement, so-called karma kanda, so-called jnana kanda, it's all kevala vishara bhanda, it's all pot of poison. Ae bhalo, Ae mando, sakalara, this all is mental, at a mental level, this is good, this is bad, it is from absolute perspective, unless we bring Lord into picture, it is all bad. So our job, if we analyze what we are up to after having joined Prabhupada's mission, our job is, Prabhupada also mentioned that this consciousness, this recognition of the Lord is not an artificial imposition.

#### Krishna consciousness, dormant consciousness, materialism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1127
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "This Krishna consciousness, recognition that I am Lord's, I belong to the Lord, everything what I have is coming from the Lord, this recognition, this consciousness is already there."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna consciousness, the recognition of belonging to the Lord, is already dormant within everyone, and its absence in materialists is due to a covered consciousness, using the analogy of dormant sexual desire in children.

This Krishna consciousness, recognition that I am Lord's, I belong to the Lord, everything what I have is coming from the Lord, this recognition, this consciousness is already there. It's dormant in all of us and the reason why today we feel, we see lot of people who are atheist, who don't believe in God, who are very hard core materialist, because this consciousness is now covered. Prabhupada gives another example that just like sexual desire is dormant in everyone, in every child, you see children, boy, girl, they are playing, they are completely oblivious, they are not very, even so called gender conscious, because that desires have not got yet fructified. That's why that age is called adolescent age, 12 years, 13 years, 14 years, then it starts dawning on them. Again, some hormonal changes happen in the body. At a biological level. So how that these desires are dormant and then you don't have to necessarily go to college to learn about how to get attracted. It's dormant. Just like at the right age, in the right circumstances, at the right time, they start manifesting.

Likewise, this dependence on the Lord, this recognition of the Lord, this recognition that Krishna is the Supreme Controller, He is the Supreme Lord, this recognition also starts manifesting in the consciousness when the circumstances, the situation is right. And that's why it is said, Shunvata, Sukatha, Krishna, Punya, Shavana, Krishna, Shri Abhadra, all that Abhadras, all that dirty things which is actually covering our real knowledge, if we have to uncover, if we have to revive our pure consciousness, Krishna consciousness, recognition of the Lord, then the process which Bhagavatam is recommending is Shunvata, Sukatha, Krishna. We have to hear about Krishna from a pure devotee of the Lord.

#### Krishna consciousness, purification, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1340

There are a lot of people who are morally very upright. If they do some mistake, they feel guilty, they apologize, they beg forgiveness, at a material level. But the real purification does not happen. He still remains a non-devotee. So what we are doing is when we are hearing about the Lord, when we are doing different devotional activities, devotional service, our real consciousness, real understanding of Krishna is getting, the right environment is getting created for all the dirty things to be removed and pure Krishna consciousness getting awakened. And in Kali Yuga, as we all know, we have heard many many times Prabhupada emphasizing, the process for reviving our dormant Krishna consciousness is to chant the names of the Lord.

#### chanting, listening, Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1392
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Good that you are chanting, I hope you are also listening."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts an anecdote about a devotee assisting Prabhupada who was chanting outside Prabhupada's room, and Prabhupada told him, "Good that you are chanting, I hope you are also listening."

There was one time a devotee who was assisting Prabhupada. Prabhupada used to travel extensively, so there were devotees who would take turn to, you know, there were some secretaries and also devotees, temple he would go. And Prabhupada at times would be late in the night, early mornings would be transcribing correspondence, writing letters. So one time early morning about 2-2.30, one devotee was waiting for Srila Prabhupada just outside his room. Prabhupada was busy writing letters, responding to the letters. And there was very dimly, there was a light in the room, dimly lit, and this devotee was waiting outside, 2.30 in the morning. And the normal thing is, just in case Prabhupada wants something, he would, the devotee can assist. So while he was waiting, and it was early morning, so no noise, no sound, pin drop silence, Prabhupada dictating something on the dictaphone, and this devotee was outside the room, chanting, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, and it was so silent, that Prabhupada could hear what the devotee was chanting. So then at one point, Prabhupada called the devotee and said, good that you are chanting, I hope you are also listening.

#### chanting, listening, honesty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1499
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore I chant fast and I finish it in six and a half minutes."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about a devotee who was told that he doesn't chant the full mantra and finishes one round in six and a half minutes, and the devotee claimed that he chants fast to prevent his mind from wandering.

So momently if somebody tells us, you know, that are you listening, so immediately we get into defensive mode, and he says, I listen, I listen, I chant nicely. It happened with one devotee in, I was speaking to him, so another devotee came and told me about him that, Prabhu, I have gone with him in the vehicle, I have also heard him that he doesn't chant full mantra. Now this is very interesting, the conversation we had. So he said, doesn't chant full mantra, I have heard him many times, and he also says that his chant, one round gets over in six and a half minutes. And this devotee who told me, was relatively a younger devotee, junior devotee, so he didn't have the courage to go and tell him, Prabhu, your chanting is not, you are missing some words. So in one conversation, I called this devotee, not specifically for this, I was having a conversation, so I told, Prabhuji, little bit be conscious, this is what this devotee has told, please do not feel hurt about this thing, we all are here to purify. If someone points out our improvement area or mistake, it's good for us, otherwise how will we improve? So this devotee has told me that, you don't chant, you know, you are missing some words. So first response was, no Prabhu, in fact I have, you know, I very attentively chant, I am very conscious that I hear each word. So I said, okay, so how much time does it take? He says, I said, he said six and a half minutes. And he says, if I chant slowly, then my mind wanders. Therefore I chant fast and I finish it in six and a half minutes.

---
#### chanting, honesty, ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1620
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When I measure, then it becomes eight minutes, but when I chant attentively, then it is six and a half minutes."
**Summary:** The speaker describes how he conducted a test with the devotee to measure his chanting speed, and despite the results showing it took longer than claimed, the devotee insisted that he chants attentively in six and a half minutes.

So then I said, let's do one thing, I am not here to prove that, in fact it's very good if you are chanting very attentively. Let's do one thing, just a small test. So I said, okay, here is a stopwatch, chant nine mantras, full, at the speed what you are chanting, and let's measure. Again my mood was not to prove that he is wrong, just to help him. Sometimes we all have blind spots, and because of our ego, we don't want to accept our mistakes or, our shortcomings. Sometimes somebody points out, we feel angry, we get upset. Why did he complain? So I said, okay, so we wrote, I said, every time you complete one mantra, one, two, three, I will write down, and stopwatch is there, in milliseconds we will be able to measure. So I said, you chant at the fast pace, whatever you are chanting. So he started chanting, one mantra, two mantra, I was noting down, at nine mantra, we stopped the stopwatch, and nine multiplied by, how many is hundred eight, twelve, so whatever number of seconds and milliseconds were there, we multiplied by twelve, and it came to eight and a half minutes. So I told him, okay, see this, you chanted the same speed, but it's coming to eight and a half minutes, so that means, if six and a half minutes you are finishing, that means some words we are, so then, his response was again very interesting. He says, when I measure, then it becomes eight minutes, but when I chant attentively, then it is six and a half minutes.

#### chanting, honesty, self-deception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1732
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "See how mind is."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the devotee's unwillingness to accept the evidence and his insistence that his chanting is correct when he is not measuring, illustrating the mind's capacity for self-deception.

See how mind is, see I, yes, in fact, I am also conscious, I also take association, I am very, I want to improve my chanting, and I have been speaking to so many devotees, so Krishna Maharaj also came, I spoke to him also, I took tips on how to improve, and whenever I measure and chant, that time, it's eight minutes, but when I chant attentively, it's six and a half minutes, something like that. So he was not willing to accept. Later on, I told him, see this is, this is an improvement area, be more conscious, at times, you can record. He says, whenever I record, it is, it is correct. I am conscious. So it's a fact that, so Prabhupada called this devotee, he says, when you are chanting, you should also listen. So that devotee says, yes Prabhupada, yes, I do listen. After all, how can you chant without listening? That was the kind of, although he didn't say, that was his, how can you chant without listening? So Prabhupada says, yes, when you chant, you should listen very carefully.

#### chanting, purification, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1812
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The process in this Kali Yuga is to chant."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of listening carefully while chanting, as it is the process to remove all the dirt and revive our relationship with Krishna in Kali Yuga.

This is the most important thing. So many times, we are, the process which will remove all the dirt, the mirror which is covered with the dust, we are not able to see the Self. We are not able to see our relationship with Krishna. The process in this Kali Yuga is to chant. Sarvabhauma Bhattacharya, when he went to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, after he was touched by, he was transformed, in Chaitanya Chaitanya Mahaprabhu mentioned, so he goes and asks Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, of all the devotional items or activities, which is the most important? Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, of all the devotional activities, the most important is chanting the holy names of the Lord. No doubt, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu, Smaranam, all these are important. Prabhupada emphasizes that anyone, for that matter, may have examples of devotees, just by listening, just by chanting, just by praying, just by offering service, they got purified. But in Kali Yuga, specifically, Kirtanadeva Krishna Sya Mukta Sangha Param Rajet.

#### chanting, listening, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/rCW0YGss-eo?t=1903
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If the process is not properly followed, then, the process is to help purify our consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker stresses the importance of chanting attentively and listening, as these practices purify consciousness, and warns that neglecting the process will slow down purification and lead to feelings of stagnation.

So, very very important for us, many times, we may be, there are two different things actually, we may be chanting, but may not be listening. We may be chanting, but we may be skipping the beads. We may be chanting, but may not be chanting full mantra. And many a times, if we are not chanting attentively, if we are not listening, because that is a process, if the process is not properly followed, then, the process is to help purify our consciousness. That whole process will take a longer time. Nama Bhasa and Shuddha Nama. So, we will be in a stage where we are chanting offenselessly, and it will take time, purification will take time. And if you are not conscious, at times, because the progress is very slow, we may feel, we may feel that I am not progressing, or lusty desires are still haunting me, or so many years I have been chanting, still I am like that only, I don't see much difference. All these kind of thoughts can start cropping up in the mind.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Mahaprabhu Gauranga Dasa | SB 1.7.31-32 | 07.02.2016
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Bhagavata Purana, devotional service, material existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=219
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by hearing about this Bhagavata Purana, one can develop unalloyed devotional service unto Lord Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of hearing the Bhagavata Purana to develop unalloyed devotional service and extinguish the fire of material existence.

Let's have a short recap. In the first six chapters, Suta Goswami explained to the sages of Naimisharanya about the importance of this Bhagavata Purana, why it was compiled by Vyasadeva, and under whose inspiration it was compiled. In many places, Suta Goswami stresses this point: simply by hearing about this Bhagavata Purana, one can develop unalloyed devotional service unto Lord Krishna. That is the ultimate goal of our life. He says, simply by hearing this Bhagavata Purana, one can develop unalloyed devotional service unto Lord Krishna. That extinguishes the fire of material existence. That is in terms of shoka, lamentation, moha, illusion, bhaya, fear. This Bhagavatam is nothing but the full of glorification of the transcendental qualities of the Lord and His unalloyed devotees like Shukadeva Goswami, Parikshit Maharaj, Pralad Maharaj, Dhruva Maharaj, Yudhishthira Maharaj, Draupadi, Kuntidevi, and many others like Uddhava, Maitreya, Ranthideva. There are many great devotees who are glorified in the stream of Bhagavatam. The stream of Bhagavatam is nothing but the glorification of the transcendental qualities of the Supreme Lord and His unalloyed devotees. Simply by hearing the glories of the Lord and His devotees, one can develop attachment. That attachment leads to unalloyed devotional service to the Supreme Lord. It is said in Bhagavatam, hearing about the Supreme Lord is the only right medicine for the conditioned souls to get cured from all material diseases such as janma, mirthu, jaravyaadi, birth, old age, disease, death. Simply by hearing about this Bhagavad Purana, one gets cured from material diseases.

---
#### devotional service, religious principles, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=375
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The ultimate religious principle for all humanity is by which one can render unalloyed devotional service unto Krishna."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the purpose of existence as engaging in pure devotional service, the ultimate religious principle, and how Parikshit Maharaj attained liberation by hearing the Bhagavad Purana.

So, ultimately, our purpose of this, the purpose of our existence is to engage in pure devotional service. Suta Goswami explained in the second chapter, savipumsan parodharmo eto bhaktir hadokshajai hayato ki aprateta eyaatma supraseedati. The ultimate religious principle for all humanity is by which one can render unalloyed devotional service unto Krishna, that devotional service should be rendered without any motivation, without any interruption. One cannot engage in the devotional service expecting something in return. We cannot do some business with the Lord. That becomes desire, motive. I want some fruit in return. I am engaged in devotional service, therefore I want something in return. That is not called pure devotional service. Pure devotional service is engaged in the service of the Lord without expecting anything in return and without interruption. Constantly one should engage in the service of the Lord. After explaining this importance of Bhagavad Purana in the first six chapters, Suta Goswami began the case study. He explained from this chapter how Parikshit Maharaj heard this Bhagavad Purana attentively from Shukadeva Goswami and engaged in the service of the Lord and went back home, back to Godhead, even in that same very life itself.

---
#### Mahabharata, war, revenge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=525
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Then he decided to put an end to Pandava dynasty."
**Summary:** This segment narrates the events of the Mahabharata war, focusing on the unfair killing of Duryodhana, Ashwatthama's anger, and his decision to massacre the Pandava dynasty.

During the Mahabharata war, both Pandavas and Gauravas fought for 18 days. At the end of, almost at the end of 18 days, all people are killed, except few people. From Pandava side, five Pandavas and their sons, five sons, Krishna and Satyaki. From Gaurava sides, Ashrathama, Kripacharya and Krithavarma. Except these people, everyone was killed. So at the 18th day of war, under the direction of Lord Krishna, Bhima broke the thigh of Duryodhana by unfair means, because he hit on the thigh. Actually, according to the warrior's code, one should not hit anyone below the waist, but he cannot be killed by any other means. Therefore, Krishna told, should kill him by unfair means. Therefore, under the direction of Krishna, Bhima broke the thigh of Duryodhana. Duryodhana fell down and he was writhing in pain. That time, Ashrathama came and saw the precarious condition of his master, Duryodhana. And he heard from Duryodhana that he was beaten by Bhima by unfair means. After hearing this, Ashrathama became very much angry. Then he decided to put an end to Pandava dynasty. Then, thereafter, with help of Krithavarma and Krishapacharya, went to the camp of Pandava in the middle of the night, and he massacred the five sleeping sons of Draupadi. After that, he took the heads and went in the presence to his master in order to please Duryodhana, but Duryodhana was not pleased by seeing the killing of five sons of Draupadi. When Draupadi came to know, when she heard that her sons were massacred by, killed by, murdered by Ashrathama, she became very much afflicted.

---
#### annihilation, time, transcendental knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=883
**Verse References:** BG 8.16, BG 2.13
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To get out of this all annihilation, what one should do, Sw tych Goswami says, one should hear the nectarian pastimes of Lord Krishna, submissively."
**Summary:** This segment describes the four types of annihilation: occasional, total, final, and constant, and emphasizes that hearing the pastimes of Lord Krishna is the only way to escape this cycle.

In Bhagavatam, it is described there are four kinds of annihilation. One is called Naimithika. Second is called Prakthika. And third is called Athyantika. And the fourth one is called constant annihilation. Naimithika means occasional annihilation. Brahma, Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, we call Brahma as just, as infinite as the Vedic. With that we are called Brahma. That is called Kappa. One Yuga consists of Satya Yuga, Treta Yuga, Dwapara Yuga, and Kali Yuga. Put together, 43,20,000 Lakhs which are one day of Brahma. That means 432 crores of years is one day time of Brahma. That is called Kalpa. Kalpa means Lord Brahma creates the living entities. The day time of Brahma is called Kalpa. And Vikalpa, creation of Lord Brahma, that is called Vikalpa. And Mahakalpa, creation of all the material elements along with Lord Brahma, that is called Mahakalpa. So, during the night of Brahma, so this is the day time of Brahma's 432 crores of years and the same as the night time, 12 hours, 12 hours. So, during the day time of Brahma, one annulation happens. That is called occasional annulation. Occasional annulation. During this annulation, all the three planets meet with destruction. All the living entities enters into the body of Lord Narayana. Even Lord Brahma also goes to the body of Narayana. He sleeps there. When he wakes up, he comes out of the body of Narayana and all the living entities will come out and accept according to the different desire, accept different bodies according to the past desires in the previous night of Lord Brahma, previous day of Lord Brahma. This is called one annulation. And second is called total annulation. Total material annulation. That happens at the life time of Brahma. One day of Brahma is 864 crores of years. One life time of Brahma is like that Brahma will live 100 years. That means 864 crores multiply into 30, multiply into 12, multiply into 100. So, put together, Brahma will live for 3.11 trillion years. years, not million, not billion, trillion years. That is the lifespan of Brahma. At the end of the lifespan of Brahma, one annihilation happens. That is called cosmic annihilation. This is what is mentioned here, that annihilation. Cosmic annihilation. Then one more annihilation called Athyantika. A person, when he understands, when he attains the transcendental knowledge, he knows, he distinguishes between the relative truth from the absolute truth. He understands that I am not this body. I am the part and parcel of the supreme person that I have guarded. Therefore, he engages in devotional service and gives up this body. That giving up the body is called Athyantika. That means final annihilation. Annihilation of this material body. This is third annihilation. And fourth annihilation, at every moment, time, invariably transforms the body of all created beings. Krishna also says in Bhagavad Gita, Dei nosmini etade, kaumaram yavanam jara, tada tehyantra praptir, dheras tatra namu yati. The soul passes from youth, childhood to youth, youth to old age. Similarly, it passes at death and enters into another body. How? Because the time changes our body. What body we had yesterday, we don't have the same body now. What body we will have tomorrow, that is different. So time, invariably, transforms all the created bodies of this material world. This causes, this transformation causes the living entity, takes birth, and, again dies. Constant annihilation of birth and death. This is the fourth annihilation. So to get out of this all annihilation, what one should do, Sw tych Goswami says, one should hear the nectarian pastimes of Lord Krishna, submissively.

#### nuclear weapons, destruction, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=1714
**Verse References:** BG 16.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The invention of nuclear weapons are not meant to give peace and prosperity to the world, but to give disturbance and destruction to the world."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the dangers of nuclear weapons, comparing them to the Brahmastra, and highlighting how false ego can lead to global destruction.

So, Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, the demons, those who have no knowledge about God, they do not understand that God is in control. They take shelter of false conclusion and who have lost themselves and who have no intelligence. They engage in horrible things. They engage in horrible, unbeneficial work, meant to destroy the world. Demons engage in Mugra Karma that is meant to destroy the world. So, today, we are all proud of nuclear weapons. As soon as one country manufactures some nuclear weapons, they will go and test. As soon as it is successfully fired out, then they think our mission is successful and we are proud of inventing nuclear weapons. But at any time, the war can take place. Just like Hitler, one person caused the world war. Similarly, at any moment, because of false ego of one person, one president, the world war can take place and it will give, it will create total havoc in the world. So, the invention of nuclear weapons are not meant to give peace and prosperity to the world, but to give disturbance and destruction to the world.

#### atomic energy, supreme will, action
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=2261
**Verse References:** BG 18.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without his will or sanction, nothing can be built up or destroyed."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that atomic energy is not powerful enough to destroy the world and that everything ultimately rests on the supreme will of the Lord.

The atomic energy is not powerful enough to destroy the world. And secondly, ultimately, it all rests on the supreme will of the supreme lord, because without his will or sanction, nothing can be built up or destroyed. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, there are five causes to complete an action. Five factors to be considered to complete or perform an action. Arjuna, it is said in the Vedanta, there are five causes to accomplish an action. They are, Adhishtanam, the body. Kartha, the doer or performer. Karanam, the senses. Cheshta, endeavor. Daivam, the supreme will of the supreme lord. That is, will of the super soul within our heart. The body is meant to... The Adhishtanam is the body. And Kartha means the doer or the soul. The soul performs an activity that brings reaction. The soul, as God desire, therefore, it wants to perform an activity. When we perform some activity, naturally we get the reaction of the activity. And how to perform the activity? The soul uses the senses. Without senses, we cannot perform an activity. Even senses are used for that we should put an endeavor. Unless we put an endeavor, we cannot accomplish an action. Just like when we do a business, we should put an endeavor. Not just simply sit and roam and sleep. We should put an endeavor to complete the activity. Even though we may put an effort, still we may not be successful. Why? The supreme will of the lord is important. If the lord does not will, we may not attain success. Therefore, the will of... We should always depend upon the will of the lord.

---
#### devotees, opulence, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=2612
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna is so kind to such a devotee, he will not give any opulence. He will take away the opulence from the devotee."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how Lord Krishna often takes away opulence from devotees to keep them dependent on Him, as opulence can hinder devotional service.

But Lord Krishna is so kind to such a devotee, he will not give any opulence. He will take away the opulence from the devotee, because that opulence gives an amper to his devotional service. Therefore, Lord takes away the opulence of the devotees and leaving like a beggar. So, once Krishna said to Yudhishthira Maharaj, same thing, yasyam anugrunaami harishye tadhanu shanahi, tadodhanum tyacinti yasya svajana dukkha dukkhidam. Krishna said to Yudhishthira, my dear Yudhishthira, if at all I favour anyone, especially favour to anyone, first of all, I will take away all his riches and make him beggar, so that his wife, his children will kick him out. Thereafter, he will suffer misery after misery. When somebody is in misery, they will always depend on Krishna. Somebody gets some opulence, they will forget Lord. Therefore, Krishna makes sure that that devotee should always depend on me. Suppose some devotees, even though they are rich, they still can remember Krishna, just like a Sudama. Krishna awarded full riches to Sudama, much more than Indra, but still, Sudama did not forget Krishna. Our case is different. If Krishna awards all riches, we will completely forget Krishna. Therefore, Krishna will not give any riches. Even if you want to become rich, we will put an end to that. Krishna will make sure that that will not be successful.

#### material nature, Lord's supervision, annihilation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=2862
**Verse References:** BG 9.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The world can be destroyed only by His will, of the Lord, and not by the whims of tiny politicians."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that material nature operates under the Lord's direction and that the world can only be destroyed by His will, not by the whims of politicians.

Material nature's law works under the direction of Lord, as confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita. The Lord says there, that natural laws work under His supervision. The world can be destroyed only by His will, of the Lord, and not by the whims of tiny politicians. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, mayadakshinaprakati soyate sacharacharam hetunane kaunteya jagadvipari vartade. The material, he says to Arjuna, Arjuna, this material nature is working under my direction, producing all moving and non-moving beings. Under its rule, this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again. Although Lord creates everything, still, He is aloof from all the material activities. He does not do any activity. He is aloof from all the material activity. He is just only a director. He does not get involved in work, but his activities are conducted by the modes of material nature.

#### devotees, protection, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Jsn6hYxnR3c?t=3775
**Verse References:** BG 4.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Under the will of Lord, everything happens."
**Summary:** This segment concludes that everything happens under the will of the Lord, who protects devotees according to their surrender.

So, Krishna always hankering to save his devotees. He personally controls the loss of nature to protect his devotees. It is called Bhaktavatsala. He does not care for any other thing. The conclusion here is, under the will of Lord, everything happens. The so-called leaders neither they can create nor destroy it. If at all it has to be destroyed, it should be sanctioned by the will of the Lord. Lord gives protection according to one's surrender. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, As we surrender to Lord, Lord will also reciprocate. Similarly, if we sincerely follow the instruction of our Guru, chanting six notes, getting up early in the morning, attending reading, hearing, spreading the Holy Name, if we sincerely follow the instruction of Lord, sorry, spiritual master Prabhupada, Krishna is certainly bound to give protection from all sides. And later, Krishna will take us back home, back to Godhead. That is the will. He is always ready to give protection to his devotees. That is the purport of this verse.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Mahaprabhu Gauranga Dasa | SB 1.8.21 | 09.10.2016
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### Krishna's Inaccessibility, Material Disqualifications, Humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H7RcFIcdms0?t=509
**Verse References:** BG 9.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To approach Krishna is very simple. For that, one should be humble, simple, without pride."
**Summary:** This segment discusses how material assets like beauty, wealth, and intelligence can be disqualifications for approaching Krishna, emphasizing that humility and simplicity are the real qualifications.

Therefore, Kunti addresses Krishna, "You are adhokshaja, you cannot be understood by material senses." And tathā parama amsāna munīna amalāpana, bhakti-yoga-dhāna-ārthaṁ kadampasīmi-istariya. You have come to this world to propagate the devotional service and to the heart of transcendentalists who can distinguish between the matter and the spirit. After all, I am a woman, how can I understand you? And today, in this verse, she prays to Krishna as, "Let me offer my obeisance unto you because, let me offer my obeisance unto the Lord who has brought me to this world." Krishna has become the son of Devaki and Vasudeva and the boy of Nanda and the covered man of Vrindavana. So in this parapa says, the Lord being thus unapproachable by any material asset, Krishna cannot be approached by any material asset. The material assets in this world are beauty, knowledge, intelligence, wealth, all other things are called material assets. With this material asset, one cannot approach or understand Krishna. Therefore, it is stated in the Bhagavad-gita, janmaiśvareśutrasyevīr yedamāna madapumān naivārati abhidhātumvai tvāmakkiñśna gocharam. O my Lord, you cannot be approached by any material asset. You cannot be approached by those who have taken birth in the good parentage, good father and mother, the family of rich aristocratic family, or high education, or beauty. By all these things, by all this qualification, one cannot approach Krishna. Who can approach Krishna? Tvāmakkiñśna gocharam, akkiñśna gochara, one who is completely exhausted by the material things, material assets. He only can approach Krishna. If you ask any intelligent scientist, if you ask him to chant the holy name of Lord, he will think hundred times before even to chant. Why? Because for everything they depend upon their imperfect senses. Thus, they cannot realize Krishna. With this imperfect senses, by our experimental knowledge, we cannot approach Lord. Therefore, Krishna is called Adokshaja. Cannot be understood by, with our limited sense perception. Our senses are imperfect. With our imperfect senses, we cannot see our own form in the night time, without help of light. So how can we see the form of Krishna with this imperfect senses? We can see Krishna with the light of devotion, not with the imperfect senses. Once, one scientist asked a question. If the Lord is unlimited, whether he can create a mass that he cannot lift? Do you understand the question properly? Scientist put forward a question. If the Lord is unlimited, whether he can create a mass that he cannot lift? Very tricky question. If you say yes, he will be caught. If you say, he will be caught. If you say yes, he can create a mass that he cannot lift. See, he is not able to lift. Then how can you say that he is complete, he is unlimited? Anyway if you ask question, you will catch. Because the scientist measure God in terms of their intelligence. They do not know the fact that Krishna and this energy and this strength goes in parallel infinitely and there is no end to it. For example, I have a strength of lifting 50 kg weight today. Tomorrow also I can lift that much only. I cannot lift 100 kg weight. That is my limited, I have got limited strength. But Krishna, today if he can lift 1 trillion ton, the next moment he can create 2 trillion ton. Next moment he can go. So Krishna and his opulence, power, strength goes in parallel. There is no end to it. And they do not know this. Similarly if you ask any rich person, there are so many rich people out there in the country. Ask them to chant the whole game, they will not chant. How many rich people in the country or the world have taken up Krishna consciousness? Practically none. Because they cannot approach Krishna with a sincere feeling. When one has got this beauty, rich, wealth, knowledge, he will be intoxicated with pride. Therefore, he cannot approach Krishna with sincere feeling. So in fact, these all are disqualifications. What? Knowledge, beauty, taking birth in a good family, these all are disqualifications to approach Krishna. To approach Krishna is very simple. For that, one should be humble, simple, without pride. This is the real qualification by which one can approach Krishna.

---
#### Humility, Simplicity, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H7RcFIcdms0?t=884
**Verse References:** BG 13.8-12
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Bowing down before Krishna, means, we acknowledge the supremacy of the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of humility and simplicity in approaching Krishna, contrasting the receptiveness of young people with the complicated minds of older individuals, and highlighting the significance of bowing down as an acknowledgment of the Lord's supremacy.

Krishna himself states this in Bhagavad Gita, amanitthum adambhitthum ayamsa shantir arjavam acharyo upasvam sauchyam sthairyam artha vinigra. Five first items of knowledge, among twenty items of knowledge, first items of knowledge is amanitthum, humility, adambhitthum, without pride, arjavam, simplicity, acharyo upasvam, approaching a bona fide spiritual master. These are the qualifications by which one can approach Krishna. So many beautiful people are there in this world, many Miss Universe, Mr Universe. How many people are taken seriously about this Krishna consciousness? Nobody. So because these are the disqualifications to know Krishna. Once, Srila Prabhupada was asked by a reporter, "Swamiji, why in your movement all young people join, not the old people?" Because, when Prabhupada started this movement, the movement is full of all young boys, maybe twenty, between twenty to twenty-five, not forty, fifty and all. Srila Prabhupada responded, he said, "The young brains, the young people, are very simple, and they are ready to accept and follow whatever they are instructed. Therefore, they are able to take up the Krishna consciousness, and they practice seriously. Whereas the old people, the brain is getting complicated, and the ages goes more and more. If you ask any boy, come to temple, and ask him to bow down in front of Krishna, and ask him to chant the holy name of Krishna, he will do it without any hesitation, without any reservation. Bowing down before Krishna, means, we acknowledge the supremacy of the Lord. By doing this, we can immediately advance in devotional service. Therefore, whenever we enter a temple, we must bow down in front of deity, in front of spiritual master. By doing this, naturally, we will make advancement on the path of devotional service. Even when we meet seniors, superiors, we must bow down. Bowing down means, we accept the superiority of the others. By doing this, we can automatically develop knowledge. One who is not humble, he can never make advancement in spiritual life. So, if you ask any old person, or old professor comes to temple, and ask him to bow down before Krishna, and ask him to chant the holy name, he will stare at you. Because, he cannot. As the ages goes up, brain is getting complicated. Therefore, it is very difficult for them, to take up devotional service."

---
#### Devotional Service, Human Form of Life, Krishna's Mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H7RcFIcdms0?t=1064
**Verse References:** SB 7.6.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by rendering devotional service unto Kṛṣṇa, one can attain complete perfection, even in this life."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the rarity and importance of the human form of life for practicing devotional service from a young age, emphasizing Krishna's causeless mercy in descending to diminish demonic influences and bestow special mercy upon devotees.

Therefore, Prahlātmārā says, kaumāra āchṛt prajño dharman bhāgavata īya durlabhaṁ manasram janma tad api adruṁ artadam. Therefore, one who has got intelligence, he must use his human form of life, to practice devotional service, from the beginning of one's life. That is called, kaumāra stage. Kaumāra starts from sixth year. So, one should practice devotional service, from the tender age of the childhood. When he becomes old, when he becomes old, it is very difficult for us, to practice devotional service. Because, this human form of body, is very rare. durlabhaṁ manasram janma durlabhaṁ. After millions of years spending, we got this human form of birth. We cannot waste it, uselessly. durlabhaṁ manasram janma tad api adruṁ artadam. Although the human form of body, is temporary, but it has got artha, it has got meaningful life. Therefore, we should use this human form of body, to achieve perfection, by performing devotional service. Simply by rendering devotional service unto Kṛṣṇa, one can attain complete perfection, even in this life. Therefore, we need to take up devotional service, in the young age. Therefore, we should train our young children, at home, to make them, to become Kṛṣṇa consciousness, from the tender age. So that, they will attain perfection, even in this life. So, second point, Prabhupāda says, Kṛṣṇa, out of His causeless mercy, He descended to this world, to show, to bestow, His special mercy upon, His unalloyed devotees. And to, diminish, the demonic persons. So, Kṛṣṇa Himself states, in Bhagavad-gītā, there are three, purposes for which, Lord comes to this material world. What is the purpose? paritrāṇaya sādhunā vināśāya duṣkṛtām dharmasvam sthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge. Lord comes for, three purposes. First purpose, He protects devotees. Second, He vanquishes demons. And, re-establish, dharma, vedic principles, religious principles. These are the external purpose, for which, Lord appears, in this world. But there is, internal purpose, for which, Lord appears. What is that purpose? It is stated, in Bhagavad-gītā, anugrāya bhaktānā māṁśrūṁ dehaṁ āsitaḥ bhaṭate tādṛśa-kṛdā yāśutvā tathparu bhave tathparu bhaved. Kṛṣṇa, when He assumes the human form of body, human form of body, to bestow His special mercy upon devotees, and He performs wonderful pastimes, to attract the attention of all the people, those who hear about the activities of Lord, and become dedicated to Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa comes to this world, to perform His wonderful pastimes, to attract the attention of all the souls, suffering souls. Just as we all come to this ISKCON, why you have come, why you are sitting and hearing?

---
#### Krishna's Mercy, Incarnations, Accessibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H7RcFIcdms0?t=1271
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, Kunti adores Krishna, is more merciful than all other incarnations."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Krishna is more merciful than other incarnations like Rama, Narasimha, and others, because He is easily approachable by everyone, unlike the other incarnations who were less accessible.

Because, we heard about Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa's pastimes. Kṛṣṇa attracted. Kṛṣṇa attracted us, therefore, we are able to dedicate our life, to serve Lord. Kṛṣṇa's, not only attracted in personal features, is also very attractive, in all His transcendental activities. So, Kṛṣṇa, when He comes, He performs wonderful pastimes, to purify us. So, by hearing about the activities of Lord, naturally the conditioned soul gets purified, and He becomes eligible to go back home, back to Godhead. So, the main purpose of Lord coming to this world is, to take us back home, back to Godhead. This is the main purpose. And third point, Kunti says here, Kunti adores this incarnation of Kṛṣṇa, is more merciful than all other incarnations. Because, Lord appeared in many incarnations. For example, Lord appeared as Rama, and He was the son of King, from very childhood. Therefore, He could not be approachable so easily. But, when Krishna, when Lord appeared in the form of Krishna, He was easily approached by anyone, everyone. Therefore, Kunti says here, that this Lord, Kṛṣṇa, is more merciful than even Lord Rama. So, as we all know, very well, that Lord appeared in many forms. Like, fish, tortoise, boar, dwarf Vamana, and horse, and Narasimha, Rama, Krishna. Like, Lord appeared in many forms. But, so many incarnations came for a particular purpose. And, they were there for a short time, to execute the purpose. And, after that, they disappeared. Just like a fish, Lord appeared as fish, to save Satyavartha. Lord appeared as Kurma, to give the shelter to Mantrachala hill, when the demons and demigods were churning the, this one, the hill. But it, there was no support. So, it was drowning inside the ocean. Therefore, Lord appeared as a Kurma, to give the rest for that hill, Mantrachala hill. And, Lord appeared as a boar. He came, to lift the earth, to place it in its arbitrary portion. And, to kill demon Iranyaksha. After that, Lord disappeared. Lord appeared as a Narasimha, to kill Iranyakashipu. When the Lord appeared in its terrific form, to kill Iranyakashipu, after killing Iranyakashipu, even the great demigods like Lord Brahma, Lord Shiva, could not approach Narasimha. Even, Lakshmi could not approach Narasimha. Then, what to speak of others, how they can approach Lord? When the Lord appeared as Rama, right from the beginning, he was the king's son, and he was the prince. So, people in general, it's very difficult to meet Lord Rama, because he was living inside the palace. He would not come out, so often. He was living inside the palace. Somebody want to meet Lord Rama, he had to take permission, and wait in a queue for long time. And, he can meet after that. So, to meet Lord Rama itself, they would have spent so much time. Then, where is, what is, where is Brahma? Where is Brahma? What is,

---
#### Krishna's Accessibility, Vrindavana, Pure Love
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H7RcFIcdms0?t=1496
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, Kunti adores Krishna, is more merciful than all other incarnations."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the accessibility of Krishna in Vrindavana with the difficulty of approaching Lord Rama, highlighting how Krishna freely associated with cowherd boys and gopis, demonstrating pure love and making Him more merciful than other incarnations.

where is the question of associating with Lord Rama? It's very difficult. Just like, when we want to meet our CM, Chief Minister, or Prime Minister, so many months we have to wait, to get an appointment. We have to meet his secretary, and explain the purpose of a visit. So many things are there. He may give the appointment or not, there is no guarantee. Because, that is their exalted position. It's not so easy to meet them. That is the position of Rama. But, when Lord appeared as Krishna, he immediately left his father, mother, King Vasudevan, Queen Devaki, and went to Vrindavana, to play an ordinary part of a covered boy, in Vrindavana. So that, everyone could approach Krishna easily. People in the Vrindavana, like particularly covered boys, would eat with Krishna, would dance with Krishna, would play with Krishna, would sleep with Krishna, would talk with Krishna. Sometimes, Krishna would be defeated by his friends, and Krishna had to carry his friends on his back. When Krishna brings lunch from home, and he would share with his friends, and would personally feed cows and cows, and monkeys. Sometimes, Krishna would make monkeys to dance, and he would dance along with the monkeys. So, everyone was attracted to Krishna. Everyone could easily approach Krishna. Everyone could associate with Krishna. Sometimes, gopis would make Krishna to dance, according to the tune. Krishna would dance. Sometimes, they would put a sari for Krishna, and make him to dance, according to their music. Krishna would dance. The yogis are trying to understand Krishna, by performing thousands of years in meditation. But still, they fail to capture Krishna. Whereas, the gopis, by their unalloyed devotional service, they would capture Krishna. Krishna would dance according to the tune of gopis. This is called pure love. So, in Vrindavana, everyone could associate with Krishna. Krishna was more approachable by anyone. Therefore, Kunti adores Krishna, is more merciful than all other incarnations.

---
#### Devaki and Vasudeva, Krishna's Choice, Past Incarnations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/H7RcFIcdms0?t=1646
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna, personally selects, His own father and mother."
**Summary:** This segment explains why Kunti glorifies Devaki and Vasudeva, revealing their exalted position as Krishna's chosen parents due to their past austerities and devotion, and highlighting Krishna's personal selection of His parents in each incarnation.

And fourth point, here, Kunti's address, Krishna appeared as the son of Vasudeva and Devaki. Why Krishna appeared as the son of Vasudeva and Devaki? What is the reason? Here, Kunti adores them. Kunti glorifies Vasudeva and Devaki, because their position is very exalted. Who are these Devaki and Vasudeva? When Krishna appeared in the prison house of Kamsa, he appeared in his 400th form, with all Chanku Chakra, Gara, Patma, in front of Mother Devaki. And Mother Devaki, when she saw the Lord appeared in his 400th form, she expressed her motherly affection, "O Lord, this form will be understood by the Yogis. This form is perceived by Yogis in their meditation. No, you appeared in this 400th form. When my brother comes to know that you appeared, he will come and kill you. Therefore, I request you, please assume the form of an ordinary child." So, she offered wonderful prayers also to Lord. Then after hearing everything, then Krishna, means Lord Vishnu, revealed to Devaki and Vasudeva, "My dear mother and father, actually in the millennium, in the Swayambhu Manu millennium, the first Manvantara, my father, Vasudeva, was one of the Prachapatis. And his name was Suthappa, that time, in the millennium of Swayambhu Manu. And you are his wife, your name was Prishni, that time. Both of you were also, ordered by Lord Brahma, to create a progeny. So, both of you went to forest and underwent severe tapasya. You control your senses and fix your mind. And tolerate, could tolerate, all kinds of material, this one, disturbance, like a rainy season, winter, wind. So, like that, even scorching sunshine, you underwent severe tapasya, by undergoing all these things, problems. You fixed in your, devotional service unto me. And you performed, devotional service for, 12,000 years, by the calculation of demigods. And you would eat, only the dry leaves, that had fallen to the ground." 12,000 years, they performed, by eating, only the dry leaves. That also, one year, one day of demigods is equal to, how many days? One year. One day of demigods is equal to, almost one year. So, 12,000 years of demigods, they have performed tapasya. That means, 12,000 years, into 360. We put, how many years? That many years, they underwent severe tapasya, by eating only dry leaves. Just imagine, once a two week, we are not able to fast, on Ekadashi days. The name of Ekadashi, we eat more on the day. So here, they underwent severe tapasya, to beget, Lord as their son. That time, because they fixed their mind, upon Krishna, and they became purified completely. And Krishna, was satisfied by their devotion, and the tapasya. Then Krishna, as in the form of Vishnu, appeared in front of them, and said to, Sutapa and Prishni, "I have been very much pleased, by your tapasya."

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Srimad Bhagavatam | Sri Mahotsaha Chaitanya Dasa | SB 1.19.1 | 17.11.2024
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### repentance, devotee qualities, sin
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P4vGNGx0lFk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Accidental sins committed by a devotee are sincerely regretted and by the great Buddha."
**Summary:** The speaker reads from Srimad Bhagavatam, discussing a pious king's regret for mistreating a brahmana and how repentance burns away sins committed by devotees.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 1, Chapter 19, Text No. 1. A pious king regretted his accidental improper treatment of the powerful brahmana who was faultless. Such repentance is natural for a good man like the king, and such repentance delivers a devotee from all kinds of sins accidentally committed. The devotees are naturally faultless. Accidental sins committed by a devotee are sincerely regretted and by the great Buddha. In the face of the Lord, all sins unwillingly committed by a devotee are burnt in the fire of repentance. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

#### Kali Yuga, ISKCON, unity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P4vGNGx0lFk?t=120
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So Prabhupada built ISKCON when Srila Prabhupada built a house in which the whole world can live."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the conflicts of Kali Yuga at various levels, from personal to international, and how ISKCON, based on Srimad Bhagavatam, provides a practical demonstration of peaceful coexistence, uniting people from diverse backgrounds for Krishna Consciousness.

So the last week, one of the important news has been that the next year in 2025, early 2025, there is going to be a world's cricket tournament, Champions Trophy, ICC Champions Trophy, which is supposed to be hosted by Pakistan and to be played in Pakistan. So India has raised objections that they don't want to send their team to Pakistan due to security reasons and also due to the tensions, political tension, which is existing, prevailing between the two countries. And Pakistan is adamant, they want the tournament to happen there only and they are demanding. They are demanding ICC, that it should go on whether they come or don't come, whether India participates or not participates. But if India does not participate, then there will be a huge revenue loss because everyone, India is the most populated country in the world and has the most crazy cricket fans, everyone wants to watch Indian team. If India doesn't play, the viewership goes down. So ICC is in trouble. So why I am sharing this is because we can see there is this fight happening at an international level between nations. So the nature of Kali Yuga is, Kali Yuga is even famous as the age of quarrel. So there exists fight at so many levels on Kali Yuga, between persons to persons, society to society, community to community, and even nation, state to state. We also know how here, Kannada, we have to, Kannadigas want people to talk Kannada. Others don't want, they don't want to learn Kannada, so there is tension. And nation to nation, as we have seen here. And therefore, it is so difficult for people to live together, even within a family, you know, or for friends, and for communities, to live together, accepting each other's differences and to stay without fighting is such a difficult thing. Yet, Srimad Bhagavatam, by its existence, exemplary devotees, narrations of the exemplary devotees, has given us practical demonstration of how we can live peacefully in this world, even in the current age of Kali, if we practice the principles mentioned in Srimad Bhagavatam. And based on these principles of Srimad Bhagavatam, only Srila Prabhupada started the International Society for Krishna Consciousness. It is an international society, coming together of the devotee people from all parts of the world, all race, caste, creed, religion, colour, coming together for the purpose of Krishna Consciousness and staying together. So, it is also said, ISKCON is also famously it is coined that Srila Prabhupada built ISKCON when Srila Prabhupada built a house in which the whole world can live.

#### ISKCON, devotee association, initiation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P4vGNGx0lFk?t=352
**Verse References:** SB 4.12.48
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The international society for Krishna consciousness has been organized for this purpose."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Srila Prabhupada's purpose for starting ISKCON, referencing Srimad Bhagavatam 4.12.48, emphasizing the importance of devotee association for understanding the Lord's pastimes and the opportunity for anyone to become twice-born through initiation.

Prabhupada in fact defines the purpose of starting ISKCON. Why did he start ISKCON? It is mentioned in Srimad Bhagavatam 4th Canto 12th Chapter 48. In the context of Drona Maharaj and Drona Maharaj's character and activities. In the end of that narration where Maitreya Rishi is narrating this. He says, Prabhupada says in the purport. It is said that only in the association of devotees can one understand the importance of the character and pastimes of the supreme personality of Godhead or his devotees. It is especially recommended that Drona Maharaj's character be discussed in a society of the twice born, which refers to the qualified Brahmanas, Kshatriyas and Vaishyas. One should especially seek the society of Brahmanas who are elevated to the position of Vaishnavas. Thus discussion. The discussion of Srimad Bhagavatam which describes the character and pastimes of devotees and the Lord is very quickly effective. Then Prabhupada states the purpose. The international society for Krishna consciousness has been organized for this purpose. What is that purpose? In every center of this society, not only in the morning, evening or noon. But practically 24 hours a day. There is continuous devotional service going on. Anyone who comes into contact with the society automatically becomes a devotee. We have actual experience that many Karmis and others come to the society and find a very pleasing and peaceful atmosphere in the temples of ISKCON. In this verse. The word Dvijanmanam means one of the twice born. Anyone can join the international society for Krishna consciousness and be initiated to become twice born. So Prabhupada opened up this opportunity for all of us. For anyone who agrees to practice the principles of Krishna consciousness and be in the association of devotees to become initiated and to become twice born. And that is the purpose of ISKCON.

#### devotee association, Kali Yuga, spiritual progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P4vGNGx0lFk?t=529
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If one thinks that one can advance in devotional service or Krishna consciousness by practicing alone, one is living in hallucination."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the necessity of devotee association for spiritual growth, stating that advancing alone is a hallucination. He then shares a story of a devotee wanting to leave ISKCON due to others' faults, to which Prabhupada responded that only he is a devotee, and the rest are trying to be devotees.

So therefore we see in the association of devotees. One can stay together despite the nature of Kali Yuga which is full of quarrel and fight. So it is possible to mature in devotional service only in the association of devotees. Therefore there is one more statement of Srila Prabhupada where Prabhupada says that if one thinks that one can advance in devotional service or Krishna consciousness by practicing alone. One is living in hallucination. To think that I can advance by myself living alone. I don't need any association of devotees. That means hallucination. It's only imagination. It is possible only in living in association of devotees. But then when we live with some devotees. When we live or when we stay with different people. It is natural that there will be some kind of friction. Some kind of disagreement. Some kind of you know fault from one person to other person. And there can be kind of disturbance between each other. So it is common. Even in the society of so called devotees. Even within ISKCON. It happened. Once one devotee came to Srila Prabhupada and told Prabhupada that I want to leave this movement. I want to leave the association of devotees. So Prabhupada was very surprised. He was very distressed. He asked why? What happened? Why do you want to leave? So he said. This devotee. He said this to me. That devotee he is so harsh. So cruel. This devotee she is so mean. How can devotees behave like this? That's why I don't want to stay with them. Then Srila Prabhupada said. Who is devotee in ISKCON now? Only one devotee is there me. All of you have just shaven up. Wearing robes. Wearing tilak, kanti mala. And acting like devotees. So actually you are trying to be devotees. So you are not actually devotees. You are trying to be devotees.

And therefore when we are practicing devotees. When we are sadhakas. It is natural that we are not perfect. We are all patients. ISKCON is a hospital. The difference between those who come to ISKCON and those who don't come to ISKCON is they don't know that they are deceased. Those who don't come. Those who are coming regularly and practicing everything. They know we have got the disease. So we are taking treatment from the doctor. And then those who are little seriously infected. They will get admitted also. Like we are all admitted. Full time. Even more means intensive care. ICU. So actually we are all practitioners. People make mistake generally. When they see devotees, kanti mala, tilak. This thing they think that oh he is sadhu. He is perfect. Sadhu bhushana. Sadhu means reservoir of all good qualities. It is true. It should be like that. But we are not pure. We are practicing. We are becoming pure.
