#### devotional service, material world, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P4vGNGx0lFk?t=774
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although the material world is blazing fire, to a devotee it appears full of pleasure."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on Dhruva Maharaj's example, explaining that devotional service in the association of devotees is the cause of further devotional service, making the material world appear full of pleasure to a devotee.

So therefore Prabhupada writes further in one more Srimad Bhagavatam purport about this. In the same context of Dhruva Maharaj's Leela Prabhupada says. Anyamadi philosopher may question us. You may be very happy in the association of devotees but what is your plan for crossing the ocean of material existence? Dhruva Maharaj's answer is that it is not very difficult. He clearly says that this ocean can be crossed very easily if one simply becomes mad to hear the glories of the lord. For anyone who consistently engages in hearing the topics of the lord from Srimad Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam and Chaitanya Charita Amrita and who is actually addicted to this process just as one becomes addicted to intoxicants it is very easy to cross the nescience of material existence. The ocean of material nescience is compared to a blazing fire. Samsara Dhabanala. But to a devotee this blazing fire is insignificant because he is completely absorbed in devotional service. Although the material world is blazing fire, to a devotee it appears full of pleasure. Vishwampurna Sukhayate. So the purport of this statement by Dhruva Maharaj is that devotional service in the association of devotees is the cause of the development of further devotional service. By devotional service one is elevated to transcendental planet Goloka and Dhavan and also there, there is only devotional service. So for the devotee both in the material and spiritual world is the same. There also devotional service, here also devotional service. Devotional service does not change but the devotee changes, the devotee improves.

---
#### devotional service, spiritual progress, devotee association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/P4vGNGx0lFk?t=892
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, it is possible to mature in devotional service only in the association of devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of an unripe mango becoming ripe to explain the progression of devotional service, emphasizing that it matures only in the association of devotees. He concludes that ISKCON was created so that devotees in the unripe stage can grow together.

Prabhupada gives wonderful example here in the purport. The example of mango can be given here. If one gets an unripe mango, it is still a mango. And when it is ripe it remains the same mango. But it has become more tasteful and relishable. When the mango becomes ripe it will be sweet, delicious, tasteful, relishable. Similarly, there is devotional service performed according to the directions of the spiritual master and injunctions and regulative principles of Shastra. That is Vaidhi Bhakti or Sadhana Bhakti. And there is devotional service in the spiritual world rendered directly in association with the Supreme Personality of Godhead. But they are both the same. There is no change. The difference is that one stage is unripe the other stage is ripe and more relishable. Therefore, it is possible to mature in devotional service only in the association of devotees. It is said that only in the association of devotees one can understand the importance of the character and pastimes of the Supreme Personality of Godhead or his devotees. So, therefore we are all unripe mangoes. So, we are all devotees but not pure devotees. We are in the process of becoming pure devotees. As long as we are following the instructions of the spiritual master, following the rules and regulations and trying to advance then we are in the path to become ripened. So, this is important. So, while we are in the path of becoming pure devotees there will be in the unripe stage there will be if you pluck an unripe stage if you taste an unripe mango what will happen? It will appear sour. Therefore, our relationships among devotees sometimes may appear sour. You are trying to bite an unripe mango. So, it will be sour. So, therefore we have to be careful and that is also one of the reason why Srila Prabhupada created ISKCON so that even unripe stage even the devotees in unripe stage, mangoes in unripe stage also can differentiate together so that together they can grow.

**Summary:** The spiritual world is managed by the Lord's spiritual (internal) energy, while the material world is managed by the Lord's external (material) energy.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/bvk1HhxuiCg?t=234
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The spiritual world is eternal (sanatana) and free from birth, death, old age, and disease.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### Symptoms of Material Modes | BG 14.10 - 14.14 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### material modes, competition, influence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Combination of all these modes are influencing us. In this way there is always competition for supremacy."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept of the three material modes (goodness, passion, and ignorance) and their constant competition for dominance, influencing our actions and perceptions.

Symptoms of material mode. What are the symptoms of a person who is in Rajoguna, Tamoguna, and Satyoguna? Sometimes the mode of passion becomes prominent, defeating the mode of goodness, O son of Bharata. And sometimes the mode of goodness defeats passion. And at other times the mode of ignorance defeats goodness and passion. Combination of all these modes are influencing us. In this way, there is always competition for supremacy. When the mode of passion is prominent, the modes of goodness and ignorance are defeated. Same is the case with ignorance. When ignorance is prominent, the influence of ignorance is prominent, the goodness, the sense of goodness a person has, and passion gets defeated. It overpowers. One can develop. This is what progressive life is all about. One can gradually with practice, by following the principles, following the recommended process, one can come out of the influence of lower modes. The prominence of some certain mode of nature is manifested in one's dealings, in his activities, in his eating.

---
#### taste, material modes, food
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=104
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "You will also notice, depending on what modes influencing us, your taste varies."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa shares a personal anecdote about traveling to Hong Kong and experiencing a change in his taste preferences due to the influence of the environment and the modes of nature, highlighting how our taste varies depending on the modes influencing us.

Many, many years back I had gone for some work to Hong Kong. And you know when you travel, you are eating, thinking about food. When you travel, your eating thing gets affected to some extent. And when I came back, I could really sense, because there where I was staying, you know, you have to manage with some makeshift arrangements because you don't have... So generally what we do is when we travel, we, you know, pack something or try if we can manage with some fruits. Again, depends on how many days we travel. You cannot be on fruits for 10 days at a stretch. So I remember, you know, and it gets affected. You get affected by the environment, the situation, people whom you are dabbling with, all that. And because it was a long stay, it's difficult to manage. So, you know, somebody had packed some pickles. And when I came back, I could sense that the desire, that inclination to have pickles increased drastically. You will also notice, it's a very fine thing, you will also notice, depending on what modes influencing us, your taste varies. If it is sattva guna, you will like to eat a particular kind of foodstuffs. If it is raja guna, another kind of masaledar, very fried, very this thing, chatka, this, you know, chatpata.

---
#### tamo guna, food, culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=202
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "What is so obnoxious for us, which we cannot even think of, is a delicacy for them."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts a vivid experience in China, observing the local cuisine featuring live animals and insects, illustrating how cultural and modal influences shape our perception of what is considered palatable or repulsive.

And if it is in tamo guna, I think I have shared this, from Hong Kong, we went to China. So there was one place, that hotel, the USP of that hotel is everything is cooked fresh. And in China, you know what is cooked. So outside that hotel, there is, you know, all kinds of reptiles, crabs, fishes, prawns, snakes, worms, all live, fresh. And morning, you know, we just stepped out of our place where we were staying, so that hotel was there, and you know, three of us, at a distance, we are wondering, what are these people eating? I heard a saying that, in China, whatever moves on the ground, except for, you know, cabs and trains, they eat. Whatever moves in water, except submarines and ships, they eat. Whatever flies in the sky, except for airplanes, they eat. So, you know, the person, he took out a snake, live snake. Somebody came in, the customer came in. So the USP is that, you know, you can, as you enter the hotel, this thing, you can order what you want to eat. So you want to eat this crab, or you want to eat this prawns. So they will take out live, it's cooked fresh, and served to you. So we were, from a distance, watching all this, and they took a snake, somebody had ordered that snake, and the person, maybe, covered the face with some kind of a bag, and dum, dum, two times he did a, you know, that, this thing. The snake got caught, and then chopped. Maybe that's the way of killing, I don't know. So we were watching, and you know, completely dazed, what's happening, and what people are eating. Sometimes, you know, when you cook some vegetables, you get some worms, green worms. There was one tray full of those green worms. And all these worms are moving. And it seems quite a delicacy there. Quite a cost leader. So we were watching, from a distance, what all these people are doing, what all these people are eating. And this person who is, you know, dressing up the whole thing, who is preparing for cooking, so he is also watching us. And it went on for five, ten minutes. And this person is not understanding as to why we are not entering the hotel and ordering something? Why are we just contemplating on what to eat and standing there and not able to take a decision? And after five minutes, he says, he basically was asking us, which one you want? That's the taste. That's the taste.

---
#### culture, taste, modes of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "What is obnoxious for one person, is nectar for another person."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about Srila Prabhupada's experience in Burma with a local delicacy called 'nafi,' illustrating how cultural conditioning and the modes of nature influence our perception of taste and what we consider palatable.

What is so obnoxious for us, which we cannot even think of, is a delicacy for them. This reminds me of a story. Prabhupada, when he went to Burma, in Burma, there is a delicacy called nafi. You can check on the net. It's a very nice drink. I'll tell you how it is prepared. It's called nafi. So what happens is, when a person brings non-veg to their home, so not everything can be cooked, not everything is eaten. There are some parts of the, you know, which cannot be cooked, intestines or maybe, I don't know. So what they do is, instead of throwing away, those parts are taken and dropped in a jar, earthen jar, pitcher. And the next day cooking happens. Every day they cook non-veg. So every day something gets deposited in that jar. And it keeps accumulating. And you know, meat doesn't stay, it decomposes. So they keep adding, keep adding, keep adding, keep adding, and it starts decaying, decomposing, decaying. And you know, when it decays, all that liquid oozes out. After three or four months, they break open this jar, take out all that stuff, and strain that liquid. And that liquid is called nafi. And especially when the festivals happen, you invite the guests and all that. Just like, you know, you have this, you know, after all this, wine also depends on how much time you have, how old it is. So, six months old nafi. One year old nafi. And little by little, sip by sip, it is served and they take it. So that's the taste.

---
#### taste, modes of nature, spiritual vs material
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=513
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker continues illustrating the influence of the modes of nature on taste by recounting an incident where neighbors in Burma complained about the 'bad smell' of desi ghee used in the temple, highlighting the contrast between material and spiritual perspectives.

So, one day, the temple in Burma, the devotees were there, Prabhupada, so they were cooking a feast, and they used, on that particular day, desi ghee, pure ghee. And when they were cooking puris in ghee, all these neighbours, you know, what are you doing? What is this bad smell coming? They started complaining. For them, desi ghee is intolerable. Bad smell. Again, the influence of moths. What is obnoxious for one person, is nectar for another person. What is nectar for one person, is obnoxious for another person. What is night for a materialist, is a day for a spiritualist. What is day for a spiritualist, is a night for a materialist. And why so much variety? So much variety. Somebody is eating snakes. Eating dogs. Influence of moths. That's the taste. That soul is under the spell of that moth. That soul is under the spell of that moth.

#### modes of nature, Krishna consciousness, transcendence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=577
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, one who is actually intent in advancing in Krishna consciousness should strive to come out of the influence of these lower modes and transcend the modes."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of transcending the lower modes of nature (ignorance and passion) to advance in Krishna consciousness, highlighting that goodness (sattva guna) serves as a springboard to pure goodness (shuddha sattva).

It's eating that raw, live octopus. It's eating that raw, live octopus. You see on the sometimes, it's very obnoxious, very difficult to see. You see on the sometimes, it's very obnoxious, very difficult to see. Live octopus. You know, it's served. They slit that thing and you actually, with that spoon or fork, you actually scoop out and eat live. And this is all not story, it's happening. Octopus, live. Just slit that back portion, it's very delicate and then you scoop out all that flesh part and eat it. There are people with that taste. Therefore, one who is actually intent in advancing in Krishna consciousness should strive, should strive to come out of the influence of these lower modes and transcend the modes. Treyagunyavishaya, nishtreyagunyabhavarjuna. Krishna says this. All this thing is a game of three modes. Treyagunyavishaya, nishtreyagunyabhavarjuna. You rise above the modes of material nature. By transcending the modes of goodness, one can be situated in pure goodness, shuddha sattva. So you have tamo guna, you have rajo guna, you have sattva guna. Sattva guna is a springboard for a person to jump to shuddha sattva, pure goodness and that is vishuddha sattva.

#### goodness, knowledge, taste
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=661
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Depending on the influence of modes, our taste varies."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the manifestation of goodness is experienced when the nine gates of the body are illuminated with knowledge, and shares a personal story about his cousin's changing taste preferences due to her association and the influence of the modes.

The manifestation of the modes of goodness can be experienced when all gates of the body are illuminated with knowledge. There are nine gates in the body. Nine gates. Anyone can help me with what are the gates? What are the nine dwara? Two eyes, two ears, two nostrils, six. Mouth, anus and genitals. Nine gates of this body. When all the gates are illuminated with knowledge, these gates dwell on the right things. In every gate, when the symptom of goodness is illuminated, one sees, hears, tastes the right thing. Depending on the influence of modes, our taste varies. When I was staying outside before joining the temple, I think I shared last session, I was staying with my relatives and my cousin, sister, she somehow because of her company in school, she was studying in a bishop carton school and because of the company and whatever the kind of friend circle, she started eating non-veg. Started eating non-veg. Started liking non-veg. And she would pester the mother, you have to bring it at home too and all that. And I could see that what she likes eating and we were together, I mean we were staying, I was staying with them and spending lot of time with them and all that. I could see that more and more because I was into Krishna Kanya, more and more we are digressing as far as our tastes are concerned. What she wants me to eat, I don't like. What I like eating, she doesn't like. When I would be coming back home, I would say, oh tender coconuts, we will have tender coconuts. No brother, I don't like it. Tender coconut is not tasteful.

**Summary:** This segment describes the symptoms of the mode of passion, including great attachment, uncontrollable desire, hankering, and intense endeavor, emphasizing that a person in passion is never satisfied.

So this is the influence of mood. It influences our eating, the way we think, thinking, feeling, willing it influences. One becomes cleansed inside and outside when one transcends the mood. The gate is illuminated with knowledge. In every gate, there is development of symptom of happiness and that is the position of goodness. O chief of Bharatas, when there is an increase in the mode of passion, the symptom of great attachment, uncontrollable desire, hankering and intense endeavour develops. When there is an increase of influence of passion, what will happen? The symptoms are great attachment, uncontrollable desire. Until I get it, I cannot be happy. I cannot just be happy. I want this, come what may, hook or crook. I want it. I will do anything to get that. There are people with that kind of a desire. And for some petty gains, okay, could be some genuine thing makes sense, but for some petty gains, come what may, I want this. That's how you have sometimes even brothers, court cases, things falling apart in the family. Uncontrollable desire, hankering and intense endeavour develops. One in the mode of passion is never satisfied. This is a symptom. It's never satisfied. Hope, chase, intense endeavour, great attachment, great attachment, great attachment, great attachment, great attachment, and one will endeavour to get indulge.

A person in knowledge in Satoguna knows that yes house is required, a decent house is required, a comfortable house is required and whatever is required he will build. As he develops a great anchoring for sense satisfaction and increase, increasing sense gratification becomes the goal. #### Krishna, marriage, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=973
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "All that is fine, but your Krishna, you say that all this is there, you have to control the senses. Your Krishna also had 1600 marriages, 16000 wives."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses a common question about Krishna's 16,108 marriages, explaining that it was not out of lust but to protect and provide for princesses who were rescued from a demon and would otherwise be ostracized.

But Krishna also had many marriages. When I was new to Krishna, this was the biggest problem which I faced when I was trying to convince others about Krishna Kanjana. All that is fine, but your Krishna, you say that all this is there, you have to control the senses. Your Krishna also had 1600 marriages, 16000 wives. What is the answer for that? Then some people say, people say that he should walk the talk. There are many who say, control it, control it. But then you know. Why did he marry so many? So the answer is, some of you may be aware, some of you may not be aware. There was a demon by name Bhau Masura. He actually captured 16,108 princes and imprisoned them, kept them with him in a fort. And these princes, they were quite great devotees of the Lord and they prayed to the Lord, please come and save us, please take us or rescue us from this captivity. And Lord who is all powerful, he did actually go and kill the demon Bhau Masura and rescued them. In Vedic times, unlike present times, the girls, because it was very, chastity was a very important thing.

#### Krishna, marriage, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=1056
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is not that out of lust, out of the great desire that I will enjoy 16,108 wives, therefore I marry so many times."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain the circumstances surrounding Krishna's marriages, emphasizing that it was an act of compassion and protection for the rescued princesses, not driven by lust or desire.

These days, you know, it's relatively a very free society now. Especially now, it's 12 o'clock, 1 o'clock. You see, 12 o'clock, 1 o'clock, we see everyone moving around. So in earlier times, a girl who has stayed out of the house captive with someone else, nobody would marry. It was unchaste. We're not going into merits now. We're not going to discuss into why it was considered and things like that. So then, when Krishna rescued them, he said, now you go back home. Be with your parents. He says, no, now nobody will accept us. We can't get married. Even our parents won't accept us. Because two days they were captive with Bhau Masura. So then Krishna says, what do you want? He says, we want you as our husband. We want you to marry us. So now Krishna, you know, sometimes this, he being the Lord, for him it's not a big thing to. Because you know, one wife, one has to think 10 times. So for him, it's not a difficult thing. So, okay, they were intensely desiring, praying for him to marry her, marry them. He married. And for each one of them, he made a separate palace. It's not that each one has to wait for 16,107 nights to, for him to visit and be with them. He made 16,108 palaces and expanded himself into 16,108 palaces. And he was there personally present in each palace with each wife. That is the omnipotency of the Lord. So it is not, that's why it is said we have to understand all this from the right sources. It is not that out of lust, out of the great desire that I will enjoy 16,108 wives, therefore I marry so many times. His devotees who were ardently praying for him to rescue and have him as their husband. Okay, please come.

#### ignorance, madness, inertia
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=1183
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When there is increase in mode of ignorance, madness, illusion, inertia, darkness are manifested."
**Summary:** The speaker transitions to describing the symptoms of the mode of ignorance, including madness, illusion, inertia, and darkness, contrasting them with the characteristics of passion.

He being omnipotent, he can do that. O son of Kunti, when there is an increase in the mode of ignorance, this is a symptom. Madness, illusion, inertia, darkness are manifested. So in Rajoguna, we saw the symptoms are great attachment, uncontrollable desire, hankering and intense endeavor. Very passionate, always wanting, wanting, wanting, working hard, working hard, working hard with no knowledge. And what is the symptom of Tamoguna? When there is increase in mode of ignorance, madness, illusion, inertia. Inertia means indolence, no desire for doing anything. It could equate with so called depression people talk about. No desire to do anything. And darkness is manifested. One wants to act whimsically and for no purpose.

#### ignorance, danger, inaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=1250
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Ignorance is not bliss, it's dangerous."
**Summary:** The speaker refutes the idea that 'ignorance is bliss,' arguing that it is dangerous and increases the likelihood of making mistakes, using the analogy of a drunk person crossing a road.

And many times people say ignorance is bliss. Ignorance is not bliss, it's dangerous. It's bliss, maybe for a moment. But I sometimes give this example, a person who is completely drunk. For him to cross the river. To cross the road and not meet with an accident, there is a possibility. He is completely drunk and trying to cross. Quite possible all the vehicles moving, the drivers may be more alert and they may stop and make him leave. But the chances of probability of he meeting with an accident is much higher. So a person who is in ignorance, for him to mess up his life is much higher. Relatively speaking to a person in passion. Even though he has capacity to work, he makes no endeavour. He likes sleeping. He doesn't want to take any initiative.

#### goodness, destination, planetary systems

**Content Type:** philosophy

**Summary:** The speaker explains that dying in the mode of goodness leads to elevation to higher planetary systems, inhabited by godly and sattvic people, similar to how some countries on Earth are more prosperous than others.

When one dies in the mode of goodness, he attained to pure higher planets. In the material world also. There are higher planets, there are lower planets. And there are, just like even in this planet earth, we have countries which are much more, so to say, prosperous. And the countries who are much more degraded or in the sense, less advanced. So likewise, when a person who dies in the mode of goodness, dominantly in goodness, his destination is something else. He goes to the planets where there is nothing. He goes to the planets where predominantly sattvic people stay. One in goodness attain higher planetary system like Brahmaloka or Janaloka and there enjoy godly happiness. Those who die in mode of goodness are elevated to planets where great sages and great devotees live.

#### devotees, consciousness, protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mKL3399ATIc?t=1579
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "What the Lord protects is, not in illusion, He protects the body. Because our idea of protection is the body, which anyway cannot be protected."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the real protection the Lord offers to devotees is the protection of their consciousness from the influence of the lower modes, even as their physical bodies may still undergo decay and disease.

So, what the Lord protects, in case of devotees, is not, because the body, today or tomorrow, it has to deteriorate and, it's not that a person is a devotee, he will not become old, he will not become diseased. He will. The nature's law will act. But what the Lord protects, is the consciousness. Because it is not that we should also not have false expectation that, now I am a devotee, so, diseases won't come. As per the law of nature, it will come. But what is real protection, sometimes we also worry, you know, I am going to the temple, I am doing this, I am also chanting, but you know, my health has started deteriorating. Why it is like that? Because physical health, beyond a point, okay, we can just delay the whole process. A person has to die at the age of 80, maybe with all good care of the health, you can add this stretch to 100 years, or 110 years, maybe. But anyway, the decay has to happen. Any material thing goes through 6 transits, decay and finally diseases, is part of the whole process. What the Lord protects is, not in illusion, He protects the body. Because our idea of protection is the body, which anyway cannot be protected. What the Lord is really interested in protecting, and which He promises He will protect, for the devotees, is His consciousness. That consciousness which has, which can go into the spell of, what we talked about, Tamoguna, indolence, madness, illusion, He protects from that, and that is real protection.

Who decides which modes will influence us? We. We attract the modes, and the modes influence us. So it goes in a cycle. Based on our thinking, feeling and willing, we attract the modes, and the modes influence us. So we, wrong actions, do wrong things, attract wrong modes, and wrong modes influence us to do wrong things. This is a negative cycle.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### THE ENEMY WITHIN | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### enemy within, self-control, spiritual warfare
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Bhagavatam says that one who says that he has many enemies but does not recognize that his greatest enemy is his uncontrolled mind and his mind is his greatest enemy, then he is mistaken."
**Summary:** The lecture begins by introducing the concept of the enemy within, emphasizing the uncontrolled mind and senses as the greatest adversaries, and initiating prayers for blessings.

The topic for the day is the enemy within. All of us have friends. We also have some people who we can call our enemies. The Bhagavatam says that one who says that he has many enemies but does not recognize that his greatest enemy is his uncontrolled mind, and his mind is his greatest enemy, if he has many enemies and uncontrolled senses, if he does not have that understanding, then he is mistaken. So today we are going to discuss what are the enemies within us, and a progressive life in the true sense involves some kind of battle with those enemies. A person is said to be successful if he has won such battles with the enemies within. So we will get into more details. Let us start the session with prayers. Request all of you to kindly fold your hands and join me. Together let's offer these prayers and, seeking the blessings of the Lord, we begin this session. Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Prishtha Yabhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamini Nama.

---
#### ignorance, karma, lust
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=274
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, all the karmas we do, the root is avidya."
**Summary:** The first enemy within is identified as ignorance (avidya), which leads to lust (kama) and engagement in activities (karma), resulting in suffering.

The first aspect which I am going to share with all of you is the enemy of ignorance. I repeat, the enemy of ignorance. Ignorance in Sanskrit is also called avidya. Bhagavatam says, avidya kama karma bhi. When we have avidya, ignorance about our true self, ignorance about why we are in this world, who is creator of this world, then because of that fundamental avidya, ignorance, we lead a life, kama, which is driven by kama, lust. The goal of life becomes enjoying our minds, enjoying our senses. Avidya kama karma bhi, and because of that kama, lust, we engage in various activities and whole life is all about engaging in those activities and enjoying or suffering the reactions of those karmas. So, all the karmas we do, the root is avidya. Because of avidya, there is kama. Because of kama, we engage in various sinful activities, material activities, and because of which we suffer the consequences in this life and in our future lives.

#### ignorance, body as self, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=456
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Spiritual life begins when a person understands that he is not the body, he is spirit soul."
**Summary:** The first kind of ignorance is accepting the body as the self, a fundamental avidya that even learned individuals can be covered by, leading to a lack of understanding about our origins and destination after death.

So, the five kinds of avidya which Mahabharata talks about, the first one is the understanding, accepting the body as self. This is one kind of avidya, accepting body as the self. In other words, thinking that I am the body. It's a very fundamental avidya and Prabhupada says that spiritual life begins, the ABC of spiritual life is, spiritual life begins when a person understands that he is not the body, he is spirit soul. So, based on this avidya, fundamental avidya, I am this body, even big, big, learned, eminent scholars, academicians, philosophers, they also are, so to say, covered with this avidya. Sometimes someone says he is a noble laureate, he is such a learned person, he is such an accomplished person. But vidvams apikar sati, even a vidvan, his knowledge, also can be taken away by maya. So, this avidya which covers our true knowledge, in fact, if you see, it is quite surprising that we lead a life with two big black boxes. Where we come from is a black box. Where we go after death, it's a black box. We have no clear understanding. A vast majority of people, in fact, 99.9999% of the population has no idea where we come from. Year after year we celebrate our birthday. Let's say a person is born in 1965, where was he before 1965? No idea.

#### sense gratification, ignorance, happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=659
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The consequence of that avidya is making sense gratification the standard of enjoyment in life."
**Summary:** The second kind of ignorance is making sense gratification the standard of enjoyment, leading to a life focused on eating, sleeping, mating, and defending, even in sophisticated ways, while neglecting the higher standard of happiness available to humans.

The second kind of avidya or ignorance is making sense gratification as the standard of enjoyment. I repeat, it's a consequence of the first kind of ignorance that I am the body, accepting the body as self. The consequence of that avidya is making sense gratification the standard of enjoyment in life. As souls, we all are seeking enjoyment. There's nothing wrong about it. Anandamayo Bhyasat. The common denominator in all our lives, irrespective of what profession we are in, what gender, what nationality, everyone is seeking happiness. Now, with this fundamental avidya, the standard of enjoyment, sense gratification becomes the standard of enjoyment. Eating, sleeping, mating, defending, gorgeously, in a very very elaborate, sophisticated way, becomes the goal of life and becomes the standard of enjoyment. If you see today's world, the modern advancement, it's all about how we can have better gadgets, better channels to watch, much clearer videos to play. It's all sense gratification. Sense gratification. We have many apps. We can order items whenever we want. In 30 minutes, it will be delivered hot to your place. And we think that we're living in an advanced world.

#### sense gratification, human life, animalistic tendencies
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=775
**Verse References:** SB 5.5.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You should not engage in sense gratification, which is so freely and so easily available in other life forms."
**Summary:** The human form of life should not be wasted on sense gratification, which is readily available in animal life. Humans should strive for a higher standard of happiness, as sense gratification is a low-class pursuit.

But a scripture says, Nayam Deho Deha Bhajaan Nriloke. Nayam. This is Srimad Bhagavatam, fifth canto. Rishabhdev is telling his sons, Nayam Deho Deha Bhajaan Nriloke. Nayam Deho Deha Bhajaan. For a soul who has accepted this human body, Deho Deha Bhajaan. Kashtan Kaman Arhate Vidh Bhujaam Yeh. You should not engage in sense gratification, which is so freely and so easily available in other life forms. Sense gratification as a source of enjoyment is available in all life forms. Even animals are dabbling in it. In fact, they are able to dabble much more freely. Prabhupada made this statement, very blunt statement. It might hurt some of us. Prabhupada said that a dog may have sex in a street, in front of everyone. A man may have in a cozy apartment, in a multi-storied building, in a cozy bed with nice aroma and perfume all around, in a very, very wonderful environment. But the standard of enjoyment is the same. The pleasure is the same. We may eat on a dining table with forks and knife in a very sophisticated way, very well-mannered way. But the enjoyment of eating is the same. Dogs may bark and protect themselves, defend themselves. We may defend using intercontinental ballistic missiles. The act is the same. The activity is the same. We may do it much more sophisticated, much more refined way, much more gorgeous way, using technology, using all the latest gadgets. But the activity is the same.

#### anxiety, bodily identification, ignorance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=1017
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everyone without an exception is in anxiety."
**Summary:** The third kind of ignorance is identifying with the body, which leads to constant anxiety, a state common to everyone regardless of their status.

The third one is, because of identifying with it, with the body, the ignorance which makes us always anxious. We are always in anxiety because of bodily identification. The ignorance makes us very anxious. Prabhupada says everyone in this world, everyone without an exception, rich man, poor man, President of United States, the Prime Minister of the country, everyone without an exception is in anxiety. Anxiety is called kuntha. The spiritual world is called vaikuntha. Vaikuntha means where there is no anxiety. Prabhupada gives an example, even a sparrow, if it comes to pick up a grain, if you are thrown some grains, the sparrow comes and it's, it's constantly looking here and there because it's in anxiety. Somebody should not throw some stone, somebody should not hit him. Dogs also, you see them barking, they all have some so-called territorial rights. If someone comes in, they start barking, they become anxious, as if somebody is threatening their territorial rights. This street is my street, how can you come? At times you would see, when we walk on this road, the dogs start barking. There is some anxiety, this is which they become perturbed. So it's common to everyone. Avidya, the third one is, which causes anxiety in us.

#### lamentation, ignorance, happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=1128
**Verse References:** BG 18.54
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When one becomes Brahma bhuta, he becomes joyful."
**Summary:** The fourth kind of ignorance is lamentation, which contrasts with the joy experienced when one realizes their spiritual identity. Most people are not truly happy, being troubled by past events or hankering for what they lack.

The fourth kind of avidya is, the ignorance which causes lamentation. Brahma bhuta prasannatma na shochati na kankshati. Krishna in the Gita says, when one becomes Brahma bhuta, when one realises that I am not the body, I am a spirit soul. In other words, when one crosses this so-called ignorance, asato ma satgamaya, tamasima jyotirgamaya. When one comes to light, when one gets enlightened, Brahma bhuta prasannatma, he becomes joyful. And when one is enlightened, he becomes happy. Tell me genuinely, how many of us are happy? That's a different thing, if anyone asks, how are you? I am fine. That's a standard answer, we have learnt to give. But deep within, if we introspect, am I really fine? Every one of us has something which is bugging us, which is troubling us, for something which has happened in the past. Hankering for something which we don't have. Brahma bhuta prasannatma, na shocchati na kankshati. Does not hanker, does not lament. This is the standard of enjoyment, standard of living, which we are entitled for. But we have settled for something which is very very tuchham, very very low. Prabhupada says, low class. Our standard of enjoyment has become the standard which even animals are dabbling in.

**Summary:** The fifth ignorance is thinking there is something beyond the absolute truth, failing to recognize that everything is a manifestation of the Lord and His energies. God is often used to fulfill material desires rather than being recognized as the ultimate reality.

For many people, sexual intercourse is greatest of material pleasure. Animals are dabbling freely. And the fifth one is, the fifth avidya, ignorance is thinking that there is something beyond the absolute truth. The real understanding, the real knowledge is, everything we see is nothing but the Lord and His energies. Everything is manifestation of Lord and His energies. But because of avidya, we see the Lord is completely out of picture. We completely take Lord out of our lives. And we use the Lord, we use God, for fulfilling some of our material desires by offering some prayers. So called religious people, we use God, and sometimes we are disappointed, why God does not respond, or reciprocate. So these are the five avidyas. First, accepting the body as a self. Second, making sense gratification as a standard of one's enjoyment. Because of identification with the body, one is in anxiety, kuntha, and is full of, fourth, because of this avidya, which covers the real knowledge, one is always full of lamentation. And the fifth avidya, which covers the real knowledge, is one thinks that there is something beyond the Lord, the absolute truth. The absolute truth, the Supreme Lord, is also called the absolute truth, because there is nothing beyond it. It's the ultimate truth, the ultimate reality, the cause of all causes. Sarva Karana Karanam. When there was nothing, He was there. So this understanding gets covered, because of one kind of ignorance.

**Summary:** The lecture transitions to discussing the five enemies: lust (Kaam), anger (Krodh), greed (Lobh), attachment (Moh), and envy (Matsarya). Lust is highlighted as a powerful force that even learned individuals can fall prey to.

It is Kaam, Krodh, Lobh, Moh, and Matsarya, and sometimes people also call it Ahankaar. In all religions, there is mention of these enemies. Panch Vikar. In Guru Granth Sahib, it's talked about as Panch Vikar. The five decoits which steal our real wealth. We all have heard about it many many times. I'll just briefly touch. Kaam is lust. Lust is a powerful force, where Krishna in the Gita says, Kaam Aesha, Krodh Aesha, Rajo Guna, Samudbhava, Maha Shano, Maha Paapma, Iha Vaidina. This Kaam is the greatest enemy of the living entity. And when this Kaam is not fulfilled, what does it do? It results in Krodha, anger, frustration. So, Kaam, lust, and it is such a great force, that even so called learned people, even so called spiritualists, have fallen to this powerful force. It's a very powerful enemy.

#### greed, attachment, dissatisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=2557
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Because of greed even though we have enough and more we're not satisfied."
**Summary:** Greed is illustrated through the example of a software company dissatisfied with a 320 crore profit because competitors made more. Attachment is shown through a man's distress over a lost umbrella, highlighting how these enemies keep us from being happy and content.

In the same quarter they were left behind so greed because of greed even though we have enough enough and more we're not satisfied we always compare he has that she has that i don't have it and we keep hankering for such things so lobe it's an enemy and then attachments there was once a person he used to come for satsang again as i mentioned it was from theakukan along road to herzog he used to tell me so if you want to go to the football league you will have to come to me i have a friend who has a good interest in gambling but he did not know the amount of money he was very very attached to his umbrella and he would always keep the umbrella very very close to him and one day it so happened that you know he went for satsang it was raining quite heavily and the umbrella was quite wet and instead of taking it along with him he kept the umbrella outside you know when the umbrella is wet so you keep it in a place so that it gets dried and you keep it open so that all the water trickles down so he kept the umbrella outside and after satsang when he went the umbrella was missing somebody had taken his umbrella so he felt so bad so bad that night he could not sleep because of his attachment to that umbrella and next few days he came for the satsang but he was visibly disturbed visibly he could see the disturbance in his face he was so attached to that umbrella even in our lives we get attached to people we get attached to things we get attached to circumstances.

#### solution, chanting, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=3684
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord has mercifully descended in this age of Kali Yuga."
**Summary:** The lecture shifts to the solution: the mercy of Lord Chaitanya and Nithyananda Prabhu, who inaugurated the Sankirtana movement, the chanting of the Lord's holy names, which is accessible to everyone regardless of qualification.

Hare Krishna, so quick recap, we covered about, we talked about Mahabharata enlightening us about five kinds of ignorance. That was the first point which we covered and then we covered about enemies of calm, Krodh, Lobh, Moha and Ahankar, Matsarya, Madha. Sometimes it's mentioned as six enemies, someone pointed out also. And move to the third point, okay, we all have enemies, we talked about a problem but what about the solution? It's easy to say, yes, we have enemies, everyone knows, yes, we have Kama, we have Krodha, what is the solution? Our scriptures are just talking about these are the enemies and you have to fight the battle. In fact, they are also giving us a solution. How to fight these enemies? Knowing that we are all very weak and enemies is very powerful, the Lord has mercifully descended in this age of Kali Yuga, Kaler Dosh Nidhe Rajan. The Lord has come as Lord Chaitanya and Lord Nithyananda Prabhu. Lord Chaitanya is compared to Sun and Nithyananda Prabhu is compared to Moon. Just like when there is lot of fog, dense fog and you are not able to see things clearly when the Sun rises, automatically as the Sun rises in the sky, the fog starts dissipating. So likewise, when the Sun of Lord Chaitanya's mercy comes into our life and automatically all that avidya will dissipate, will go away. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is none other than Krishna Himself who came 500 years back and He started or inaugurated the Sankirtana movement, the chanting of the Lord's holy names.

#### chanting, knowledge, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=3803
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All Your energies and potencies You have packed, You have invested in Your Naama."
**Summary:** Chanting is powerful because the Lord has invested all His energies and potencies in His name, making it accessible to anyone without qualification. By giving our attention to the Lord's name, we associate with Him and receive His knowledge, destroying ignorance.

And He mentions in His Shishtashtaka, Naam Naam Akari Bahudha Nija Sarva Shaktis O Lord, You are so kind, so merciful that You have descended in the form of His Naama and Naam Naam Akari Bahudha Nija Sarva Shaktis All Your energies and potencies You have packed, You have invested in Your Naama. Tatra Pita Niyamita Smar Nena Kala And how magnanimous You are, how merciful You are, how gracious You are that there is no hard and fast rule. Anybody can chant, anybody with no prior qualification, with no extra knowledge can chant and can win over all these avidyas, all these enemies. Now, chanting of the Holy Name, why it is so powerful? Because the Lord is reservoir of Sat, Chit and Ananda. Chit means knowledge, pure knowledge, real knowledge. He is reservoir. He is an ocean of Sat Chit Ananda. And that same Lord who is an ocean of Sat Chit Ananda has come in the form of His name. So when we associate with the Lord by giving our attention, when we touch that Lord, touch the Lord in the form of His name, how do we touch the Lord? Today we are having this body which is made of matter. Even the sound that we vibrate is matter. But if we give our attention, attention which is spiritual energy coming from the soul, a dead body does not have attention. Dead body does not have consciousness. So when we give attention and touch the Lord who is reservoir, whose ocean of Sat Chit Ananda, the Chit energy, the Chit energy flows into our system and destroys all avidya.

#### chanting, solution, maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=4070
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by chanting the names of Krishna, we can easily wipe out this avidya."
**Summary:** The solution to overcoming ignorance and enemies is simply chanting the names of Krishna, which is like the sun dissipating darkness. Maya is not a separate entity but the absence of the Lord in our consciousness.

So we talked about enemies, we talked about five kinds of avidya, we talked about five other enemies like Kaam, Krodh and the solution is one. Asto Eki Mahaguna There is one good quality and what is that? Simply by chanting the names of Krishna, we can easily wipe out this avidya. Krishna Surya Sama Maya Haya Andhakaar The Lord, Lord's holy name is like Surya. It's like sun. And Maya is illusion. Avidya is like darkness. Just like when the sun rises, darkness has no independent existence. Darkness means when the light is not there, we call it dark. This darkness is not some separate thing. Likewise, this Maya is not a separate thing. Maya is when the Lord is not in our lives, Lord is not in our consciousness, we are in Maya. The moment the Lord comes into our mind, into our consciousness, when we chant the names of Krishna, then automatically the Maya, the avidya gets dissipated from our life.

#### surrender, happiness, self-control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=4176
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We will experience higher form of happiness, higher joy."
**Summary:** Surrendering to the Lord brings a higher form of happiness, easier control of senses and mind, and victory over enemies like anger. It leads to satisfaction, joy, and freedom from hankering and lamentation.

The question is, if we surrender to the Lord, surrender to the Lord super soul who is in our heart, what kind of changes we will experience in our life? As I mentioned, we will experience higher form of happiness, higher joy. In fact, we as souls are wanting to experience that real happiness, but in illusion, in Maya, we are chasing some wrong happiness, thinking that I'll be happy. If I have a bigger car, better car, bigger house to live in, much better food to eat, I'll be happy. So we will experience higher happiness. We'll also be able to easily control our senses. We'll be able to easily control our minds. We'll be able to overpower our enemies like anger. Many times people are very short tempered. Small, small thing, they get irritated, they get angry, and they know it is wrong, but they cannot help it. So the changes, what a person will notice that he is able to control his mind, control his senses. It's a very big victory. Jesus Christ said, what profit the man if he wins the whole world, but loses his own soul. In today's world, everyone is wanting to conquer the world, but the enemies within are not conquered. In fact, the enemies have conquered him. So these are the changes we will see. We'll experience satisfaction. We'll experience joy. Brahma bhuta prasannatma. He'll become joyful. Na shochhati na kankshati. There'll be no hankering. There'll be no lamentation. There'll be no krodha, excessive krodha. We get angry, we get irritated, we get frustrated, we get bewildered. All that will soon vanish from our lives and we can experience that kind of state of existence.

#### illicit sex, karma, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/GNc7gacoWzY?t=4293
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Senses, mind and intelligence are sitting places of lust."
**Summary:** Illicit sex reinforces bodily concepts and fans lust, which can never be satisfied. Regulated sex within marriage is permitted, but any other indulgence reinforces ignorance and bodily identification.

Why illicit sex is wrong and how it is wrong according to laws of nature if the opposite party is also agreeing to do this. In fact, sex is very much permitted in Grahasta Ashrama. Krishna in the Gita says that dharma aviruddho kamo asmi. I am Kama, which is not against me, but against religious principle. So if a person is indulging with his wife in a regulated way as per the prescriptions in our scriptures, there is nothing wrong. But any other kind of sex indulgence reinforces bodily concept, reinforces all the kind of avidyas which we talked about. It fans that Kama which we talked about. Avidya kama karma bhi. Because of Kama, which will never be satisfied. Bhagavad Gita says it is like putting ghee into fire. The fire becomes even more. So any amount of illicit sex will not satisfy us. In fact, Osho gave a philosophy that just indulge so that a time will come when you will have enough of it and you will give up. But it doesn't happen because Kama is the ground. Kama is the greatest enemy of the living entity. Because of Kama, we are in this material world. And the Lord who has created this world is giving his verdict, is giving his opinion. Senses, mind and intelligence are sitting places of lust. From the very beginning, regulate your senses, control your mind. In Vedic times, the first ashram was Brahmachari ashram where a person would learn how to control the mind, how to control the senses. And with that training, a person may enter into Ghrastha ashram and there is nothing wrong about it.

#### envy, divine plan
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=122
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "And this was the beginning of the enmity between the two, between the two brothers of the same family."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Duryodhana's envy of Yudhishthira and his humiliation in the palace of illusions, which Krishna orchestrated to instigate the conflict leading to the destruction of the Kaurava dynasty.

A very warm welcome to all of you. After Maharaja Yudhishthir did a great sacrifice called Rajasuya Yajna, he became famous not just on this planet but across other heavenly planets as well. And thus, he was famous. Duryodhana became envious. How come he has become so famous? And meanwhile, they had got a very nice palace constructed by Maya Demon, the architect of the demons, of the lower planetary systems. Just like Vishwakarma is there, he builds it for the heavenly planets, Maya Demon does it for the lower planets. And circumstantially, it so happened, Maya Demon was living in a forest and Arjuna saved him by being burned down in the forest fire. So, he became very pleased. So, thus he made this very unique palace. So, the demons are very, very expert in conjuring many tricks, creating illusions. So, in this palace, there was such an arrangement that unless a person knows the internal architecture, he would misinterpret land for water and vice versa. So, Duryodhana, once he decided to see Yudhishthira Maharaj and he entered the palace, he was anyway very envious and angry. And the doorkeeper, there was some misunderstanding with them. He somehow entered in anger. Sword was there in his hand. And when he entered, he wanted to cross the place. So, he mistook land as water, and then, in this way, he fell in the water. And all the ladies, the Pandavas were there, Draupadi was there, and Krishna also was staying at that time. And all the thousands of queens of Krishna were also there in the palace. All of them started laughing loudly at this instance of Duryodhana. So, Yudhishthira Maharaj wanted to stop. Please don't do that. But Krishna encouraged them. Yes, please laugh. He wanted to encourage. Krishna had an elaborate plan. And this was the beginning of the enmity between the two, between the two brothers of the same family. The Pandavas also are actually Kauravas. They belong to the Kuru dynasty. But nevertheless, because of this faction, we call them Pandavas and Kauravas. So, Krishna had this plan to annihilate all the hundred sons of Dhritarashtra. And thus, Krishna encouraged. Okay, please laugh. Let him get offended, so that he does all these steps which he later took. And eventually, that led to the destruction of the entire Kaurava dynasty.

#### illusion, Maya, perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=301
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In a similar fashion, Maya creates some allurements, and people think, oh, this is my happiness, and happiness is perceived as distress."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of Duryodhana mistaking land for water in the illusory palace to explain how Maya, Krishna's external potency, tricks conditioned souls into misinterpreting happiness and distress, spiritual and material.

So, just like an ordinary person of this universe can conjure such tricks that land can be taken as water, where you are perceiving land, it is water, and where you are perceiving water, it is actually land. In a similar fashion, Maya, the external potency of Krishna, it plays such amazing tricks on small conditioned souls, souls like us, that we mistake happiness as distress, distress as happiness, spiritual as material, and something material is taken as spiritual. So, that is why it is told one should never rely upon his senses. One's senses cannot lead one to perfect knowledge. Using one's senses, one is thinking, oh, this is land. Just like, so these stories are very, very powerful. So, just like Duryodhana was thinking, oh, here is land. Let me step carefully onto it. Let me avoid water. But actually, that was water. The perception was created that there is land. There is no water over there, and he fell in. In a similar fashion, Maya creates some allurements, and people think, oh, this is my happiness, and happiness is perceived as distress.

#### festivals, service, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=380
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, actually, a sincere devotee, for him, every day is a festival. His life is nothing but service of God."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of celebrating Vaishnava festivals by dedicating time to serving God, even abstaining from eating, to transcend the bodily concept of life.

So, now we are going to have a very nice festival of nostalgia and Simha Chatur Dashi. So, it is very, very important that we celebrate these festivals very nicely. And how do we celebrate the festival? It is very simple. We save the time from all other activities, even from eating. Cooking, eating, all this consumes a lot of time. And then after eating, you become lethargic. You feel sleepy. So, we avoid eating also. We save all the time. And entire time, why? Why do we save it for? To engage ourselves in the service of God. So, actually, a sincere devotee, for him, every day is a festival. His life is nothing but service of God. But usually, living entities are not interested to serve the Lord, because serving the Lord is distress. Who does it? What happens? And, you know, those of you who have started recently chanting, and if you have picked up a good number of rounds, 4 rounds, 8 rounds or 16 rounds, people would tell you, 2 hours you are chanting? Why? Why are you wasting time? Do something worthwhile in this time. They think, oh, this is, and I cannot do it. Oh, it is very difficult. They find it difficult. They find it burdensome. So, this is the way how the material world is created. So, nevertheless, people want to enjoy here. So, through these festivals, through enjoyment, the training is given to living entities, so that they can eventually develop attraction and understanding of God. So, please do not miss to celebrate these festivals. Save time. Do fasting. And along with fasting, don't forget the main activity. Sometimes people just fast and think festival is a success. No.

#### austerity, self-realization, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=485
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, when we do this tapasya, we are able to realize, oh, this is land and this is water, this is spirit and this is matter."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that fasting during festivals helps one transcend the bodily concept of life through austerity (tapasya), which begins with waking up early and taking cold baths, leading to the realization of the difference between spirit and matter, and culminating in chanting and hearing about Krishna.

We fast so that we can come out of bodily concept of life. When we do austerity, voluntarily we do, we take discomforts in our life. The austerity begins with getting up early in the morning. People like to sleep a lot and snooze the clock unlimited times. That is very, very pleasant. But getting up early in the morning before the day breaks is not pleasant. However, that is required. That is tapasya. And then taking cold water bath is another tapasya. Two times, three times bath a day is tapasya. And like this, so many other things are there, which a person is supposed to undergo so that he can understand that he is not the body. So, when we do this tapasya, we are able to realize, oh, this is land and this is water, this is spirit and this is matter. And along with that, when we do tapasya, then we engage ourselves in the service of God. And the service begins with chanting and hearing. So, as much as possible, please try to chant, try to hear the pastimes connected with Lord Narsingh Dev.

#### devotion, transcendence, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=555
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That even the boiling oil was not able to affect his body."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts the story of Prahalad Maharaj, who endured extreme tortures from his atheistic father, Hiranyakashipu, but remained unaffected due to his advanced spiritual life and absorption in chanting the glories of the Lord.

So, Prahalad Maharaj, when he was asked by Lord Narsingh Dev, so he was very greatly harassed by his atheistic father, Hiran Nekashipu. So, finally Lord Narsingh Dev appeared out of great compassion to save his devotee from this harassment and to kill the demon. Demon was killed and then Narsingh Dev asked Prahalad Maharaj, so, you have suffered so much. Please let me know, how can I help you? So, Prahalad Maharaj, so, apparently we can see that Prahalad Maharaj suffered so much. Prahalad Maharaj was attempted to be killed so many times by his father. He was put under the foot of the elephant. He was thrown from the mountain cliff, and boiling oil and snowfall he was subjected to and so many other miseries. Poison, mother was forced to administer poison to the child. But he survived and Hiran Nekashipu was surprised, what is the source of your power? I have done so much of tapasya to get these powers and I am not able to defeat you, kill you. So, he was facing great harassment. And finally, Lord Narsingh Dev told, please ask me, what do you want in life? So, Prahalad Maharaj tells, my dear Lord, I do not have any problem in my life. So, do you want liberation? No, no, I am not at all. I am not at all afraid by Vaitaranya. I am not at all afraid by any of the difficulties of this material world. Nobody could have faced severe tortures and difficulties like Prahalad Maharaj. But Prahalad Maharaj was so much advanced in his spiritual life.

#### spiritual absorption, chanting, divine protection

That is very extraordinary state of spiritual life, that even the boiling oil was not able to affect his body. So, very exalted personalities are able to display, very exalted pure devotees are displaying such features, as was displayed by Prahalad Maharaj, Haridas Thakur, Bhishma and other such devotees. So, he was so much absorbed. So, this is a stage of spiritual life, when a person is so much absorbed that he is not at all affected by any of the laws of nature. Hot, cold, this thing doesn't affect him at all. So, Prahalad Maharaj is telling, I am not at all having any difficulty. Why I am not having difficulty? He reveals the secret. Just like now, some people may be very happy who have got vaccine. So, it is not easy to make vaccine. You need to have lot of intelligence how to make it. And then probably you will not be affected by the disease. And there are great many patents about various such medicines. So, what is that thing which Prahalad Maharaj has got? Hiranyakashyapu was also very happy. He was very amazed that what is this special power you have got? I am throwing you from mountain, nothing is happening. The elephant is trying to trample you, nothing is happening. Poison I give it to you, nothing happens to you. What is that thing you have got? What is this power? So, here Prahalad Maharaj is revealing what is my power? Why I am not at all disturbed by all these calamities? He is telling, Tvad viryagayan mahamrita magna chittaha. Chittaha means consciousness. Because, my consciousness is absorbed in Tvad viryagayan, in chanting your glories.

#### chanting, nectar, spiritual power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=783
**Verse References:** BG 9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotee is serving the Lord in his incarnation of holy name, and he gets unlimited bliss simply by chanting the holy name."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the confidential science of chanting the holy names of Krishna, as mentioned in the Bhagavad Gita, which allows a devotee to attain such an advanced spiritual status that the laws of nature do not affect him, and he experiences great nectar within his heart.

So, this is a very confidential science. Man manabhav madbhakto madbyaaji maham namaskuru satatam kirtayanto maham. Krishna tells in 9th chapter of Bhagavad Gita, always keep on chanting my holy name. So, some people think this is an ordinary affair. Simply by chanting the names of Krishna, what will happen? And some atheists tell, who are, apparently they have some belief in God. No. Does any father say to the child that, you chant papa papa papa at some movie at Shon. You know, you simply ask the father and father will give you. No. The devotee is not chanting for any demand. The devotee is serving the Lord in his incarnation of holy name, and he gets unlimited bliss simply by chanting the holy name. Tvad viryagayan mahamrita magna chittaha. And the devotee is absorbed, when he is absorbed in chanting, the glories or chanting the names of God. He retains such an advanced platform spiritual status, that nothing in this material world, no laws of nature act upon such devotee. So, Prahlad Maharaj is telling, I do not need anything. Maha amrita. And when a person meditates upon singing the glories, glorifying the Lord, chanting his qualities, so, he feels a very great nectar within his heart. Maha amrit. Amrit means nectar. A person feels unlimited happiness, by coming in contact with nectar. And he becomes eternal. And singing the glories of Lord, are described by Prahlad Maharaj as Maha Amrita. Great nectar. So, I am absorbed in this great nectar. I am relishing your glories.

#### illusion, Maya, restrictions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=881
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Maya creates such an illusion for the living entity, that happiness is perceived as distress, just like very tasty food appears bitter."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that because we are affected by Maya, we perceive happiness as distress, like a sweet candy tasting bitter due to jaundice, and that we are already restricted in many ways by our desires and attachments.

So, I do not have any problem in my life. However, because we are now affected by the influence of Maya, as we'll be seeing in the next class, Srila Rupa Goswami explains in next verse, verse number 7, how when a person is affected by Maya, Maya creates such an illusion for the living entity, that happiness is perceived as distress, just like very tasty food appears bitter. The sugar candy sweet, it is supposed to be in nature. But when a person is affected by high jaundice, the sweet sugar candy appears bitter. The nature appears converse. It is sweet. It is appearing bitter. This is the influence of external energy. So, ordinarily it may appear, because all of us are conditioned by this illusion. So, ordinarily in the beginning one may perceive, oh, this is a painful activity. It is painful to follow rules and regulations of the Vedas. My life would become very, very restricted. We don't understand our life is already so much restricted. Morning to evening, we are forced, we are forced to do some work, much against our wish. So many desires, I am forced. So much restriction is already there. If the food is not there of my choice, I become disturbed. Is it not restriction? You are restricted by the food. If I am not able to watch my favorite match, football or cricket, I become disturbed. I become restless. Are you not restricted, controlled by it? If I do not have sufficient money, I lose money, I become disturbed. Are you not restricted by the money? Are you not being controlled by it?

#### control, freedom, spiritual power
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=991
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Simply by chanting the name, glorifying Krishna, a person becomes so powerful that he cannot be disturbed by a person who was able to push all the universal controllers, the devatas out of their places."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to explain how we are controlled by various external factors like criticism, unkind words, money, and desires, and that true freedom comes from following the basic restrictions of spiritual life, which initially appear restrictive but ultimately bring great pleasure.

If there is some criticism you are not able to tolerate, are you not restricted by it? If some other person says something unkind to you, are we not disturbed by it? So, are we not restricted by other people? They are governing our behavior. So, so much we are restricted. We are being controlled by so many ropes in the hand of various people, in the circumstances, money, name, fame, all these things are controlling us. Lust, anger, greed, helplessly living entities, already helpless. Already he is so much restricted, under illusion he thinks, so I am free. But actually when he follows these few basic restrictions, regulations which may appear like restrictions just in the beginning, then a person becomes used to doing these activities. Then he feels great pleasure in doing these activities. So, just like a sugar candy appears bitter, these activities initially a person has to follow as a matter of discipline. And very soon when followed strictly, these activities give great pleasure to the living entity. So, Prahlad Maharaj is telling, I am experiencing extraordinary pleasure simply by chanting, singing your glories. So, this is the secret. It is not ordinary instruction in the Bhagavad Gita. Simply by chanting the name, glorifying Krishna, a person becomes so powerful that he cannot be disturbed by a person who was able to push all the universal controllers, the devatas out of their places.

#### lamentation, aversion, spiritual bliss

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of lamentation even in advanced devotees, explaining that Prahalad Maharaj, despite his supreme bliss, laments for those who are averse to God and cannot relish the nectar of Krishna's service.

But Prahlad Maharaj then tells, but yes, I have got one lamentation. Oh, this person has got the supreme bliss. He has got the most confidential knowledge. Doing that activity makes a person completely quarantined from all the disturbances of this world. But then what is this factor which is affecting him? Something which is causing great lamentation for such an advanced personality. So, Prahlad Maharaj reveals, Soche tato vimukh chetasa indriyartho maya sukhae bharam udvahato vimudhan. Soche means lamentation. Brahma bhuta prasannatma na sochati na kankshati. A person who has become Brahma Bhuta, who has realized Aham Brahmasmi, I am spirit soul, na sochati na kankshati. He does not lament after anything in this world. If you lose name, fame, property or people around you, you don't lament. Neither kankshati, neither you hanker for anything, because you understand you are not the body and everything belongs to Krishna. And getting these things do not satisfy me. I am spirit soul. Only spiritual activity, engaging myself in the service of Supreme Spirit God does satisfy me. So Brahma Bhuta, our self realized soul, he does not lament. So Prahlad Maharaj, but he is lamenting? Yes. So there is this unique lamentation, present in the hearts of all such very exalted devotees also. And what is that lamentation? vimukha cetasa indriyartho. Those people who have become vimukha, averse to you. And as soon as a person becomes averse to God, he cannot relish this Maha Amrita, the great nectar of engaging in service of Krishna, remembering Him.

---
#### sense enjoyment, illusion, misery
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=1213
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is actually distress, which is perceived as happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that those averse to God seek pleasure in sense enjoyment, which is illusory and ultimately leads to misery, like a moth drawn to a flame, and emphasizes the importance of understanding the knowledge of the Vedas to avoid this trap.

Then what happens? We need some pleasure, living entities. Then he starts taking pleasure in sense enjoyment. trochetato vimukha cetasa indriyartho. Indriyartho means, just for satisfying the senses, maya sukhae bharam udvahato vimudhan. I am just lamenting for these souls who are making their life miserable simply by indulging themselves in the process of enjoyment of senses, which is not real pleasure, which is maya sukhae bharam udvahato vimudhan. Actually, there is no pleasure at all in enjoying the senses, which includes the mind. Mind dictates us. It puts so many demands. And if we satisfy the demands of the mind, if we satisfy the desires of the senses, that is what is preached in the great universities and modern education as success. But Prahlad Maharaj tells, no, it is a trap like Duryodhana got trapped in the palace constructed by maya demon. Land is taken as water, water as land. So it is maya sukhae. It is actually distress, which is perceived as happiness. And the person, conditioned person is jumping. Person or animals, all the conditioned living entities, like a moth dashing into fire, thinking, oh, this is my happiness. So in this way, simply by indulging in sense enjoyment, person creates more and more misery for himself. So this is why it is, that's why it's very, very important to understand the knowledge of Vedas, Bhagavad Gita, Srimad Bhagavatam.

#### Vaishnava Aparadha, devotional service, teacher
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/BnXZAWkijXQ?t=1464
**Verse References:** —
