**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So only a pure devotee can be situated in perfect knowledge, Krishna tells, because of their proximity, their intimate relationship with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to define Vaishnava Aparadha, explaining that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu instructed Sanatana Goswami and Rupa Goswami about the elements of devotional service and the advantage of associating with pure devotees, who are situated in perfect knowledge due to their intimate relationship with Krishna.

What is this Vaishnava Aparad? So it is told in the Vedas when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was instructing Sanatana Goswami, Rupa Goswami, either of the brothers, then he explained very nicely the various elements of devotional service, how we can take advantage of association of pure devotees. So only a pure devotee can be situated in perfect knowledge, Krishna tells, because of their proximity, their intimate relationship with Krishna. Krishna reveals everything about himself. So that is why it is told shat karma nipuno vipra mantra tantra visharada avaishnavo gurur nasyat vaishnava shvapacho guru. So a teacher of the society is not supposed to take salary like teachers are taking today. So if anybody is taking salary, it means he needs some sense enjoyment for his own self and he has no faith in God that God will maintain me. All these animals are maintained without any job and I am supposed to be intelligent than animals and I am working harder than animals for same physical maintenance which animals are having anyway very very easily without doing any job and any education facing Monday test and ITJE and all those NEET and CAs and all such examinations. Simply for eating, mating, sleeping, defending, animals are able to do it without such rigor which we undergo. So that is why a teacher never does job. He understands, Krishna maintains. And his only task is to understand Krishna and explain others about Krishna. So he is only supposed to be engaged in six activities as we have discussed.

He does not accumulate for his satisfaction. In this, we accept charity. Otherwise, without charity also Krishna maintains such brahmanas. So, I am going to tell you that I am going to tell you that I am going to tell you about the six brahmanas. But, however, a person is very expert in the six features. shat karman nipuna vipra. A person is very learned, very scholarly in the Veda's and he is expert in these six activities but scriptures are mentioning mantra tantra visharada. He is very expert in mantras. Now, there are three kinds of actually science: Mantra, tantra, yantra. So now we are using mantra. Yantra means machines. So this is another science to mantra tantra visharada. So a person is very very powerful, he can chant these wonderful mantras also but still he should not be accepted as spiritual master even though he's performing magical feats, he's expert scholar, he's expert in six kinds of activities. So what is the qualification of being a guru? vaishnava a person does not deserve to become spiritual master if he's not a vaishnava. This is the verdict of the vedas. vaishnava means devotee of vishnu krishna or any of other expansions of the vishnu krishna or any of other expansions of the vishnu krishna or any of other expansions of lord vishnu and vaishnava shvapacho guru some people tell a guru should be born in a brahmana family any person who does not belong to brahmana caste cannot be guru this is a wrong notion so that is why it is told in the vedas a vaishnava guru nasyat and vaishnava if a person is vaishnava shvapacho guru even though he has taken birth in the family of dog eaters he can be accepted as spiritual master.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### THE MISSING POINT | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### happiness, self-interest, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=28
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Many times we might wonder what is missing?"
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the topic of the lecture, 'The Missing Point,' and explains that the session will explore why happiness remains elusive despite individual and collective efforts.

The topic for the day is the missing point. Many times we might wonder what is missing? Why, in spite of our efforts individually and collectively to be happy, to experience satisfaction, contentment, still it remains elusive? It's missing, and we can see it. It's quite obvious if you see the world around us. So what's the missing point? We'll cover this in today's session based on Krishna's teachings in the Gita.

---
#### self-interest, Vishnu, happiness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=228
**Verse References:** SB 11.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The real swartha, the real self-interest is Vishnu, they do not know."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the first point: 'They do not know,' explaining that most people are unaware that their real self-interest is Vishnu, according to the Bhagavatam.

So, today in the discussion on the missing point, we will be broadly covering three important aspects. Each will have a title, and like we have done in our previous sessions, as we move forward, I would request all of you to kindly mention it in the live chat after we have covered each point. The first point is, they do not know. I repeat, they do not know. One might wonder, who is 'they' and what they do not know. So, in the Bhagavatam, he said, na te vidhu, vidhu means to know, na te vidhu, swarth gatim hi vishnu, they do not know. 'They' means the entire populace, by and large, the majority of the people in this world. So, na te vidhu, vidhu means to know, swarth gatim hi vishnu, the real swartha, the real self-interest is Vishnu, they do not know.

---
#### happiness, soul, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=326
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Each one of us are unique, but still there is one thing in common and that is all of us without an exception, rich man, poor man, Indian, American, educated, uneducated, every one of us without an exception, we are pursuing happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the universal pursuit of happiness, noting that despite diverse backgrounds, everyone seeks happiness, which is a natural quality of the soul.

It's quite interesting to observe that each one of us, each one of us, without an exception, we all have different professions, different backgrounds, born in different circumstances, different goals, different aspirations. Each one of us are unique, but still there is one thing in common, and that is all of us without an exception, rich man, poor man, Indian, American, educated, uneducated, every one of us without an exception, we are pursuing happiness. We are wanting to be happy. If we are already happy, we want to increase it. And if we are not happy, we are wanting to be happy. If we are miserable, we are trying to get out of that miserable condition so that we can be happy. And as I mentioned, it's not wrong to pursue happiness because that's the quality. It's the quality of a soul. Anandamayo bhyasat. To be happy. To be happy, to be joyful is not a sin. A soul seeks ananda.

---
#### happiness, effort, modern life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=414
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "People are working very hard, people are not lazy to pursue happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the desire for happiness with the reality of modern life, where people work harder than ever but don't necessarily achieve greater happiness.

So, but in spite of our desire, our inclination, our aspiration to be happy and to maximize our happiness, and in spite of we putting a lot of efforts, people are working very hard, people are not lazy to pursue happiness. There was a time when life was quite relaxed. People would work for seven to eight hours in their offices, in their workplace. I have seen that particular life, living in a township, where people would come back from their workplace at six o'clock in the evening, 6.30. And then, those were the days when you didn't have, you know, internet, you didn't have cable television and all that. So, people would invariably have their early dinner by 8, 8.30. And you will see people strolling, having a night walk, socializing, talking to each other, some people engaging in some sports. But we don't see that everyone is busy. And we are working very hard. Gone are the days where a person works six hours, seven hours, eight hours. These days, 10 hours, 9 hours, 12 hours is very common. We see people working even 14 hours a day. All for happiness.

---
#### happiness, contradiction, statistics
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=505
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We are seeking happiness. We are pursuing happiness. We are working very hard for it."
**Summary:** The speaker points out the contradiction between the pursuit of happiness and the increasing rates of unhappiness, diseases, and suicides, suggesting that current efforts are not yielding the desired results.

Now, here, there is a contradiction. We are seeking happiness. We are pursuing happiness. We are working very hard for it. Everyone, world over, the scientists, the architects, the builders, the engineers, the doctors, everyone is working very hard so that we can be happy. This world can be a wonderful place. A wonderful place for us to all live. But statistics, the numbers prove otherwise. If you see statistics, numbers in terms of the diseases, if you see in terms of hypertension, blood pressure, anxiety, depression, suicides, abortions, the numbers speak a different story. On one side, we want to be happy. But on the other side, what we are getting, in spite of we working very, very hard, very strenuously to achieve that happiness, what we are getting in return is something different. We are more and more unhappy. We are more and more unhappy. We are more and more unhappy.

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public

**Summary:** The speaker questions whether people were happier in the past, before the advent of modern technology and social media, leading to the fundamental question of what the missing point is in the pursuit of happiness.

If we could somehow, although we don't have very easy way to measure happiness, if we could somehow quantify and measure, just like you can measure temperature, it's very objective, 98.7, 101.2. You can measure very objectively. If we were to have some measuring scale to measure happiness and if we were to measure happiness an average person is enjoying or rather is experiencing today in this world, an average person in this world, what he is experiencing, if we could somehow measure, some of you may agree with me that man, 25 years back, minus internet, minus all the gadgets, minus all the modern means of transportation, all the modern means of transportation and communications, all the mobile phones, Facebook, social media, all what we have made for our happiness, communication, transportation, all that, man, 25 years minus all this, maybe was quite happier, much more happier. Some of you may agree with this, what I am saying. So this brings us to a very fundamental question. What is the missing point?

#### happiness, self-interest, Vishnu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=691
**Verse References:** SB 11.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "They do not know, swarth gatim, their real self-interest is Vishnu."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates that the intent to be happy is not wrong, but the missing point is that people do not know their real self-interest lies in Vishnu, according to the Bhagavatam.

Where are we going wrong in our pursuit of happiness? Intent is not wrong. To be happy is not wrong. To work hard, we are doing that. We are not lazy. We are not laid back. But in spite of working very hard and pursuing happiness, why is it that we are getting something which is different from what we are pursuing? Very different from the goal what we have set, that I want to achieve. That I want to be happy. And that's a common goal. The details may vary. Somebody might want to be happy by becoming an architect. Somebody might want to be happy by being a chartered accountant. Somebody might want to be happy to apply for a visa and, you know, go and settle down in some developed country, be in US, in Europe, some other country. Details may vary, but then the end objective is the same. So what is the missing point? So Bhagavatam is saying, na te vidhu. They do not know, swarth gatim, their real self-interest is Vishnu. They should be pursuing Vishnu. The satisfaction of Vishnu.

#### analogy, tragedy, key
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=776
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Okay, while we climb the stairs, let's engage ourselves."
**Summary:** The speaker begins an analogy about three friends who, upon finding the elevator broken in their 30-story apartment building, decide to climb the stairs and tell tragic stories to pass the time.

So one time there were three friends. They were staying on the 30th floor of a building. Tall building. And they were sharing a house, an apartment. The 30th floor of the building. So one day after office hours, they came back home. And they reached the ground floor. All of them came back together, probably working in the same office. And they saw a notice. Apologize for inconvenience caused. The lift is under emergency repair. So lift was not functioning. They are staying on the 30th floor. And they have come back from the office. Completely tired and exhausted. And there they have this notice. Inconvenience regretted. What can they do? It's an emergency repair. So these friends, they thought, Okay, now we have no option but to take the stairs. Reach the 30th floor. And we have no option. So they all talked to each other and said, Okay, while we climb the stairs, let's engage ourselves. Rather than engaging in some meaningless discussion. Let's recall some anecdote, some story. And just for a change, let it be something which is tragic. Let us recall something which we have heard of, some story, some anecdote, which is tragic. And let's have a competition. After we reach our home, our apartment on the 30th floor, we will decide whose story, whose story will take turns. Each one will have to tell a story, an anecdote which is tragic. And we will decide whose story or anecdote was the most tragic. Let's have this competition and I am sure the time will pass very quickly. So they all started off.

**Summary:** The first friend shares a tragic story about a woman who suffered seven miscarriages before finally having a child, only to lose him in a freak accident at the age of seven.

The first one began. And he said, the first time when I heard this story, there were tears in my eyes. It's so tragic. So unfortunate. And then he started off. I know a lady. She had seven miscarriages. She wanted to have a child. She and her husband planned. And unfortunately, every time, be it third month or eighth month, or sixth month, every time for seven times, they had miscarriages. They were in anxiety. They went to many doctors. They consulted many physicians. They took various medications. The desperate need to have a child. And eventually, by Lord's grace, it attempt, they begot a child. And because they had gone through so much of pain, so much of difficulty, so much of anxiety, they were very, very attached to this child. They loved him so much. They pampered him with everything he wanted. Right from the day he was born and by the time, this time, as of today, as of present, he was seven years old. So whenever he would go home, go outside for playing or whatever it is, the mother would anxiously wait. She was so attached. The only child they had. And one day, while this child had gone in the neighborhood with a small bicycle, small bicycle, to play and move around with the friends, while he was returning back, and then he stopped and he said, it's so tragic, it's so tragic. He says, while he was returning back, a car hit him from back and he was spot dead. He was spot dead. And when this, I can't explain to you when the mother, the parents heard about this, what they went through, what they went through, their whole life collapsed. They were shattered. They were broken hearted. I don't think there could be anything more tragic than this. For a mother who has tried to beget a child, had a miscarriage seven times, eventually got it, the only child, and it died untimely because of freak accident. By the time they had reached the 10th floor, and then the second one started.

He says, yes, what you have told is quite tragic, but what I'm going to share with you is even more tragic. It's, it's, I mean, when I heard this story, it took me days to reconcile, to settle down with the shock, with the grief, which I experienced on hearing this real life story. And then he went on to explain that there was a family. And this was, in one sense, a role model. This family, generally in families, you know, there's always a tiff, the undercurrents, misgivings, fights. But here it was quite unique. The husband and wife, they were quite loving, caring. Never ever they had fought in their life. And the children, the children, very good upbringing, very brilliant, excellent in academics. And one fine day, this whole family had gone on a vacation. And they were returning back. And they had a nice time. The entire family, they were in a car. And unfortunately, unfortunately on that day, the line man, generally, you know, in the railway signals, wherever the road crosses the tracks, you have, you know, a line man who closes this thing. He unfortunately was drunk. That day, somehow, he missed putting the bridge, whatever is called, to stop the people to move and cross the track. He forgot that and this. They had, they did no mistake. They were just returning back, normal speed, following all the regulations. They suddenly crossed the track and by that time, the engine and the train came and they were completely smashed, including the car. The entire family collapsed and died on spot. #### tragedy, irony, key

**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "I have forgotten the key to our apartment downstairs."
**Summary:** The third friend, after much reluctance, reveals his 'tragic' story: he forgot the apartment key downstairs, rendering their arduous climb pointless.

By that time, you know, couple of them had started crying. He says, it is very unfortunate. And by the time, they had almost reached the 25th, 26th floor. And then, the turn was for the last one to share his story. And then, he was quite reluctant, quite, should he share, the story is going to be very tragic. It will shatter all of them, including the friends who were there. They will experience the shock of their lives. So, he was reluctant and they kept climbing. So, others said, no, no, share, whatever it is. He says, I have heard both of your stories, but I think the one I am going to speak is very tragic. So, they all said, why don't you speak? And he was reluctant somehow to share this. He says, somehow, I don't have the courage to share this with you. I am sure you all will be so shocked, so much dejected hearing this. And they kept encouraging. And by the time, they had almost reached the 29th floor. They kept climbing and they reached the 29th floor. And then, he says, actually, my story is very small. It is a one-line story. And then, he said, I am sorry to say, it is very difficult for you to take it in stride. I have forgotten the key to our apartment downstairs. And by the time, these two friends, they were completely, you know, ready to hit him. They have spent all the time climbing the stairs. By the time, they reached their apartment, 30th floor. With great difficulty, climbing the stairs one by one. And this person is saying that he has forgotten the key.

#### self-interest, Vishnu, analogy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=1393
**Verse References:** SB 11.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker connects the story to the lecture's theme, warning against forgetting the 'key' of Vishnu consciousness, which would render all material achievements meaningless at the time of death.

It should not happen that we also do the same mistake. And if we are in the same predicament, Na te vidu svart gatim hi Vishnu. They do not know. Then, we will also experience the same thing. Na te vidu svart gatim hi Vishnu. The point when we leave this body, leave this world, we will experience that pain. All what we have painstakingly worked on, acquired, achieved, degrees, bank balance, money, property, assets. Everything will be left behind. It is, we have heard it many times. We also say it. Kya leke aaye the, kya leke jayenge. But, we will experience that. It will be just like, you know, sometimes, a dope test. Recently, a boxer from India was, failed a dope test. Or sometimes, when such things happen, they are stripped of their memory. They are stripped of their medals. It is a painful experience. Lance Armstrong, you know, he was a cycling champion for 8 years. I don't know, it is a very rigorous race for cycling competition. And, he was found doping. Taking drugs. And, he was stripped of all his medals. So, all the achievements will be stripped off. And, we will feel a sense of dejection. That whatever I have worked for, whatever I have achieved, has no meaning. I have to just leave behind everything and just go empty handed. So, what is that main thing which, if we don't have, it's all worthless. It's all meaningless. And, that Bhagavatam is saying, Na te vidhu svart gatini Vishnu. They do not know, that their real self-interest is Vishnu.

#### watering the root, faith, doubt
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=1557
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "He stopped going to the temple, stopped believing in God."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the second point, 'Watering the Root,' and shares a story about a young man who stopped believing in God and going to the temple after watching the movie 'Oh My God' due to its sarcastic portrayal of religious practices.

The second point is, watering the root. I repeat, watering the root. I am sure some of you might have watched the Oh My God, OMG movie. Quite a lot, although we don't watch movies. But, many people spoke to me, Swamiji, have you watched? Swamiji, have you watched? What's the rebuttal? How do you explain what is being mentioned? Lot of things were sarcastically portrayed, and many people were completely shaken, unless they had good spiritual understanding. There is a family known to me, the younger son, he went to that movie, saw the movie, and after that day, he stopped going to the temple, stopped believing in God. He saw in movies certain things, just like, you know, they are doing Abhisheka, and that milk going in the drain, and so many other things, which were very sarcastically portrayed. So, you know, he felt that this is all blind belief, superstitious, we all believe and worship statues, idols, there is no God, and till date, although other family members are practicing Krishna consciousness, till date, he is not budging from his position, and he does not want to chant, does not go to the temple, does not want to follow any spiritual or religious practice.

#### doubt, compassion, worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=1673
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The satisfaction I got, by seeing the smile, on the face of that child, was so satisfying."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an anecdote about a pious woman who, after seeing the movie, questioned the value of offering milk to a Shivling when she could instead give it to a hungry child and witness immediate happiness.

So, when I heard so much, I also watched, because since we conduct many sessions, it's important to know what is, what are people watching, so that, you know, we can address those doubts, which might have crept in the minds of people. So, I was speaking to one lady, she told me, that, this doubt came in my mind also, and she is quite pious, quite religious. So, every, I think, I don't remember, one day in a week, she would go to a Shiva temple, and she would carry a pot of milk, and offer that milk, to that Shivling. So, this lady told me, that one day, I, instead of going to the temple, and offering that milk to Shivling, I saw, a poor child, on my way to the temple. And I could make out that, he had not eaten, was quite lean, it was quite obvious, and he was asking for food, asking for money. So, I just, gave that milk to that boy, and as if he was so hungry, and so thirsty, immediately took that pot, and he drank that milk, and he, the smile, what I saw on his face, was ethereal. The satisfaction I got, by seeing the smile, on the face of that child, was so satisfying. So, the question was, we offer milk, to Shivling, but here we see, a live person, and we offer that, and we can see an immediate, change, happiness on that face, which is very satisfying. So, why should we not, do that? Why should we go to the temple, do Abhishek, why should we, you know, offer garland to the deity, Archa Vigraha, after all, who knows, God's there, not there, it's a statue, it's an idol, we all just have some belief, some faith, but here, it's a boy, who is just there, in the street, hungry, and you give that, you can see the smile, it's quite, quite clear, quite obvious, that you have, helped someone, and, you also experience that joy, the happiness, the satisfaction, of having helped, someone.

#### worship, spiritual understanding, analogy

So, then she asked me, what's wrong in it? Can we not, just do that? Yes, logically speaking, prima facie, at the face value, it looks quite, appealing, and that's what the movie, has taken advantage of, and when people do not know, proper spiritual understanding, of what we do, why we do, then we can get, completely bewildered. So, I gave her an example, let's say, there is, a tree in our, garden, and, for whatever reason, the tree is, completely, drying up, it's drying up, not completely, it's drying up, and, what are the symptoms? The leaves are turning, yellowish, shriveled, and you can make out, that, because of, the hot, the summer, the, there is, definitely, some, deprivation, of water, and, because of which, it is drying up.

#### analogy, root, Vishnu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SgNHnFQL5cs?t=1917
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "You put the water, on the root, and, this, everything."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of watering a tree to explain that just as watering the root nourishes the entire tree, pleasing Vishnu satisfies the entire world.

Now, you have, a water can, you can see, all the leaves, the twigs, drying up, where do we, go and, put water? I asked her, do we, pour water, on the leaves, seeing, that the leaves, are drying up? She said, no. She told me, I said, I asked her, where do we, put the water? She told, at the root. Then, I said, but the roots, are not drying up, in fact, the whole trunk, of the tree, is quite, nourished, it's only the leaves, which are drying up, and, it was quite obvious, this is the right way, to water a plant, a tree, you put the water, on the root, and, this, everything. Same, is the case, here. Naate vidu, swarth, gatimhi, Vishnu, Vishnu, Krishna, is the root. Yatha, taror, moola, nishe chanena, Yatha, taror, taror, means, the tree, moola, nishe chanena, just like, in a tree, if you pour water, on the root, the water, is distributed, water reaches, every, part of that tree. Likewise, if we, please, Vishnu, if we please, Krishna, if we satisfy, by our works, by our actions, by our deeds, by our thoughts, then, yasmin, tushte, jagat, tushta, by satisfying, him, the entire, world, can get satisfied.

In the recent times, because of COVID, there is, no shortage of, contributions, donations, and the relief work, which has happened. In fact, when, the second wave came, and there was, lot of this thing, many countries, were apparent, not having good terms, with India, also sent planes, loaded with, relief, materials. ## HG Amitasana Dasa

### THE MOST MISUNDERSTOOD VERSE OF GITA | HG Gaurmandal Das | GITA LIFE Live | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### misinterpretation, Bhagavad Gita, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=120
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Despite having the Bhagavad Gita, the knowledge said by God, there is no doubt, God gave the Bhagavad Gita, tanta confusion was created because of Srila Prabhupada explains, interpretações erradas."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the Bhagavad Gita as a widely read but often misunderstood book due to misinterpretations, leading to confusion in spiritual life.

Greetings to all of you in today's session. Today we are going to discuss the most known yet misunderstood verse of Bhagavad Gita. Bhagavad Gita is one of the most widely read books, and across all the nationalities, casts, creed, religion, people have been reading Bhagavad Gita for centuries. Srila Prabhupada explains that the Bhagavad Gita is as brilliant as the sun. You don't need any telescope to see the sun. The sun is very bright and self-accusative. It is enormous, very clear. But because of interpretações erradas, it caused a great disaster in the life of the people. Despite having the Bhagavad Gita, the knowledge said by God, there is no doubt, God gave the Bhagavad Gita. And the Bhagavad Gita is part of the Mahabharata, which is for people less intelligent. So, for people less intelligent, they are, in a certain way, supposed to take instructions from the Mahabharata. And the Bhagavad Gita is just a part of it. It's an ABCD of spiritual life. But still, tanta confusion was created because of, Srila Prabhupada explains, interpretações erradas.

---
#### interpretation, Kurukshetra, Dharmakshetra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=230
**Verse References:** BG 1.1
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Why are you saying that Kurukshetra means this body? I would say that Kurukshetra means bread. I would say that Kurukshetra means garlic. Or Kurukshetra means horse. My name of horse is Kurukshetra."
**Summary:** The speaker criticizes the misinterpretation of the first verse of the Bhagavad Gita, specifically the terms Dharmakshetra and Kurukshetra, and argues against symbolic interpretations that deviate from the literal meaning.

Direct from the first verse of the Bhagavad Gita, the people created a disaster. And what is that? In the first verse of the Bhagavad Gita, it is mentioned dharmakshetre, kurukshetre, samveta, yuyutsava, mamaka, pandavas, chaiva, kim akurva sanjaya. Dharmakshetre, kurukshetre. Now, it is very, very clear. So, Dhritarashtra is asking Sanjaya about the Dharmakshetra, place of religion, and the name is Kurukshetra. Samveta yuyutsavaha. The desire is to fight. My sons se juntaram, mamaka e pandavas. The pandavas se juntaram e meus filhos se juntaram. What they did? The desire is to fight. So, this verse is very, very clear. Srila Prabhupada explains, even if you don't know the Sanskrit, Dharmakshetra, it is em hindi, it is a significado direto. Dharmakshetra means place of religion. And Kurukshetra, the name also is said. And if you read other Vedas, Kurukshetra always was called place of religion. So, Kurukshetra, we know that is a trato de terra and is a place of religion. And the references are in many literaturas about it. So, what is the need de dar alguma interpretação a isso? The interpretação is necessária when something is not clear, when the verse is very críptico and a mensagem muito complexa is being dita. Could have some verses in which the nature of the personality supreme is being described, the nature of the world spiritual is described, what may not be very easy to understand. But here is very simple. A fato histórico is being given, a narração. So, you are taking essa Kurukshetra as not a peça de terra, but Kurukshetra as this body, as some say. Many famous publicadores têm publicado seu Bhagavad Gita em que os comentadores disseram que essa Kurukshetra is, na verdade, this body. And os pandavas are our sensos, Kauravas are os vices within of ourselves. So, pravopatas, como assim, we can interpretar de qualquer forma. Why are you saying that Kurukshetra means this body? I would say that Kurukshetra means bread. I would say that Kurukshetra means garlic. Or Kurukshetra means horse. My name of horse is Kurukshetra. So, como assim, you can continue dando interpretações ilimitadas. But, even in the world literário, if we have to interpretar qualquer coisa, must be done only when o significado direto is not evident. Senão, no use of nenhum livro de conhecimento.

---
#### interpretation, direct meaning, law
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=392
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Therefore, o significado direto must be taken."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of taking the direct meaning of scriptures and laws, using examples from science and law to illustrate how misinterpretations can render knowledge useless.

If a scientist has given a declaração and then you start to interpretar aquela declaração, then no use of this book of science. If an advogado has given alguma lei and you start to interpretar a lei de suas próprias maneiras, is necessário that isso be done when o significado direto is not evident. But, when o significado direto is evident, if a pessoa mata alguém, he has to be preso até a morte. So, preso para ser preso de quê? We understand that he has to be preso em uma notícia and he has to perder a vida. You say that preso means that he has to be preso em um esforço no parque, no parque da sociedade. So, assim you can interpretar or dar outra interpretação. So, qual é o uso dessa interpretação? Therefore, o significado direto must be taken. Continue a trabalhar muito duro. Você não está ganhando lucro? Continue a trabalhar muito duro. Não se preocupe com o resultado. Apenas continue a trabalhar muito duro.

---
#### spiritual instructions, Krishna, relationship with God
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=513
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Na verdade, todas as instruções espirituais, Bhagavad Gita ou Srimad Bhagavatam, estão lá para desmotivar uma pessoa na vida espiritual. Demônios querem tirar as pessoas fora de Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that true spiritual instructions aim to detach individuals from material motivations and connect them with Krishna, emphasizing that God desires a relationship with His children.

Na verdade, todas as instruções espirituais, Bhagavad Gita ou Srimad Bhagavatam, estão lá para desmotivar uma pessoa na vida espiritual. Na verdade, essa motivação é uma tendência demoníaca. Demônios querem tirar as pessoas fora de Krishna. Então é muito fácil entender o que é Deus se pudermos nos entendermos. O que é Deus? A fonte, a origem de tudo. Então a origem deve ter tudo que está lá na criação. Então é por isso que o Veda Vyasa descreve o que é Deus. A verdade absoluta é a origem de todos. Então é por isso que todas as tendências que nós temos, todas essas tendências devem estar presentes na verdade absoluta. Nós queremos falar, nós queremos andar, nós queremos ouvir, nós queremos socializar. Nós produzimos filhos para que nossos filhos possam estar conosco e nós possamos se divertir com eles. Então por que essa tendência está lá em nós? Porque essa tendência deve estar presente na origem suprema, a nossa origem também. É muito, muito simples. Senão, a tendência de comer, andar, dormir, socializar, não pode se desenvolver na criação, a menos que esteja lá na fonte da criação. Então todas as qualidades, formas, belezas, o que quer que nós vejamos ao nosso redor, isso deve estar vindo da origem da criação, da verdade absoluta. Então nós somos, assim como nós queremos que nossos filhos, nossos membros de família estejam conosco e vivam conosco e participem do nosso aproveitamento de uma forma similar, Krishna também deseja que nós devemos viver com Krishna. Então ninguém pode separar Deus dos seus membros de família. Deus é eterno. Porque todas as energias estão sob seu controle. Ninguém pode colocar Deus à morte. Mas nós estamos nascendo e morrendo repetidamente, porque essas energias não estão em nosso controle. Nós somos forçados a ser forçados a morrer, mas ninguém pode forçar Deus ou as pessoas que são muito amados a Deus, que estão servindo a Deus. Portanto, é muito simples entender o propósito da criação. Nossa tendência é manter nossos relacionamentos conosco. Tendência similar que Deus tem. Nós somos todos anjos de Deus. Nós viemos de Deus. Portanto, como os pais desejam que seus filhos estejam conosco, Deus deseja que nós vivemos com ele.

---
#### science, God, purpose of creation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=644
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Nós não estamos interessados em conhecer Deus. Nós não estamos interessados em conhecer a fonte. Nós não estamos interessados em conhecer a mente de Deus."
**Summary:** The speaker criticizes the modern focus of science on material analysis rather than understanding God's mind and purpose behind creation, suggesting that true science should aim to please God.

E simplesmente, se nós vivermos com Deus, então nós teremos uma vida eterna. Não há morte, não há idade velha e não há doenças. E Deus é muito opulento. Então, nós estamos tentando entender o mundo inteiro. Nós estamos tentando analisar os elementos da mesa periódica, os dispositivos semicondutores, o chip, a química no chip. E o chão e todas as folhas insignificantes. Mas nós não estamos interessados em conhecer Deus. Nós não estamos interessados em conhecer a fonte. E nós não estamos interessados em conhecer a mente de Deus. Nós devemos entender qual é a mente de Deus por trás dessa criação. E essa foi a base na qual as ciências foram iniciadas. Como muitos cientistas famosos disseram, eu acho que ele foi Einstein ou Newton, ou qualquer um deles. Eles disseram, eu não estou interessado, eu acho que é Einstein. Nesse fenômeno ou nesse fenômeno, eu quero saber qual é a mente de Deus. A ciência estava lá para entender a origem da criação. Mas então, como acontece com qualquer processo de yogi, uma vez que você começa a se aproximar da verdade absoluta, você desenvolve diversos poderes. E quando o yogi é atraído pelos poderes, ele não será capaz de se aproximar de Deus. Então, os yogis são deviados pelos poderes místicos. Diferentemente, a ciência, também desenvolve alguns poderes místicos. E agora todos os estudantes da ciência estão perdidos nesses poderes místicos, criando instrumentos de aproveitamento do senso. E o propósito está perdido. A ciência está perdida. Há apenas tecnologia agora que está criando instrumentos de aproveitamento do senso. Mas não. Nós devemos estar interessados em saber qual é a mente de Deus, por que Deus a criou. E nós devemos apenas estar interessados em dar prazer a Deus.

---
#### God, technology, simple living
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=757
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Ninguém é realmente um cientista real. Um cientista real significa um que tenta entender."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that true scientists should focus on understanding God and the source of creation, rather than creating instruments for sense gratification, and criticizes the over-reliance on technology that disrupts simple living.

Se Deus está satisfeito conosco, é isso ou não? Ou é muito difícil? É muito simples entender que Deus criou tudo e todos. Qualquer pequena máquina que você queira... Então qualquer pequena máquina que nós temos que fazer, então ela precisa de um enorme esforço. Eu estava falando com um devoto que está nesse negócio de desenvolver algumas máquinas muito complexas. Então vai demorar muitos anos para desenvolver uma máquina simples. E esse corpo é tão complexo. Os cientistas estão tentando criar robôs humanoides. Então, na verdade, tudo isso é um grande enganamento para as massas. Ninguém é realmente um cientista real. Um cientista real significa um que tenta entender. Pode haver, mas é muito raro. Principalmente, é apenas... É apenas sobre criar instrumentos para satisfazer os sentidos das pessoas. Mas, na verdade, nós devemos estar interessados em saber o que é Deus, qual é a fonte de criação. Então agora o cheiro está acontecendo. A chamada comunidade, tecnologia, ciências... Todo o esforço está indo apenas para resolver alguns problemas que não são significativos ou problemas que são criados pela tecnologia em si. Como tudo começou? Tudo foi muito simples. E as pessoas estavam vivendo um estilo de vida simples, sem nenhuma máquina. Você estaria lá no campo e você cultivaria suas propriedades e viveria muito tranquilamente. Então eles não saberiam que nós tínhamos uma máquina. Por que você está usando um buraco? Isso leva muito tempo. Deixe-nos ter um trator. E então eles trouxeram tractores e outras coisas. E agora o buraco é inútil, sem trabalho. E então começaram... Eles começaram as casas de assalto, começaram a matar os buracos. É por isso que o desastre está disposto a acontecer.

---
#### Krishna, technology, materialism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=877
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Nós somos todos filhos de Krishna em vestes diferentes."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that humans are all children of Krishna and should not destroy nature or rely on unnecessary technology, as it leads to disaster, and criticizes the materialistic focus on sense gratification.

Nós somos todos filhos de Krishna em vestes diferentes. Nós poderíamos nos tornar buracos. Nós poderíamos começar a viver em vestes de monstro ou qualquer outra coisa. E se nós os matarmos, a natureza não nos permitirá viver tranquilamente. Então o desastre de guerra está disposto a acontecer. Então, de qualquer forma, nós estávamos usando... O trator não era necessário. Automóveis não eram necessários. Elefantes estavam lá. E... Camelos estavam lá. Oxen... Krishna fez uma organização. Mas não havia nenhum problema real. Mas nós queríamos resolver esse chamado problema insignificante. E, portanto, o que nós criamos ao nosso redor... Um grande desastre. Industrialização. E o planeta vai ser destruído. Então as pessoas dizem... Não, eles estão criando robôs que imitam seres humanos. Um dia eles poderão criar seres humanos também. Então nós temos que entender. Esqueça de criar seres humanos. Deixe qualquer um criar um grão de arroz no laboratório. Um pedaço de sangue no laboratório. Quando houver algum acidente, nós queremos... Oh, por favor, dê um pouco de sangue. E esse grupo de sangue... Nós não podemos nem mudar o grupo de sangue. Nós não podemos fazer essa manipulação. Nós não podemos criar um pedaço de sangue em nossos laboratórios. Nós não podemos criar um pedaço de arroz em nossos laboratórios. Nós não podemos criar um pedaço de água em nossos laboratórios. As pessoas estão morrendo. Muitos, muitos países desenvolvidos... Eles se tornam secos às vezes. Na Índia e em outros países. E deixando sozinho... Criar um pedaço de água. Muita água está lá. A água salina está lá no oceano. Apenas transformar isso em água potável. Isso também a tecnologia não pode fazer. Então, quem nós estamos enganando? Na verdade, todo o mundo está cheio de materialistas que estão simplesmente interessados em satisfazer seus próprios sensos. Ninguém está realmente interessado em qualquer real alcançamento científico ou resolver os problemas da vida. Mas eu quero fazer algum dinheiro e criar instrumentos de aproveitamento do senso. E então isso cria... Dezenas de outros problemas. Instrumentos de aproveitamento do senso.

#### materialism, God, satisfaction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=993
**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker criticizes the endless cycle of creating and solving problems caused by materialistic pursuits, emphasizing humanity's dependence on God and the need to seek His satisfaction.

E então você resolve esses problemas. Dessa forma, está acontecendo. Mas na verdade, nós não podemos criar qualquer coisa significativa. Um pedaço de arroz não pode ser criado em laboratório. Nós estamos dependentes de Deus. Um pedaço de água não pode ser criado. Ou até mesmo desalinado. Um pedaço de sangue não pode ser criado. O que se fala em criar órgãos como a língua, o fígado e outras coisas no corpo? E o que se fala em criar seres humanos? Eles nunca poderão fazer isso. Primeiramente, criar um pedaço de água, um pedaço de sangue ou uma simples célula. Uma célula simples. Deixe-a ser criada. E depois, nós falaremos de órgãos complexos e tantos outros sistemas complexos dentro desse corpo. Não é? Então, nós estamos completamente dependentes de Deus. Então, em vez de se esforçar para este avanço científico insignificante, que é nada. Nada, mas simplesmente algumas coisas que estamos criando em nosso meio, mas não podemos fazer nenhum contribuinte significativo para resolver os problemas reais da vida. Os problemas reais da vida são a morte, a idade velha, a doença, a fervura, o fome, a grudade. Essas coisas não podem ser resolvidas por qualquer deiro científico. Para isso, nós temos que nos depender de Deus. Deus nos dará graças. Ele nos dará vales. E tantas outras coisas. então estamos completamente dependentes de Deus então Deus nos quer, você por favor esteja comigo, você por favor venha para mim então devemos tentar entender Deus como podemos vir para Deus, qual é a mente de Deus como podemos satisfazê-lo então é simples saber que há um Criador um pedaço de água não podemos criar um pedaço de sangue não podemos criar então há alguém que precisa de engenharia complexa que criou tudo então é alguém que criou tudo deixe-me tentar satisfazê-lo isso deve ser inteligente.

**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a deep, dark well (andhukupam) to describe materialistic family life, warning that it appears attractive but is ultimately empty and leads to suffering.

Assim como Shreemad Bhagavatam também hitvaatma paatam griham andhukupam vanam gato yad harimashrayetam Bhagavatam está dizendo griham andhukupam nós pensamos, oh deixe-nos trabalhar muito bem deixe-nos estudar muito bem e ter um bom emprego ou um bom negócio então teremos uma vida de casa muito boa em que teremos muita alegria tanta alegria mas Bhagavatam, a última versão do mais avançado escolar de todos os tempos Maharshi Ved Vyas está sendo escrito lá hitvaatma paatam griham andhukupam e todos os sages, todos os seers da verdade disseram este griham, esta vida de casa para a qual planejamos para a qual estudamos tanto para a qual construímos diversos negócios e empregos e nossos perfis e nosso corpo e músculos para que eu possa ter uma boa vida de casa e eu acho que essa vai ser a fonte da minha alegria mas em Bhagavatam está mencionado andhukupam é como um velho profundo profundo e profundo então por que a vida de casa é chamada de um velho profundo e profundo? então é explicado muito bem então, às vezes um velho que não tem água é abandonado é muito profundo e está vazio não tem água e porque está abandonado é cobrido de uma espécie fina de terra então, às vezes algum viajante que está lá indo na selva ele pode errar para ser apenas uma peça de terra cobrida de terra não percebendo que é um velho profundo e profundo e cai nele então, às vezes um viajante que está lá indo na selva ele pode errar por isso uma pessoa pensa que é apenas um velho profundo essa vida de casa é um velho profundo é uma peça de terra muito bonita mas ele não pode ver a escuridão dessa vida de casa e ele cai nele então é por isso que a vida de família materialista é chamada de velho profundo profundo abandonado uma pessoa pensa que é um velho profundo mas na verdade é um velho profundo e ele cai nele.

#### material life, family life, pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=1340
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Nós somos muito amigáveis se eu tiver uma mulher linda, um marido ou se eu tiver uma mulher linda, eu tenho um marido muito bonito e muito lindos filhos, nós vamos ter uma hora muito legal e algumas independências financeiras então nós vamos gostar daquilo, mas é dito por um Mit suta ramani samaje, from spouse, from friends, samaj in society, I have created this lifestyle. What is the pleasure which I am getting from this? It is like tatal saikate, like a traveler who is lost in the desert."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the analogy of the deep well, explaining that people approach family life seeking pleasure, but it is like searching for water in a dry well, ultimately unsatisfying.

E então outra razão por que é chamado dessa maneira é porque não há água nisso geralmente uma pessoa se aproxima de um velho por causa de água assim uma pessoa pode se aproximar desse velho pensando que oh haveria água nisso e eu vou cozinhar minha fome então nós somos muito amigáveis se eu tiver uma mulher linda um marido ou se eu tiver uma mulher linda eu tenho um marido muito bonito e muito lindos filhos nós vamos ter uma hora muito legal e algumas independências financeiras então nós vamos gostar daquilo mas é dito por um Mit suta ramani samaje, from spouse, from friends, samaj in society, I have created this lifestyle. What is the pleasure which I am getting from this? It is like tatal saikate, like a traveler who is lost in the desert. Vari bindu sama, and he is very thirsty, he is dying out of thirst. And if you give him just one drop of water, will that satisfy such a lost traveler? Not at all. In a similar fashion, we are hankering for unlimited pleasure. But the pleasure which we get is a drop of water in the desert. Mana kabu sukhan hai pa, a person is never satisfied. So we approach this family life for pleasure, but there is no pleasure. Just like a person approaches well for water, but there is no water in it.

#### material life, suffering, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=1453
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "From our own efforts, we cannot come out of it. So that rope is the instruction given by spiritual master."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the analogy of the deep well, stating that once someone falls into materialistic family life, they cannot escape on their own and need the help of a spiritual master's instructions.

That is why it is called andhukupam. And another reason, third reason why it is called andhukupam is, once we fall into that pleasure, we are not satisfied. Once we fall into that well, there is no water in it, covered by grass, it is abandoned. So nobody goes to, in a regular well, people would keep on coming to fetch water, but nobody goes to an abandoned well in the jungle. So thus, if a person falls into it, he may keep on crying for help, but nobody would be able to hear and help that person. So thus, people are crying for help. We see so many, everywhere there is suffering, they are crying. Please help us, governments, please help us. There are bombs, there are wars. There is pestilence, there is stress, there is anxiety. Even the celebrities are opening up. So earlier, internet was not there, connectivity was not there. Now we all know. The most successful of the people are also crying, and they are telling you, please open up about your mental state. So the most successful of the people are having very, very terrible time, so it is a great illusion. We can be happy without satisfying God. We are connected to God like a leaf to the tree. So thus, we keep on crying, but nobody would be able to help us. And fourth, the reason why it is called deep, dark, well, Andhukupam is, from Andhukupam, there is only one way to come out. And only when a person from outside, he drops a rope. From our own efforts, we cannot come out of it. So that rope is the instruction given by spiritual master. That is the only way if we hold on to that rope, if we hold on to the instruction, then we can come out of this Andhukupam. Otherwise, there is suffering. So all these things are threadbare, discussed in Srimad Bhagavatam. In Kalyuga, when people are completely ignorant, this Purana will give light. So Srimad Bhagavatam very clearly and openly discusses all these things.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that Srimad Bhagavatam describes the basis of household life as sex pleasure, which is ultimately third-class and unsatisfying, and distinguishes between grahasthas (those in household life for spiritual advancement) and grahamedhis (those in it for personal enjoyment).

So then further, Srimad Bhagavatam explains, what is the basis of this house of light? What is the greatest source of enjoyment, which we think? That is sex pleasure. But Srimad Bhagavatam mentions, yan maithunadi grahamedhi sukham hituchham. So grahamedhi, actually, the kind of householders which the society is producing today, they are not grahasthas. So actually, that is grahamedhi. So most of us are being trained as grahamedhi. Grahamedhi means, one who enters into household life for personal enjoyment, which is based on sex enjoyment. This is why we marry, isn't it? We think, now we can have sex unlimited times. And society also will not object. And I will be very happy doing this sexual activity. But then nobody is satisfied by doing that. Everyone marries and tries to enjoy this act. But there is so much of embarrassment. So Prahalad Maharaj is telling, tuchham, this is third class pleasure. It appears very very attractive, just like miraj in the desert. But it is tuchham, it is third class pleasure. Neha tripyanti kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana, swakarati kripana. A vida doméstica não é ruim, a menos que seja a vida de um grahastha, não a vida de um grahamedhi.

#### family life, detachment, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=1674
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Ashram significa que elas entram na vida doméstica para o prazer de Krishna, para o avanço espiritual."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts grahastha and grahamedhi life, explaining that grahasthas enter household life for Krishna's pleasure and spiritual advancement, while grahamedhis do so for personal enjoyment, leading to envy and dissatisfaction.

Então, idealmente, os Vedas estão dizendo que você, por favor, pratique a detachamento neste mundo e você pode continuar como um brahmachari. Mas isso pode ser muito difícil para as pessoas ordinárias seguirem. Então, é por isso que a vida grahastha também é perfeitamente certo se uma pessoa é realmente grahastha, se ela está situada em um grahastha ashram. Ashram significa que elas entram na vida doméstica para o prazer de Krishna, para o avanço espiritual. Mas grahamedhi significa que uma pessoa entra na vida doméstica para o prazer pessoal. Mas isso é uma atividade muito, muito desagradável. Para um prazer pessoal, se a gente entra, então nós nos tornamos grahamedhi. Medhi significa enveja. O coração está cheio de enveja. Agora, eu não estou satisfeito quando vejo meus amigos e relacionados indo em frente a mim no desenvolvimento econômico. Eu quero se encaixar e superar o seu status. E meu coração está continuamente quebrando. E se eles sofrem, eles não se tornam sucessivos, eu recebo paz na minha vida. Isso é chamado de grahamedhi. Sociedade de competitividade intensa. Eu quero derrotar os outros. Não há amor. Há apenas enveja. Então, grahamedhi, neha tripyanti, ele nunca está satisfeito em uma vida doméstica como essa. Portanto, idealmente, permane dedicado ao serviço de Krishna. Não entre nesse andhupupam. Mas se você quiser entrar em uma vida doméstica, então você pode se tornar grahastha. Em grahastha ashram também, geralmente, essa é uma instrução geral, permanecer sozinho é muito difícil. Se você puder fazer isso, nós podemos tentar. Mas, se não for possível, nós podemos entrar em uma vida doméstica.

#### family life, devotion, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1CmMmO_jJKk?t=1793
**Verse References:** BG 2.47
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Você tem o direito de fazer o seu dever, não nos resultados."
**Summary:** The speaker advises that the basis for entering household life should be finding a devotee partner to advance spiritually, and emphasizes the importance of following the instruction of karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana (BG 2.47), which means you have the right to do your duty, not on the results.

Now, here, the base não deveria ser o quanto eu vou receber dores, quanto eu vou receber prazer físico ou mental. Não. Apenas veja se eu estou recebendo um devoto ou não. Tenha um parceiro devoto, e ambos nós podemos então avançar em vida espiritual. Esse deveria ser o objetivo quando entramos em grahastha ashram. Então, se uma pessoa for um devoto, para a satisfação de Krishna, então ele também pode ser feliz. Mas ele tem que segurar essa corda que o mestre espiritual, Krishna, está colocando dentro da vila. E qual é essa instrução? Essa é a instrução desse shloka: karmaṇeva adhikāraste mā faleshu kadācana karmaṇeva adhikāraste mā faleshu kadācana. So, karmaṇeva adhikāraste mā faleshu kadācana Então, o significado

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### THE POWER OF PRAYER | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### prayer, Vaishnavism, Gita Life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Although prayers mean something which we express from our heart, words really don't matter, it's the feelings, it's the bhava, which actually is the main thing."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa introduces the topic of the power of prayer, outlining the session's agenda, including the importance of prayer, do's and don'ts, and authorized prayers, emphasizing the significance of feelings over words.

Thank you. Hare Krishna. Warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session. The topic for the day is the power of prayers. It's a very interesting topic. Today we will learn why it is important for us to offer prayers, some do's and don'ts about the prayers. All of us do pray in one form or another, but sometimes, who knows, unknowingly, unintentionally, we may be praying the wrong way. So we will talk about the do's and don'ts. And towards the end also I would like to share some authorized prayers. Although prayers mean something which we express from our heart, words really don't matter, it's the feelings, it's the bhava, which actually is the main thing. But nevertheless, I will share with you some authorized prayers. So before we get into this discussion, let's all fold our hands and offer prayers together.

#### relationship, communication, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=254
**Verse References:** BG 15.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Prayer is an important means by which we communicate with our supreme father, Lord Shri Krishna."
**Summary:** The lecture transitions to the importance of prayer, emphasizing it as a means of communication to nurture our relationship with Lord Krishna, who is a person with feelings and emotions, and we are his part and parcel.

So the first aspect which we will talk about today is the importance of prayers. Why we all need to pray on a regular basis? What is the importance? What is the need? For any relationship to develop, we need to communicate. Communication is very important. Communication is the key to nurturing a good relationship. So likewise Lord Krishna is a person. He is not some abstract force, some power. He is a person with feelings, with emotions. And Krishna in the Gita says, mamaivamso jiva loke jivabhuta sanatana. We are his amshas. We are his part and parcels. We have a relationship with him. That's a different thing that today we have forgotten that relationship. We are not very conscious of that relationship. Just like a child may forget who his father is, but still the father does exist. He has a father. He may not remember him. He may not be grateful to him. He may not be obligated to him. He may forget him, but still the father does exist. So likewise Krishna says, mamaivamso jiva loke jivabhuta sanatana. We are his amshas. We are his part and parcel. We have a relationship with him. But unfortunately today we have forgotten that relationship. That relationship has covered our attention to him. Our consciousness which we should be thinking of him is now absorbed in thinking of so many other things. So if we have to nurture that relationship, then we need to on a regular basis communicate, speak. Just like any relationship between husband and wife, let's say father and a child, they talk to each other. They communicate to each other. So likewise, prayer is an important means by which we communicate with our supreme father, Lord Shri Krishna.

---
#### guidance, faith, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=416
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Prayer is an important tool or an important medium by which we can seek that direction, clarity from the Lord."
**Summary:** Prayer is also important for seeking clarity and resolving dilemmas, as exemplified by Arjuna in the Bhagavad Gita, and for gaining direction from the Lord, who is all-knowing and present in everyone's heart.

Prayer is also important because many a times in our lives, we will come across some dilemmas, some confusion, some doubts. We will be on crossroads, knowing which path to choose. An example being Arjuna in the battlefield of Kurukshetra. He was in a dilemma. He was in confusion whether he should fight this war or not. So in that confused bewildered state, he approached the Lord, revealed his mind, revealed his confusion, revealed his dilemma and sought clarity. So prayers are also important because it can give us clarity. It can help us resolve dilemmas. We all will go through doubts, confusions, challenges in life, and we will need direction. So prayer is an important tool or an important medium by which we can seek that direction, clarity from the Lord. Having faith that the Lord is, the Lord is all knowledgeable. The Lord is all knowing. He knows everything. He knows the past. He knows the present. He knows the future. He is there in everyone's heart. He knows what is good for us. He is the supreme creator. He is the source of all knowledge. So with that faith, we can seek direction from the Lord.

---
#### gratitude, glorification, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=512
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Prayer is also an important way whereby we can express our gratitude, our thanks for all what the Lord has done for us and continuing to do for us."
**Summary:** Prayer serves as a means to express gratitude for the blessings the Lord has bestowed upon us, including the basic necessities of life, which are often taken for granted, and to recognize our limitations and dependence on the Lord.

Now I am a soul surrendered unto you. Arjuna tells Krishna, please instruct me. So we can, in our prayers, seek direction from the Lord and seek his advice, his instructions, his guidance. So that's the second aspect. One is communication to nurture the relationship. And any relationship required, requires communication, some interaction. Second is, when we need some directions, guidance. Third is, sheer glorification. Lord has given us so many things. Some we are conscious of, some we have taken for granted. For example, even small insignificant things like the air we breathe, the water we drink, the sunlight, the food we eat, seldom we stop and we thank the Lord. We have taken it for granted as if it's our birthright. For example, we have taken it for granted that we can go anywhere, we can meet anyone, we can do anything. But during lockdown, we got some iota of that understanding that how we are limited. We have limitations. Suddenly the conditions can change and our so-called freedom, so-called independence can be curtailed. See what's happening in Afghanistan. What's happening to millions of Afghans who are now refugees in different countries. So, prayer is also an important way whereby we can express our gratitude, our thanks for all what the Lord has done for us and continuing to do for us. All the things which He has blessed us with. So prayer is an important medium to express our gratitude, our thanks.

**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Prayer is also important because at times when we feel weak, when we are in a difficult situation, we all will need strength, we all will need help, we all will need some support."
**Summary:** Prayer is essential for seeking strength and support during difficult times, illustrated by the story of a wealthy man who, despite his resources, felt helpless when his son needed urgent medical care during the pandemic.

Prayer is also important because at times when we feel weak, when we are in a difficult situation, we all will need strength, we all will need help, we all will need some support. And at times, in spite of having everything, we will feel that we are helpless. Just yesterday I was speaking to a gentleman who came to meet me. He was talking about that this was during the second wave, May month, when it was peak of the infections and all the hospital beds were all full, there was shortage of oxygen. So a person who is very very rich, filthy rich, his son about 22 years old started having breathing problems. He was sick, he was diagnosed with COVID and he had breathing issues. So he was from a relatively smaller city close to Jaipur. And this person rushed to Jaipur and on his way called up this person whom I was speaking to yesterday and had said that I am coming to Jaipur, please do something, arrange a bed for me, arrange a bed for my son, he is sick, he needs oxygen support. So the person said wait, wait, wait, let me just check up the availability, just wait. He says no, I have started from my home, I am moving towards Jaipur, please arrange, do something. He is in bad state. And he also said money is not an issue, I am carrying with me 20 lakh cash. Anything is required, you want to pay to the hospital, you want to pay to the doctor, you want to pay to the nursing home, pay for any medicine, there is no shortage of money, I am carrying with me 20 lakh rupees cash. I am on my way to Jaipur, please do something.

---
#### helplessness, faith, change
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=802
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "For first time in my life, I saw that in spite of having the best of contacts, in spite of having the money, in spite of having good relationships, I saw that it was all, I was so helpless."
**Summary:** Continuing the story, the speaker describes the father's frantic attempts to secure a hospital bed for his son, highlighting the helplessness felt despite wealth and connections, and the subsequent shift towards religiousness after the son's recovery.

Now this was the force, imagine the circumstances. So this person frantically starts calling the doctors whom he knows, starts calling the IS officers whom he knows, but nobody is lifting his hand. But nobody is lifting the calls. The doctors who have worked under him, they are not lifting the calls. Some near and dear ones who are in the medical profession, they are not lifting the calls. The regular doctors, the family doctors whom he regularly visits, good acquaintance, they are not lifting the calls. So he is feeling helpless. And then this person lands up in Jaipur. There is no bed available. Finally with great difficulty, they could manage to get into a hospital and that son for some time had to be put on oxygen support in a corridor of the hospital. So this person was telling, for first time in my life, I saw that in spite of having the best of contacts, in spite of having the money, in spite of having good relationships, I saw that it was all, I was so helpless. Luckily that person could get that oxygen support and he could recover. So the next question I was asking, did he recover? 22 year old boy. So since then this person also has become quite religious and he was telling that how he has little bit calmed down. Otherwise he was very passionate. Money, money, money, more money. So he says I have calmed down a bit. I have seen that when the time comes, the time comes, these are all not going to help.

#### strength, death, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=911
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We all will need strength. We all will feel helpless at times."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the universal need for strength and support, even for those who appear to have everything, referencing a doctor needing prayer for her sister's surgery and quoting Srimad Bhagavatam on the illusion of security provided by material possessions and relationships in the face of death.

So we all will go through phases. The other day our doctor called me and her sister is going through major surgery. She is diagnosed with cancer and major surgery. And she called up, although she is a very eminent doctor, helps us quite often. We also consult her. But she was needing some help, some support, some strength. Her sister was about to get into the ICU and the preparations were on for the surgery, operation theatre. And she called me. So we also need prayers for strength, for support. At times we will feel weak. At times we will feel incapable. In spite of that, in spite of having everything, we will feel that they are not enough. Srimad Bhagavatam says that this Sainya, the soldiers, Deha Aapta Kalatra Deshu Atma Saineshu Asat Swapi Deha means this good, strong, stout body. Aapta, relatives, friend circles. Kalatra Deshu, good, loving wife. Children, relatives, near and dear ones. Kalatra Adishu. Adishu means et cetera, like that. When we are surrounded by an army of well-wishers, with lot of money, with lot of support, with lot of insurance policies, we get a feeling that we are now safe, we are now secure. Bhagavatam says, Asat Sainya. These Sainya, these soldiers, which we have gathered around us, to protect us, they are Asat Sainya, they are fallible soldiers. When the actual war will start, when the actual time will come, you will see that these Sainya, the soldiers, will not be capable, will have limitation in how much they can support us, how much they can protect us. Tesham Pramata Nidhanam Pashyan Apeena Pashyati A person who is maddened, who is in illusion, who is bewildered with Deha Aapta, Deha means I and mine, who is in this illusion. Tesham Pramata Nidhanam He doesn't think that eventually one day death will come and all of them will be helpless. I will have to surrender to death in spite of having so many protective layers around me. I will have to surrender to death in spite of having so many protective layers around me. I will have to surrender to death in spite of having so many protective layers around me. This is statement of the Bhagavatam.

**Summary:** Prayer is also about spending time with the Lord without a specific agenda, similar to how families spend time together out of love, nurturing the relationship through interaction and shared feelings.

So, we all will need strength. We all will feel helpless at times. And then, so this is another thing we need prayers for. And then we need prayers also, just to, as I said, communication. We all have, when we get up in the morning, we have some rituals. Yes, it's very very important that we spend time with the Lord. We spend time with our loving Father. Even if we don't have any agenda, just like the family gets together and talks to each other, there is no well-defined agenda, there is no particular thing to achieve. They just spend time together because they love each other. Prayer is also an important aspect where we just spend time with the Lord with no particular agenda. There is no checklist, there is no tick marks to be done. We just want to spend time with our loving Father. Be in His presence, in His association. This also nurtures the relationship. As I said, investing time, investing energy, investing feelings, interacting, these all are important aspects whereby any relationship gets nurtured. So these are the few reasons. There are many more reasons. I just thought before I could share few of these reasons why it is important to incorporate prayers in our daily schedule.

Faith that the Lord is listening to my prayers. If you are offering prayers with doubts, I am offering prayers, don't know whether Lord is hearing or not hearing, not sure whether He will respond, He will reciprocate, He will answer my prayers, He will answer my prayers. So, one of the very important components of prayer is faith. Yes, Lord does exist. Yes, Lord is my loving Father. Yes, He listens to me.

There are so many great devotees, Lord is busy with them, He is interacting with them, who will hear my voice? Just like a person can think, how can I meet the Prime Minister of the country? How will He listen to me? Why will He listen to me? Such limitations don't exist in the Lord. The Lord has the ability to listen to each and every Jeeva, each and every person. Sometimes, you know, when we are, when there are a lot of people reporting to us or something, at times somebody wants our time, but we are not able to give up, we give our time, because we ourselves are so busy. We find that, yes, we have limitation, but that's not the limitation with the Lord. So, with that faith, faith is a very important component, where we have the faith that, yes, the Lord exists, He listens to my prayers,

#### Guru, prayer, channel
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=1424
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is important that when we offer prayers, we go through our Guru, the spiritual Master, and then Gauranga, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and then the previous Acharyas, and then to the Lord."
**Summary:** It's important to approach the Lord through the proper channel, starting with the Guru, then Gauranga, the previous Acharyas, and finally the Lord, rather than directly addressing the Lord.

Another important aspect, it's not right that we simply start talking to the Lord. It is important that when we offer prayers, we go through our Guru, the spiritual Master, and then Gauranga, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, and then the previous Acharyas, and then to the Lord. It is not okay to start with prayer directly speaking to the Lord, O Lord, please give me this, give me that, O Lord, You are great. Whenever we offer prayers, whenever we have a session, we are praying to the Lord, it is always important, just like even the session we start, we start with Nama, Om, Vishnu, Pada, we offer prayer to Shila Prabhu, we offer prayer to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, we offer prayers to previous Acharyas, we offer prayers to Krishna. So we have to go in that order. Just like if you get an appointment with, a crude example, with Narendra Modi, for getting that appointment, you have to go through a process. You have to speak to someone who is close to him. He in turn will speak to somebody in the Prime Minister's office, his secretary or whatever, and then he in turn will speak to the Prime Minister, and he will communicate, and that thing will come down to that person.

#### Guru, glorification, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=1517
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It's a good etiquette that we go through our spiritual master, through the Guru, who is in turn dear to Krishna."
**Summary:** Going through the Guru, who is dear to Krishna, is a good etiquette, similar to seeking a recommendation from someone close to a powerful figure, ensuring a more favorable reception.

Although yes, we can speak to the Lord, but it's a thing, it's a good etiquette that we go through our spiritual master, through the Guru, who is in turn dear to Krishna. When we go through Guru, when he recommends, when he, for example, somebody is very powerful and is very close to Narendra Modi, when we approach him, that I want to meet him, then for sure if he recommends our case to Narendra Modi or the Prime Minister, definitely he will give an appointment. So, it's always good that we go through proper channel, like even in the government circles, proper channel of moving the file. So, we go through a proper channel. Another important component of the prayer is that we simply don't start off with, Oh Lord, I am feeling weak, I need your help, I need your guidance. That's not the right way. If you see all the authorized prayers in the Bhagavatam, we have prayers of Kunti Maharani, prayers of Dhruva Maharaj, prayers of Prahlad Maharaj, prayers of Ambarish Maharaj, so many great devotees. You will always see the prayers starting with glorification of the Lord.
