We are amsa of the Lord, this trait is also there in its pure form in the Lord. Any person, when we praise him, or when we glorify him, he feels good. In fact, lot of people are doing many things for glorification, so that they get appreciated, they get glorified, people praise them. So, Lord being the Supreme Father, the original person, we being His amsa,

#### glorification, qualities, Krishna

That trait in a pure form is also there in the Lord, and He gets pleased when a devotee glorifies Him, praises Him. And He has unlimited glories, He has unlimited qualities. Sometimes somebody praises us, yes, that praise may not be genuine, or there may be relative praise. For example, somebody may say, you are so fine, you are so handsome. Somebody may say that, but then it's a relative truth. Today a person may be handsome, tomorrow after 10 years, 15 years, that beauty is gone. Somebody is rich today, wealth is gone. But that's not the case with the Lord. So, you see all exalted devotees glorifying the Lord, praising the Lord for all the exalted qualities He has. Not directly jump to the Krishna. Even in the company for that matter, if there is a whole structure, although that's not a good example, but just to get a sense. For every small thing, you don't go to the chairman. If you have to go to the chairman, there is a proper channel. You go to your manager, the manager in turn speaks to the thing. And if at all you want to meet the chairman, you can go through them. Then another important aspect of the prayer is revealing the mind, the heart. Like Arjuna did in the battlefield of Kurukshetra. #### mind, heart, honesty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/1f5z4GPrd6k?t=1793
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Although the Lord is all-knowing, but nevertheless we can, in our prayers, reveal that I am feeling weak. I am feeling helpless."
**Summary:** Despite the Lord being all-knowing, it's important to reveal one's true feelings and weaknesses in prayer, rather than maintaining a facade or covering up reality.

Although the Lord is all-knowing, but nevertheless we can, in our prayers, reveal that I am feeling weak. I am feeling helpless. Although I have everything, but I am seeing that all this is of no use. So reveal our mind, reveal our heart, reveal our... Even if we are feeling weak, there is no harm in revealing. Sometimes it says, no, no, no, we shouldn't reveal our weakness. We always project something what we are not. Otherwise people will take undue advantage of my weakness. There was a person who came to meet me. He was actually in a very, very bad financial state. He didn't even have money to pay the monthly rentals for the house in which he was staying. But for the last eight months or one year, he was projecting, because his daughter has to get married, he was projecting as if everything is okay. Although he is borrowing money from some people, but it's all very secretly done. Although he wants to sell the car, but he is not selling the car. Otherwise people will get a sense that he is in bad financial state. So many times we maintain a farce to cover up the reality. So when we pray to the Lord, when we approach the Lord, we don't have to have some kind of screens covering the reality. We can reveal our heart, reveal our mind, reveal the reality.

#### Guru Parampara, knowledge, purpose of life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SXs1lpG0rUo?t=229
**Verse References:** BG 15.7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "All living entities are part and parcel of Krishna and they are struggling in this material world with their mind and senses."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of Bhakti Vinod Thakur's appearance, the purpose of life, the cycle of birth and death, and Krishna's teachings in the Bhagavad Gita, highlighting the role of time in obscuring spiritual knowledge.

So today is a very auspicious day, a day when a very important acharya, His Divine Grace Bhakti Vinod Thakur, appeared on this planet earth. So it is mentioned in the scriptures to give us real knowledge, to give us real light in our life, so that we can understand what is the purpose of our life, why we are trapped in this cycle of birth and death, why we are suffering, what is our real position, what is real happiness. As Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita that Mavev Aam Sho Jeeva Loke Jeeva Bhuta Sanathana. All living entities are part and parcel of Krishna and they are struggling in this material world with their mind and senses. But because of ignorance, they are not able to understand their real position, the position of this miserable material world, where we are going through this repeated cycle of birth and death, birth, death, old age and disease. These are the miseries of this material world. So Krishna comes here in this material world, from the spiritual world, so that he can give us this real knowledge about our existence, how we can become free from this cycle of repeated birth and death. So as all of us know, Krishna appeared on this planet earth 5,000 years back and he gave this knowledge of Bhagavad Gita. But even though Lord appears in this material world, but with the passage of time, this knowledge gets lost, because in this material world, there is something called time and time destroys everything. So because of which, although Lord comes here, he gives us this real spiritual knowledge, but with the passage of time, everything gets lost.

---
#### Guru Parampara, authorised knowledge, spiritual life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SXs1lpG0rUo?t=376
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have to approach authorised channel through which we can get pure knowledge, uncontaminated knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the importance of the Guru Parampara, the authorized channel through which spiritual knowledge is passed down, and the necessity of receiving knowledge from a bona fide source to avoid speculation and attain true understanding of Dharma.

So to revive this knowledge, Krishna sends his pure devotees in a proper authorised channel, which is called Guru Parampara. So Krishna established four authorised Guru Parampara, through which this knowledge is getting passed on to the suffering living entities in this material world. So Krishna came 5,000 years back and then after him, we are receiving this knowledge, in a disciplined succession. Just like Krishna gave this knowledge, first of all to Lord Brahma, then Narad Muni, then Vyas Dev, and like that this Parampara continues. So first of all we have to understand that when it comes to spiritual knowledge, or when it comes to understanding of Dharma, then we have to receive this knowledge from a proper bona fide authorised channel. We cannot speculate when it comes to spiritual knowledge. We cannot speculate when it comes to true understanding of Dharma. Just like today, it has become a very normal trend that people try to define Dharma in their own way. Especially when we see in the newspapers, there is a spiritual column in which people give their own definitions about Dharma, about God, and like that. But this kind of speculation cannot help us to become free from the cycle of birth and death. We have to approach a bona fide source. We have to approach authorised channel through which we can get pure knowledge, uncontaminated knowledge. So Krishna appeared five-thousand years back and he established this authorised bona fide parampara through which we can get pure, uncontaminated knowledge. And in this parampara, different Acharyas have come at different times to give us pure, uncontaminated spiritual knowledge about our real existence.

---
#### Acharya, Bhakti Vinod Thakur, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SXs1lpG0rUo?t=526
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Bhakti is not some sentimental activity. Bhakti is purely scientific."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Bhakti Vinod Thakur as an important Acharya in the parampara, highlighting his role in reviving the true understanding of Krishna Consciousness and devotion after it had been distorted, particularly in Bengal.

So in this bona fide parampara, one of the very important Acharya appeared today and his name is His Divine Grace Bhakti Vinod Thakur. So Krishna appeared five-thousand years back and Krishna again appeared in this parampara five-hundred years back in the form of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Just like a teacher gives knowledge and then he personally instructs all the students by his practical example. So Krishna first of all gave this knowledge that all of us are part and parcel of Krishna and our real sorupa is Jeevara sorupo Nitya Krishna das. All of us are part and parcel of Krishna and our real constitutional position is to render pure devotion service to Krishna. Only when we understand our constitutional position then we can become free from this cycle of repeated birth and death. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu appeared five-hundred years back and he practically demonstrated that how we can practice pure devotion service. Who is a Vaishnava? Who is a devotee? He gave a pure understanding about devotion service. But then when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu left this planet just within a span of two-hundred and two-fifty years like that again this knowledge got lost. In fact the time came that in Bengal the devotees, the Vaishnavas, were compared to a person who is simply engaged in sinful activities, just like a playboy, a person who is only wearing Kanti Mala and wearing Tilak but he is engaged only in sinful activities. A Vaishnava was compared to a prostitute. So this kind of understanding started emerging up all over India especially in Bengal about the Vaishnavas about the devotees.

---
#### Acharya, Bhakti Vinod Thakur, Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SXs1lpG0rUo?t=677
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have to approach a bona fide Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes Bhakti Vinod Thakur's role in re-establishing the true understanding of Krishna Consciousness and Vaishnavas, highlighting the importance of approaching a bona fide Guru to overcome ignorance.

So again in order to re-establish this understanding about Krishna Consciousness, about Vaishnavas, about devotees, His Divine Grace Bhakti Vinod Thakur appeared on this planet Earth. And he brought back true understanding of who is a Vaishnava. What is Bhakti? Bhakti is not some sentimental activity. Bhakti is purely scientific. What is Krishna Consciousness? What is a platform of pure devotion service? Who is a devotee? So Bhakti Vinod Thakur brought this understanding back in this material world. So this is the role of a Guru, Acharya. So this Parampara, if we see, all the Acharyas have appeared to re-establish true understanding of Dharma. So it is very important for us that when we are trying to understand about Dharma, about our real purpose of life, about our constitutional position, we have to approach a bona fide Parampara. Just like suppose we get trapped in some legal implications or we have to approach a lawyer, or suppose if we get some bodily problem, some physical problem, so we cannot speculate about the disease that oh maybe I am having this kind of disease, so I should approach one doctor. No, we cannot speculate, we have to approach one doctor and that doctor will let us know what is our real problem, what is our real solution. Similarly all of us are trapped in deep ignorance and that is why we have to approach a bona fide Guru.

---
#### Bhakti Vinod Thakur, Grhastha, Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SXs1lpG0rUo?t=801
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anybody can become Guru in preaching Krishna Consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights Bhakti Vinod Thakur as a bona fide Guru in the Parampara, emphasizing that he was a grhastha (householder) and demonstrated that anyone, regardless of their background, can practice Bhakti and become a Guru by preaching Krishna Consciousness.

So like this Bhakti Vinod Thakur is one of the bona fide Guru in this Parampara. And the special thing was from his life what we can learn that Bhakti Vinod Thakur was not a sanyasi, he was not a brahmachari, he was leading a grhastha life but still he is recognized as a bona fide Guru. Why? Because even though he was in a grhastha life, in a family life, but he was dedicated to service of preaching this message of Krishna Consciousness. So especially when we are in family life then generally lot of people think that it is very difficult to practice Krishna Consciousness or maybe grhasthas are not qualified to practice Krishna Consciousness or Bhakti, maybe only brahmacharis and sanyasis can practice Bhakti but that is not true. He actually showed by his personal example that it is not because a person is a grhastha or in a family life that he cannot practice Bhakti. No. Anybody can practice. In fact anybody can become Guru in preaching Krishna Consciousness. As Chaitanya Mahaprabhu taught about Krishna. So the person who knows science of Krishna Consciousness he can actually become a Guru irrespective of his background whether he is a grhastha whether he is a brahmachari whether he is a sanyasi or a vantrastha.

So at that time Bhakti Vinod Thakur he was looking after management of Jagannath temple also and in India anywhere if you are travelling and suppose you are feeling hungry so if there is a temple nearby and if you go to the temple the prasadam will be always available. So that was the standard of Jagannath temple that anytime if somebody goes to this temple they can go and they can get prasadam. So all the constable they arrested him and this yogi because he had some mystic powers he made all the constables and even Bhaktivinoda Thakur to suffer with high fever in fact all the family members of Bhaktivinoda Thakur they got a very high fever 105 degrees Celsius like that so when Bhaktivinoda Thakur came back to his house so his wife asked Bhaktivinoda Thakur that I think you have you know you have done a mistake that person maybe he is a Vishnu that's why all of us are suffering with fever so Bhaktivinoda Thakur told his wife that let us suffer with this fever and let us die I don't care but I will not leave this yogi who is misguiding innocent people so this is a standard of Acharya this is a standard of Guru this is a standard of pure devotees they know true principle of Dharma and they establish true Dharma they don't get bewildered by all this juggle río words and other kinds of mystic things so Bhaktivinoda Thakur was our district Magistrate so then the day came for hearing of this pseudo yogi and District Magistrate he was sitting on the bench bhaktivinoda Thakur and then bhaktivinoda Thakur observe lot of hairs on the head of this yogi that Mahavachri Margis So immediately he called some constables and told them that please cut all his hairs. So when the constables they called some barber from outside, nobody was inclined to cut the hairs because they were all thinking that Bhakti Vinod Thakur himself is suffering with fever and this yogi can kill all of us. So nobody was coming forward to even cut his hairs and then Bhakti Vinod Thakur himself went and then cut all his hairs of this yogi and then after some days this yogi took some poison and then he died. So like that Bhakti Vinod Thakur practically demonstrated that what is the position of Acharya. Acharya is not ordinary person. All these Acharyas when they appear in this guru parampara they come to establish real dharma, real understanding of religion.

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that true Dharma and enlightenment can only be attained through a bona fide source of knowledge, not through arguments or simply reading scriptures, and that knowledge is given according to time, place, and circumstances.

So let's see. So like that Bhakti Vinod Thakur demonstrated lot of examples. In fact in Jagannath Puri he even used to punish all these Pandas in the temple when they would not properly deal with the visitors in Jagannath Puri. So Bhakti Vinod Thakur was very strict and this is a way how a pure devotee or guru leads his life. And this is a proper source for attaining enlightenment, attaining pure transcendental Krishna consciousness. This knowledge what Krishna gave us 5,000 years back. Only from a bona fide source when we get knowledge we can understand two dharma, two religion. Because it is mentioned in the scriptures that we cannot understand dharma by arguments. It is mentioned in one of the scriptures that Tarko Pratishtha Shutayo Vibhinna means by tarka, by logics, by arguments. We cannot understand dharma by arguments. It is mentioned in one of the scriptures that Tarko Pratishtha Shutayo Vibhinna. We can understand dharma. Tarko Pratishtha Shutayo Vibhinna Na Sa Vrishir Yasya Matam Na Bhinnam. So we can understand by arguments. Then if somebody thinks no, no I will understand dharma, I will not approach any acharya, I will not approach any guru, I will understand dharma by reading the scriptures because scriptures are given by directly God. No, even by reading scriptures we can understand two dharma, because scriptures are also dharma. And also the knowledge what is there in the scriptures is based on the circumstances in which Lord gives his knowledge. So whenever Lord appears, even whenever his pure devotee appears, when they give this knowledge about dharma, they give according to time, place and circumstances. And another thing is the capacity of the individuals to understand dharma, how much they can understand.

Namaste Saraswati Deve, Gauravani Pracharinai, Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Paashyatyade Shatarinai. Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya, Prabhu Nithyananda, Sri Advaita Gadhadhara Sri Vasadi Gaurabhakta Vrindha. He Krishna Karuna Sindho Dina Bandho Jagatpate, Gopesha Gopika Kanta Radha Kanta Namostute. Tapta Kanchan Gaurangi Radhe Vrindavaneshwara, Vrishabhanu Sute Devi Pranamami Hari Priye. Vanchakalpat Rubhyascha Kripa Sindhu Bhaye Vachah, Patitanam Pavnebhyo Vaishnavebhyo Namo Namaha. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare.

#### Lord Balarama, spiritual strength, pleasure
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=245
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "From Balram we get the spiritual strength whereby we become qualified to enjoy real genuine spiritual pleasure."
**Summary:** The speaker outlines the structure of the session, then introduces Lord Balarama, explaining the meaning of his name as strength and pleasure, and how he provides the spiritual strength to attain real bliss.

So, today instead of having 3 parts in our discussion, we are going to have 5 parts. It's also going to be a little different from regular sessions, in the sense after each part we will have a video being played. So I request, and the videos are going to be very small and crisp, about one and a half, two minutes, three minutes. Otherwise, usually we wish to have, you know, kirtans in between for about 10 minutes. So today we'll have different sections and each section will be followed by a video, a short video, two minutes, three minutes. So it's going to be very interesting. As we proceed further, it will be a mix of many things, some discussion, some videos, and then some explanation with respect to the video or what we have so far covered. So first we are going to discuss about the birth of Lord Balram. Before we get into this section, I'll just briefly mention, we all have heard about Shri Krishna Balram. There are many, many temples where we have the deities of Shri Krishna Balram. In Vrindavan, the presiding deity, the Iskcon temple is also called Shri Krishna Balram Mandir. So Balram, as we all know, is the elder brother of Lord Shri Krishna. He has a very, very special position and in the various sections which follow today, we'll be discussing about how Balram is so special and what does the word Balram actually mean. Bala, as we all know, stands for strength. It's a Hindi word, Bal, and Ram, and Rama also means pleasure. From Balram we get the spiritual strength whereby we become qualified to enjoy real, genuine spiritual pleasure. We all are pleasure-seeking beings and in this world everyone is running behind pleasure, everyone is seeking pleasure from the Lord, happiness. And it's nothing wrong because the intrinsic quality of a soul is Anandamayo Bhyasat, to be happy, to be in Ananda. Unfortunately, not knowing what is real pleasure, under the influence of Maya or illusion, we seek wrong kind of pleasures, which momentarily, temporally gives us some kind of happiness, some kind of pleasure, but unfortunately that very pleasure becomes the cause of our distress or misery. So, Balarama refers to that personality who gives us the strength by which we become happy. So, Balarama refers to that personality who gives us the strength by which we can become qualified to relish our real spiritual bliss, spiritual Ananda, which actually we all are hankering to enjoy. So, first we'll talk about the birth of Balarama.

---
#### Lord Balarama, birth, Yoga Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thus, although Balram appeared as the son of Devaki, he was transferred to the womb of Rohini, but people thought that it was a miscarriage."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Balarama's birth, explaining how he was transferred from the womb of Devaki to Rohini under the influence of Yoga Maya to protect him from Kamsa.

As we all know, Kamsa was quite worried about the children born to Devaki, his sister, because he had heard that the child born to Devaki would be the cause of his death. So, the seventh child which was supposed to be born in the womb of Devaki, when Yoga Maya was thus ordered. Yoga Maya is the energy of Lord Sri Krishna. One is Maha Maya, that energy of Krishna which puts us in illusion. And then the seventh child, and there is another energy of the Lord which is called Yoga Maya which assists the Lord in enacting various Leelas, various pastimes for the pleasure of the Lord. When Yoga Maya was thus ordered by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, she circumambulated the Lord. Yoga Maya and Mahamaya are energies of the Lord and assist the Lord. They work under the order of the Supreme Lord, including the Maya Shakti which overwhelms us with illusion. Devi Yesha Gunamayi Mamamaya Dhurateya. It is my energy, Krishna is saying. So Yoga Maya was ordered by the Supreme Personality of Godhead and appeared within the material world. According to his order, when the Supreme Personality of Godhead was transferred Lord Shesha from the womb of Devaki, the seventh child of Devaki was transferred from the womb of Rohini, the womb of Rohini, both of them were under the spell of Yoga Maya. Because of the spell of Yoga Maya, they could not understand, comprehend that the child has been transformed from one womb to another. This influence of Yoga Maya, Shlaprabhupada mentions, is also called the Yoga Nidra. So they were under the influence of Yoga Maya, Yoga Nidra and during that such spell this transfer of one womb of Devaki to the womb of Rohini, they could not understand, comprehend that the child has been transformed from one womb to another, the womb of Rohini happened. When this was done, people understood that Devaki's seventh pregnancy was a miscarriage. Thus, although Balram appeared as the son of Devaki, he was transferred to the womb of Rohini, but people thought that it was a miscarriage.

#### Lord Balarama, expansions, Nityananda Prabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=680
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Therefore it is particularly mentioned that Balarama is the protector of the devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the narration of Balarama's birth and explains how he is also known as Rohini Nandana and Sankarshana. He then discusses Balarama's expansions as Lakshmana and Nityananda Prabhu, emphasizing his role as the protector of devotees.

Thus, although Balram appeared as the son of Devaki, he was transferred to the womb of Rohini. So, Rohini Nandana, Balram is also said to be the son of Rohini, but actually he is the seventh son of Devaki. Therefore, Balram is also called the elder brother of Lord Sri Krishna. When Krishna was present, Balram became his elder brother. When Rama was present, the same Balram, his expansion became his younger brother, Lakshmana. It is said that Lakshmana once spoke to Lord Ramachandra that in this life I have got an opportunity to serve you as your younger brother. I aspire in some life I may get an opportunity to serve you. I want you to be your elder brother. That is how in this particular pastime of Lord Sri Krishna, the same expansion of the Lord appeared as the elder brother, as the seventh child of Devaki. Shri Baladeva appeared as Lord Ramachandra and this is Balram, the expansion of Lord Ramachandra, appearing as Lord Lakshmana in the pastime of Lord Ramachandra. The same Balarama, the expansion of Lord Sri Krishna appeared as Nithyananda Prabhu. We all chant the Panchatattva mantra even before our session, before chanting each mala. Sri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda, Sri Advaita Gadadhara, Sri Vasadi Gaurabhakta Vrinda. So this Nithyananda Prabhu is expansion of the Lord and is none other than the Lord Baladeva. And the great Lord Nithyananda Prabhu exhibited his causeless mercy by delivering the fallen souls, the two brothers, Jagai and Madai. Therefore it is particularly mentioned that Balarama is the protector of the devotees. He protects the devotees. He gives them spiritual strength. He protects them from the danger, protects them from illusion. And that's how Balarama in the form of Nithyananda Prabhu was distributing the mercy of the Lord and delivering the fallen souls. The Lord also declared to Yogamaya that his plenary expansion, Ananta Sesha, was within the womb of Devaki. So, Balrayama is also the expansion of Ananta Sesha. On account of being forcibly attracted to the womb of Rohini under the spell of Yogamaya, he is also known as Sankarshana. He got forcibly attracted to the womb of Rohini. Therefore he is also called, Balram is also called Sankarshana and would be the source of spiritual progress for Rohini.karna. In an exclusive video of Ph.k. Nimal Hatsotsukam, I am mentioning HD's page in the description of this video instead source of all spiritual power or Bala by which one could attain the highest bliss of life which is also called Rama, Balarama.

#### Lord Balarama, spiritual master, mercy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=1072
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shri Balrama is the protector of the devotees of the Lord...Baladeva acts as the spiritual master of all the devotees and by his causeless mercy the fallen souls are delivered."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Balarama's role as the original spiritual master, the protector of devotees, and the mercy incarnation of the Lord, whose representatives are the great spiritual masters.

So spiritual master is very, very important to enlighten us and it is said that Balrama is the original spiritual master. There are many spiritual masters and all these spiritual masters are representatives of the original spiritual master which is Lord Balrama. Shri Balrama is the protector of the devotees of the Lord, we just now covered. Baladeva acts as the spiritual master of all the devotees and by his causeless mercy the fallen souls are delivered. Krishna is very very merciful. He personally descends and sends his representatives. He sends his incarnations, sends his expansions to deliver the fallen soul. Although we are hell-bent on enjoying independent of the Lord, we have rebelled against the supremacy of the Lord, and the Lord has created this material world, still he personally comes. He sends his representatives. So by his causeless mercy the fallen souls are delivered. By his divine grace only one can approach the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Without the mercy of the spiritual master we cannot approach the Supreme Personality of Godhead. And Shri Balrama is the mercy incarnation, that mercy of the Lord. We say he is all merciful, Kripa Sindhu. He is the ocean of mercy. The mercy incarnation of the Supreme Lord is Lord Balrama and all the spiritual masters, great spiritual masters are the representatives of that original mercy incarnation, Lord Balrama. So they are manifested as spiritual masters, the saviors and as pure devotees. Shri Balrama appears in various forms along with incarnations of Lord Balrama. Lord Balrama appears in various forms along with incarnations of Lord Shri Krishna, thus manifesting different characteristics in his loving service to the Lord. In this world we see nothing but the Lord and his energies, Lord and his incarnations, his expansions. So Balrama being his principal expansion, the mercy incarnation, delivers the fallen souls and he is engaged in the service of the Lord. So Balrama is the mercy incarnation. He is serving the Lord by distributing the mercy of the Lord to the fallen souls.

In his incarnation as Lakshmana, the younger brother of Lord Ramachandra, Balrama enacted the role of Lord's servitor. So in all incarnations, it's basically serving the Supreme Lord. Yet in his manifestation with Lord Shri Krishna, Balrama serves him in every aspect. Serves him as his elder brothers, serves him as his associates and serves him by distributing his mercy to the fallen souls. As a friend, as a brother, by fanning him, by putting him to sleep, the very, very sweet loving pastimes which were enacted between Lord Shri Krishna, Balrama and his covered boyfriend. By fanning them, putting them to sleep, glorifying him with Vedic invocations and expanding himself and his house, umbrella, attire, jewelry etc. So these are all manifestations which are meant to serve the Lord.

#### Lord Balarama, spiritual strength, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=1820
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To be Krishna Conscious is to declare a war against Maya."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that spiritual strength comes from Lord Balarama and Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, emphasizing the need to conquer the enemies of Krishna Consciousness and sustain oneself in spiritual life against Maya.

So, that spiritual strength comes from Lord Balrama. The Balama comes from Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and both of them are so merciful that in this age of Kali, one may easily take shelter of the lotus feet. They are very, very merciful and we can take shelter of their lotus feet. If one has no connection with Nithai, as I mentioned earlier in the Leela of Lord Chaitanya and Nithyananda Prabhu, Nithyananda Prabhu is Lord Balrama himself. If one has no connection with Nithai or Nithyananda Prabhu or Balrama and even though one may be very learned scholar or a Jnani, or has taken birth in a very respectable family, these assets will not help. As I mentioned, somebody may be very physically strong, may be very intellectual, may have lot of grasping power, he may be very educated, he may be a big scholar, he may be very big Pandit, but all these assets will not help. We must therefore conquer the enemies of Krishna Consciousness with the strength received from the Lord. Today, all of us have challenges, we face difficulties. Even in our spiritual journey, we come across obstacles. We need strength to carry on. It's a fight against Maya. Prabhupada says, to be Krishna Conscious is to declare a war against Maya. We need strength. Imagine a person who is completely weak. Let's say he wants to run a marathon. He cannot if he is physically very weak. Likewise, if we have to sustain in spiritual life and be strong enough to not fall victim to Maya, then we need spiritual strength and that strength comes from Lord Balrama.

#### Lord Balarama, spiritual strength, devotee protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=1951
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By the mercy of Lord Balrama, we get the spiritual strength whereby we become qualified to enjoy genuine spiritual pleasure."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the meaning of Balarama's name and his role as the source of spiritual strength, emphasizing his ability to protect devotees from envious persons and obstacles.

Balrama means strength and Rama means enjoyment. Balrama means one who gives you spiritual strength or enjoying spiritual life or blissful life and that is called Balrama. By the mercy of Lord Balrama, we get the spiritual strength whereby we become qualified to enjoy genuine spiritual pleasure. But Balrama is the Sesha incarnation and therefore he can save us from the angry serpents or envious persons who are always ready to attack. We have examples of many exalted devotees who faced many obstacles, many enemies. Example being Prahlad Maharaj, how he was tormented by his father Renekashyapu who did not want him to practice Krishna consciousness. Many examples, Haridas Thakur was caned in 21 marketplaces. So there are many envious people who would torment the devotees. And then here it says that Balrama being an incarnation of Sesha can save us from angry serpents or envious persons. Serpents are envious, likewise they are envious people and by the mercy of Lord Balrama we can protect ourselves. This is something that should very atiter the people who are able to delay personality changes, and such people it is mentioned are always ready to attack.

#### spiritual strength, self-realization, Lord Balarama
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=2085
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One cannot attain the supreme or any form of self realization without being sufficiently favored by Balrama."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes the Upanishads, stating that self-realization is impossible without Balarama's favor, and emphasizes that spiritual strength, not physical strength, is necessary to overcome obstacles.

In the Upanishad it is stated… Naya Maatma Balah Kinen Labhiyah. The purport is that one cannot attain the supreme or any form of self realization without being sufficiently favored by Balrama. We can pray to Lord Balrama to give us his mercy, his favour. If we get his mercy, we get his bala, spiritual strength. We get strength to ward off various obstacles which might come in our spiritual journey. He saves us from envious people who can be experienced in the form of various difficulties in our spiritual path. Bala does not mean physical strength. No one can attain spiritual perfection by physical strength. As I said, health also are of many types. Likewise, strength also. It's not just the physical strength. One must have the spiritual strength by which one can cross over all obstacles in spiritual life. And that strength is infused by Balrama or Sankarsana, Ananta or Shesha. Ananta or Shesha is a power which sustains all the planets in their different positions. Materially, this sustaining power is known as the law of gravity.

#### Tattva, Lord Balarama, incarnation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9kiQ1f9Y8LA?t=2509
**Verse References:** BG 9.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Balarama is the immediate expansion of Vishnu Tattva or Lord Shri Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of Tattva, explaining that Balarama is the Supreme Personality of Godhead and an immediate expansion of Vishnu Tattva, distinguishing him from Jeeva Tattvas like Devi and Devatas.

Now we are going to talk about very important section, and I request all of us to be very, very attentive. We are going to talk about Tattva. Krishna also mentions this in the Bhagavad Gita, Tattva Darshi. Tattva Darshi means one who has seen the truth. What is the truth? The Tattva about Lord Balarama's incarnation. What is the truth? What is the underlying fact for Lord Balarama's incarnation? Who is Lord Balarama in truth? So it is said that amongst various names of the relatives, the name of Rama or Balarama, eldest brother of Lord Shri Krishna is added with the word supreme personality of Godhead. Just like we say Krishna the supreme personality of Godhead, the same word, the personality of Godhead, supreme personality of Godhead is also used for Balarama. Lord Balarama is the immediate expansion of Vishnu Tattva or Lord Shri Krishna. Here I would like to mention there are various Tattvas. One is the Jeeva Tattva. We all are under the category called Jeeva Tattva. Even Devi and Devatas fall in this category of Jeeva Tattvas. Some Jeevas can get empowered by the Lord just like some citizens can potentially become Chief Ministers or Prime Ministers. Likewise some Jeevas can also be empowered by the Lord if they have Jeeva Tattva, a lot of pious credits. They can be empowered to manage some aspects of universal affairs under the direction of Supreme Lord and they are the Devi and Devatas. In India, especially among the Hindus, sometimes we feel that the innumerable Gods and Goddesses can be empowered by the Lord, and you can worship any one of them as per your liking, as per your taste, and it's all one and the same. So long as you're worshipping someone, so long as you're worshipping and visiting some temple, it's all fine. You should have some faith, you should have some belief and you should worship some God, depending on your taste, depending on your inclination, depending on the temple closest to your house.

**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Krishna distinguishes between Devi/Devatas and Himself, emphasizing that Balarama belongs to the Vishnu Tattva category, which includes Narayana, Vishnu, and Rama.

But this is not what Krishna is mentioning in the Bhagavad Gita. Krishna in the Bhagavad Gita clearly demarcates between Devi and Devatas and Him, the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Whenever Krishna is speaking, that verse is preceded by Shri Bhagwan Vacha. The Supreme Personality of Godhead said, Devi and Devatas are of the category of Jeevatattvas but they are empowered Jeevatattvas. Just like Lord Brahma, Indra, they all are Jeevatattvas but they are empowered personalities. There's another Tattva, another category and that is called the Vishnu Tattva. One is Devi and Devatas and there's another category called Vishnu Tattva. And personalities in this category includes Narayana, Vishnu, Lord Ramachandra, Lord Vamana, Keshavadhrita, Narahari Rupa, Lord Narsimha. They all are of Vishnu Tattva category. And here Prabhupada is writing, Lord Balrama is the immediate expansion of Lord Shri Krishna and is also in the category of Vishnu Tattva. Balrama is the expansion of the Lord and therefore there is no difference in potency between Lord Shri Krishna and Lord Balrama. In material world we have an estimation. If so and so is below him or you know we all have chairman, vice chairman and all this kind of differentiation, hierarchy. But in the category of Vishnu Tattva, Narayana, Vishnu, Rama, Balrama, they being of the

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### THE UNSEEN CONTROLLER | Bhagavad Gita Chapter 9 Verses 7 - 10 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### time, creation, Vedic knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the Mahapralaya where Krishna says this creation, this universe is created and then annihilated, it's at the end of millennium."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of a millennium in the context of Brahma's life and the Vedic knowledge of creation and annihilation.

Okay, so the topic today is Unseen Controller. We are covering three verses from the 9th chapter, verse number 7 to 10. We will chant this verse together, please repeat after me, Sarvabhutani Kaunteya, Prakriti Myanti Mamikam, Kalpakshai Punastani, Kalpada Vishjamiham, Kalpada Vishjamiham, Kalpada Vishjamiham. O son of Kunti, at the end of the millennium, every material manifestation enters into my nature, and at the beginning of another millennium, by my potency, I again create. So these are the words spoken by Lord Krishna to Arjuna. He says that at the end of the millennium, what is the meaning of millennium? Thousand years of Brahma's life is one answer. One yuga is one millennium, anybody else? What is one millennium? So one millennium is, yes Vikas, okay, one millennium is one life of Brahma. Lord Brahma lives for hundred years, and to give you some idea about the duration of life of Lord Brahma, one day of Lord Brahma is called one kalpa, and one kalpa is the combination of thousand cycles of four yugas. What are the four yugas? Satya Yuga, Treta Yuga, Dwapa Yuga, and Kali Yuga. So the four yugas combined together form one Mahayuga, and thousand Mahayugas form one Kalpa, sorry, one Kalpa, and similar is the duration of the night of Lord Brahma, and like that, one month, one year, and hundred years.

#### creation, Vedic knowledge, authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=161
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The first created being Brahma was imparted this knowledge about this creation."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the origin of Vedic knowledge, its transmission to Brahma, and its subsequent division into the four Vedas.

So the Mahapralaya, where Krishna says this creation, this universe is created and then annihilated, it's at the end of the millennium. Who is the first created being of the universe? Lord Brahma. So the first created being is Brahma, and this knowledge about this creation was imparted to Lord Brahma. Tene Brahma Hridaye Adi Kavaye. So this Vedic knowledge is not the Bhagavad Gita, Mahabharata, Ramayana, Mahabharata, Supanishad, Itihasas. All this, these are all Vedic knowledge. And this knowledge is also called Apogea. It is called Krushaya because it is not man-made. Just like we have an understanding that whenever anybody produces a gadget, whatever we buy in the market, it comes with the manual. And the manual is made because the manufacturer knows if the product has to be optimally, efficiently used, the user has to be guided, has to be told what this product is all about, the do's and don'ts, how to use it effectively. So likewise this creation, this gigantic creation, there is an intelligent creator. There is a lot of order. The sun rises in the morning, the seasons change, it rains. There is an intelligent creator. It has not happened by chance. It is not an accidental creation. So we will come to that in today's session, why we say it is not an accidental creation. It is an intelligent creator behind it. So he created this universe. The first created being, Brahma, was imparted this knowledge about this creation. And Tene Brahma Hridaye. And this knowledge was coming down and it is also called Shruti. Shruti means through aural reception. And this Vedavyasa, who is the literary incarnation of God, put this knowledge into writing and further subdivided into four parts. And that is how we have the four Vedas: Veda, Samveda, Yajurveda, and Atharva Veda.

#### cosmic order, spiritual world, material world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=293
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Beyond this material world, there is a spiritual world which is not vyakta and avyakta."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the temporary nature of the material world, contrasting it with the eternal spiritual world, and Krishna's role in creation and annihilation.

So thousand cycles of four yugas form one day of Brahma. One day of Brahma is also called one Kalpa. And like that, one lifetime of Brahma, which is hundred years. At the end of that, the dissolution or annihilation of the universe happens. Text 8: The whole cosmic order is under me. By my will, it is manifested again and again. And by my will, it is annihilated at the end. See, Lord Krishna is saying these words. We have different Devi and Devatas being worshipped in India specifically. But nowhere you will find somebody very boldly declaring this whole creation has come from me. Under my will it is being manifested. It is being created and annihilated. So here Lord is very boldly declaring, by my will it is manifested again and again. So the creation comes about, stays for some time, it is maintained, and it is annihilated. So this material world which we are part of now, it is temporary in the sense, it is created at a time. It remains for some time and then it gets annihilated. Unlike this material world, there is a spiritual world which Krishna talks about in the Gita. Parastasma tu bhavanyo vyakta avyakta sanatana. This world which we are part of is called vyakta. Vyakta means manifest. Vyakta. Avyakta means unmanifest. It is manifest for a while, and then it becomes unmanifest. It is temporary. Parastasma tu bhavanyo. Beyond this material world, there is a spiritual world which is not vyakta and avyakta. And that's what the Lord is inviting us to come back. And by my will it is annihilated at the end.

#### control, analogy, space technology

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of satellite control to illustrate the complexity of managing systems and hints at a greater controller.

Now here, the Lord is very boldly saying, the whole cosmic order is under me. In present times, only people like Nirav Modi can make a statement: the entire banking system and the funds are under me. Now, let's take one example. Krishna is saying that yes, this whole cosmic creation, it has come about by my will. It remains for some time by my will, and then eventually it gets destroyed. Let's take one example. Today we are living in a world where a lot of advancement has been made in the field of space technology. We have sent rockets, satellites in space. We are trying to go to other planets. So typical satellite, can anyone tell me what is the weight, the mass, how much does it weigh? Satellite, typical satellite, man-made satellite. What is the mass? What is the weight? So, okay, I don't know the exact thing, but yes, we have very small satellites to huge ones, but in few tons. Now, to manage those satellites, a typical satellite, we have, as we all know, a huge array of computers in a control room where the scientists are monitoring the movement of satellites day and night, 24/7. In fact, I had a friend of mine working in ISRO, and he was posted in a place near Tumkur, near Bangalore. And he was on and off attending our sessions, Gita sessions. So one day I asked him, I have not seen you for last three months. Where were you? So he said, you know, it's very hectic. You know, the satellites were launched recently. So I am in the IT department. We have to make sure all these computers are up and running so that the monitoring happens very distinct. And he says, any time in the day, even in the night, 2 o'clock in the night, I can get a call and I have to attend to it because it's so critical. So he very jokingly said that people say that we control satellites. You ask me, I'll tell you, satellites control us. So, you know, typical control room. And around the clock, critical parameters, they are not just moving on their own. The parameters like orientation of the satellite, the spin, velocity at which it moves, it has to be monitored. Orbit decay corrections, if timely corrections are not made, it can go tangent, can go off the orbit. Earth satellite distance, heat dissipation, the electronic gadgets, electronic parts, you know, they get heated. And then there are ways and means to control it. There are ways and means to handle that heat so that they function properly. And then cosmic radiation. So these parameters have to be constantly monitored.

**Summary:** The speaker shares Newton's story about the model of the solar system to illustrate the concept of intelligent design and emphasizes the purpose of human life.

So, next day, Newton took him to his lab, his friend, and then in that lab, there was a very nicely designed three dimensional model of the solar system. So, there was sun and there was different planets moving in their respective orbits, three dimensional model. So, the moment this friend saw, it was really beautiful model, nicely simulated, this is wonderful, beautiful, excellent, who has done it? So, Newton said, you know, suddenly it appeared from nowhere. Last night I was working, it was not there and today morning I learned it up in my lab, it was there. So, the friend said, come on, Newton, don't joke. Who made this model? Again, he repeated. He says, believe me, it just came from nowhere. He says, come on, how can I believe this? So, Newton said, my dear friend, for you to reconcile with the fact that this model came from nowhere, it's so difficult, what to speak about this gigantic universe, it's come from nowhere, it just came on its own. So, there is an intelligent creator, which here in this particular world, Krishna is saying, I have created. And any creation means there is a purpose. The satellites are sent in space, not for whimsically. Government spends a lot of fund. The intelligent people working behind it for a purpose. So, this knowledge, what we are understanding in the Gita, Krishna is sharing, why this world is created? Why this world is created? Why are we in this world? What is the purpose of this creation? Otherwise, animals also are living, they also have souls, they also are living, eating, sleeping, mating, defending, they are also doing. But as human beings, we can understand. We can understand this broader picture, what is this creation, why it is created, who is God, why we have come here, why there is birth, death, old age, disease. How do I fit in this big picture, where I have come from, where do I go after death? This understanding is called Athato Brahma Jignasa. Now that you have got human form of life, try to inquire, try to understand. In fact, that is one of the prime purposes of human form of life.

#### material energy, creation, Vishnu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=1379

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord creates these material universes through Maha Vishnu."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the sum total of material energy and the role of Maha Vishnu, Garbhodakshaya Vishnu, and Shirodakshaya Vishnu in the creation and maintenance of the universe.

If we don't do that, then we are polished animals. Animals are eating on the streets with their, you know, this thing, they are eating with nice cutlery or on a dining table with forks and knives. The activity is the same. So, the sum total of material energy, which manifests in the form of the universe, is called the sun. The sun is called Maha Tattva, the aggregate of all material elements, just like, you know, I said, it comes into being, it stays for some time, and it gets annihilated. So, material elements, just like a potter, there is a mud, it makes a pot, and after the pot gets destroyed, it again becomes mud. The aggregate of material elements is called Maha Tattva, which is energy of the Lord. Now, when this creation, this universe comes into being, the Lord expands as Maha Vishnu, and through the breathing of Maha Vishnu, different universes come about. From that Maha Tattva, from the breathing of Maha Vishnu, when he exhales, the universes come out, and when he inhales, the universes go back into being. That is also called Maha Vishnu or Karunadakshaya Vishnu. In each universe, the Lord further expands as Garbhodakshaya Vishnu. And from the navel of this Garbhodakshaya, Garbo means the Garbha, and from the navel of the Garbhodakshaya Vishnu comes the lotus flower, the stem of which has the 14 planetary systems, and of the lotus flower is Lord Brahma. And in that particular universe, the Lord further expands as Shirodakshaya Vishnu, which is also called the Supersoul or Paramatma, which is present in each and every atom, which is present in each and every living being.

So, this is sum and substance. These are aggregate material elements. The Lord creates these material universes through Maha Vishnu. Then we have Garbhodakshaya Vishnu in each and every universe. And the first created being is Lord Brahma. And the first created being is Lord Brahma. And the first created being is Lord Brahma. The universe stays for the duration of Lord Brahma, which is 100 years, which comprises each day's one Kalpa, which is equivalent to one Maha Yuga, which is 1000 cycles of 4 Yugas. Now, we have these days some debate going on on Darwin's theory of evolution. There was one person who was saying that, you know, this is not a fact. There has been a debate on this particular topic. So far, we have studied in our schools that we evolved from apes, our ancestors were monkeys, and then survival of the fittest, and then we evolved. So, what we understand from our books of science is that there was a time when there was a big bang. And then, basically, building blocks of life, amino acids and all that came together by some arrangement. And the mature combination of those amino acids, which are building blocks of lives, resulted in a very, very primitive life form. It could be single cellular living beings. And then, with passage of time, survival of the fittest, they adapted to the changes which were happening in the surroundings, in the climate, in the nature around. And then they adapted. Just like we adapt. By chance, it is very, very hot in Pervahy. It becomes very hot, we'll adapt. We'll try to do something. We'll try to put ACs. We'll try to put fans. And beyond all that, we'll try to adapt. We'll somehow make some changes and adapt. We'll get used to. So, making used to, getting used to, slowly, this unit of animals became more and more complex, and then eventually the most developed life form is man. So in other words, with this understanding, there was a big bang, there is no creator. There is a big bang and big explosion, and then the life forms came together, life came into existence, and slowly it evolved.

#### transmigration, soul, karma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=1664
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So body is like a dress, based on our karma, we get into a different body."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of houses in Mumbai to explain the transmigration of the soul through different species based on karma.

But what understanding we get from our Vedic literatures is completely different. Just like, yes there is a transmigration of the soul, Vedas also says there is a transmigration of soul. But it is not the transmigration or change of the body. Just like in Mumbai, we have slums, we have 1 BHK house, we have 2 BHK house, we have 3 BHK house, we have bungalows and much bigger houses. Now a person who comes to Mumbai, maybe he is poverty stricken, he starts with staying in a slum. And slowly as he starts earning money, his financial situation improves, he probably moves to a bigger house, a better house, and then eventually maybe lives in a bungalow. It is not that a slum has got evolved into a 1 BHK house with passage of time. The person who is staying in that 1 BHK house has moved to a 2 BHK house. Do you understand that? There is a difference. The house, this house has not changed, the person has transmigrated from one house to another. So what Vedas are saying is, there are 84 lakh species of life ever existing. They are like, in Mumbai city, slums are also there, 1 BHK house is there, 2 BHK also is there. But the soul is moving from one form of life to another life. Do you understand? Yes. So body is like a dress, based on our karma, we get into a different body. Based on our financial situation, we can stay in a 1 BHK house or a 3 BHK house. If my financial situation improves, I move to a bigger house. If for some reason my financial situation deteriorates, I move to a smaller house. So the Vedic understanding is, all these species of life are existing. They are living from the dawn of creation.

#### science, cause and effect, Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=1791
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In the Gita we understand Sarvakarana Karanam. He is the cause of all causes."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the limitations of science in unraveling the ultimate cause and introduces the Gita's concept of the cause of all causes.

Now, how does science work? We are not against science, we use science, we respect science for all the technological innovations which we all use. We embrace those things in our daily life. But can science unravel all the mysteries of the universe? Now let's understand how the science works. For example, Sir Isaac Newton, he saw an apple falling. He thought, why did the apple fall? He thought, why did the apple fall? This led to the understanding of law of gravity, the Newton's law of gravity. Now Newton's law of gravity, he saw the apple falling from the tree, made him think, and then he said that there is a cause for that. It suddenly didn't fell on its own. There was a cause and the cause was gravitational force. Now, what science does is, we see an effect. Then we try to understand what's the cause of that. Now the cause itself is an effect of another cause. We can go one step, two step, three step. Like for example, the effect is rain. What's the cause of it? The clouds. All the clouds is a cause of rain, but itself is an effect of evaporation of water. Now evaporation of water is the cause of clouds. But that itself is an effect of sun. So two steps we have gone in understanding of why it rains. But what caused the sun? We stop there. So what caused the gravitational force? We very proudly say Newton's law of gravity. Newton didn't create gravity by the way. He figured out that it is a gravitational force. So likewise we have an effect and for that effect we have a cause. But what's the cause of that cause? In the Gita we understand Sarvakarana Karanam. He is the cause of all causes. The entire material creation is set into motion by him under his will. Stays for some time under his will, and is destroyed by his will.

Well, there is a life form which can help you fulfil that desire. Okay, next. O Dhananjaya, all this work cannot bind me. I am ever detached, seated as though neutral. It should not happen. But because we have a free will. We have a free will. We are not like a robot or a computer where a program is installed and it just simply executes it. And by the way, why Krishna has given us a free will? Why the Lord has given us a free will? He could have given no free will and there would be nothing wrong in this world. He could have forced all of us to do all good things. There won't be any terrorists. There won't be any police. Everyone would be so nice. Why the Lord has given us free will?

#### free will, love, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=2379
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Real love is when you have got a freedom not to love him and you choose to love him."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a boy forcing a girl to love him to explain the importance of free will in genuine love for God.

A very fundamental question. It is because of mischiefs of free will, we are here in this material world. In this material world, there is janma, mrityu, jaravyaadi. Based on our ichcha, because of free will, we are ichcha, different desires. Because of that, we are getting different life forms. The very fundamental question is, why that free will is there? Anyone? Why has he given us free will? He could have not given us a free will. He could have so called dictated us to do a particular thing in a particular way. The world would have been a different place. No. No. No. Okay, let me give you an answer. Let's say a boy loves a girl, and there is no reciprocation from a girl's side. But he is madly in love with that girl. He proposes her and then finally she refuses. And one find that this person goes to her place and takes a revolver, okay, and puts it on her head, and says, better you love me, otherwise I will shoot you. I will kill you. So she raises both hands and says, Prakash, I love you. I love you. I love you a lot. Will Prakash love that love? Yes, no. Because it is forced. Lord can force you to obey him. Lord can force you to love him. But there is no love. Real love is when you have got a freedom not to love him and you choose to love him. That is the meaning of free will. The Lord has given us a free will. And this whole process of purification is you learn to use our free will properly. And when we learn to use our free will properly and we are in completely alignment with the Lord, we are purified. And when we are purified, we go back to the spiritual world.

So it is said that after having gone through so many years of, so many lives of this thing, because purification also is not easy. bahunam janmanam ante, bahunam janmanam ante jnanvan mam prapadyante vasudevam sarvam iti samahatma durlabha. That Atma is very, very rare, because he has learned and experienced this way, that way, to be happy, to be enjoyed, this way, that way, that way, that way, and getting frustrated, frustrated, frustrated. vasudevam sarvam iti samahatma durlabha. And once that person has come to that understanding, he gets elevated to the spiritual world. And having gone that, the example is given when you tell a child, don't play with fire, he will not listen. Again tell better, don't play with fire, he will not listen. he became a great suntan. cineBhūrda, nunsā synādhi Prabhupāda. A wonderful machine, it is taking the picture and it will move. But there is an operator. Where is the machine which is working without operator? Can you give any example? Here is a machine which is working without operator. So how do you think that the nature machine is working without the Supreme Operator, God's instruction? How do you think it? This is not very reasonable. We have to judge. There are different evidences. One of the evidence is hypothesis. That hypothesis is that because we see that no one is working without the Supreme Operator, we are not able to judge. No machine works without operator. Therefore we should conclude it. Even though we do not know what is God, what is the nature, we must conclude it that the nature which is working under some Supreme Operator, that is God. It is not necessary to see the operator, but we can guess that there must be operator. So human life is... It is meant for finding out who is the true operator. That is human life. Otherwise it's cats and dogs like. They are eating, sleeping, mating, and dancing. That's all. That is not human life.

He is giving evidence in the Bhagavad Gita. Now here I am. Under my direction, the prakriti, the nature, material nature is work. #### artist, creation, beauty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=3008
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So likewise when we see beautiful pieces of art in this creation around us, which is in abundance, we should ask ourselves, who is the artist?"
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of Mona Lisa and landscapes to illustrate that the beauty in nature implies an artist, who is Krishna.

By its rule, this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again. Now I will give you another example. What is this picture? Anyone knows the name? Loudly everyone. Mona Lisa. Who is the painter? So we see a beautiful piece of art, we know that there is an artist. We see a landscape, a nice beautiful landscape painted by someone, we know that there is an artist behind it. Somebody has put the brushes together, the colours together and using his artistic skills has put this nice piece of art. Another piece of landscape, we know there is an artist. Likewise in natures around us, we see beautiful pieces of art, like the flowers. Unlike Mona Lisa painting, it is a two dimensional painting, you see beautiful flowers, very aromatic, colourful, fragrant, three dimensional, you can feel it. You see beautiful birds, so much of symmetry. The colours. Beautiful butterflies. So when we see a beautiful piece of art like Mona Lisa or a painting, it is very very clear in our mind that there is an artist. There is somebody who has brought this piece of art into creation, into existence. So likewise when we see beautiful pieces of art in this creation around us, which is in abundance, we should ask ourselves, who is the artist? So here in this verse, Krishna is saying, who is the artist? Who is the artist? So here in this verse, Krishna is saying, under my direction, prakriti suyate sa cara acharam, moving and non-moving beings. So tree, as mentioned, is a non-moving being. Flower, all this is a non-moving being. And the supreme director is the Lord. He is the background of all the material manifestation.

#### direction, material nature, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Lyfd1ApUVsM?t=3133
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Material nature without the superintendence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead cannot do anything."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes that material nature works under Krishna's direction, even though He is detached from the creation.

All the living entities in different forms and species, Krishna says, aham bija pradapita, I am the seed giving father. Different bodies, they get according to their past desires and their deeds. Based on our inclination, based on our unfulfilled desires, life after life, the soul gets different bodies and moves from one form to another. Material nature without the superintendence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead cannot do anything. Just like these days we have driverless cars, you know, Google has come up with cars and all that. Is it really driverless? Yes? No? Is it moving on its own? No. Just like we remotely control a projector or an AC or something like that. It's a technology that instead of we physically pressing the button, I have something, a gadget whereby I can control that gadget through some, but still there is an operator. Even driverless cars, there is somebody who is controlling the monitoring of those cars, maybe through other infrared rays or whatever it is. So there is an operator. So Krishna here is saying, without, this whole creation, this material nature is working under my direction. Yes, the Supreme Personality is detached from, although this creation is there, it's moving, he is neutral.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### THREE KINDS OF FAITH  | BG 17.01 - 17.03 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### faith, modes of nature, scripture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YpQtfOobF9I?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So different permutation and combination of influence of modes influencing a person results in different natures, different behaviors."
**Summary:** The lecture begins with an introduction to the three kinds of faith based on the modes of material nature, as described in the Bhagavad Gita, and how these modes influence a person's nature, activities, and choices.

So the topic is three kinds of faith, based on the teachings of His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. This is text 17.01. Arjuna said, "O Krishna, what is the situation of one who does not follow the principles of scriptures but worships according to his own imagination? Is he in goodness, in passion or in ignorance?" Anyone can help me with the name of the chapter? The divisions of faith. So this chapter predominantly talks about faith. And when we're talking about three kinds of faith, faith in the mode of goodness, faith in the mode of passion, faith in the mode of ignorance. So in this chapter, we'll be hearing Krishna extensively talking about the influence of modes. We will get to understand foods in the mode of goodness, food in the mode of passion, food in the mode of ignorance. Sacrifice in the mode of goodness, in the mode of passion, in the mode of ignorance. Faith in the matter of goodness. So just to refresh our understanding, we all are getting influenced by the modes of material nature. And they are predominantly how many number? Three. So just like in one of the earlier session, we talked about that how in printing industry using four colors, we can create unlimited number of shades. And I remember we talked about the dots C, M, Y, K. So different permutation and combination of influence of modes influencing a person results in different natures, different behaviors.

#### modes of nature, behavior, worship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YpQtfOobF9I?t=100
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If a person is predominantly influenced by sattoguna, accordingly, his choice of food, his taste, his nature, his inclination, the kind of worship he does will be different."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how the modes of passion (rajoguna) and goodness (sattoguna) affect a person's nature, activities, food choices, faith, and the type of worship they engage in.

If a person is predominantly influenced by rajoguna, his nature will be one type, his activities will be one type, his choice of food will be one type, his faith will be of one type. If a person is predominantly influenced by sattoguna, accordingly, his choice of food, his taste, his nature, his inclination, the kind of worship he does will be different.

#### modes of nature, time, activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/YpQtfOobF9I?t=180
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Morning time, the temples open. You will have all the temples, morning, 5 o'clock, 6 o'clock, the mangal aarti, darshan aarti and all that."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that individuals are helplessly acting under the influence of the modes of nature, which are in constant competition, and provides examples of how the modes of goodness, passion, and ignorance are predominant at different times of the day, influencing activities.

And Krishna says in the Gita that we helplessly act under the influence of the modes of nature. And there is some kind of a competition between these modes. Sometimes mode of sattoguna, which is goodness, overpowers the other two. We are predominantly under the influence of sattoguna. Anyone can help me, what time of the day generally sattoguna is predominant? Morning time. If you also notice, if you have had a good sleep, when you wake up in the morning, you are fresh. You are more calm. You are more relaxed. Day time, post noon is rajoguna, which is predominant. And night time it is tamoguna. That's why you see activities also. Morning time, the temples open. You will have all the temples, morning, 5 o'clock, 6 o'clock, the mangal aarti, darshan aarti and all that. You will hardly find temple having aartis at 10.30 in the night. You will hardly find any pubs, bars, operational at 6 am in the morning, opening. You will hardly find, you know, as I said, pubs, bars, invariably open or other function late in the evenings or night.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker quotes the Gita, explaining that material nature (prakriti) with its three modes (gunas) causes all actions, and that ego (ahamkara) leads us to believe we are the doers.

So the divisions of faith based on the influence of modes of material nature. Krishna also in the says, Gita says, prakriti kriyamani. Prakriti means this material nature, which has three modes. Prakriti kriyamani, according to the modes, gunai karmani sarvasha. These gunas are the resultant. The cause of all the actions we perform. They are the ones who are goading us or pushing us to act in a particular way. Gunai karmani sarvasha. Karmani means actions. Sarvasha means all. Prakriti kriyamani. Gunai karmani sarvasha. Ahamkara vimudatma kartaham itimat. Out of ahamkara falls ego. We think we are the doers.

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker states that Vedic culture aims to cultivate higher modes, and that progress involves reducing the influence of ignorance (tamo guna) and increasing the influence of other modes, clarifying that goodness (sattva guna) is not the highest.

So Vedic civilization. The Vedic culture is all about cultivating higher modes. So if a person is in tamo guna predominantly, progress for him, advancement for him is where the percentage of influence of tamo guna reduces and the percentage of other gunas increase. He is well situated. He is well situated if he is predominantly under the influence of tamo guna. Is tamo guna the highest? How many of you say tamo guna is the highest? It is the best mode to be situated in? Tamo guna is the highest? How many of you say tamo guna is not the highest? Others? Satva guna? How many of you say undecided? Don't know. **Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that while goodness (sattva guna) is better than passion and ignorance, it is not the highest state, but rather a stepping stone to pure goodness (shuddha sattva) and genuine spirituality, emphasizing that even sattva guna is a material mode.

So Satva guna is not the highest. It is better than Raja guna. Raja guna is better than tamo guna. But Satva guna is not the highest. There is... Satva guna is mode of goodness. It is a springboard. It is a stepping stone to enter into Shuddha Satva which is pure goodness, which is transcendence, which is genuine spirituality. Even Satva guna is a material mode. We are in material consciousness. Still that we may be in ignorance. But Satva guna is pure. And Satvic ignorance. So we will go further in this.
