## HG Amitasana Dasa

### TRAPS OF MAYA | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### prayer, confidential knowledge, blessings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=26
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is very important that we pray to the Lord, seek His blessings and only by the blessings of great Acharyas of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, of Lord Shri Krishna, we can understand these confidential subject matters."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of prayer and seeking blessings to understand confidential spiritual knowledge.

A warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session. We have a very juicy topic today. The topic is traps of Maya. We are trapped by Maya. So what are the traps? How do we get entangled? How does Maya cheat us? These are some of the subjects which we will cover in today's discussion. So before we get going, let's start the session with prayers. I request all of you to kindly fold your hands. You can also join me in offering the prayers. It is very important that we pray to the Lord, seek His blessings, and only by the blessings of great Acharyas of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, of Lord Shri Krishna, we can understand these confidential subject matters. So please join me in offering the prayers.

---
#### confidential knowledge, mercy, spiritual understanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=267
**Verse References:** BG 4.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Unless we have the mercy, unless we have the blessings of the Lord, we cannot understand the subject matter."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that despite the widespread availability of the Gita, its true understanding requires the Lord's mercy and blessings, not just scholarly intelligence.

Sometimes it so happens that people think that anyway first 10 minutes it's all about prayers and introduction, so I can always join at 4:40. Main thing is to hear, but prayers are equally important. As I mentioned, Krishna in the Gita says this subject matter is most confidential subject matter. Guhyam means confidential. Yes, there are so many people talking on Gita. There are so many books written on Gita, so many editions of Gita available in the marketplace. You google Bhagavad Gita and you can download a free copy, so how can it be confidential? It is confidential because unless we have the mercy, unless we have the blessings of the Lord, we cannot understand the subject matter. We may be very scholarly, we may have very good analytical skills, we may be very intelligent materially, but not necessarily we will be able to grasp the subject matter.

---
#### maya, illusion, material desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=338
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Likewise maya also is illusory energy of the lord, just like miraj is an illusion."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of Maya, explaining it as an illusion similar to a mirage in the desert, which deceives those searching for water.

So today we are going to talk about Maya. Maya basically is a word which comprises, it's a combination of two words, Ma and Ya. Ma means not, and Ya means this, that which is not. We have all studied in school about Miraj in the desert. Even when we have gone on highways we get a feeling that there is some water body, something simmering like water, or in a desert Miraj, and at times it so happens that animals who are parched with thirst and they are looking frantically for water, they get cheated with a Miraj and they run towards that water body, thinking that there is water, and they keep running, running. So they keep running in search for water and eventually die out of thirst and they never get water in desert.

---
#### maya, illusory energy, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=418
**Verse References:** BG 7.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna says in the Gita that this illusory energy of mine is very very difficult for a person, for a conditioned soul to overcome, to come out of the influence of maya."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Maya as the Lord's illusory energy, emphasizing its difficulty to overcome and highlighting surrender to Krishna as the means to be freed from it.

So likewise maya also is illusory energy of the lord, just like miraj is an illusion. Likewise maya, it's a shakti of the lord, it's energy of the lord. Krishna says in the Gita that this illusory energy of mine is very very difficult for a person, for a conditioned soul to overcome, to come out of the influence of maya. It's not easy. In fact the entire spirituality, the path of spirituality involves waging a war, a war against maya. Maya is trying to defeat a sincere practitioner. It's energy of the lord. Maybe we'll discuss some other time why Krishna has created this energy called maya, which covers our real knowledge, which traps us, which entangles us. On one side we say that lord is all good, all kind, he is our loving father, then why does he have to have a shakti which traps us, which entangles us, which beats us, mama maya dhuratya, dhuratya means it's very difficult to surmount. And then Krishna says, maam eva ye prapadyante, if you surrender to me, maya metam taranti te, I will free you from this illusory energy.

#### maya, surrender, law abiding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=523
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Just like the police department for a person who has broken the law, who is a criminal, it just acts very very nastily on that person, torments that person, binds that person, gets hold of that person, imprisons that person if he has broken the law, but the same police department for a person who is law abiding, he has nothing to fear."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a police department to illustrate how Maya acts as a force that punishes those who disobey the laws of God, while those who surrender and are law-abiding have nothing to fear.

To give you a crude example, just like the police department for a person who has broken the law, who is a criminal, it just acts very very nastily on that person, torments that person, binds that person, gets hold of that person, imprisons that person if he has broken the law, but the same police department for a person who is law abiding, he has nothing to fear. Police department is also a department of the government which is meant for the welfare of the citizens, but still there is a department which is meant to punish the criminals. So likewise today we will not discuss why maya shakti entangles us, why it beats us, we will talk about what are the various ways in which we get trapped.

So like we have been doing in previous classes just for the ease of retaining what we have heard, we will broadly classify our discussion in three parts. You could call it three aspects, you could call it three principles. So the first principle is little longer, it is little difficult to remember, so I want all of you to be very very alert when I say this word and I will be asking you like I have been doing in earlier sessions to write it out in the live chat, not now, at some point in our discussion. The first principle which we have to remember as far as maya is concerned, the principle is existentially false, experientially true. I repeat, please hear carefully, existentially false, experientially true. Mirage in a desert existentially does not exist, water does not exist, existentially false, experientially as far as experience is concerned, it is real.

#### holy name, deities, spiritual experience, maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=685
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In the case of the Lord's name, in the case of prasadam, in the case of the deities, it is existentially true, the Lord is there, existentially true because it is reality it is not illusion, it is existentially true, the Lord is there but experientially false because today we are in maya."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the illusion of Maya with the reality of the Lord's presence in His name, deities, and prasadam, explaining that while these are existentially true, our current state in Maya prevents us from fully experiencing them.

The other day we were hearing one class from Madhu Pandit Prabhu, soulful japa in which he gave a very nice example. The chanting of the holy names is glorified by all the acharyas. The Lord has so mercifully come in the form of his nama holy name and when we chant actually the Lord is there, just like in the deities in the temple, the Lord is there archa vigraha, the deities are non different from the Lord, the Lord's name is non different from the Lord. It is kripa of the Lord that the Lord has descended in the form of his name and naam naamino abhinatvam, the name of the Lord and the Lord are abhin, are non different. In the case of the Lord's name, in the case of prasadam, in the case of the deities, it is existentially true, the Lord is there, existentially true because it is reality it is not illusion, it is existentially true, the Lord is there but experientially false because today we are in maya. Prabhupad gives an example that when a person is jaundice, you give him a sweet thing to eat it will taste bitter, not because the thing what he is eating is bitter, because he is in a diseased condition, he is not able to experience the sweetness of a sugar candy.

#### maya, illusion, sense objects, material attraction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=831
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "What maya does, false objects, sense objects it creates which are existentially false but experientially true opposite, just like the mirage in a desert."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Maya creates false sense objects that appear experientially true but are existentially false, using the example of attraction to beauty.

So the Lord has come in various forms avataras, like the holy name, like the deities, the Lord is there available to us, accessible to us, existentially true, but experientially we are not able to experience because right now we are in a diseased state, we are under the influence of maya. To explain this he gives an example, just like just for discussion sake, if we were to cover our tongue with some kind of a thin polythene sheet, let's say some way we cover our tongue with a polythene sheet and you place a rasgulla on the tongue. Now we can say existentially it is true, rasgulla is there and it is very sweet existentially, but experientially we don't feel because there is a sheet of polythene which is covering the tongue. Now what maya does, false objects, sense objects it creates which are existentially false but experientially true opposite, just like the mirage in a desert, existentially false, there is no water. The so called beauty of this material world, so called beautiful women to which madly vice versa man to woman and woman to man get helplessly attracted, innumerable movies, innumerable song glorifying so called beauty of the opposite sex, it is existentially false, it doesn't last, it is temporary, but experientially it is true. When a man is mad in love with a woman for him that woman is embodiment of sat chit and ananda, he doesn't want anything, he is ready to give up anything even forgets eating sleeping mad in love, experientially true, but existentially false.

So once there was a person who got attracted to a beautiful women and he tried making lot of advances, but somehow this girl was not interested in this man and also this man was not of a good character. And this girl although she was very beautiful, she was also very saintly in character. And this man was stalking, was following this girl and one day just enamoured by the beauty of that girl. Many times so called love in this material world, we call it as love but actually Prabhupada says it is lust. That's why you see many many so called marriages end up with divorces. There was a time when they were in love, they could not live without each other, but then end up in divorce. So so this man was chasing this girl and this girl was quite saintly in character and she understood that this man is by and large interested or rather got enamoured by her beauty external beauty. So this girl when this man proposed this girl, she said definitely we will meet, but my request to you can we meet after a week, I am busy with something, but for sure I would also like to meet you, but can we meet after a week. So the man accepted, after all time will pass and he will be able to fulfil his wish, he will be able to spend time with this beautiful girl. So this girl what she did was during this seven days she took lot of laxatives and some of the things which makes a person vomit. And this seven days you know she because of those laxatives passed stool and you know vomited and she did one not so pleasant thing but just to teach that man a lesson all her vomit and loose stool she collected in some pots. And stored them very carefully. Not a pleasant thing to do but just to teach that man a lesson. So after seven days this man comes to the same house.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=1760
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that Maya has two energies, Avarnatmika Shakti and Prakshapatmika Shakti, and that Avarnatmika Shakti covers the real knowledge of the soul.

Shila Prabhupada explains, Maya has two energies, two parts. One is called Avarnatmika Shakti and the second one, the second division is called Prakshapatmika Shakti. Avarnatmika Shakti, what it does is, it covers the real knowledge of the living entity, of the soul. Shila Prabhupada shares with us this wonderful story once Indra was cursed by Brahaspati. I have shared this story earlier, I will just briefly touch upon it so that we can take the learning from this story. Indra was cursed by Brahaspati and he became a hog, a pig. And he came to this material world. And he had a she-wife, a pig-wife. And in due course of time, had many children, piglets. And Indra in the form of this pig became very attached to his wife, to his children. And got busy with so-called, quote-unquote, household affairs. Taking care of them, tending to them, enjoying them. And living in, as usual, filthy place. Pigs live in a very filthy place, full of garbage, sewage, all that. All body covered with all kinds of obnoxious things.

#### maya, real knowledge, attachment, illusion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eCCn4trqYFc?t=1858
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Maya covers the real knowledge. What happens is, although we are living in a very, very so-called miserable life, slapped left, right, center by Maya, experiencing all kinds of pains and miseries, we think that this life is great."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of Indra as a pig, illustrating how Maya covers real knowledge and leads to attachment to a miserable material life.

So a time came when Brahma realized that in the Indraloka, because of absence of Indra you know, there is lack of proper management. So he came back and said, you know, it's all done now, please come back. But Indra in the form of pig had got attached to that so-called worldly life. Had forgotten the so-called life in Indraloka, the heavenly happiness, the heavenly enjoyment. And he got attached to his wife, attached to his children. And he was reluctant to go back. He says, no, this is fine, I am enjoying. Although he is living in a filth. He is thinking that he is happy. So Maya covers the real knowledge. What happens is, although we are living in a very, very so-called miserable life, slapped left, right, center by Maya, experiencing all kinds of pains and miseries, we think that this life is great. We get attached to this world. We get attached to our family. We get attached to so many things. Krishna says, come back to me. Surrender to me. Come back to my spiritual world. We don't want to go. So our real life is like this, we don't want to go back to our spiritual world. We don't want to go back to our spiritual world. We don't want to go back to our spiritual world.

You all have heard so much, you always look forward to, it will be a very pleasant experience, I can go and enjoy that beautiful country, replete with all kinds of resources. Shila Prabhupada, when he went to America, on the order of his Guru, he is writing, Oh Lord, why are you taking me to a miserable place? Because he is not an illusion. A self-realized soul can see that they may have big, big skyscraper buildings, they may have all material resources, but under the influence of Maya, they all are entangled, they all are suffering. The standard of life, the standard of enjoyment is very base. When he went to America, he saw hippies. You know, they were all from good families, rich families, but they have given up those families, and they are drunk, lying on the streets, at times naked. For them, this is life, this is enjoyment, womanizing, drinking, smoking, gambling. But a person who is self-realized can see how much they are suffering, forgetful of the real purpose of life, real goal of life. So what Maya does, the second principle is, covers our real knowledge. ## HG Amitasana Dasa

### The Modes of Nature | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### conditioned soul, modes of nature, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oQ8SewLnF2I?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are his amshas, but under the modes of material nature, we have got conditioned and living a very, very abominable life."
**Summary:** Krishna explains that we are parts of him, but conditioned by the modes of material nature, leading to a miserable existence.

So, Krishna says we are His amshas, but under the modes of material nature, we have become conditioned and are living a very, very abominable life.

---
#### sattva guna, analogy, spiritual potential
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oQ8SewLnF2I?t=28
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Conditioned by sattva guna is like the spark falling on a dry grass; there is a good possibility that it will ignite the fire again."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a spark and dry grass to illustrate how sattva guna can help revive our original consciousness.

So, when we are, the example is given just like a spark. When it separates from the fire, if it falls on dry grass, there is a possibility that it can again catch fire. The dry grass is conducive for the spark to ignite fire. So, likewise, when the living entity is conditioned by the modes of material nature, but predominantly nature, when we are in sattva guna, the material energy acts through these modes. Sometimes sattva guna is predominant, sometimes raja guna is predominant, sometimes tamo guna is predominant. So, conditioned by sattva guna is like the spark falling on dry grass. There is a good possibility that it will ignite the fire again. The spark becomes fire.

---
#### sattva guna, raja guna, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oQ8SewLnF2I?t=84
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we are predominantly in sattva guna, then we can cultivate devotional service, remembrance of the Supreme Lord, cultivate knowledge, and again revive our original consciousness, which is Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that being predominantly in sattva guna allows us to cultivate devotional service and revive Krishna consciousness, while being influenced by raja guna extinguishes that consciousness.

So, likewise, when we are predominantly in sattva guna, then we can cultivate devotional service, remembrance of the Supreme Lord, cultivate knowledge, and again revive our original consciousness, which is Krishna consciousness. When that spark falls on wet grass, it very soon gets extinguished. It does not ignite that wet grass. So, likewise, when the living entity, that amsha of the Lord, is predominantly under the influence of raja guna, then that original consciousness gets extinguished.

---

So, in Kali Yuga, predominantly people are under the influence of raja guna and tamo guna. And that's why Krishna consciousness activities have no relevance for them. It doesn't make sense. If you invite somebody for a Bhagavad Gita lecture, it doesn't make sense. Invite for a movie, invite for a cricket match, invite for IPL, people queue up. Makes sense.

#### tamo guna, conditional life, consciousness

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

And then, if that same spark falls on water, it gets completely extinguished. So, likewise, when we are predominantly in tamo guna, then whatever little bit we have of consciousness of the Lord is completely extinguished. And conditional life in its full force becomes, so to say, comes about. Full force of conditional life acts on the jiva.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### The Real Discomfort | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### suffering, materialism, spiritual solution
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Vu11E0Sv6bc?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But in human form of life we have the ability and opportunity to find solution to the actual problem, actual discomfort that the living entity which is implicated in the meshes of these modes conditioned."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that people are struggling to find temporary comforts and solutions to material problems, but the real discomfort is the cycle of birth and death, for which Krishna provides a solution that can be attained in the human form of life.

So Prabhupada says if every one of us analyzes what's happening in this world, people are struggling and everyone is trying to find a solution to the challenges they are facing individually, collectively, nationally, and internationally. So there's a struggle for existence, different kinds of kleshas hitting the living entities, and attempts are being made to find peace and happiness. So Prabhupada says the whole world, people are very active to find solutions to find temporary comforts. Like, for example, somebody does not have a house, he will be struggling hard how he can own a house, take a loan from the bank, pay the EMI. Somebody who doesn't have a car, he is facing a problem. When it rains, he gets drenched. He is trying to get hold of a car. The cars are there, roads are not. Okay, the government is struggling. So we are trying to find solutions to temporary problems, temporary comforts. But unfortunately, in conditioned life, completely bewildered, we have completely lost sight of the real problems, the real discomforts. We all are facing some discomforts, and we are trying to find solutions, but those solutions are of temporary discomforts, which Krishna says will come and go, like the changing season, summer and winter. But the real discomfort, for which nobody is anxious about or is trying to find a solution, which Krishna says is a subject of knowledge, is Janma Mrityu Jaravyaadi. Janma Mrityu Jaravyaadi. Because life after life we are caught up in the cycle of birth and death and taking material bodies, we have to face all these discomforts for which we are finding solutions. But in human form of life we have the ability and opportunity to find solution to the actual problem, actual discomfort that the living entity which is implicated in the meshes of these modes conditioned and taking birth again and again.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### The Root Cause of Dissension in Spiritual Life | SB 6.11.24  | Sri Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### devotional life, servitude, disciplic succession
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This verse gives the sum and substance of devotional life, one must first become a servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and explains Prabhupada's purport, emphasizing the importance of being a servant of the servant of the Lord and engaging body, mind, and words in Krishna's service.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto number six, chapter number 11, text number 24. Aham hare tava padaika-mula dasa nu daso bhavitasmi bhuyaha manas maratya su patir gunamste. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace AC Bhaktivedanta Swami. Translation: Oh my Lord, O Supreme Personality of Godhead, will I again be able to be a servant of your eternal servants who find shelter only at your lotus feet? Oh Lord of my life, may I again become their servant, so that my mind may always think of your transcendental attributes, my words always glorify those attributes, and my body always engage in the loving service of your Lordships. Vivekananda Prabhu audiobook, kanupala. The verse gives the sum and substance of devotional life. One must first become a servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord, dasa nu dasa. Prabhupada advised and he also showed by his own example that a living entity should always desire to be a servant of the servant of Krishna, the maintainer of Gopis. This means that one must accept a spiritual master who comes in the disciplic succession and is a servant of the servant of the Lord. Under his direction, one must then engage one's three properties, namely his body, mind, and words. The body should be engaged in physical activity under the order of the spiritual master, the mind should think of Krishna incessantly, and one's words should be engaged in preaching the glories of the Lord. If one is thus engaged in the loving service of the Lord, one's life is successful. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta purport. So this is a very nice verse and Prabhupada in the purport explains this verse is the sum and substance of devotional life. Where it is being mentioned that may I again become your servant so that my mind may always think of your transcendental attributes, my words always glorify those attributes, my body always engage in the loving service of your Lordship. This is a very nice verse and Prabhupada in the purport explains this verse is the sum and substance of devotional life.

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of accepting a bona fide spiritual master in disciplic succession and highlights the rarity of spiritual inclination in the material world, contrasting it with those who seek material gains through religion.

So Prabhupada explains that first one should become servant of the servant of the servant of Krishna or Lord. Dasa Dasan Dasa. This means that one must accept a spiritual master who comes in the disciplic succession and is a servant of the servant of the servant of the Lord. So all of us are very fortunate. First and foremost in this world, not many people are spiritually inclined in this material world. Even if people are religious, it's for material gains, it's to fulfill their material desires. And even if we come across somebody who is spiritually inclined, has some bhakti, has some devotion, still we find that there are so many gurus who are not bona fide. They may have desire to surrender, to serve with body, mind, words, soul, with body, mind, words, but they may be doing it to someone who is not authorized, who is not coming in disciplic succession. Srila Prabhupada is a bona fide guru. He is a pure devotee. He is coming in a disciplic succession. He is a servant of servant and servant of Krishna and we are trying to be his servant. So today we will discuss about the services what we render, the devotional life what we are leading, some pitfalls, some...

#### illusion, protection, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=297
**Verse References:** SB 3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, we all should always pray to the Lord for protection, because this Maya, this illusory energy of the Lord is very powerful."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Lord Brahma's prayers for protection from illusion while engaged in creation, emphasizing the power of Maya and the need for devotees to constantly pray for protection from pride, arrogance, and anger.

Lord Brahma in the third canto of Srimad Bhagavatam, Srimad Bhagavatam is offering prayers to the Lord. In the prayer he is offering because he has been assigned the task of the creation, Prajapati Brahma. So he is praying to the Lord that while I am engaged in your service, so called material activities, creating. So he is saying let me not be subject to illusion. Because we have some leelas, some pastimes where even Brahma is bewildered. So then, Srila Prabhupada explains in the purport, just like Lord Brahma who is the super most being, he is afraid of falling down. And just like that model, he is afraid of falling down and is praying to the Lord for protection. Similarly, we all should always pray to the Lord for protection, because this Maya, this illusory energy of the Lord is very powerful. Mohyanti Suryaya, Surya means even big, big devatas, we have examples of Shiva, Lord Brahma and other Indra. They all are demigods, they are not like ordinary living entities. So anytime we can be subject to illusion, we can forget Krishna, we can be overcome, we can be overpowered by pride, arrogance, anger. So we have got this opportunity to serve a pure devotee. Somehow, our good fortune, we have come in touch with Srila Prabhupada. But we should always have fear of falling down. Now, on one side we should say, we should be abhayam, we should be fearless. But there is healthy fear that I should, I can fall down. And that fear will inspire us, motivate us to pray to the Lord, feelingly. Just like Lord Brahma is praying.

#### Krishna anxiety, devotion, service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=476
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna anxiety is where we're anxious, how I can become a nice devotee, how I can chant my rounds nicely, why I'm not able to chant nicely, why my mind is getting, how can I do it?"
**Summary:** The speaker explains the concept of 'Krishna anxiety' as a positive force in spiritual life, contrasting it with material anxiety and highlighting the devotee's eagerness to serve Krishna and fulfill His orders.

In spiritual life, everything has a place, even fear has a place. Even anxiety has a place. Srila Prabhupada uses this word in one place, very nice. He says, Krishna anxiety. Sometimes we think that spiritual life means, it should be all very, and generally that is the understanding people have, shanti, shanti, shanti. The ultimate goal for many people is to achieve absolute peace. Common, if you see, understanding of this thing is shanti, peace. Peace of mind, peaceful life. So Srila Prabhupada explains about Krishna anxiety. Just like I was telling, Brahma is fearful of fall down. What is Krishna anxiety? Anxiety for Krishna. Then Srila Prabhupada explains, what is material anxiety and what is Krishna anxiety? If we are anxious about ourselves, our future, our security, what will happen to me in old age? Who will take care of me? That is material anxiety. But if you are anxious, how will I preach? How can I reach this message to more people? How can I distribute more and more books? Srila Prabhupada said, if you can distribute more and more books, but I am not able to do it, how can I do it? That anxiety is Krishna anxiety. Then Srila Prabhupada explains that how even mother Yashoda is anxious about Krishna. Is Krishna safe? Demons may come and disturb Krishna. So she is always in anxiety. So then Prabhupada explains that this anxiety is good. This Krishna anxiety will purify us. He gives example that everyone in this world, those who are even God conscious, they consider God as our father, even in Christianity. Our father in heaven, hallowed be thy name. Father means father takes care of the child. You ask something to the father and father will provide. So, whereas a devotee of the Lord, he does not ask Krishna, he is not taking Lord as order supplier, but he wants to serve the Lord. He is anxious to fulfill the orders of the Lord. Anxious to meticulously obey the instructions of the Lord.

**Content Type:** philosophy

**Summary:** The speaker continues to elaborate on Krishna anxiety, using examples of eagerness to serve and the anxiety of being late for devotional activities, distinguishing it from material anxiety.

This Girutu is thought of as Hindu Germani. This Rupa Goswami, he explains that if eagerness to serve Krishna, if somewhere it is available even with millions of dollars, you should go and buy it. So, likewise Prabhupada explains that Krishna anxiety is not a cheap thing. And we are getting a glimpse of sometimes in our service, there is anxiety, the pujaari and there is fear. They're running, it's getting late, it's getting late. That is just to trigger, just to give a glimpse of what is Krishna anxiety. Or we are running morning, Mangal Aarti, everyone is rushing, you're getting late. I should not be late, I should reach on time. That anxiety is Krishna anxiety. So as I was mentioning, fear has a place, anxiety has a place, but in a pure form, that anxiety which kills us, anxiety which increases our blood pressure, anxiety which gives us hypertension, blood pressure, that is material anxiety. So Krishna anxiety is where we're anxious, how I can become a nice devotee, how I can chant my rounds nicely, why I'm not able to chant nicely, why my mind is getting, how can I do it?

#### introspection, motive, sankirtana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=764
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should constantly introspect. What is the motive behind my, for whose pleasure am I doing? Many times we may be doing things, but for whose pleasure? Whose satisfaction?"
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of introspection and examining the motives behind our actions, ensuring they are for Krishna's pleasure and not for personal glorification, and introduces the concept of Sankirtana as collective glorification of the Lord.

So Madhubanabhu in one place, he was mentioning that in spiritual life, Krishna is sentient, just like, you know, any other person. He has feelings, he has emotions, he's watching, he's knower of everything, he's there in our heart. He knows our intentions. Behind every activity, what is the motive? For whom are we doing? So, we should constantly introspect. What is the motive behind my, for whose pleasure am I doing? Many times we may be doing things, but for whose pleasure? Whose satisfaction? It could be for our own satisfaction. It could be for our own glorification. It could be for our own name and fame. I'm a bigger devotee, I'm a better devotee, I'm a stronger devotee, I'm a senior devotee, I'm an advanced devotee, I'm a pure devotee. One time, Srila Prabhupada wanted all the devotees in the movement to cooperate. He explains that, what is Sankirtana, this, I, I just made a note of it. Prabhupada explains, there is a Sanskrit sentence explaining what is Sankirtana. Bahubhir Militva Kirtayate Sankirtana. What is Sankirtana? Bahubhir Militva. When many come together, Kirtayati, and they glorify the Lord, it is called Sankirtana. And ours is a Sankirtana movement.

#### cooperation, unity, sankirtana
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=881
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "United we stand, divided we fall."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the importance of cooperation and unity in the Sankirtana movement, quoting Srila Prabhupada and emphasizing that the movement can only be destroyed from within through dissension and hypocrisy.

Sometimes when we are living in a community, community living has lot of advantages. Sankirtana is possible. Bahubhir Militva, coming together. Srila Prabhupada was once explaining purport of one particular bhajan, and there he explains that even though he is Lord himself, but he is saying, how many fruits can I distribute alone? So then he explains that even I, he was explaining to the devotees, this was in the US. So he is saying, even I, old man alone, how much can I do? Today because all of you are there, and you are helping me push this movement, I am getting the credit. And then Prabhupada quotes this word, bahubhir militva kirtete sankirtana. Then he says, everyone should come together, cooperate, to distribute this message of the Lord, to glorify the Lord. Alone, then he says, united we stand, divided we fall. Srila Prabhupada also said, nobody can, this movement will go on. Nobody can destroy this movement from outside, it can only be destroyed from inside, from within, dissension, quarrel, hypocrisy.

#### unity, envy, dissension
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=982
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The root cause of dissension is envy."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Srila Prabhupada's broader vision of unity among different religions and highlights envy as the root cause of dissension within the movement, emphasizing the need to overcome differences and work together.

So, then he says, let all of them come together, and then he says, let the Muslims, let the Christians. Prabhupada had a very broader vision. Sometimes, you know, even in the name of religion, we see a lot of fights, lot of conversion happening, because their understanding is, my God is better than your God. My religion is better than your religion. So everyone should follow my religion, my faith. But Prabhupada said, let all Christians, everyone come together, Muslims. Let us all glorify the Lord. And then he says, very nice expression, he says, just like in a garland, you see this garland, we have white flower, we have orange. Likewise, we have many colorful garlands. When there are many multicolored flowers, it looks more grander, more appealing, than just one flower. He says, let all of them come. Here also, you see, we are all, if we analyze ourselves, we have devotees from all different parts of the country. So we all come together, we all unite, we all serve the mission of Srila Prabhupada, united. And that will really take us, take this movement to the next level. Once there was a group of devotees, two group of devotees, they had some misunderstanding, some fight. And they reached, they informed Prabhupada that these two groups have got formed. And there is a lot of misunderstanding between the two. So Prabhupada said, no, they should give up their differences and try to come together and work together. Then the devotee says, Prabhupada, we have tried, but it doesn't look like they will be able to work together. So then Prabhupada said, the root cause of dissension is envy. Sometimes even we can become envious of our God brothers. Envious because somebody has some good qualities. Envious because somebody has better service. Envious because somebody is doing better than me. So this is root cause of dissension. This envy.

#### envy, material world, spiritual advancement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=1139
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the root cause why we have come in this material world is envy. Envy of Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker delves deeper into the concept of envy, stating that it is the root cause of our presence in the material world and a dangerous thing that can dry up our spiritual life, emphasizing the need for introspection and sincere service.

Matsarya. Even the root cause why we have come in this material world is envy. Envy of Krishna. Krishna bhulia bhoga vancha kare. So we have forgotten Krishna and we have wanting to envious of Krishna. Why Krishna is the enjoyer. And we have come in this material world. So likewise, envy is a very dangerous thing. Prabhupada says dissension, the root cause is envy. So we should always be little introspective. What is my motive for the service? Am I envious? We hear somebody doing better, somebody collecting better or somebody this thing. What happens in my heart? What is my feeling? So envy, just like Prabhupada explains, Krishna anxiety. The spiritual life, just by reading, just by memorizing verses, not necessarily we can make spiritual advancement. Not necessarily we can get vijnana, realizations. We can get knowledge. We can get information. We can memorize all the verses. But to get realization, we require divya jnan, hrida prakarshito.

#### sacrifice, anxiety, spiritual vision
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=1241
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "People are suffering there because of lack of Krishna consciousness. I am going there so that I can do some good to them."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of taking Krishna anxiety and engaging in authorized service, even if it's not pleasing to the mind, highlighting Srila Prabhupada's example of going to America out of compassion for those suffering from a lack of Krishna consciousness.

So for that, we have to take Krishna anxiety. Whatever authorized service, even if it is not very pleasing to the mind. Just like Lord Brahma. He has been given the task, create. But he doesn't know how to create. Complete darkness. So when we do our assigned service, take anxiety, there is an element of sacrifice. Madhubru in one place says, when we sweat for Krishna. When we sweat for Krishna. Sweat for Krishna means take anxiety, take pain. Draupad took so much of pain, so much of anxiety. He was very nicely settled in Vrindavan. Total spiritual environment. Samadhis are there. Radha Damodar Mandir. The deities are there. Kirtan is going on. Once when he was in Bombay, he was, he went to Sumati Murarji's. He was going quite frequently. Trying different things to somehow get passage to America. So his office staff members one time comment, Swamiji, such an old man, why do you want to go to America? Do you want to go and see America and enjoy there? Generally when anybody goes to America, there is an element of, Oh, it's a prosperous country. So much of sense gratification is easily available. So Prabhupada said, I'm an old man. What is there for me? My life is all over. Then he says, people are suffering there because of lack of Krishna consciousness. I am going there so that I can do some good to them. That's why. This is the mood.

That's why Prabhupada in the Jaluduttara, Oh Lord, why are you taking me to a horrible place, miserable place? What is material vision? Oh, prosperous country. What is spiritual vision? And then they took him to a place where they make chapatis. They have a very big langar. So he saw that, they took him around, and a lot of volunteers doing seva. Actually, there is some learning for all of us. In Amritsar, in Golden Temple, people come in Mercedes Benz and some big, big cars, rich people. But they will come and do some menial services. The service of collecting the slippers is in great demand. A person may park his Mercedes, he'll come and, you know, do the slippers service. Sometimes in our mind, this thing can come. And then when he tried to inquire, he said, you know, this service, only assigning to me, but later on when we dug, you know, my peers, they are not assigned this service. He's feeling, how can I be assigned this service? So these all are anarthas. We have to be watchful. Srila Prabhupada says, those who are envious will dry up. Our spiritual life will dry up. Those who are sincere, they'll flourish. So if we are finding our service boring, hackneyed, spiritual life very dull, somewhere we are short-circuiting. Otherwise, spiritual life is full of ananda. #### Krishna consciousness, material consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=1630
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Wherever Krishna is not there also, Krishna is brought in. Because always Krishna, what is there inside will come out."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Krishna consciousness with material consciousness, explaining that a devotee always brings Krishna into conversations, while material consciousness eliminates Krishna, and uses an analogy of squeezing an orange to illustrate that what's inside will come out.

Krishnaite. See, Prabhupada was at a very different level of consciousness. Always Krishna conscious. Always thinking of Krishna. What is material consciousness? Wherever Krishna is possible in a conversation, Krishna is eliminated. When we meet together sometimes, if we are not careful, we are meeting together, but we'll talk about something. Oh, this is happening, you know, India Pakistan war, it shouldn't have happened. Whereas a devotee, wherever Krishna is not there also, Krishna is brought in. Because always Krishna, what is there inside will come out. One time, in a class, a teacher was giving a demonstration to the students. She took an orange in her hand, and said, if I squeeze this orange, what will come out? So the student said, the orange juice will come out. So then, the teacher asked, are you sure? He said, yes. Why the orange juice will come out? So, the students started, you know, wondering, what kind of question is this? If you squeeze orange, the mango juice will not come out. Why this orange juice will come out? Then one of the students said, because there is, it is orange, and inside it, it is orange juice. What is inside, will come out when you squeeze it.

So likewise, Prabhupada also explains, that verse is there, bhajan karo, antakale, at the time of death, it will all be tested. What is inside, what is there in our consciousness, with whom we are attached, that will come out. So, we have to be introspective. What is going on inside? Am I envious? Am I jealous? #### Krishna consciousness, cooperation, servitude
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sA7dgJjc4rk?t=1808
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Be Krishna conscious, and try your best to make others Krishna conscious."
**Summary:** The speaker shares Srila Prabhupada's response to the question of what would please him most: becoming cent percent Krishna conscious and helping others become Krishna conscious, emphasizing the importance of cooperative effort and the mood of servitude.

So direct question, what will please you the most? And there are many quotes, we all, there are quotes like, if you distribute my books. Many instances, many contexts, Prabhupada has given, what will please. So, in this particular thing, Prabhupada says, if you become cent percent Krishna conscious. If you become Krishna conscious, that will please me the most. And then he says, be Krishna conscious, and try your best to make others Krishna conscious. ## HG Amitasana Dasa

### The Scam of Illusion (Maya) | HG Amitasana Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### illusion, suffering, desire
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qSayLmZ9Uus?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "That very pleasure which the fish is seeking becomes the cause of death of that fish."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains how Maya, or illusion, tricks us into seeking pleasure that ultimately leads to misery, using the analogy of a fish and a baited hook.

Maya's trap is, Maya's scam is which Krishna busts in the Bhagavad Gita. Krishna says that this very pleasure, this very pleasure—anyone would like to help me with the second line? The scam is, Maya fools us that this very pleasure which we so emphatically, enthusiastically seek, hoping that it will make me happy, that very pleasure becomes the cause of my misery. Now, here is a crude example you can relate with. Bhagavatam gives this example of a fish. Now, you see the fisherman, when he goes to catch a fish, what does he do on that fishing hook? He puts a bait and ensures that bait is a worm which is very, very tempting for the fish to eat. Now, fish gets tempted, it wants happiness, it is seeking happiness, it goes for that bait and that very pleasure which the fish is seeking becomes the cause of death of that fish. Today, if we are in a messy situation in life, if we do root cause analysis, work backwards, if I am entangled, hum Maya jaal mein phasay huay hain, if you work backwards, kaise phasay main, how did I fail, how did I get entangled, because we moved with that understanding, just like that fish, yeh mil jaye, I will enjoy like that, I will be very very happy, I will be very very excited and that very excitement, that very happiness became the cause of my entanglement. Agar hum log aaj phasay huay hain, some people can realize, we are all stuck, we are all entangled, phasay huay, Maya jaal mein phasay huay, how many of us believe Maya jaal mein phasay huay hain, please raise your hands, soch rahe hain log, karey nahi karey, you have time after the class you can think, why, if you do the root cause analysis, it's because of this understanding which makes us believe that, yes, this happiness I seek, with all enthu, with all this thing I go for it, and I get entangled.

---
#### transcendence, happiness, senses
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qSayLmZ9Uus?t=196
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Real pleasure, what will completely satisfy us, which we all are actually wanting to experience, is atyantikam, anta means ending, atyantikam means it is unending, and atyendriyam."
**Summary:** HG Amitasana Dasa explains that we are all pleasure-seeking beings, but real, lasting pleasure is atyantikam (unending) and atyendriyam (experienced through transcendental senses).

Sukham atyantikam atyendriyam, nothing wrong about seeking Sukham, we all are pleasure seeking beings, but real pleasure, what will completely satisfy us, which we all are actually wanting to experience, is atyantikam, anta means ending, atyantikam means it is unending, and atyendriyam. Atyantikam. That which we will experience from our transcendental senses, not material senses.

## HG Amitasana Dasa

### The Significance of Reading & Hearing | HG Amitasana Dasa |SB 1.12.3 | 2019-06-16
**Speaker:** HG Amitasana Dasa

#### hearing, chanting, Srimad Bhagavatam, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9m8J1hcpex4?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 1.12.3
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Transcendental realization is attained by such serious hearing and chanting and not otherwise."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the importance of chanting and hearing Srimad Bhagavatam, emphasizing that both the speaker and listener benefit equally from this process.

Shukadev Goswami imparted transcendental knowledge to Maharaj Pariksit during the remaining seven days of his life, and Maharaj Pariksit heard him properly, just like an ardent student. The effect of such a bona fide hearing and chanting of Srimad Bhagavatam was equally shared by both the hearer and the chanter. Both of them were benefited. Out of the nine different transcendental means of devotional service to the Lord prescribed in the Srimad Bhagavatam, either all of them, some of them, or even one of them are equally beneficial if properly discharged. Maharaj Pariksit and Shukadev Goswami were serious performers of the first two important items, namely the process of chanting and the process of hearing, and therefore both of them were successful in their laudable attempt. Transcendental realization is attained by such serious hearing and chanting and not otherwise.

---
#### bona fide spiritual master, hearing, chanting, unauthorized practices
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9m8J1hcpex4?t=60
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "One should not, therefore, become a victim of these unauthorized advertisements made by some bogus representatives of Vedic knowledge."
**Summary:** This segment warns against bogus spiritual masters who claim to inject spiritual force through electrical currents, contrasting this with the genuine process of hearing from a bona fide spiritual master like Shukadev Goswami.

There is a type of spiritual master and disciple much advertised in this age of Kali. It is said that the master injects spiritual force into the disciple by an electrical current generated by the master, and the disciple begins to feel the shock. He becomes unconscious, and the master weeps for his exhausting his store of so-called spiritual assets. Such bogus advertisement is going on in this age, and the poor common man is becoming the victim of such advertisement. We do not find such folk tales in the dealings of Shukadev Goswami and his great disciple Maharaj Pariksit. The sage recited Srimad Bhagavatam in devotion, and the great king heard him properly. The king could not feel any shock or electrical current from the master, nor did he become unconscious while receiving knowledge from the master. One should not, therefore, become a victim of these unauthorized advertisements made by some bogus representatives of Vedic knowledge.

---

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that ardent hearing from a bona fide spiritual master is the only way to receive transcendental knowledge, dismissing the need for medical performances or mysticism.

The sages of Naimisharyna were very respectful in hearing about Maharaj Pariksit because of his receiving knowledge from Shukadev Goswami by means of ardent hearing. Ardent hearing from the bona fide spiritual master did not escape them due to their hearing was more than enough to their benefit. The sages of Naimisharyna were very respectful in hearing about Maharaj Pariksit because of his receiving knowledge from Shukadev Goswami by means of ardent hearing is the only way to receive transcendental knowledge, and there is no need for medical performances or cold mysticism for miraculous effects. The process is simple, but only the sincere party can achieve the desired result. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta.

And Shukadev Goswami, Pariksit Maharaj in the last seven days of his life, he just heard Srimad Bhagavatam, Shravanam, nothing else. And Shukadev Goswami just spoke. So Prabhupada mentions that out of these nine processes, Shravanam and Kirtanam are the most important in the sense. All the processes, although are very potent, Prabhupada in one place says unless we have heard sufficiently about the Lord, even worship of the Lord in the temple, worship of the deities can become a burden.

It's a matter of time. And every day from one or two hours, we may not read or hear. But the daily basis is how to chant and not just think about it. It is a matter of time. It is a matter of time. One time, Srila Prabhupada was with his senior disciples and just to test their understanding, Prabhupada told them, do you really believe in Krishna? So all of them, you know, because they were disciples of Prabhupada, yes, Prabhupada. So then Prabhupada challenged them, have you seen Krishna? Why do you believe in Krishna? So it's not a very difficult question because all along they have heard. So one of them said, Prabhupada, we are chanting Hare Krishna mantra, which is Krishna himself. And when we are chanting the Hare Krishna mantra, our hearts are getting purified. Cheto Darpanam Arjanam. And when our heart will get purified, we'll be able to see Krishna face to face. So Prabhupada says, that means you have not seen Krishna as of today. It's blind faith. So then another devotee, he says, Prabhupada, our souls are not pure. Our senses are material senses, our blunt senses. The senses have to be purified and when the senses get purified, we'll be able to see Krishna. So Prabhupada says, that means you have not seen Krishna. It's blind belief.

We have faith in you. You are a bona fide spiritual master. Therefore, we believe Krishna is there. So Prabhupada says, I am an old man. You know, when people become old, they speak so many things. He says, I am an old man. I could be senile. I could be speaking so many things. Just because I am speaking, you will believe. That means it's blind faith. One old man is speaking and you are believing. So what to answer now? So one of them said, Prabhupada, no, it's there in Bhagavad Gita also. Krishna is saying, I am the Supreme Lord. I am the creator of everything. Everything is emanating from me. I am the supreme controller. Prabhupada says, how can you have faith in a book? Somebody would have written 300, 400 years back. So then Prabhupada says, we believe in Krishna because when we serve Krishna, when we chant the names of Krishna, we experience a sweet mellow. We experience sweet reciprocation of the Lord.

I was reading an article. This article is about a very special man. Special in the sense, he has broken one record. And the record is not very great record in the sense. He has watched one Bollywood movie. Very famous Bollywood movie of Shah Rukh Khan. He was a great fan of Shah Rukh Khan. So there is a fan club also. And this person, article came describing how much devotion he has. He has watched this movie 365 times. So in this interview, he is being asked, did you not get bored? Generally, you know, movie you watch 2 times, 3 times. If it is really great, some people have watched Sholay and all that for 5 times. Maximum 7 times. Here this person has watched this movie 365 days, 65 times. So then they asked him, can you see it again? He says, yes, I can. Now imagine one movie which has so much of masala. Songs are there. Comedy is there. And cameraman has really, you know, the whole director has done the whole direction in such a way that audience should not get bored. Lot of thought has gone in. What dialogues they should speak. When they should speak which dialogue. How the scene should change. So much of thought has gone in. So much of experience has gone in. In making that movie. Crores of rupees have spent on that movie. That movie I am talking about was shot in Switzerland. So whole team would have gone. And this person watched that movie 365 days. It has become a record. It has become a big news.

Two hours every day. Day after day. Day after day. One year. Two year. Three year. Five year. If it was any material song. Material sound vibration. We couldn't have chanted for so long. Take any good song. You know sometimes some song becomes super hit. Everywhere you go. Every color tune is that. Ring tones are that. Everywhere people are using that song. It is being played. #### Kshetra, Kshetragna, Body as field
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7M-mLHpRhfk?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Kshetra means field, this body is a Kshetra."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of the Supreme Truth from Bhagavad Gita, Chapter 13, verses 13-17, and reviews the concepts of Kshetra (field) and Kshetra Gna (knower of the field), explaining that the body is the Kshetra.

Today we are going to discuss from the 13th chapter, and the title for the discussion today is the Supreme Truth based on the teachings of Srila Prabhupada. This is text 13 to 17 what we are going to cover today. I shall now explain the knowable, knowing which you will taste the eternal. This is beginningless and it is subordinate to me. It is called Brahman, the spirit, and it lies beyond the cause and effect of this material world. Today these 3-4 verses are quite technical in nature, but I would like to explain them so that we can easily understand. If you remember, in our previous class we talked about Kshetra and Kshetra Gna. Kshetra means field, this body. This body is a Kshetra. Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc. Kshetra means field. This body is a Kshetra. Like for a farmer, the field of his activity, the land in which he tills the land, cultivates, grows the crops, etc.

#### Soul, Supersoul, Material elements
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7M-mLHpRhfk?t=94
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All what we see in this world is interaction of these twenty-four elements, the soul, super-soul, these twenty-four elements under the influence of Kala."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Kshetrajna, explaining that it refers to the soul and the Supersoul, and reviews the 24 elements that comprise the Kshetra, including the five gross elements, senses, sense objects, subtle elements, and consciousness, all influenced by time (Kala).

Kshetrajna. Kshetrajna is the soul. In this Kshetra, there are two Kshetrajnas. What are the two Kshetrajnas? The soul and the super soul. Now some of you were there, some of you were not there. The Kshetra comprises of 24 elements. Let's do a quick recap what those 24 elements are. So five gross elements, earth, air, water, fire, ether. Five senses, Karmendriyas and five Gyanendriyas. So we have five working senses and five knowledge acquiring senses. What we generally talk about senses is Gyanendriyas, eyes, ears, nose, tongue and touch skin. Through these senses we acquire knowledge, it's called Gyanendriyas. So we have five material elements. We have five Gyanendriyas and then we have five Karmendriyas, five working senses. Our hands, legs, genitals and mouth. And then we have five sense objects. Slip Swi- vector rule. And then we have five sense objects like for the eyes, ears and tongue. So we have those bones, tongue, aired areas where our senses are meant to go and we will know things through the senses. So when we bring the sense ofako Männer & sense ofoka Menada, we are ready for the middle and lower body and inner body. When we take notes, sense objects, when doing these centersk, when doing these satisfied senses. So it's painful to listen and For eyes, it is Rupam. For the tongue, it is taste. For ears, it is Shabda. For skin, it is Sparsha. And what is left now? For the nose, it is Gandha, smell. So it becomes twenty. Five sense objects, five working senses, five knowledge acquiring senses and five gross elements. Twenty. Then we have three subtle elements, Manas, Buddhi and Ahankara. That makes it twenty-three. And the twenty-fourth element is consciousness under the influence of the modes. Under the influence of contaminated consciousness, Rajoguna, Tamoguna, Satoguna is the twenty-fourth element. So these twenty-four elements and then there is soul and super-soul. And all what we see in this world is interaction of these twenty-four elements, the soul, super-soul, these twenty-four elements under the influence of Kala. Kala is time.

**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of people investing in an MBA for long-term gain to illustrate the principle of accepting short-term austerities for long-term good, and quotes 'Tapo divyam shudhyet brahma saukhyam tu anantam,' explaining that human life is meant for divine tapasya, which purifies consciousness and leads to eternal happiness, contrasting this with the meager material happiness available even to animals, and emphasizes the importance of understanding Kshetra, Kshetragna, and the Supersoul to relish nectar in this life.

I know many people, they are working in good MNCs but they stop working, they take a bank loan, 20 lakh rupees, get admitted into an MBA college. They have stopped earning, they have taken a break from their career, they have invested 20 lakh rupees, taken the pain of going to a college, studying 8-10 hours a day. Immediately it appears that they are going through lot of hardships. Yes or no? But long term it is good for them, beneficial for them. So even in our material life we see many times we, for a long term good, we are willing to work. For a long term good, we are willing to take some short term austerities. And in human form of life specifically, to enjoy this nectar, the real nectar, which is spiritual happiness, tapo divyam shudhyet brahma saukhyam tu anantam. It is said that human life is meant for tapo divyam. Tapo divyam means tapasya. What kind of tapasya? Divyam. Divine. Not concocted. Not one's own mind, one concocts some kind of a tapasya. Divine tapasya, recommended, authorized tapasya. For example chanting. It is a tapasya. At times we may not like doing it. We may not feel good doing it. At times it may look very boring. Tapo divyam, when we do that tapasya, what will happen? Shudhyet, we will get purified. All that Kshetra which has become contaminated, the senses are helplessly getting pulled towards sense objects. The consciousness has got contaminated. And the Kshetragna is struggling in that Kshetra. Tapo divyam shudhyet, what will happen? Shudhyet, we will get purified. And with that purified consciousness, what will we experience? Brahma sokhyam tu anantam. We will experience happiness which is untainted, which is lasting. And anantam, which is eternal. We are eligible for, we are qualified for, we have entitlement for enjoying that kind of happiness. But unfortunately, we have settled for something which is very very meager. And that material happiness, our Shastra says, it is available, if you analyze, what is that topmost material happiness which people at times even get into scandals. Recently we had Me Too. A lot of people, it's material happiness there. But that happiness is abundantly available even in other life forms. A dog is enjoying that kind of a happiness. The senses coming in contact with the sense objects in the material domain. So, here the Lord is saying, one can relish nectar in this life with proper understanding of Shetra, Shetragna and the Supreme Lord, the Supersoul, the Supreme Shetragna.

Now what about this Shetragna, the Supreme Knower, the Supersoul? Everywhere are His hands and legs. His eyes and faces. And He hears everything. In this way, the Supersoul exists. So the Supersoul, the Shetragna, the Soul is knower of only this field, this body. Whereas the Supersoul is knower of all the bodies, the entire creation, both material and spiritual. Abhigna Swarat. He is Supreme Knower. He is conscious of everything. Now how is it possible that a person knows everything? The example is given. Just like a sun may be situated in one place in the sky but it diffuses sunlight everywhere. You might fly to Delhi and then again when you look up on the sky you see that sun is there. The same sun in the sky is diffusing heat and light everywhere. Likewise the same Supreme Lord is present everywhere, in everyone, in every atom as Supersoul. Everywhere are His hands and legs, His eyes and faces and He hears everything. Material life consists of the understanding that eat, drink, be merry, enjoy life. We are not accountable for our actions. Who is seeing? So long as I can beat the law, I can go scot free, I should be happy. But a devotee understands that I might cheat the law. I might even apparently go scot free. I might be intelligent enough to break the laws and not get caught. But not with the case of the Supreme Lord. He is watching everything. So for a devotee, for a materialist, he is conscious that I should not get caught. For a devotee, he is more conscious of displeasing the Lord. He has that conscience which always keeps him on the right track. For a materialist, it will be very displeasing to the Lord. He is conscious of the pleasure and displeasure of the Lord.

#### Devotion, Accountability, Supreme Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7M-mLHpRhfk?t=950
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "I couldn't escape two eyes watching me."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story of three disciples given the task of eating an apple where no one is watching, illustrating that true realization comes from understanding the omnipresence of the Lord, who is always watching.

One time there was a Gurukul and typical Gurukul where the disciples go and get trained under the guidance of a Guru. So their training got completed, three of them. And generally we have, even in academics, after the session gets over, the academic session, we have an exam. So the Guru, just to test whether they have learned their lessons sufficiently well, he gave them a final test. And the test was, he gave each one of them an apple in their hand and they said, Go and eat this apple and come back. But only one condition you have to keep in mind, that do not eat the apple in the presence of anyone. Eat the apple in a place where no one is watching you. So the disciples, for them, they said, what a simple test. After two, three years of all the training, such a simple test the Guru has given. So one of them went the backyard of the ashram, Gurukul, there was a forest. And typically in forest you don't find human beings. He went deep in the forest, ensured that nobody is watching, ate the apple and came back. So he was, you know, big smile on his face, he has, you know, final exam he has cleared, he has passed. The second one, who was relatively more wise, more intelligent, he said, Maybe, it's not the human beings which are not watching, but there are many birds, many insects, they also have eyes. They also are living entities, they are watching. So he went deep in the forest, looked for a cave and when he entered the cave, it was pitch dark. And after entering the cave with a boulder, he made sure that the entry point of the cave is also closed and it was pitch dark. Even if there are some insects, there is nobody going to watch in that dark, this thing. He ate the apple and came back. Heart of heart, he had this poor fellow, the first one who had come, you know, he has failed the test. I have passed the test because I have eaten the apple when nobody was watching. And late in the evening, the third disciple, he comes back with a sullen face, very disappointed, apple still in his hand. And he was quite dejected that, you know, such a simple test, unfortunately I have failed that test. I couldn't find a place where I could eat the apple and nobody was watching. So then one of them asked, didn't you find a place? He said, wherever I went, I found, I couldn't escape two eyes watching me. So then one of them said, you could have come with me. I went deep inside a cave, it was pitch dark, you could have told me you are not finding the place, I would have taken you there. He said, I even went in that pitch dark cave, but still I could not escape those two eyes. So I have come back convinced that there is no place in the world where I can escape the eyes of the Lord. The Guru got up and embraced the disciple. You have actually understood the message, you are the realized one.

#### Devotion, Accountability, Supreme Lord, Impersonalism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7M-mLHpRhfk?t=1166
**Verse References:** BG 9.26
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord is Bhavagrahi. He is more interested in our Bhava, in our love, in our devotion."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a devotee's accountability stems from knowing the Lord is always watching, leading to a conscientious life, and emphasizes that the Lord is present everywhere, accepting offerings of love and devotion, even in the mind, and addresses the philosophical debate about the Lord's senses, clarifying that while the Lord is the source of all senses, He is without material senses, leading some to believe He is impersonal.

So what brings accountability in the life of a devotee? Why he becomes a good devotee, a sincere devotee? Becomes a thorough gentleman because he knows that the Lord is always watching him. He always has that conscience. Moment he does wrong, it signals him that yes, he had made a mistake. When he by mistake kills an ant, it feels bad. I shouldn't have done it. Now, the Lord is present everywhere. His hands are present everywhere. He is watching everything. He is conscious of everything. That's why in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna says, If you offer me with love, a flower, a fruit, a water, I will accept it. How will he accept? He is there, everywhere. We have examples of great devotees who have even done Manas Puja. Manas Puja means even worshipping the Lord in the mind, because they could not afford to do Puja with gross objects. That's why they said Bhavagrahi Janardana. The Lord is Bhavagrahi. He is more interested in our Bhava, in our love, in our devotion. And if we have that, He will accept it, because He is present everywhere, present in everything. He is conscious of everything. The Supersoul is the original source of all senses. Yet, He is without senses. Now, this is very intricate. He is unattached. Although He is the maintainer of all living beings, He transcends the modes of nature. And at the same time, He is the master of all modes of material nature. So, here it is said that the Supersoul is the original source of all senses. Yet, He is without senses. So, there are philosophers who say that, This is the word which says that Lord is impersonal. Impersonal means, He does not have a form. Does not have a body. He is not a person, because person means senses. He doesn't have eyes. He doesn't have ears. He doesn't have nose. He doesn't have tongue. He doesn't have a body. He doesn't have legs. He doesn't have arms. He doesn't have hands. In other words, He is impersonal.
